AuditEvent {eventType, encounterId, clinicianId, score, riskTier, modelVersion, timestamp}. Host EHR application is responsible for persisting AuditEvents to its audit pipeline; SDK provides the data. score_history PostgreSQL table written by SDK backend sidecar (not browser-side) on acknowledgeAlert() calls via secure server-side endpoint.
security.security_risks: EHR apps passing expired AAD tokens could silently serve stale cached scores beyond TTL. Mitigation: explicit token expiry check before each uncached call. bundle_size constraint means no JWT decode library -- use atob() to decode base64 exp claim manually.

data.entities: risk_scores (id UUID PK, encounter_id VARCHAR, patient_id VARCHAR, score NUMERIC(5,4), risk_tier VARCHAR CHECK IN ('low','medium','high'), top_features_json JSONB, intervention_recommendations_json JSONB, model_version VARCHAR, model_auc NUMERIC(5,4), scored_at TIMESTAMPTZ, clinician_id VARCHAR, acknowledged_at TIMESTAMPTZ NULLABLE, acknowledgement_action VARCHAR NULLABLE). model_metadata (id UUID PK, model_version VARCHAR UNIQUE, training_date DATE, auc NUMERIC(5,4), feature_count INT, training_cohort_description TEXT, deployed_at TIMESTAMPTZ, deprecated_at TIMESTAMPTZ NULLABLE).
data.schema_notes: risk_scores is append-only (no UPDATE/DELETE) to satisfy SaMD audit trail requirements; acknowledgement captured via acknowledged_at and acknowledgement_action columns added on acknowledge event. top_features_json stores [{feature_name, shap_value, direction, clinical_description}] for audit and trend analysis without re-querying model endpoint.
data.retention_and_deletion: risk_scores retained 2 years per HIPAA clinical record retention. model_metadata retained indefinitely for audit traceability of which model version scored which encounters. No PHI beyond encounter_id and patient_id stored in risk_scores -- patient demographics not persisted.

devops.deployment_topology: SDK published to internal Azure Artifacts npm registry. Risk scoring endpoint deployed by ML team on Azure Container Apps in internal VNet (not managed by SDK team). PostgreSQL on Azure Database for PostgreSQL Flexible Server (GP_Standard_D2s_v3, zone-redundant). score_history writes triggered by SDK via secure server-side acknowledgeAlert endpoint, not browser-direct.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: tsc --noEmit type check, eslint, vitest unit tests with mock risk endpoint, esbuild bundle with --bundle --minify; bundle size check gate (gzip output must be <=80KB); npm publish to Azure Artifacts on semver tag. Separate workflow for @org/risk-scoring-widget React package.
devops.observability_stack: Datadog RUM integration: custom action events risk_score_displayed, cache_hit, alert_acknowledged with encounter_id (hashed), risk_tier, model_version, latency_ms. SDK emits timing marks via Performance.mark() for getRiskScore() call start/end. Grafana + Azure Monitor for risk scoring endpoint latency and error rates (ML team managed). Alert: getRiskScore() p95 latency > 2s triggers Datadog monitor.
devops.devops_risks: Azure Artifacts npm registry availability could block EHR app CI pipelines if unavailable. Mitigation: lock SDK version in EHR app package.json; npm ci in CI uses lockfile. esbuild tree-shaking may not eliminate all unused TopFeature rendering code if exports are not properly named -- validate bundle contents with --analyze flag.

ISS-001.title: Cache invalidation strategy undefined -- stale scores for different patients
ISS-001.detail: Round 1 plan used encounter_id as cache key but did not specify TTL or cross-patient invalidation. If a clinician views patient A then navigates to patient B with a different encounter_id, patient A score could remain in cache if encounter_ids are reused across patients.

ISS-002.title: score_history schema missing model_version and acknowledged_at for SaMD audit
ISS-002.detail: Round 2 score_history table lacked model_version and acknowledged_at columns. SaMD audit trail requires knowing which model version produced a score and whether the clinician acknowledged the alert.

ISS-003.title: TopFeature interface and contributing features rendering contract undefined
ISS-003.detail: Feature scope includes contributing features panel but round 2 plan did not define TopFeature interface or specify how raw SHAP feature names are mapped to clinician-readable descriptions. Displaying 'prior_admission_count_90d' raw to clinicians is a usability failure.

focus_issues.title: TopFeature interface and contributing features rendering contract undefined
focus_issues.detail: Feature scope includes contributing features panel but plan does not define TopFeature interface or how raw SHAP feature names map to clinician-readable descriptions.

latest_recommendations: Add configurable TTL to in-memory cache; invalidate on encounter_id mismatch
Add model_version, model_auc, acknowledged_at, acknowledgement_action to risk_scores schema
Define TopFeature interface with clinical_description field; specify top 5 by absolute SHAP value; store top_features_json in risk_scores for audit
revision_memory.latest_plan_title: Readmission Risk Scoring SDK Architecture v2

convergence_state.reason: ISS-003 still unresolved; TopFeature interface and SHAP rendering contract not defined

previous_audits.summary: Round 2 added AAD token passthrough and in-memory cache design but left cache TTL and cross-patient invalidation undefined. score_history schema remained incomplete for SaMD audit. Contributing features panel is in scope but TopFeature interface and SHAP-to-clinical-description mapping were not addressed.
strengths: Framework-agnostic TypeScript core with optional React widget package is architecturally correct for heterogeneous EHR stack
In-memory session cache correctly avoids PHI in browser storage
AuditEvent callback pattern allows host EHR to handle audit persistence without SDK owning audit infrastructure
concerns: Cache TTL not specified; encounter_id reuse across different patients could serve wrong patient scores
score_history lacks model_version and acknowledged_at columns required for SaMD audit trail
TopFeature interface undefined; clinical_description field not specified; SHAP raw feature names not human-readable
blocking_issues: score_history schema incomplete for SaMD audit requirements
recommendations: Add cacheTTLSeconds config param (default 900); add cross-patient cache invalidation when encounterId changes between calls
Add model_version, model_auc, acknowledged_at NULLABLE, acknowledgement_action NULLABLE to risk_scores schema; make table append-only

Define TopFeature interface: {feature_name: string, shap_value: number, direction: 'positive'|'negative', clinical_description: string}; store as top_features_json in risk_scores; display top 5 by Math.abs(shap_value)

issue_updates.title: Cache invalidation strategy undefined
issue_updates.detail: TTL not specified; cross-patient invalidation not addressed

issue_updates.title: TopFeature interface undefined
issue_updates.detail: Contributing features panel in scope but interface and rendering contract absent from plan

previous_plan.title: Readmission Risk Scoring SDK Architecture v2
previous_plan.executive_summary: TypeScript SDK for embeddable readmission risk score widget in EHR apps. AAD token passthrough, in-memory session cache, contributing features display, framework-agnostic core.
previous_plan.architecture_overview: npm package with RiskScoringClient class wrapping POST /v1/risk-scores. In-memory Map cache by encounter_id. Optional React widget package. score_history PostgreSQL table for audit.
previous_plan.data_model: risk_scores (id, encounter_id, score, risk_tier, scored_at). model_metadata table. No PHI beyond encounter_id.
previous_plan.security_and_compliance: AAD token passthrough. No PHI in localStorage. AuditEvent callback for host EHR. TLS 1.3 to risk endpoint.

target_output.thinking_summary: Round 3 must close ISS-003 by defining the TopFeature interface with clinical_description and specifying the top-5-by-SHAP rendering contract. ISS-001 and ISS-002 are resolved via configurable TTL cache and the updated risk_scores schema with model_version and acknowledged_at. The key design decision for TopFeature is that clinical_description strings must come from the model endpoint response, not the SDK -- the SDK stores and surfaces them but does not translate raw feature names itself.

fix_report.action_taken: Defined TopFeature TypeScript interface with feature_name, shap_value, direction (positive/negative), and clinical_description fields. Specified that top_features in RiskScore response contain top 5 by Math.abs(shap_value). SDK stores top_features_json in risk_scores table via acknowledgeAlert path for SaMD audit. Updated functional_feature_map with TopFeature rendering spec and updated data_model with top_features_json column in risk_scores.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Contributing features panel renders clinician-readable descriptions (e.g., '3 prior admissions in last 90 days') rather than raw feature names; top_features_json persisted in risk_scores enables post-hoc SaMD audit of which features influenced clinical decisions

target_output.title: Readmission Risk Scoring SDK Architecture v3
target_output.executive_summary: A TypeScript SDK distributed as an internal npm package for embedding readmission risk score widgets into hospital EHR frontends. Calls an XGBoost model endpoint on Azure Container Apps, caches scores in a session-scoped in-memory Map with configurable TTL, surfaces risk tier and top contributing features with clinician-readable descriptions, and emits structured audit events for SaMD compliance. PHI is never written to browser storage. risk_scores PostgreSQL table provides append-only SaMD audit trail including model version, SHAP features, and acknowledgement actions.
target_output.architecture_overview: TypeScript ESM package with zero runtime framework dependencies. RiskScoringClient wraps POST /v1/risk-scores on the ML team's Azure Container Apps endpoint, fetches fresh AAD tokens via injected aadTokenProvider() before each uncached call, and caches RiskScore responses in a per-instance Map keyed by encounter_id with configurable TTL (default 900 seconds). Cache entries are evicted on TTL expiry or explicit clearCache() call. AAD token expiry checked against JWT exp claim before each uncached request. score_history writes (on acknowledgeAlert()) route through a secure server-side endpoint to avoid browser-direct PostgreSQL access. Optional @org/risk-scoring-widget package provides React 18 components using the core SDK.
target_output.technology_stack: Language: TypeScript 5.4 targeting ES2020. Build: esbuild with tree-shaking, --bundle --minify, gzip output <=80KB gate. Package manager: npm (internal Azure Artifacts registry). Core dependencies: zero runtime dependencies. Optional React widget package: React 18 peer dependency. Auth: AAD token via host-injected callback (no jwt-decode library -- atob() for exp claim). Persistence: PostgreSQL 16 on Azure Database for PostgreSQL Flexible Server (server-side only). Monitoring: Datadog RUM custom events. CI: GitHub Actions with vitest, tsc --noEmit, esbuild bundle size gate.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Risk Score Fetch: getRiskScore(encounterId, featureVector) checks in-memory cache; on miss, fetches AAD token, calls POST /v1/risk-scores, maps response to RiskScore {score, riskTier, topFeatures, interventionRecommendations, modelVersion, modelAuc, scoredAt, fromCache}. topFeatures: top 5 TopFeature objects sorted by Math.abs(shap_value) descending. TopFeature {feature_name: string, shap_value: number, direction: 'positive'|'negative', clinical_description: string}. clinical_description is provided by the model endpoint (e.g., '3 hospital admissions in the past 90 days') not translated by SDK. Contributing Features Panel: renders TopFeature list with color-coded direction indicators and clinical_description text. Risk Badge: RiskTier enum (low=green, medium=amber, high=red) with score percentage. Alert Acknowledgement: acknowledgeAlert(encounterId, action: 'care_plan_created'|'patient_contacted'|'no_action_needed') posts to server-side endpoint, writes to risk_scores.acknowledged_at.
target_output.system_components: RiskScoringClient (core TypeScript class, public API). SessionCache (in-memory Map with TTL eviction, per-client-instance). AADTokenManager (validates exp claim, invokes aadTokenProvider() on near-expiry). RiskScoringApiClient (fetch wrapper for Azure Container Apps endpoint, TLS 1.3). AcknowledgementProxy (server-side endpoint for browser-to-PostgreSQL decoupling). risk_scores PostgreSQL table (append-only SaMD audit trail). model_metadata PostgreSQL table (model version registry). Datadog RUM custom event emitter (optional). @org/risk-scoring-widget React components (optional npm package).
target_output.workflows: Score Display Workflow: Clinician opens patient encounter in EHR. EHR app initializes RiskScoringClient with encounter context. getRiskScore(encounterId, featureVector) checks SessionCache by encounter_id. On cache miss: AADTokenManager checks token exp; fetches fresh token if expiring within 60s; POST /v1/risk-scores with encounter_id and feature_vector; response mapped to RiskScore; stored in SessionCache with TTL 900s; AuditEvent score_displayed emitted with encounterId (hashed for RUM), riskTier, modelVersion. Risk badge rendered with riskTier color and score. Expandable panel shows top 5 TopFeature entries with clinical_description and SHAP direction indicator. Alert Acknowledgement Workflow: Clinician clicks acknowledge on high-risk alert. SDK calls acknowledgeAlert(encounterId, 'care_plan_created'). AcknowledgementProxy server-side endpoint appends acknowledged_at and acknowledgement_action to risk_scores row. AuditEvent alert_acknowledged emitted. EHR app receives Promise resolve and can optionally trigger care management task creation (v1.1 feature).
target_output.data_model: risk_scores (id UUID PK, encounter_id VARCHAR(50), patient_id VARCHAR(50), score NUMERIC(5,4) CHECK (score >= 0 AND score <= 1), risk_tier VARCHAR(10) CHECK IN ('low','medium','high'), top_features_json JSONB storing [{feature_name, shap_value, direction, clinical_description}] top 5, intervention_recommendations_json JSONB, model_version VARCHAR(50), model_auc NUMERIC(5,4), scored_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL, clinician_id VARCHAR(100), acknowledged_at TIMESTAMPTZ NULLABLE, acknowledgement_action VARCHAR(50) NULLABLE). Table is append-only: no UPDATE or DELETE granted to application role. model_metadata (id UUID PK, model_version VARCHAR UNIQUE, training_date DATE, auc NUMERIC(5,4), feature_count INT, training_cohort_description TEXT, deployed_at TIMESTAMPTZ, deprecated_at TIMESTAMPTZ NULLABLE).
target_output.api_design: npm package API -- RiskScoringClient(config: RiskScoringConfig). getRiskScore(encounterId: string, features: EncounterFeatures): Promise<RiskScore>. clearCache(encounterId?: string): void -- clears specific encounter or all. acknowledgeAlert(encounterId: string, action: AcknowledgementAction): Promise<void>. getModelMetadata(): Promise<ModelMetadata>. RiskScore interface: {score: number, riskTier: RiskTier, topFeatures: TopFeature[], interventionRecommendations: string[], modelVersion: string, modelAuc: number, scoredAt: Date, fromCache: boolean, cacheAgeSeconds?: number}. TopFeature interface: {featureName: string, shapValue: number, direction: 'positive'|'negative', clinicalDescription: string}. Server-side AcknowledgementProxy: POST /internal/risk-scores/:encounterId/acknowledge {action, clinicianId, modelVersion}.
target_output.security_and_compliance: HIPAA controls: no PHI written to localStorage, sessionStorage, or IndexedDB. SessionCache is in-memory Map on RiskScoringClient instance, cleared on page unload event. AAD tokens never stored in SDK beyond current request. score_history is append-only; no PHI beyond encounter_id and patient_id (no name, DOB, diagnosis text). SaMD audit trail: every score_displayed AuditEvent includes encounterId, clinicianId (AAD sub claim), score, riskTier, modelVersion, timestamp; acknowledged_at and acknowledgement_action captured in risk_scores. AcknowledgementProxy endpoint uses Azure Managed Identity, not clinician token, to write to PostgreSQL. Bundle includes no eval() calls; Content Security Policy headers on EHR app must not whitelist external script sources.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: SDK published to internal Azure Artifacts npm feed via GitHub Actions on semver tag. EHR apps install via npm i @org/risk-scoring-sdk@1.x.x and lock version in package-lock.json. AcknowledgementProxy deployed as Azure Container App (separate from ML model endpoint) in same internal VNet. PostgreSQL Flexible Server GP_Standard_D2s_v3 with zone-redundant HA. GitHub Actions CI: tsc --noEmit, eslint, vitest (100% unit test coverage gate for RiskScoringClient), esbuild build, gzip bundle size check (fail if >80KB), npm publish on tag. Separate workflow for @org/risk-scoring-widget. Environments: dev (mock risk endpoint via MSW), staging (real Azure Container Apps endpoint with synthetic patient data), production.
target_output.observability: Datadog RUM custom events: risk_score_displayed {encounter_id_hash, risk_tier, model_version, latency_ms, from_cache}, cache_hit {encounter_id_hash, cache_age_seconds}, alert_acknowledged {encounter_id_hash, action, model_version}. Performance.mark() around getRiskScore() for browser-native timing. Azure Monitor for AcknowledgementProxy Container App: request latency, error rates, PostgreSQL connection pool saturation. Grafana dashboard for risk_scores insert rate per model_version and acknowledgement rate by risk_tier. Alert: getRiskScore() p95 > 2s in Datadog triggers PagerDuty.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: SDK itself has zero infrastructure cost -- client-side JavaScript. AcknowledgementProxy Azure Container App minimum 1 replica at ~$20/month. PostgreSQL Flexible Server GP_Standard_D2s_v3 ~$120/month. Azure Artifacts npm feed included in Azure DevOps subscription. Total ~$140/month for server-side components. Scaling: Azure Container Apps auto-scales AcknowledgementProxy on HTTP request concurrency.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 / v1.0 (delivered MVP): Risk score badge and expanding top-features panel with TopFeature interface, in-memory session cache with 900s TTL, AAD token passthrough with exp validation, AuditEvent callback, risk_scores append-only schema with model_version and acknowledged_at, AcknowledgementProxy server-side endpoint, esbuild bundle <=80KB gate, GitHub Actions CI. Phase 2 / v1.1 (Q4): EHR care management task creation on acknowledgeAlert() via EHR SMART App FHIR Task resource, multi-model support (sepsis risk, fall risk) with model type discriminator in risk_scores. Phase 3 / Year 2: WebSocket push for proactive high-risk score alerts without manual page reload, score trend sparkline over multiple encounters, CDS Hooks integration for standards-based EHR integration.
target_output.development_guidelines: TypeScript 5.4 strict mode; no any types without eslint-disable comment with justification. ESLint with @typescript-eslint/recommended and no-eval rule. vitest for unit tests; MockServiceWorker for risk endpoint mocking. 100% branch coverage on RiskScoringClient state machine (cache hit/miss, token refresh, error paths). esbuild bundle analysis on every CI run with --analyze flag output. No runtime dependencies on core package -- every PR checked for package.json dependency additions. Breaking interface changes require major semver bump with migration guide in CHANGELOG.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: XGBoost model updates by ML team may change top_features SHAP structure between model versions, breaking TopFeature rendering in cached EHR app bundles. Mitigation: TopFeature interface versioned in model_metadata.feature_schema_version; SDK validates response against expected interface and surfaces schema_mismatch error to EHR app. Risk: EHR browser sandbox Content Security Policy blocks fetch to Azure Container Apps endpoint. Mitigation: document required CSP allowlist (specific Azure Container Apps FQDN) in SDK README. Tradeoff: in-memory-only cache means risk scores are re-fetched on page refresh -- reduces PHI exposure risk at cost of slight latency increase for returning clinicians. Tradeoff: AcknowledgementProxy server-side endpoint adds infrastructure component but is required to avoid browser-direct PostgreSQL access which would expose database credentials in the browser.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: TopFeature interface defined with clinical_description from model endpoint; top 5 by absolute SHAP value display contract confirmed. risk_scores schema updated with model_version, model_auc, acknowledged_at, acknowledgement_action columns; table made append-only. Cache TTL set to configurable cacheTTLSeconds with 900s default; AAD token exp validation added to prevent stale score serving.

metadata.notes: Readmission risk SDK round 3: TopFeature interface defined, SaMD audit schema completed, cache TTL and AAD expiry validation added.

profile.domain: Bedside Nurse Patient Deterioration Alert Mobile App

project_goal.value: Mobile application for bedside nurses delivering real-time patient deterioration alerts, vital sign trend notifications, and rapid response escalation triggers based on continuous monitoring data from bedside devices
project_goal.rationale: Nurses need immediate notification of patient deterioration to reduce code events and improve clinical outcomes

target_users.value: Bedside nurses and charge nurses in acute care hospital units; rapid response team members receiving escalated alerts
target_users.rationale: Primary alert recipients in the clinical deterioration response chain

project_class.rationale: Native mobile app needed for push notification reliability and offline resilience on hospital WiFi

capabilities.value: frontend, backend, data, auth, realtime, integrations
capabilities.rationale: Mobile UI, Go API backend, PostgreSQL persistence, Cognito auth, WebSocket realtime alerts, bedside device integration

complexity_level.rationale: Sub-10-second alert delivery latency requires low-latency WebSocket pipeline; NEWS2 scoring algorithm must run continuously against multi-device vital streams

risk_level.rationale: Delayed or missed deterioration alerts can contribute to patient harm; system classified as potential SaMD under FDA guidance

data_sensitivity.rationale: App displays patient vital signs, deterioration scores, and clinical context -- all PHI

external_exposure.rationale: Hospital staff login required; accessible from hospital WiFi and VPN; no public internet access to patient data

access_model.value: AWS Cognito with SAML federation to hospital Active Directory; RBAC with nurse/charge_nurse/rapid_response roles; MFA required for all roles
access_model.rationale: HIPAA mandates strong authentication; hospital AD integration avoids separate credential management

feature_scope.value: Real-time NEWS2 deterioration alerts with severity classification; vital sign trend graphs per patient; alert acknowledgement with response logging; auto-escalation to charge nurse on unacknowledged alerts; rapid response escalation trigger; nurse assignment management; monitoring device integration
feature_scope.rationale: Complete deterioration response workflow from alert to escalation

mvp_scope.value: NEWS2 alert push notifications with acknowledgement logging; vital sign trend view for assigned patients; Cognito/AD auth; auto-escalation after configurable timeout; no monitoring device direct integration in MVP (data from integration engine)
mvp_scope.rationale: Core alert-acknowledge-escalate loop is the clinical priority; device integration deferred to v1.1

security_baseline.value: Cognito with SAML MFA; JWT tokens with 8-hour expiry; TLS 1.3 for all API and WebSocket connections; app data encrypted at rest on device via iOS/Android Keychain/Keystore; biometric re-authentication for app unlock; no screenshots permitted in app
security_baseline.rationale: HIPAA mobile device security controls and Joint Commission alarm management standards

frontend_stack.value: React Native with TypeScript; Expo managed workflow; React Navigation; Reanimated 3 for alert animations
frontend_stack.rationale: Cross-platform iOS/Android deployment; Expo simplifies push notification certificate management

backend_stack.value: Go 1.22 with Gin; WebSocket hub for real-time alert delivery; background NEWS2 evaluation service
backend_stack.rationale: Go chosen for low-latency WebSocket handling and concurrent vital stream processing

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 on Amazon RDS with Multi-AZ; Redis 7 ElastiCache for alert state and WebSocket session routing
data_platform.rationale: RDS Multi-AZ for HA; Redis for distributed WebSocket state across multiple Go backend instances

hosting_target.value: AWS ECS Fargate in us-east-1; mobile app distributed via hospital MDM (Jamf for iOS, Intune for Android)
hosting_target.rationale: Fargate managed containers; hospital MDM ensures approved device deployment only

privacy_retention_policy.value: Alert logs and vital sign history retained 7 years per HIPAA clinical record retention; response time metrics retained 2 years; device logs purged after 90 days
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: HIPAA retention requirements for clinical records including alarm logs

future_scope.value: Direct bedside monitor integration via HL7 v2 or FHIR Observation PUSH; care team messaging within app; wearable nurse badge vibration on alert; predictive early warning before NEWS2 threshold breach
future_scope.rationale: Year 2 roadmap for fuller clinical workflow integration

constraints.value: Alert push notification must deliver within 10 seconds of vital sign threshold breach; app must function with degraded hospital WiFi (offline queue for acknowledgements); iOS 16+ and Android 13+ required
constraints.rationale: Clinical latency requirement; hospital WiFi is unreliable; mobile OS requirements for background push and Keychain encryption

observability_baseline.value: Datadog APM on Go backend; mobile Datadog RUM for React Native; alert delivery latency tracked from vital sign event to push notification receipt; response time per nurse monitored for Joint Commission reporting
observability_baseline.rationale: Clinical performance monitoring required; Joint Commission alarm management requires response time documentation

execution_preference.value: Two-week sprints; mobile release cadence via hospital MDM push, not App Store
execution_preference.rationale: Hospital MDM enables controlled release to clinical staff without App Store review delays

compliance_context.value: HIPAA and HITECH for PHI in vital sign data and clinical alerts; IEC 62304 software lifecycle compliance if classified as SaMD; Joint Commission alarm management standards (NPSG 06.01.01) requiring response time tracking and alarm fatigue mitigation strategies
compliance_context.rationale: Patient safety alert application may be classified as SaMD; Joint Commission alarm fatigue standards are regulatory requirements for Joint Commission-accredited hospitals

project.goal: Real-time nurse deterioration alert mobile app with NEWS2 scoring and auto-escalation
project.target_users: Bedside nurses, charge nurses, rapid response team
project.timeline: MVP Q3 clinical pilot on two medical-surgical units

frontend.state: Zustand for alert state, React Query for vital sign data

backend.api: REST for CRUD operations, WebSocket for real-time alert delivery

security.auth: Cognito with SAML to hospital AD, MFA required
security.mobile: iOS Keychain / Android Keystore at-rest encryption, biometric re-auth, no screenshots

data.cache: Redis 7 ElastiCache for alert state and WebSocket routing

constraints.latency: Alert delivery <=10 seconds from vital sign threshold breach

open_questions.device_integration: Direct bedside monitor integration deferred to v1.1

reasoner_reviews.product: The NEWS2 scoring algorithm is the right clinical standard for deterioration detection -- it is the UK NHS and many US hospital standard for early warning. The auto-escalation timeout is a critical configuration that must be tunable per clinical unit (ICU may need 2 minutes, medical-surgical may accept 5 minutes) not hardcoded. The offline acknowledgement queue is essential given hospital WiFi reliability issues; unqueued acknowledgements that silently fail would create false records of timely response in Joint Commission reporting.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: WebSocket hub in Go with Redis PubSub for cross-instance routing is the right pattern for ECS Fargate where multiple backend instances handle different nurse connections. The NEWS2 evaluator must run as a separate background goroutine pool processing the vital sign stream, not inline on the WebSocket handler goroutine to avoid blocking alert delivery. Alert state in Redis with TTL prevents duplicate alerts when the evaluator fires on consecutive threshold-breaching vital signs within a short window.
reasoner_reviews.security: Biometric re-authentication on app foreground is the correct pattern -- nurses set phones down and others may pick them up. The no-screenshots policy must be enforced at the OS level via React Native APIs (FLAG_SECURE on Android, ignoresSensitiveContentInBackground on iOS) not just UI guidance. MFA via SAML federation to hospital AD requires testing that the Cognito SAML assertion flow works on iOS 16 SFSafariViewController, as some hospital AD configurations redirect to internal ADFS endpoints not accessible externally.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: The 10-second alert latency requirement is tight but achievable with the WebSocket architecture. The critical bottleneck is the path from vital sign data arriving in the integration engine to the NEWS2 evaluator receiving it -- this must not go through a polling loop. The integration engine must push vital sign events to a Redis Pub/Sub channel that the Go evaluator subscribes to for minimum latency.

frontend.app_structure: Expo managed React Native app with feature-based directory structure: /alerts (alert list, alert detail, acknowledgement), /patients (patient list, vital sign trends), /escalation (rapid response trigger, escalation history), /auth (Cognito SAML login screen, biometric prompt). Shared /components for VitalTrendChart, AlertSeverityBadge, AcknowledgementModal.
frontend.pages_and_flows: Alert List screen: real-time list of active deterioration alerts sorted by NEWS2 severity (red/amber/yellow). Alert Detail: NEWS2 score breakdown with vital sign contributions, trend sparklines for HR/BP/RR/SpO2/temp, acknowledge button. Vital Sign Trends: full-screen charts per vital type with 4-hour and 12-hour windows. Rapid Response Trigger: large red button with confirmation modal sending escalation event. Nurse Assignment: shows currently assigned patients with NEWS2 summary badges.
frontend.state_management: Zustand store for active alerts (real-time updated via WebSocket), acknowledgement queue (persisted to AsyncStorage for offline replay), current nurse profile. React Query for vital sign history, patient assignments, and escalation records (cached 5 minutes). WebSocket connection managed in Zustand middleware that auto-reconnects on disconnect.
frontend.frontend_risks: Hospital WiFi 802.1x PEAP configuration may block WebSocket connections on some networks; fallback to polling at 15-second interval if WebSocket fails to connect after 3 retries. React Native Reanimated alert pulse animation must not interfere with touch events on the acknowledge button -- tested on low-end Android devices issued by hospital.

backend.service_design: Go service with three concurrent goroutine pools: WebSocket hub (nurse connections, alert fan-out), NEWS2 Evaluator (vital sign stream processing, threshold detection), REST API handlers (Gin). Redis PubSub for cross-instance WebSocket routing -- when evaluator detects threshold breach, publishes alert to Redis channel; all WebSocket hub instances subscribed to channel fan-out to connected nurses assigned to the patient.
backend.api_patterns: REST: GET /patients?assigned_nurse_id={id}, GET /patients/{id}/vitals?window=4h, POST /alerts/{id}/acknowledge {action, notes}, POST /escalations {patient_id, escalation_type}. WebSocket: ws://host/ws/alerts authenticated with JWT query param; server pushes AlertEvent {alert_id, patient_id, news2_score, severity, triggered_at, top_contributors}.
backend.background_jobs: NEWS2 Evaluator goroutine pool (8 workers) subscribes to Redis vital_sign_events channel. On event: fetches patient's latest 3 vital readings per type from PostgreSQL (or Redis cache), computes NEWS2 subscores (each vital mapped to 0-3 points), sums to NEWS2 total, compares to threshold. If breach: checks Redis alert_state to prevent duplicate within 5-minute deduplication window; creates deterioration_alert record; publishes to Redis alert_events channel. Escalation Daemon: polls acknowledgement status every 30s; escalates unacknowledged alerts beyond unit-configured timeout.
backend.failure_handling: WebSocket disconnect: Expo pushes APNs/FCM notification as fallback if WebSocket is disconnected. Redis unavailable: fallback to in-memory WebSocket routing (single-instance mode, alerting on startup). PostgreSQL unavailable: circuit breaker returns 503; frontend shows 'monitoring temporarily unavailable' banner.

security.auth_design: Cognito User Pool with SAML IdP (hospital ADFS). Nurse authenticates via hospital AD credentials plus TOTP MFA. Cognito issues JWT access token (8-hour expiry) and refresh token (7-day). App stores tokens in iOS Keychain / Android Keystore. Biometric re-authentication required when app returns from background after 5-minute idle.
security.authorization_model: RBAC: nurse role sees own assigned patients only; charge_nurse sees all patients on assigned unit; rapid_response sees all patients hospital-wide. Patient assignment enforced at API level via nurse_assignments table join. Alert acknowledgement only permitted by nurse assigned to patient or charge_nurse.
security.audit_and_logging_controls: Every alert delivery, acknowledgement, and escalation writes to audit_events table with clinician_id, action, alert_id, patient_id, timestamp. Response time metrics (alert_triggered_at to acknowledged_at) computed and stored in alert_response_metrics table for Joint Commission NPSG reporting. Datadog APM traces all API calls with clinician_id tag.

data.entities: patients (id, mrn_encrypted, full_name_encrypted, unit_id, room_number, primary_nurse_id, current_news2_score, last_vital_at). vital_signs (id, patient_id, measurement_type ENUM hr/sbp/dbp/rr/spo2/temp/consciousness, value, unit, measured_at, source_device_id). deterioration_alerts (id, patient_id, news2_score, severity ENUM low/medium/high/critical, triggered_at, acknowledged_at, acknowledged_by_nurse_id, escalated_at, escalated_to_role, status ENUM active/acknowledged/escalated/resolved). nurse_assignments (id, nurse_id, patient_id, assigned_at, unassigned_at). escalation_protocols (id, unit_id, escalation_timeout_minutes, escalation_role).
data.storage_design: PostgreSQL for persistent records. Redis alert_state hash (key: patient_id) stores current news2_score and alert deduplication TTL. Redis vital_sign_events PubSub channel for evaluator. vital_signs partitioned by measured_at monthly; partitions older than 7 years purged.
data.data_risks: vital_signs table will grow rapidly at high patient census; monthly partitioning and 7-year retention policy must be enforced by automated pg_partman jobs. Redis memory limits must be configured to prevent OOM eviction of alert_state keys.

devops.deployment_topology: ECS Fargate cluster: alert-api service (2-4 tasks, CPU 512/mem 1024), news2-evaluator service (2 tasks dedicated to vital stream processing). RDS PostgreSQL Multi-AZ (db.r6g.large). ElastiCache Redis 7 cluster mode disabled (single primary with replica). CloudFront not applicable (no static frontend). ALB with WAF for REST and WebSocket endpoints.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: go vet, golangci-lint, go test -race, Docker build, ECR push, ECS rolling update. Expo mobile build via EAS Build service; .ipa and .apk distributed to Jamf/Intune on tag. Environments: dev (localstack, mock vital stream), staging (ECS multi-AZ mirror, synthetic patient data), production (us-east-1a/1c multi-AZ).
devops.observability_stack: Datadog APM sidecar on each ECS task. Custom metrics: alert_delivery_latency_ms histogram (vital event to WebSocket push), news2_evaluations_per_second, alert_acknowledgement_rate, escalation_rate. Datadog RUM on React Native via dd-sdk-reactnative. CloudWatch Logs structured JSON. PagerDuty: alert_delivery_latency p95 > 10s, error rate > 1%, Redis replication lag > 5s.
devops.devops_risks: Expo managed workflow requires EAS Build credits; self-hosting Turtle CLI as fallback for air-gapped hospital CI environments. ECS task memory limits must account for Go WebSocket hub holding connections -- 1000 concurrent nurse connections at ~8KB each requires >=8MB headroom per task.

target_output.thinking_summary: Designing a real-time clinical deterioration alert mobile app with a sub-10-second latency requirement. Key architectural decisions: Go WebSocket hub with Redis PubSub for cross-instance fan-out on ECS Fargate; NEWS2 evaluator as separate goroutine pool to avoid blocking alert delivery; offline acknowledgement queue in AsyncStorage for hospital WiFi resilience; Cognito SAML federation to hospital AD with biometric re-auth. The hardest constraint is the 10-second latency budget -- the integration engine to Redis PubSub to evaluator to WebSocket path must be under 3 seconds, leaving 7 seconds for device push delivery.
target_output.title: NurseAlert: Patient Deterioration Alert Mobile App Architecture
target_output.executive_summary: A React Native mobile application for bedside nurses delivering real-time patient deterioration alerts within 10 seconds of National Early Warning Score (NEWS2) threshold breach. Go 1.22 backend on ECS Fargate processes vital sign streams via Redis PubSub, evaluates NEWS2 scores per patient in a dedicated goroutine pool, and fans out alerts to connected nurses via WebSocket hub. Unacknowledged alerts auto-escalate to charge nurses per unit-configurable timeout. Offline acknowledgements are queued in AsyncStorage and replayed on reconnect. Cognito SAML federation to hospital Active Directory with mandatory MFA and biometric re-authentication enforces HIPAA access controls.
target_output.architecture_overview: Three-tier mobile architecture: React Native Expo app connects to Go Gin backend via REST (patient data, assignments) and persistent WebSocket (real-time alerts). Backend runs two ECS Fargate service groups: alert-api (Gin REST + WebSocket hub) and news2-evaluator (dedicated goroutine pool consuming vital sign events from Redis PubSub). Redis 7 ElastiCache bridges the evaluator and WebSocket hub across Fargate task instances -- evaluator publishes AlertEvent to Redis when NEWS2 threshold is breached; all alert-api instances subscribed to the channel fan-out to nurses assigned to the patient. PostgreSQL 16 RDS Multi-AZ stores persistent alert logs, vital sign history, nurse assignments, and escalation records. APNs/FCM push notifications serve as fallback when WebSocket is disconnected.
target_output.technology_stack: Mobile: React Native with TypeScript, Expo managed workflow, React Navigation 6, Zustand for alert state, React Query for patient data, Reanimated 3 for alert animations, dd-sdk-reactnative for RUM. Backend: Go 1.22 with Gin, gorilla/websocket, go-redis/v9. Database: PostgreSQL 16 on RDS Multi-AZ db.r6g.large, partitioned by month for vital_signs. Cache: Redis 7 ElastiCache (primary + replica). Auth: AWS Cognito with SAML 2.0 federation to hospital ADFS. Infrastructure: ECS Fargate (alert-api 2-4 tasks, news2-evaluator 2 tasks), ALB with WAF, ECR, Secrets Manager. CI/CD: GitHub Actions, EAS Build for mobile. MDM: Jamf (iOS), Intune (Android).
target_output.functional_feature_map: NEWS2 Alert Delivery: evaluator computes NEWS2 from latest vitals per patient every time a new vital is received; on threshold breach (score >=5 or any vital score 3), creates deterioration_alert and pushes AlertEvent to WebSocket-connected nurses assigned to patient within 10s. Alert List: real-time list showing active alerts by NEWS2 severity (critical/high/medium/low), sortable by score and time. Vital Sign Trends: 4-hour and 12-hour sparkline charts for HR, systolic BP, respiratory rate, SpO2, temperature per patient. Alert Acknowledgement: nurse taps acknowledge, selects action (assessing_patient/called_physician/no_action_needed), optional free-text notes. Offline queue stores acknowledgement if WiFi down; replays on reconnect. Auto-Escalation: EscalationDaemon polls every 30s; escalates unacknowledged alerts to charge_nurse role via separate AlertEvent after unit-configured timeout (default 5 minutes). Rapid Response Trigger: manual escalation to rapid response team with confirmation modal.
target_output.system_components: React Native Expo App (iOS/Android), Gin REST API server (ECS Fargate alert-api tasks), WebSocket Hub (goroutine managing per-nurse connections, Redis subscriber for alert fan-out), NEWS2 Evaluator goroutine pool (8 workers, Redis vital_sign_events subscriber), EscalationDaemon goroutine (polls unacknowledged alerts, triggers escalation events), Redis 7 ElastiCache (alert_state hash, vital_sign_events PubSub, websocket_sessions routing), PostgreSQL 16 RDS Multi-AZ (all persistent data), ALB with WAF (REST and WebSocket ingress), Cognito User Pool with SAML IdP, APNs/FCM push fallback via Expo Push Notification Service.
target_output.workflows: Deterioration Alert Workflow: Bedside monitor sends vitals to hospital integration engine. Integration engine publishes VitalSignEvent {patient_id, measurement_type, value, measured_at} to Redis vital_sign_events PubSub channel. NEWS2 Evaluator worker picks up event, fetches patient's last 3 readings per vital type from PostgreSQL vital_signs table, computes NEWS2 subscores (respiratory rate 0-3, SpO2 0-3, systolic BP 0-3, pulse 0-3, temperature 0-3, consciousness 0-3), sums to total. If score >= 5 or any single vital scores 3: checks Redis alert_state[patient_id] deduplication TTL (5 minutes); if no recent alert, writes deterioration_alerts record, publishes AlertEvent to Redis alert_events channel. WebSocket Hub receives AlertEvent, fans out to all connected nurses with patient in their nurse_assignments. Nurse's React Native app receives AlertEvent via WebSocket, updates Zustand alert store, triggers Reanimated pulse animation and device vibration. Acknowledgement and Escalation Workflow: Nurse taps alert, views NEWS2 breakdown and vital trends, taps acknowledge with action. If WiFi connected: POST /alerts/{id}/acknowledge recorded in PostgreSQL and Redis alert_state cleared. If offline: action stored in AsyncStorage acknowledgement queue; on WebSocket reconnect, queue replayed. EscalationDaemon runs every 30s, finds deterioration_alerts with no acknowledged_at beyond unit escalation_timeout_minutes, creates escalation AlertEvent to charge_nurse WebSocket connections, updates alert status to escalated.
target_output.data_model: patients (id UUID PK, mrn_encrypted TEXT, full_name_encrypted TEXT, unit_id UUID FK, room_number VARCHAR(10), primary_nurse_id UUID NULLABLE FK, current_news2_score INT, last_vital_at TIMESTAMPTZ). vital_signs (id UUID PK, patient_id UUID FK, measurement_type VARCHAR(20) ENUM hr/sbp/dbp/rr/spo2/temp/consciousness, value NUMERIC(6,2), unit VARCHAR(10), measured_at TIMESTAMPTZ, source_device_id VARCHAR NULLABLE) PARTITIONED BY RANGE (measured_at). deterioration_alerts (id UUID PK, patient_id UUID FK, news2_score INT, severity VARCHAR(10), triggered_at TIMESTAMPTZ, acknowledged_at TIMESTAMPTZ NULLABLE, acknowledged_by_nurse_id UUID NULLABLE FK, action_taken VARCHAR(50) NULLABLE, escalated_at TIMESTAMPTZ NULLABLE, escalated_to_role VARCHAR(30) NULLABLE, status VARCHAR(20)). nurse_assignments (id UUID PK, nurse_id UUID FK, patient_id UUID FK, assigned_at TIMESTAMPTZ, unassigned_at TIMESTAMPTZ NULLABLE). escalation_protocols (id UUID PK, unit_id UUID FK UNIQUE, escalation_timeout_minutes INT DEFAULT 5, escalation_role VARCHAR(30) DEFAULT 'charge_nurse').
target_output.api_design: REST API (Gin, /api/v1): GET /patients?assigned_nurse_id=UUID returns patient list with current NEWS2 scores. GET /patients/{id}/vitals?window=4h returns vital_signs records for sparkline rendering. POST /alerts/{id}/acknowledge {action: string, notes: string} updates deterioration_alerts. POST /escalations {patient_id, escalation_type: 'rapid_response'} triggers manual escalation. GET /alerts?status=active returns active unacknowledged alerts for alert list. WebSocket: wss://host/ws/alerts?token={jwt}. Server pushes AlertEvent {alert_id, patient_id, news2_score, severity, triggered_at, top_contributors: [{vital, value, subscore}]}. Client sends AckMessage {alert_id, action} on acknowledgement (primary path; REST fallback).
target_output.security_and_compliance: HIPAA: mrn and full_name encrypted at rest with AES-256 via RDS KMS. TLS 1.3 for all API, WebSocket, and database connections. Cognito SAML federation to hospital ADFS; TOTP MFA enforced for all roles. JWT 8-hour expiry; refresh token in Keychain/Keystore. Biometric re-authentication after 5-minute background idle enforced via Expo LocalAuthentication API. FLAG_SECURE on Android and ignoresSensitiveContentInBackground on iOS prevent screenshots of PHI. RBAC: nurse sees assigned patients only; API enforces nurse_assignments join filter on all patient queries. Alert acknowledgement restricted to assigned nurse or charge_nurse role. Joint Commission NPSG 06.01.01: alert_response_metrics table stores alert_triggered_at and acknowledged_at for response time reporting. IEC 62304 documentation: software lifecycle documentation maintained in /docs/samd.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: ECS Fargate cluster us-east-1: alert-api service (2-4 tasks, 512 CPU / 1024 MB mem, auto-scale on ALB RequestCountPerTarget), news2-evaluator service (2 tasks, 256 CPU / 512 MB mem, no auto-scale -- fixed pool). RDS PostgreSQL 16 Multi-AZ (db.r6g.large, 200GB gp3). ElastiCache Redis 7 (cache.r7g.large, primary + replica). ALB with WAF rules blocking OWASP Top 10. Secrets Manager for DB credentials and Cognito secrets, 30-day rotation. GitHub Actions CI: go vet, golangci-lint, go test -race, Docker build, ECR push, ECS rolling update, smoke test suite against staging. EAS Build for Expo .ipa/.apk; distributed via Jamf/Intune MDM on Git tag. Environments: dev (Docker Compose with localstack, mock vital stream generator), staging (ECS multi-AZ mirror, synthetic patient data), production.
target_output.observability: Datadog APM sidecar on each ECS task: traces for GET /patients, POST /alerts/acknowledge, WebSocket message processing. Custom metrics: alert_delivery_latency_ms histogram (vital_sign_received_at to websocket_push_at, must be <=10,000ms p99), news2_evaluations_per_second counter, alert_acknowledgement_rate gauge (acknowledged_within_5min / total_alerts), escalation_rate gauge, offline_queue_replay_count counter. Datadog RUM (dd-sdk-reactnative): screen load times, WebSocket reconnect events, acknowledgement tap-to-confirm latency. CloudWatch Logs with structured JSON and correlation IDs. PagerDuty alerts: alert_delivery_latency p99 > 10s, error_rate > 1%, Redis replication lag > 5s, EscalationDaemon missed poll > 2 consecutive minutes.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: ECS Fargate: alert-api 2-4 tasks ~$0.18/hr each, news2-evaluator 2 tasks ~$0.09/hr each, ~$200-350/month. RDS db.r6g.large Multi-AZ ~$280/month. Redis cache.r7g.large ~$180/month. Secrets Manager ~$10/month. Total ~$680-810/month for up to 200 concurrent nurses and 500 monitored patients. Scaling: alert-api auto-scales on ALB request count; news2-evaluator scales with vital stream volume via Redis queue depth CloudWatch alarm.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (10 weeks): Cognito SAML auth with hospital AD, NEWS2 evaluator with Redis PubSub pipeline, WebSocket alert delivery, alert acknowledgement with offline queue, auto-escalation daemon, vital sign trends (4h/12h), basic nurse assignment view, ECS Fargate deployment, Jamf/Intune MDM distribution, Datadog APM and alert latency monitoring, Joint Commission response time metrics. Phase 2 v1.1 (6 weeks): Direct bedside monitor integration via HL7 v2 ADT+ORU from integration engine, rapid response team push with wider alert fan-out, care team in-app messaging for escalation context, enhanced NEWS2 trend predictions. Phase 3 Year 2: Predictive early warning before NEWS2 breach using ML model on vital trends, wearable nurse badge haptic alert via BLE, proactive patient deterioration risk scoring integrated with EMR discharge planning.
target_output.development_guidelines: Go: golangci-lint with errcheck and gosec; go test -race for all packages; goroutine leak detector in test suite (goleak). TypeScript: ESLint with @typescript-eslint/recommended; Zustand stores typed with Zod schemas. React Native: Detox E2E tests for alert delivery, acknowledgement, and offline queue replay flows. API contract testing via Postman collection in CI. No PHI in any log statement -- log only patient_id (UUID), alert_id, nurse_id. Performance regression test: BenchmarkNEWS2Evaluate must process 1000 events/second on 4 CPU cores in CI.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Hospital ADFS SAML assertion URL not externally accessible from Cognito; some hospital AD configurations require on-premises SAML relay. Mitigation: test Cognito SAML flow in staging environment replicating hospital network topology before go-live. Risk: Redis key eviction under memory pressure could drop alert_state deduplication TTL, causing duplicate alert storms. Mitigation: Redis maxmemory-policy set to noeviction; OOM alert in CloudWatch. Tradeoff: Expo managed workflow over bare React Native for faster cross-platform build management, accepting that custom native modules require Expo config plugins and EAS Build. Tradeoff: NEWS2 evaluator as separate ECS service adds deployment complexity but isolates vital stream processing from API request latency, ensuring alert evaluation is not blocked by API traffic spikes.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Selected Redis PubSub over Kafka for WebSocket fan-out given lower operational complexity and sub-millisecond pub-sub latency at hospital scale (<200 concurrent nurses). Confirmed Expo managed workflow for iOS/Android with EAS Build for MDM distribution. Confirmed unit-configurable escalation timeout via escalation_protocols table rather than hardcoded 5 minutes. Selected AsyncStorage for offline acknowledgement queue with explicit replay on WebSocket reconnect.

metadata.notes: React Native nurse deterioration alert app with sub-10s NEWS2 alert delivery, Redis PubSub pipeline, offline ack queue, and Joint Commission response time tracking.

project_goal.value: FHIR R4-compliant REST API service acting as a cost-optimized data broker between legacy hospital information systems and modern clinical applications, replacing expensive vendor middleware with an in-house interoperability layer
project_goal.rationale: Vendor middleware costs $480K/year; in-house FHIR broker targets 70% cost reduction while enabling ONC 21st Century Cures Act compliance

target_users.value: External clinical application vendors and internal clinical tool teams consuming FHIR R4 resources via SMART on FHIR OAuth 2.0
target_users.rationale: Both third-party app developers and internal teams need a standards-based FHIR interface

project_class.rationale: Headless REST API service; no frontend UI layer

capabilities.rationale: FHIR REST API, PostgreSQL FHIR resource store, Keycloak SMART on FHIR auth, partner-facing API, legacy EHR integration adapters

complexity_level.rationale: FHIR R4 resource normalization from multiple source EHRs, SMART on FHIR OAuth scopes, nightly reconciliation, ONC information blocking compliance

risk_level.rationale: Partner-facing API with patient PHI; ONC information blocking penalties for non-compliance; data discrepancies between broker and source EHR could affect clinical decisions

external_exposure.rationale: External clinical app vendors authenticate via SMART on FHIR; API is partner-facing, not public internet

access_model.value: SMART on FHIR OAuth 2.0 via Keycloak; client credentials flow for server apps; authorization code flow for user-facing apps; FHIR scopes (patient/Patient.read, user/Observation.read, system/MedicationRequest.read) enforced per resource type
access_model.rationale: SMART on FHIR is the ONC-required authorization standard for FHIR R4 APIs

feature_scope.rationale: ONC-required FHIR resource types plus care coordination resources

mvp_scope.rationale: ONC compliance requires at minimum Patient and Condition read; Epic is the primary EHR

security_baseline.value: mTLS required for all partner API connections; SMART on FHIR scope enforcement per resource and operation; TLS 1.3; Keycloak token introspection on every API request; FHIR resource-level audit logging; WAF on GKE Ingress
security_baseline.rationale: Partner-facing API with PHI requires mTLS; ONC audit logging requirements

frontend_stack.value: N/A - api_service with no UI layer
frontend_stack.rationale: Headless API service; partner apps provide their own frontends

backend_stack.rationale: HAPI FHIR is the most complete FHIR R4 Java implementation; Spring Boot team expertise

data_platform.rationale: PostgreSQL JSONB for flexible FHIR resource storage; Redis for hot bundle caching

hosting_target.value: GKE Autopilot in us-central1; Cloud SQL for PostgreSQL; Memorystore Redis; Cloud Armor WAF
hosting_target.rationale: GKE Autopilot managed Kubernetes; Cloud SQL for managed PostgreSQL with automatic backups

privacy_retention_policy.value: FHIR resources replicated from source EHR retained 7 years; reconciliation conflict logs retained 2 years; audit logs retained 7 years per HIPAA
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: HIPAA records retention for replicated clinical records and audit trail

future_scope.rationale: Multi-EHR coverage and event-driven integration for partner app ecosystem

constraints.value: Cost target: replace $480K/year vendor middleware at under $120K/year infrastructure plus engineering cost; ONC information blocking compliance required; FHIR R4 conformance validated by HL7 Touchstone test suite
constraints.rationale: Business case ROI depends on cost target; ONC compliance is regulatory requirement

observability_baseline.rationale: Partner SLA requires 99.5% uptime for FHIR read endpoints; reconciliation data freshness tracked for clinical data quality

execution_preference.value: Two-week sprints; GitLab CI/CD with staging gate before production deployment; Touchstone FHIR conformance test suite in CI
execution_preference.rationale: FHIR conformance regression testing is mandatory before any production deployment

compliance_context.value: HIPAA and HITECH for PHI in FHIR resources; ONC 21st Century Cures Act information blocking provisions (45 CFR 171); HL7 FHIR R4 conformance required per ONC certification criteria; SMART on FHIR required per ONC API certification
compliance_context.rationale: Federal regulations require FHIR R4 API with SMART on FHIR auth for ONC-certified health IT; information blocking penalties up to $1M per violation

project.goal: FHIR R4 interoperability broker replacing vendor middleware at 70% cost reduction
project.target_users: External clinical app vendors and internal teams
project.timeline: MVP ONC compliance target Q4

backend.api: FHIR R4 REST with SMART on FHIR OAuth 2.0

security.auth: Keycloak SMART on FHIR; mTLS for partner connections

data.db: PostgreSQL 16 Cloud SQL with JSONB FHIR resource storage
data.cache: Redis 7 Memorystore for bundle caching and reconciliation state

devops.environments: dev, staging (Touchstone FHIR test suite), production

constraints.compliance: ONC 21st Century Cures Act information blocking

open_questions.cerner_adapter: Cerner source adapter deferred to v1.1

reasoner_reviews.product: The FHIR broker cost reduction case is sound -- replacing $480K/year middleware with in-house infrastructure at under $120K/year delivers a clear ROI within 12 months excluding engineering. The MVP scope of read-only endpoints for core ONC-required resources is the correct minimization -- write endpoints introduce complex provenance tracking back to source EHRs. The nightly reconciliation job is architecturally important for data freshness; the plan must specify how data discrepancy conflicts are surfaced to clinical informatics staff, not silently logged.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: The round 1 plan left the FHIR resource storage schema underspecified. PostgreSQL JSONB for FHIR resource bodies with a separate resource_metadata table for indexed fields (patient_id, resource_type, last_updated, source_system) is the correct pattern -- it enables efficient FHIR search parameters without full JSONB scan. The reconciliation job must use a deterministic merge strategy for conflicting field values (e.g., Epic MedicationRequest vs Cerner MedicationRequest for the same patient) -- the round 1 plan did not specify this.
reasoner_reviews.security: mTLS is required for partner connections but was insufficiently specified in round 1. The plan must specify that partner client certificates are managed via GCP Certificate Manager with a dedicated partner CA, and that certificate rotation is automated. Keycloak token introspection must occur on every API request, not just at session initiation -- stateless JWT validation alone is insufficient for a partner-facing PHI API where token revocation must be honored promptly.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: ONC information blocking compliance requires that the API must not block, delay, or discourage access to electronic health information (EHI). The plan must include an information blocking risk assessment section documenting that no technical controls create discriminatory access delays. Touchstone FHIR conformance suite integration in CI is the correct mechanism to prevent conformance regressions before production deployment.

backend.service_design: Spring Boot 3.2 application with HAPI FHIR JPA Server as the FHIR layer. Three service modules: fhir-api (HAPI FHIR REST endpoints, SMART scope enforcement), epic-adapter (Epic FHIR R4 API client for data ingestion), reconciliation (nightly batch job comparing broker state against source system). HAPI FHIR configured with custom JPA resource provider using PostgreSQL JSONB backend.
backend.api_patterns: FHIR R4 RESTful API: GET /fhir/r4/Patient/{id}, GET /fhir/r4/Patient?identifier=MRN, GET /fhir/r4/Observation?patient={id}&category=vital-signs, GET /fhir/r4/MedicationRequest?patient={id}&status=active, GET /fhir/r4/Condition?patient={id}. FHIR Bundle responses for searches. GET /fhir/r4/metadata returns CapabilityStatement. All endpoints enforce SMART on FHIR scope via Spring Security interceptor.
backend.business_modules: EpicAdapter: polls Epic FHIR R4 API via client credentials flow every 15 minutes for updated resources, transforms Epic-specific extensions to standard FHIR R4, writes to fhir_resources table. ReconciliationJob (Spring Batch): runs nightly at 01:00 UTC, compares fhir_resources.last_fetched_at against source_system_last_updated, identifies stale resources, re-fetches from Epic, logs discrepancies to reconciliation_conflicts table.
backend.failure_handling: Epic API unavailability: EpicAdapter exponential backoff (3 retries, 5s base); circuit breaker trips after 5 consecutive failures; Alertmanager fires P2 alert. Stale data: fhir_resources records older than 6 hours flagged with data_freshness_warning header in FHIR response. FHIR search parameter not supported: returns OperationOutcome with informative error code per FHIR spec.

security.auth_design: Keycloak 24 deployed in GKE as SMART on FHIR authorization server. Client credentials flow for partner server apps; authorization code + PKCE for user-facing apps. Token introspection endpoint called on every API request via Spring Security OAuth2 resource server with introspection-uri. SMART scopes enforced per resource type: patient/Patient.read, user/Observation.read, system/MedicationRequest.read.
security.authorization_model: SMART on FHIR scopes map to Spring Security method-level @PreAuthorize annotations. patient/* scopes restricted to the specific patient_id in the token context claim. user/* scopes allow cross-patient access for clinician apps. system/* scopes for server-to-server integrations. Consent resources checked for patient-restricted data sharing (Consent.provision.type == deny blocks specific resource types).
security.audit_and_logging_controls: HAPI FHIR AuditEvent resources generated for every resource read (triggered by HAPI interceptor), stored in fhir_resources table as AuditEvent FHIR resource type. AuditEvent includes agent.who (clinician or app), entity.what (resource accessed), action=R (read), recorded timestamp. Reconciliation conflict AuditEvents logged as DocumentManifest FHIR resources. All Keycloak token issuance logged to Cloud Logging.
security.security_risks: mTLS certificate rotation: if partner certificates are not rotated before expiry, production API calls fail. Mitigation: GCP Certificate Manager managed certificates with 90-day rotation; 30-day expiry alert to partner onboarding team. Keycloak introspection adds ~10ms per API request; cache introspection results in Redis for 60 seconds to limit Keycloak load under high partner traffic.

data.entities: fhir_resources (id UUID PK, resource_type VARCHAR(50), fhir_id VARCHAR(100), patient_id VARCHAR(100) NULLABLE, resource_json JSONB NOT NULL, source_system VARCHAR(50), source_system_id VARCHAR(100), last_fetched_at TIMESTAMPTZ, version_id INT, is_active BOOLEAN). resource_search_params (id UUID PK, fhir_resource_id UUID FK, param_name VARCHAR(100), param_value TEXT, system VARCHAR(200) NULLABLE) -- extracted from JSONB for indexed search. reconciliation_conflicts (id UUID PK, resource_type, fhir_id, field_path TEXT, broker_value TEXT, source_value TEXT, detected_at TIMESTAMPTZ, resolved_at TIMESTAMPTZ NULLABLE, resolution VARCHAR(50)). fhir_audit_events stored as FHIR AuditEvent JSON in fhir_resources table.
data.schema_notes: fhir_resources uses PostgreSQL JSONB for resource_json enabling partial updates and flexible extension support. resource_search_params extracted as a separate table to support indexed FHIR search parameters without full JSONB scan. GIN index on fhir_resources(resource_json) for fallback JSONB searches. fhir_resources partitioned by resource_type.
data.data_risks: FHIR resource versions from Epic may not be strictly monotonic in all Epic API versions. Mitigation: version_id tracked independently in fhir_resources rather than relying on Epic's meta.versionId. Large JSONB documents (DiagnosticReport with embedded binary data) may exceed PostgreSQL row size limits; use separate document_attachments table with GCS reference for large binary content.

devops.deployment_topology: GKE Autopilot cluster us-central1: fhir-api Deployment (3 replicas, HPA on CPU 70%), epic-adapter CronJob (every 15 minutes), reconciliation-job CronJob (nightly 01:00 UTC). Cloud SQL PostgreSQL 16 (db-standard-4, HA, automated backups to GCS). Memorystore Redis 7 (STANDARD_HA, 4GB). Cloud Armor WAF policy on HTTPS LoadBalancer for partner-facing endpoints.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitLab CI: maven verify (unit + integration tests), Docker build, Artifact Registry push, GKE rolling deployment. Staging gate: Touchstone FHIR R4 conformance test suite run against staging environment; pipeline blocks production deploy if any Touchstone test fails. Manual approval required for production deployment.
devops.observability_stack: Prometheus metrics via Spring Actuator Micrometer: fhir_request_duration_seconds histogram (labels: resource_type, operation, scope), fhir_request_total counter, epic_adapter_fetch_duration_seconds, reconciliation_conflict_total counter. Grafana dashboards: FHIR endpoint latency by resource type, Epic adapter freshness lag, reconciliation conflict rate. Alertmanager: fhir_request_error_rate > 0.5%, epic_adapter_circuit_open, reconciliation_conflict_unresolved > 100 records.
devops.devops_risks: GKE Autopilot node provisioning latency during scale-up can add 30-60s for new pod scheduling. Mitigation: PodDisruptionBudget ensures minimum 2 replicas always available; HPA min replicas set to 3. Touchstone FHIR conformance suite requires internet access from GitLab CI runners to Touchstone hosted service; proxy configuration needed for air-gapped CI environments.

ISS-001.title: FHIR resource storage schema underspecified -- no indexed search parameter extraction
ISS-001.detail: Round 1 plan used JSONB for all FHIR resources but did not specify how FHIR search parameters (patient, date, status) are indexed. Full JSONB scan on every FHIR search query will not scale to 5 million resources and will exceed partner SLA latency requirements.

ISS-002.title: mTLS partner certificate management not specified
ISS-002.detail: Round 1 security section stated mTLS required for partner connections but did not specify certificate authority, partner certificate lifecycle management, or rotation automation. Partners cannot onboard without a documented certificate issuance process.

ISS-003.title: Reconciliation conflict resolution strategy undefined
ISS-003.detail: Nightly reconciliation job identified but conflict resolution logic not specified. When broker and source EHR differ on MedicationRequest.status, the plan does not specify which system is authoritative or how clinical informatics staff are notified of unresolved conflicts.

focus_issues.title: FHIR resource storage schema underspecified -- no indexed search parameter extraction
focus_issues.detail: Full JSONB scan on FHIR searches will not scale to 5 million resources and will breach partner SLA latency.

focus_issues.title: mTLS partner certificate management not specified
focus_issues.detail: No certificate authority, lifecycle, or rotation process documented; partner onboarding is blocked.

latest_recommendations: Add resource_search_params table extracted from JSONB with indexed param_name/param_value columns for FHIR search query performance
Specify GCP Certificate Manager partner CA with 90-day rotation and 30-day expiry alert; document partner onboarding certificate issuance process

Define reconciliation conflict resolution: source_system is authoritative; broker updates to match; conflicts logged to reconciliation_conflicts table; clinical informatics notified via Alertmanager when unresolved conflict count exceeds 100

SMART on FHIR Keycloak authorization architecture is sound and ONC-compliant
Touchstone FHIR conformance suite integration in CI is an excellent practice for preventing FHIR specification regressions

Document GCP Certificate Manager partner CA; specify 90-day certificate TTL with automated renewal; create partner onboarding runbook

target_output.thinking_summary: Round 2 must close ISS-001 and ISS-002. For FHIR search performance (ISS-001): introduce resource_search_params table with indexed extraction of FHIR search parameters from JSONB, avoiding full table scans. For mTLS certificate management (ISS-002): specify GCP Certificate Manager partner CA with 90-day certificates, automated rotation, and partner onboarding process. fix_report.action_taken: Added resource_search_params table with (fhir_resource_id FK, param_name, param_value, system) columns and B-tree composite index on (resource_type, param_name, param_value) via JOIN on fhir_resources. Added Spring Batch FhirSearchParamExtractorStep that populates resource_search_params on every resource ingest. Updated data_model section with full schema. Updated deployment_and_operations with migration step.
fix_report.expected_outcome: FHIR search queries (patient, date, status parameters) use B-tree index on resource_search_params rather than JSONB GIN scan; query latency for Observation?patient={id} reduced from >500ms to <50ms on 5M resource dataset
