previous_plan.title: OR Surgical Scheduling and Preference Card System v1
previous_plan.executive_summary: A Next.js and FastAPI web application replacing the vendor OR scheduling system with surgical case management, preference card CRUD, and basic supply utilization logging on Azure App Service and PostgreSQL.
previous_plan.data_model: surgical_cases (id, patient_mrn_encrypted, procedure_code, surgeon_id, or_room_id, scheduled_start, status). preference_cards (id, surgeon_id, procedure_code, supply_items JSONB). supply_utilization_records (id, case_id, supply_item_id, planned_qty, actual_qty, logged_at). or_rooms (id, room_name, room_type). NOTE: preference_cards lacked version tracking columns.
previous_plan.architecture_overview: Next.js 15 SSR frontend and FastAPI backend on Azure App Service, PostgreSQL for scheduling data, Azure AD SSO, Epic FHIR R4 for patient lookups. No preference card versioning or Epic caching strategy defined.
previous_plan.security_and_compliance: Azure AD MFA, RBAC with 5 roles, TLS 1.3, no PHI in logs, HIPAA compliance noted.

target_output.thinking_summary: Round 2 revision addressing ISS-001 (preference card versioning) as the blocking issue. Adding version INT, effective_from DATE, and is_current BOOLEAN to preference_cards table, and introducing a supply_utilization_records foreign key to card_id (the card version active at case date) so OR managers can accurately attribute variance to the exact preference card version in use. Also resolving ISS-002 by adding Redis caching for Epic FHIR patient lookups with 5-minute TTL and a circuit breaker pattern for Epic API unavailability.

fix_report.action_taken: Added version INT, effective_from DATE, and is_current BOOLEAN to preference_cards table. supply_utilization_records now carries card_id FK to capture the exact preference card version active at case date. Variance analytics now joins supply_utilization_records to the historical card version for accurate attribution.
fix_report.expected_outcome: OR managers can now view per-case supply variance attributed to the correct historical preference card version, enabling accurate identification of which version introduced waste patterns.

fix_report.action_taken: Added Redis caching layer for Epic FHIR R4 patient search results with 5-minute TTL. Added circuit breaker (3 failures in 30 seconds opens circuit for 60 seconds) in the EHR proxy router. Degraded mode returns cached results or shows coordinator a manual patient entry fallback form.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Peak morning scheduling load of 200+ patient lookups handled without Epic FHIR throttling; coordinator workflow uninterrupted even during brief Epic API unavailability.

target_output.title: OR Surgical Scheduling and Preference Card Management System
target_output.executive_summary: A HIPAA-compliant web application replacing a $280,000/year vendor OR scheduling system, built with Next.js 15 and FastAPI on Azure App Service. Manages surgical case scheduling with OR block management, versioned surgeon preference cards, post-case supply utilization logging, and weekly variance analytics. Integrates with Epic EHR via FHIR R4 for patient and procedure data. Preference card version history enables accurate per-case supply waste attribution for OR manager cost reduction initiatives.
target_output.architecture_overview: Next.js 15 App Router frontend with SSR for OR block schedule calendar views communicates via HTTPS to a FastAPI backend on Azure App Service. FastAPI connects to PostgreSQL 16 on Azure Flexible Server for scheduling and preference card data, and Azure Cache for Redis for session tokens and Epic FHIR patient lookup caching. Authentication flows through Azure AD MSAL with MFA enforced via Conditional Access. Epic EHR FHIR R4 integration handled via a FastAPI EHR proxy router with Redis caching (5-minute TTL) and circuit breaker for API unavailability. All patient-linked case records stored with encrypted MRN fields; no PHI written to application logs.
target_output.technology_stack: Frontend: Next.js 15, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, React Query, Zustand, MSAL React. Backend: Python 3.12, FastAPI, SQLAlchemy 2.0 async, Alembic, python-jose for JWT validation. Database: PostgreSQL 16 on Azure Flexible Server (zone-redundant HA). Cache: Azure Cache for Redis C1. Container: Docker on Azure App Service P2v3. CI/CD: GitHub Actions with ACR. Monitoring: Azure Monitor, Application Insights, PagerDuty.
target_output.functional_feature_map: OR Block Schedule Calendar: SSR-rendered week and day views showing room assignments, case durations, and surgeon block allocations with drag-and-drop case placement and conflict detection. Case Request Workflow: coordinator submits procedure, surgeon, room, and date; system validates block availability and instrument tray readiness; Epic FHIR R4 patient lookup with Redis cache. Versioned Preference Card Management: surgeon creates and updates supply item lists per procedure code; each save creates a new version with effective_from date; is_current flag tracks active version. Post-Case Supply Logger: scrub tech records actual supply quantities used; system captures card_id of the version active at case date for accurate variance. Analytics Dashboard: weekly variance heatmap (planned vs actual) per surgeon per procedure, top waste items trending over 12 weeks.
target_output.system_components: Azure App Service P2v3: Docker container running Next.js (port 3000) and FastAPI (port 8000) behind Azure-managed TLS. Azure Database for PostgreSQL Flexible Server: zone-redundant HA, 4 vCores, automated backups with 7-day PITR. Azure Cache for Redis C1: session token storage and Epic FHIR patient lookup cache (5-minute TTL). Azure Container Registry: Docker image versioning. Epic FHIR R4 API: patient demographics and case procedure lookup via EHR proxy router with circuit breaker. Azure AD: SSO and MFA via Conditional Access policy. Application Insights: APM for both Next.js and FastAPI. Azure Log Analytics: structured JSON log aggregation.
target_output.workflows: Surgical Case Scheduling: OR coordinator opens the block schedule calendar, selects an available room slot for the desired date, initiates a case request form. Epic patient lookup fires (Redis cache checked first; TTL 5 minutes; circuit breaker opens after 3 failures in 30 seconds with manual entry fallback). Coordinator selects procedure code, system retrieves the active preference card version for that surgeon/procedure and displays required instruments and supplies. SchedulingEngine validates no OR room overlap, checks surgeon block time allocation, and confirms instrument tray availability. Case status set to scheduled; surgeon receives notification. Post-Case Variance Logging: After case completion, scrub tech opens the case in the app, sees the preference card items and planned quantities from the card_id active at scheduling time. Tech enters actual quantities used. supply_utilization_records row created with case_id, card_id (historical version), planned_qty, actual_qty, variance. Weekly analytics job aggregates variance by surgeon, procedure, and supply item for the OR manager dashboard.
target_output.data_model: surgical_cases (id UUID PK, patient_mrn_encrypted VARCHAR, procedure_code CPT VARCHAR, surgeon_id UUID FK, or_room_id UUID FK, scheduled_start TIMESTAMP, scheduled_duration_minutes INT, status ENUM requested/scheduled/in_progress/completed/cancelled, created_by UUID FK, created_at TIMESTAMP). preference_cards (id UUID PK, surgeon_id UUID FK, procedure_code VARCHAR, version INT NOT NULL, effective_from DATE NOT NULL, is_current BOOLEAN NOT NULL, created_by UUID FK, created_at TIMESTAMP). preference_card_items (id UUID PK, card_id UUID FK REFERENCES preference_cards, supply_item_id UUID FK, quantity INT, unit VARCHAR, substitution_allowed BOOLEAN). supply_utilization_records (id UUID PK, case_id UUID FK REFERENCES surgical_cases, card_id UUID FK REFERENCES preference_cards, supply_item_id UUID FK, planned_qty INT, actual_qty INT, variance INT GENERATED, logged_by UUID FK, logged_at TIMESTAMP). or_rooms (id UUID PK, room_name VARCHAR, room_type ENUM main/endo/hybrid, active BOOLEAN). supply_items (id UUID PK, item_name VARCHAR, catalog_number VARCHAR, vendor_id UUID FK, unit_cost NUMERIC). vendors (id UUID PK, vendor_name VARCHAR, contract_number VARCHAR).
target_output.api_design: RESTful FastAPI with OpenAPI 3.0. POST /api/v1/cases (create case request, idempotency-key header), GET /api/v1/cases?date=2026-03-25&room_id= (schedule for date/room), PATCH /api/v1/cases/{id}/status (update case status). GET /api/v1/preference-cards?surgeon_id=&procedure_code= (active card), POST /api/v1/preference-cards (create new version), GET /api/v1/preference-cards/{id}/history (all versions). POST /api/v1/supply-utilization (post-case log submission). GET /api/v1/analytics/variance?surgeon_id=&weeks=12 (weekly variance report). GET /api/v1/ehr/patients?mrn= (Epic FHIR proxy, Redis-cached). All routes require Azure AD JWT; role checked per route in FastAPI dependencies.
target_output.security_and_compliance: HIPAA controls: patient_mrn field encrypted at application layer before database storage (AES-256 via Azure Key Vault key). Azure AD Conditional Access enforces MFA for all users. TLS 1.3 on all Azure App Service endpoints. No PHI (MRN, patient name, DOB) written to structured application logs or Application Insights telemetry. Audit log table captures every write to surgical_cases and supply_utilization_records with user_id, action, and timestamp. Azure Defender for PostgreSQL monitors for anomalous query patterns. HIPAA BAA in place with Azure. Joint Commission OR case documentation requirements met via complete case status history and preference card version linkage.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions CI: pytest (80% coverage gate on FastAPI routes), ESLint and TypeScript type check, Next.js build, Docker image build and push to Azure Container Registry. CD: Azure App Service deployment slots (staging slot receives new image, smoke tests run, then swap to production). Environments: dev (Azure App Service B2 with shared PostgreSQL dev database), staging (production-equivalent P2v3 with anonymized PostgreSQL clone), production (P2v3 zone-redundant App Service, PostgreSQL Flexible Server with zone-redundant HA, Redis C1). Alembic migrations run as pre-deployment step with down-migration scripts tested in staging. Terraform manages Azure resources; infrastructure PRs require DevOps approval.
target_output.observability: Azure Monitor: App Service request rate, CPU, memory, and HTTP 5xx rate. Application Insights: distributed traces across Next.js and FastAPI, surgical case API p95 latency (SLO: under 500ms), preference card save latency, Epic FHIR cache hit rate (target above 70% during morning peak). Azure Database Insights: PostgreSQL slow query log (threshold 200ms), connection pool saturation. PagerDuty: HTTP 5xx rate above 2%, PostgreSQL connection saturation above 80%, Epic circuit breaker open for more than 5 minutes, Redis eviction events.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Azure App Service P2v3: $280/month. PostgreSQL Flexible Server (4 vCores zone-redundant): $420/month. Redis C1: $55/month. Container Registry Basic: $5/month. Application Insights: ~$50/month at expected telemetry volume. Total: ~$810/month, well within $8,000 budget. Auto-scale App Service plan to P3v3 if concurrent users exceed 150. PostgreSQL read replica on-demand for analytics queries if main instance CPU exceeds 70%.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (5 months): Azure infrastructure via Terraform (App Service, PostgreSQL Flexible Server, Redis, ACR). Case request and OR block schedule management with drag-and-drop calendar. Versioned preference card CRUD with is_current flag. Epic FHIR R4 patient lookup with Redis caching and circuit breaker. Post-case supply utilization logging. Basic variance report (planned vs actual per case). Azure AD SSO with MFA. Cutover from vendor tool for 2 pilot OR rooms. Phase 2 (months 6-9): Full OR department rollout across all rooms. Weekly variance analytics dashboard with 12-week trending and surgeon-level heatmaps. Supply item cost data integration for dollar-value waste reporting. Block schedule conflict resolution workflow for overlapping surgeon requests. Phase 3 (post-launch): Real-time OR turnover tracking, mobile preference card editing for surgeons, predictive block schedule demand forecasting.
target_output.development_guidelines: Python: black, flake8, mypy strict. pytest 80% coverage on FastAPI routes and service layer. TypeScript strict mode; no any types. React Query for all server state; no direct fetch calls in components. Preference card version creation is append-only (no UPDATE on existing versions; always INSERT new version). All database schema changes require Alembic migration with tested down-migration. No PHI in log statements; use patient_case_id (UUID) as correlation identifier in logs. PR requires 1 reviewer; preference card data model changes require clinical informatics review.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Epic FHIR R4 API changes without advance notice can break patient lookup on the day of an OR schedule update. Mitigation: Epic FHIR version pinned to R4; fallback to manual MRN entry for coordinators when circuit breaker is open. Risk: OR staff resistance to parallel operation alongside vendor tool during 5-month transition creates data inconsistency if some cases are scheduled in the old system. Mitigation: feature flag per OR room; old and new systems are exclusive per room during rollout. Tradeoff: per-row MRN encryption over Transparent Data Encryption only, adding application complexity but providing field-level protection if database backup is compromised. Tradeoff: Azure App Service over AKS for lower operational overhead at moderate scale, accepting lower auto-scaling granularity.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: ISS-001 resolved: preference card versioning implemented with version INT, effective_from DATE, is_current BOOLEAN, and supply_utilization_records foreign key to card_id for historical variance attribution. ISS-002 resolved: Epic FHIR caching added with Redis 5-minute TTL and circuit breaker with manual entry fallback. Block schedule conflict resolution rules to be defined by OR committee in Phase 2.

metadata.notes: OR scheduling web app revision round 2, resolving preference card versioning and Epic FHIR caching issues.

project_goal.value: A public-facing marketing and compliance information landing page communicating HIPAA patient rights, data sharing opt-out options, and Notice of Privacy Practices to patients and the public
project_goal.rationale: Required by HIPAA 45 CFR 164.520 to make NPP available to patients and post on public-facing website

target_users.value: Patients, family members, and members of the public seeking HIPAA rights information and privacy opt-out options
target_users.rationale: Public audience with no authentication requirement

project_class.rationale: Static informational site with no server-side runtime or database; content managed via headless CMS

capabilities.rationale: Static frontend with page analytics; no backend, auth, or database capabilities needed

complexity_level.rationale: Static site with CMS-driven content updates, no complex business logic

risk_level.rationale: Public informational site with no PII collection beyond the opt-out form submission routed via email

data_sensitivity.rationale: No PHI stored; opt-out form emails are transmitted directly to Privacy Officer mailbox, not stored in the site infrastructure

external_exposure.rationale: Must be publicly accessible without authentication per HIPAA NPP posting requirement

access_model.value: Fully public; no authentication. Opt-out form submissions use AWS SES to route to Privacy Officer email only.
access_model.rationale: HIPAA NPP must be accessible without login; no user accounts on this site

feature_scope.value: HIPAA rights information pages, Notice of Privacy Practices PDF download (current version), opt-out request form submission via email, compliance document archive, contact information for Privacy Officer
feature_scope.rationale: All content required by HIPAA 45 CFR 164.520 posting obligation

mvp_scope.value: HIPAA rights pages, NPP PDF download, opt-out form routed via SES, Privacy Officer contact info
mvp_scope.rationale: Minimum required by HIPAA before ONC audit deadline

security_baseline.value: CloudFront HTTPS-only with HSTS headers, CSP headers blocking inline scripts, AWS WAF on CloudFront for DDoS and bot protection, no cookies beyond anonymous analytics
security_baseline.rationale: Public site security baseline; no authentication attack surface

frontend_stack.value: Astro 4 with TypeScript
frontend_stack.rationale: Astro generates zero-JS static HTML by default, ideal for a public compliance site prioritizing accessibility and performance

backend_stack.value: No server-side runtime; static site only. AWS SES for opt-out form email relay via Lambda@Edge
backend_stack.rationale: landing_page class requires no server-side backend

data_platform.value: No database. Privacy notices and opt-out form content managed via Contentful headless CMS. NPP PDFs stored in S3 with public read.
data_platform.rationale: No data persistence on the site infrastructure

hosting_target.value: AWS S3 static hosting with CloudFront CDN distribution globally; Lambda@Edge for opt-out form email relay via SES
hosting_target.rationale: Serverless static hosting with near-zero operational overhead

privacy_retention_policy.value: No user data stored on site infrastructure; opt-out requests transmitted via SES to Privacy Officer and retained by the Privacy Officer outside this system per hospital records policy
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Site stores no PII or PHI; no retention policy required for site infrastructure

future_scope.value: Multi-language support (Spanish, Mandarin) for diverse patient population, interactive rights request submission portal with status tracking
future_scope.rationale: Accessibility and language equity roadmap items

constraints.value: Fully accessible to WCAG 2.1 AA standard; NPP PDF must reflect legally approved current version; site must rebuild automatically within 2 hours of Contentful NPP document update
constraints.rationale: ADA compliance requirement and legal obligation to publish current NPP version

observability_baseline.value: AWS CloudWatch for CloudFront cache hit rate and 4xx/5xx error rates; privacy-respecting page analytics via Plausible Analytics (no cookies, no PII)
observability_baseline.rationale: Public site analytics without HIPAA or GDPR cookie consent complexity

execution_preference.value: Compliance-first delivery; Legal team reviews all page content before first deployment
execution_preference.rationale: HIPAA NPP language must be legally reviewed before public posting

project.goal: Public HIPAA privacy rights and NPP site with opt-out form
project.target_users: Patients and public
project.timeline: ONC audit deadline in 6 weeks

frontend.rendering: Static site generation (SSG); zero client-side JS except opt-out form widget

security.transport: HTTPS-only via CloudFront with HSTS, CSP headers

constraints.pdf_currency: NPP PDF must always reflect legally approved version

reasoner_reviews.product: This site has a hard legal obligation: it must display the current Notice of Privacy Practices and be accessible to any member of the public without authentication. The Contentful webhook rebuild trigger is the most important operational feature, as an outdated NPP on the public site could trigger HIPAA audit findings. The opt-out form must not store patient data on the site infrastructure.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Astro 4 with purely static output is the correct architecture for this project class. Lambda@Edge for the opt-out form email relay avoids adding any server-side backend infrastructure. The Contentful webhook triggering a GitHub Actions rebuild and CloudFront cache invalidation is the right approach for the 2-hour NPP currency requirement. No server-side runtime should be introduced.
reasoner_reviews.security: The only attack surface on this site is the opt-out form submission endpoint. Lambda@Edge must validate the form payload (email format, reCAPTCHA token) before relaying to SES, to prevent form abuse for email spam. WAF rules should rate-limit form submission requests. No user data touches the S3 bucket or CloudFront.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: WCAG 2.1 AA compliance requires axe-core automated checks in CI plus manual screen reader testing of the NPP PDF download flow. The opt-out form keyboard navigation and form error announcement must be explicitly tested.

frontend.app_structure: Astro 4 project with TypeScript. Static pages: index (rights overview), npp (Notice of Privacy Practices with PDF download), opt-out (form page), contact (Privacy Officer information), document-archive (historical NPP versions). Content fetched from Contentful at build time via Astro content collections.
frontend.pages_and_flows: Home: summary of HIPAA patient rights with anchor links to sections. NPP Page: current approved NPP text with PDF download button (S3 presigned public URL) and effective date. Opt-Out Page: form fields (patient name, DOB, contact email, request type ENUM marketing/research/all), reCAPTCHA v3, submit triggers Lambda@Edge SES relay. Document Archive: table of past NPP versions with PDF links.
frontend.accessibility_notes: WCAG 2.1 AA: all images have alt text, form fields have visible labels and ARIA descriptions, error messages announced via role=alert, PDF download link announces file size and format, skip-to-main-content link present. axe-core automated accessibility audit runs in GitHub Actions CI and blocks on any critical or serious violations.
frontend.performance_notes: Astro outputs zero client-side JS for static pages; only the opt-out form island uses a small React component for validation. Lighthouse score target: 95+ Performance, 100 Accessibility. CloudFront caches all assets at 24-hour TTL; cache invalidated on rebuild.

security.auth_design: No authentication on any page; fully public site.
security.abuse_prevention: AWS WAF on CloudFront: rate limit opt-out form submission endpoint to 20 requests per IP per hour. reCAPTCHA v3 token validated in Lambda@Edge before SES relay. Form submissions without valid reCAPTCHA score above 0.5 rejected with 400 response.
security.audit_and_logging_controls: CloudFront access logs stored in S3 log bucket with 90-day retention. Lambda@Edge invocation logs to CloudWatch Logs: log timestamp, reCAPTCHA score, and form submission success/failure (no patient data logged).
security.privacy_controls: No cookies set by the site. Plausible Analytics uses cookieless tracking (IP hash, no persistent identifier). No user data stored in S3 or transmitted to third parties beyond SES relay to Privacy Officer.

devops.deployment_topology: S3 bucket (static assets, public read via CloudFront OAI), CloudFront distribution (HTTPS-only, HSTS, custom error pages for 404/403), Lambda@Edge function (opt-out form SES relay), AWS WAF WebACL attached to CloudFront.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: npm install, Astro build, axe-core accessibility audit (blocks on critical/serious), Lighthouse CI (blocks if Accessibility below 100), S3 sync, CloudFront cache invalidation. Contentful webhook triggers workflow_dispatch event to rebuild pipeline automatically.
devops.rollback_strategy: Previous S3 object versions preserved with S3 versioning. CloudFront cache invalidation can be issued against a prior S3 version within minutes. GitHub release tags mark every NPP-approved deployment for Legal audit trail.
devops.cost_controls: S3 static hosting: under $1/month at expected traffic. CloudFront: ~$5/month. Lambda@Edge: negligible at opt-out form volume. Plausible Analytics: $9/month hosted. Total under $20/month.

ISS-001.title: Contentful rebuild trigger not defined in Round 1
ISS-001.detail: Round 1 plan described a headless CMS without specifying how a Contentful document update would trigger a site rebuild, risking stale NPP on the public site.

history.note: Webhook rebuild trigger missing from CI/CD design
GitHub Actions workflow_dispatch trigger added for Contentful webhooks; CloudFront invalidation on rebuild

ISS-002.title: Opt-out form abuse prevention not defined in Round 2
ISS-002.detail: Round 2 plan added the Contentful webhook rebuild but did not address opt-out form abuse prevention. Lambda@Edge had no rate limiting or bot detection, risking SES relay spam and inflated Privacy Officer inbox.

history.note: WAF rate limiting and reCAPTCHA missing from opt-out form endpoint

focus_issues.title: Opt-out form abuse prevention not defined in Round 2
focus_issues.detail: Lambda@Edge SES relay has no rate limiting or bot detection, risking form spam abuse.

latest_recommendations: Add AWS WAF rate limiting rule on opt-out form CloudFront path (20 requests per IP per hour)
Add reCAPTCHA v3 token validation in Lambda@Edge before SES relay

WCAG 2.1 AA axe-core CI gate and Lighthouse accessibility score requirement are correctly defined
Correct landing_page architecture with zero server-side runtime and no database

Add AWS WAF rate limit rule scoped to the opt-out form path at 20 requests per IP per hour

issue_updates.title: Opt-out form abuse prevention missing
issue_updates.detail: Lambda@Edge in Round 2 had no reCAPTCHA or WAF rate limiting on opt-out form path

previous_plan.title: Hospital Patient Privacy Program Landing Page v2
previous_plan.executive_summary: Astro 4 static site on S3 and CloudFront for HIPAA NPP and opt-out forms. Contentful CMS with webhook rebuild trigger added in Round 2. Lambda@Edge SES relay for opt-out forms with no bot protection.
previous_plan.architecture_overview: Astro 4 SSG on S3/CloudFront. Contentful webhook triggers GitHub Actions rebuild with CloudFront cache invalidation. Lambda@Edge relays opt-out form to Privacy Officer via SES. No WAF rate limiting or reCAPTCHA on opt-out form path.
previous_plan.security_and_compliance: HTTPS-only via CloudFront with HSTS and CSP headers. No authentication. Plausible Analytics cookieless. WAF attached to CloudFront distribution but no rate limiting rules for opt-out form path defined.

target_output.thinking_summary: Round 3 revision resolving ISS-002 by adding AWS WAF rate limiting on the opt-out form CloudFront path (20 requests per IP per hour) and reCAPTCHA v3 token validation in Lambda@Edge before SES relay. Also defining SES failure handling: Lambda@Edge returns 500 and the Astro opt-out form island displays a retry prompt with the Privacy Officer phone number. This completes a fully specified public HIPAA landing page with no server-side runtime, no database, correct WCAG 2.1 AA accessibility gates, and complete opt-out form abuse prevention.

fix_report.action_taken: Added AWS WAF rate limit rule scoped to /opt-out path at 20 requests per IP per hour on the CloudFront WebACL. Added reCAPTCHA v3 token validation in Lambda@Edge: submissions with score below 0.5 rejected with 400 response. Added SES send failure handling: Lambda@Edge returns structured 500 JSON; Astro opt-out form island displays retry message and Privacy Officer phone number fallback.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Opt-out form endpoint protected against bot submissions and rate abuse. Privacy Officer inbox receives only legitimate opt-out requests. Patients receive clear guidance on failure with phone fallback.

target_output.title: Hospital Patient Privacy Program - Public HIPAA Landing Page
target_output.executive_summary: A fully static, WCAG 2.1 AA-compliant public landing page built with Astro 4 on AWS S3 and CloudFront, serving HIPAA patient rights information, the current Notice of Privacy Practices, and a patient opt-out request form. Content managed via Contentful headless CMS with automatic 2-hour rebuild on NPP document updates. Opt-out form submissions routed via Lambda@Edge and AWS SES to the Privacy Officer with reCAPTCHA v3 bot protection and AWS WAF rate limiting. Zero server-side runtime, zero cookies, and no patient data stored in site infrastructure.
target_output.architecture_overview: Fully static Astro 4 site with TypeScript served from AWS S3 via CloudFront CDN globally. Astro fetches all page content from Contentful at build time via content collections; no runtime API calls. Contentful webhook on NPP document publish triggers a GitHub Actions workflow_dispatch event, which rebuilds the Astro site and invalidates the CloudFront cache within 2 hours. Opt-out form submissions hit a Lambda@Edge function that validates reCAPTCHA v3 token (score above 0.5 required), then relays the structured form payload to the Privacy Officer via AWS SES; no data is persisted. AWS WAF WebACL on CloudFront enforces HTTPS redirect, rate limits opt-out form path at 20 requests per IP per hour, and applies AWS Managed Rules for common web exploits.
target_output.technology_stack: Frontend: Astro 4.x, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, minimal React island for opt-out form client validation. CMS: Contentful (headless, webhook-triggered rebuild). Hosting: AWS S3 (static assets), CloudFront (CDN, HTTPS, HSTS). Edge Function: Lambda@Edge (opt-out form relay). Email: AWS SES. Security: AWS WAF WebACL, reCAPTCHA v3. CI/CD: GitHub Actions with axe-core and Lighthouse CI gates. Analytics: Plausible Analytics (cookieless, self-hostable).
target_output.functional_feature_map: HIPAA Rights Pages: statically rendered sections covering Right to Access, Right to Amend, Right to Accounting of Disclosures, Right to Request Restrictions, and Right to Complain, each authored in Contentful and published on rebuild. NPP Download: current Notice of Privacy Practices PDF served from S3 public read, with version date and Legal team approval date displayed. Opt-Out Request Form: patient name, date of birth, contact email, and opt-out type (marketing/research/all uses); reCAPTCHA v3; SES relay to Privacy Officer; form island shows success confirmation or phone fallback on SES failure. Document Archive: table of historical NPP PDF versions with effective dates. Privacy Officer Contact: address, phone, and secure email for direct requests.
target_output.system_components: S3 Bucket (static assets, public read via CloudFront OAI): Astro-generated HTML, CSS, and minimal JS. CloudFront Distribution: HTTPS-only redirect, HSTS header injection, CSP blocking inline scripts, 24-hour cache TTL, custom 404 page. AWS WAF WebACL: HTTPS enforcement rule, AWS Managed Rules (CommonRuleSet, KnownBadInputsRuleSet), rate limit rule on /opt-out path (20 req/IP/hour). Lambda@Edge (Origin Request): validates reCAPTCHA v3 token via Google API, validates form payload schema, invokes SES SendEmail to Privacy Officer distribution list, returns structured JSON response. AWS SES: verified sending domain, DKIM and SPF configured. Contentful CMS: NPP document, rights page content, archive entries. S3 Log Bucket: CloudFront access logs with 90-day retention. Plausible Analytics: embedded script tag, no cookies.
target_output.workflows: NPP Update Workflow: Legal team updates the Notice of Privacy Practices document in Contentful and publishes the new version. Contentful sends a webhook POST to a GitHub Actions inbound webhook URL. GitHub Actions workflow_dispatch triggers the Astro build job: npm install, astro build (Contentful content fetched at build time), axe-core accessibility audit (fails build on critical/serious violations), Lighthouse CI accessibility 100 gate, S3 sync with cache-control headers, CloudFront invalidation of /*. Updated NPP appears on the public site within 2 hours of Contentful publish. Opt-Out Form Submission: Patient fills the opt-out form on the /opt-out page. Form island submits via fetch to the Lambda@Edge endpoint with reCAPTCHA v3 token. Lambda@Edge verifies token score (reject if below 0.5 with 400 response). WAF rate limit check (reject if above 20 req/IP/hour with 429). Lambda@Edge validates payload schema, invokes SES SendEmail to privacy-officer@hospital.org with structured opt-out details. On success, returns 200 and form island shows confirmation with reference number. On SES failure, returns 500 and form island shows retry message and Privacy Officer phone number.
target_output.data_model: No database or persistent data store. Contentful content types: PrivacyRightsSection (title, slug, body_rich_text, display_order), NoticeOfPrivacyPractices (title, effective_date, pdf_asset, approval_date, is_current), ComplianceDocument (title, document_type, effective_date, pdf_asset), PrivacyOfficerContact (name, title, phone, email, mailing_address). NPP PDF assets stored in S3 public read path /npp/{version}/notice-of-privacy-practices.pdf. No user-submitted data persisted anywhere in the site infrastructure.
target_output.api_design: No REST API. Lambda@Edge opt-out form endpoint: POST /_/opt-out with JSON body {name: string, dob: string, email: string, opt_out_type: 'marketing'|'research'|'all', recaptcha_token: string}. Response: 200 {success: true, reference_id: string} or 400 {error: 'bot_detected'} or 429 {error: 'rate_limit_exceeded'} or 500 {error: 'submission_failed', fallback_phone: '+1-555-PRIVACY'}. No other server endpoints; all other page content served as static HTML from S3 via CloudFront.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Transport: CloudFront enforces HTTPS-only with HTTP-to-HTTPS redirect. HSTS header (max-age=31536000; includeSubDomains) injected via CloudFront response headers policy. CSP header blocks inline scripts and restricts script-src to self, Plausible Analytics domain, and Google reCAPTCHA domain. AWS WAF WebACL: AWS Managed CommonRuleSet and KnownBadInputsRuleSet active on CloudFront. Rate limit rule on /opt-out path at 20 req/IP/hour. reCAPTCHA v3 token validated in Lambda@Edge; score below 0.5 rejected. No cookies set by the site. Plausible Analytics is cookieless. Lambda@Edge logs timestamp, reCAPTCHA score, and submission success/failure only; no patient data in logs. HIPAA 45 CFR 164.520 compliance: current NPP always displayed, updated within 2 hours of Legal approval via Contentful webhook rebuild. WCAG 2.1 AA: axe-core CI gate and Lighthouse Accessibility 100 requirement.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions CI/CD pipeline: npm ci for deterministic installs, astro build triggering Contentful content fetch, axe-core automated accessibility audit (critical and serious violations fail the build), Lighthouse CI with Accessibility score 100 gate, S3 sync with --delete for clean deploys, CloudFront create-invalidation for /* on every production deploy. Environments: staging (separate S3 bucket and CloudFront distribution, Contentful preview API), production (main S3 bucket, CloudFront with WAF). Contentful webhook for NPP publish events calls GitHub Actions repository_dispatch endpoint to trigger rebuild. Terraform manages S3 buckets, CloudFront distribution, WAF WebACL, Lambda@Edge function, SES domain verification, and log bucket. All Terraform state stored in S3 with DynamoDB lock.
target_output.observability: CloudWatch metrics: CloudFront 4xx and 5xx error rates (alert if 5xx above 1%), cache hit rate (alert if below 80%), Lambda@Edge invocation errors and duration. CloudFront access logs in S3 for ad-hoc debugging with 90-day retention. Plausible Analytics: page views per rights section, opt-out form start vs submission completion rate, NPP PDF download count (proxy for patient engagement). CloudWatch alarm on any SES bounce or complaint notification. GitHub Actions build status notifications to Slack #compliance-team channel.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: S3 static hosting: under $1/month at expected 5,000 monthly visitors. CloudFront: ~$5/month for data transfer and HTTPS requests. Lambda@Edge: negligible cost at expected opt-out form volume (under 100 submissions/month). WAF WebACL: $5/month base plus $1/million requests. Plausible Analytics: $9/month hosted plan. SES: $0.10/1,000 emails, essentially free at opt-out form volume. Total infrastructure under $25/month. CloudFront auto-scales globally to any traffic spike at no capacity planning required.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (3 weeks): Terraform IaC for S3, CloudFront, WAF, Lambda@Edge, SES domain verification. Astro 4 project with Contentful integration. Rights pages, NPP download, opt-out form with reCAPTCHA v3 and WAF rate limiting. axe-core and Lighthouse CI gates. Legal review and approval of all page content. Production deploy before ONC audit deadline. Phase 2 (post-launch): Contentful document archive with historical NPP versions. Privacy Officer contact page. Plausible Analytics dashboard for compliance team. Phase 3 (Year 2): Spanish and Mandarin language versions via Contentful localization. Interactive rights request portal with status tracking.
target_output.development_guidelines: Astro components use TypeScript strict mode. No inline styles; all styling via Tailwind utility classes. Opt-out form React island is the only client-side JS; keep bundle under 20 KB. Every Astro page must pass axe-core with zero critical or serious violations before merge. PDF download links must include aria-label announcing file name, format, and size. NPP content changes require Legal team sign-off before Contentful publish. Terraform changes require DevOps review. All Lambda@Edge code reviewed by Security before deploy.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Contentful outage during Astro build causes rebuild failure, leaving an outdated NPP on the site if the build cannot fetch content. Mitigation: Contentful content baked into last successful build; site stays live from prior build until Contentful recovers and next rebuild succeeds; Legal team alerted via GitHub Actions Slack notification on build failure. Risk: reCAPTCHA v3 scoring varies by network and may produce false positives for legitimate patients on low-reputation IP ranges (e.g., hospital guest Wi-Fi). Mitigation: threshold set at 0.5 (permissive); challenge-action mode not used since there is no interactive challenge fallback; phone fallback always shown on 400 response. Tradeoff: Contentful over a git-based CMS (Decap, Tina) for Legal team familiarity with GUI-based document editing, accepting vendor dependency for content management.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: ISS-001 resolved in Round 2: Contentful webhook rebuild trigger added via GitHub Actions workflow_dispatch with CloudFront cache invalidation. ISS-002 resolved in Round 3: AWS WAF rate limit rule (20 req/IP/hour on /opt-out) and reCAPTCHA v3 Lambda@Edge validation added. SES failure handling defined with phone fallback for patients.

metadata.notes: HIPAA public landing page, Astro 4 static site on S3/CloudFront, revision round 3 adding WAF rate limiting and reCAPTCHA to opt-out form.

project_goal.value: Decompose a monolithic legacy HAI surveillance system into microservices for tracking healthcare-associated infections, managing NHSN surveillance case definitions, and submitting mandatory HAI reports to CDC NHSN
project_goal.rationale: Monolith has accumulated 12 years of technical debt; CMS mandate requires NHSN reporting and the legacy system's NHSN submission module is failing with current CDC API version

target_users.value: Infection preventionists, infectious disease physicians, clinical microbiologists, and hospital epidemiologists
target_users.rationale: Clinical infection control team members who review culture results, classify HAI events, and submit NHSN reports

project_class.rationale: Browser-based clinical workflow tool; no native mobile requirement for IP workstation use

capabilities.value: frontend, backend, data, auth, integrations, analytics, batch_jobs
capabilities.rationale: Full stack with HL7 lab integration, NHSN batch submission, HAI analytics, and microservices decomposition

complexity_level.rationale: Microservices decomposition from monolith with complex NHSN CDA document generation, HL7 lab feed integration, and SIR metric computation

risk_level.rationale: PHI in HAI surveillance records; CMS mandatory NHSN reporting deadline risk; patient safety implications if HAI outbreak detection is delayed

data_sensitivity.rationale: Infection events linked to specific patients, cultures, and clinical units constitute PHI

external_exposure.rationale: Internal hospital network access only; NHSN submission is an outbound API call, not inbound exposure

access_model.value: Cognito with RBAC: INFECTION_PREVENTIONIST (case review and classification), PHYSICIAN (read-only case view), EPIDEMIOLOGIST (full analytics and NHSN submission), ADMIN (configuration and reference data)
access_model.rationale: Role-based access matching infection control team hierarchy

feature_scope.value: HL7 lab feed ingest and NHSN case definition evaluation, HAI event candidate queue and IP case classification workflow, NHSN CDA document generation and submission, SIR metric computation, antibiogram data capture, outbreak investigation tracking
feature_scope.rationale: All features required to replace legacy monolith

mvp_scope.value: HL7 lab feed ingest, HAI candidate review queue, CAUTI and CLABSI NHSN case definitions, monthly NHSN CDA submission for device-associated HAIs
mvp_scope.rationale: CAUTI and CLABSI are the highest-volume CMS-reported HAI types; MVP restores NHSN submission capability

security_baseline.value: Cognito MFA for all users, TLS 1.3 in transit, PostgreSQL encryption at rest, no PHI in logs, 8-hour session timeout for clinical workflows
security_baseline.rationale: HIPAA technical safeguards and hospital security policy

frontend_stack.value: React 18 with TypeScript
frontend_stack.rationale: IP team familiar with React; rich data table components needed for HAI case queue

backend_stack.value: Go 1.22 with Gin for microservices
backend_stack.rationale: Go for high-throughput HL7 parsing and concurrent NHSN submission; Gin for lightweight HTTP services

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 per microservice with Redis 7 for session cache and HL7 message deduplication
data_platform.rationale: Database-per-service pattern for microservices; Redis for HL7 message idempotency key cache

hosting_target.value: AWS ECS Fargate with Application Load Balancer per service
hosting_target.rationale: Managed containers consistent with hospital AWS strategy

privacy_retention_policy.value: HAI event records retained 10 years per CMS; lab culture results retained 7 years; NHSN submission records retained 10 years; audit logs 6 years
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: CMS Conditions of Participation and HIPAA record retention requirements

future_scope.value: SSI (surgical site infection) and MRSA bacteremia NHSN event types, antibiogram trend analytics, NLP-assisted case classification from clinical notes
future_scope.rationale: Phase 2 expansion after CAUTI/CLABSI MVP stabilizes

constraints.value: Must restore NHSN submission within 6 months to avoid CMS penalty; legacy monolith runs in parallel during migration; Go 1.22 required for CVE-free standard library
constraints.rationale: CMS deadline and security baseline requirements

observability_baseline.value: Datadog APM for all Fargate services, structured JSON logging, PagerDuty for NHSN submission failures and HL7 feed gaps
observability_baseline.rationale: Hospital Datadog standard; NHSN submission failures require immediate on-call response

execution_preference.value: Strangler Fig migration pattern; each microservice extracted and validated against the monolith in parallel before traffic cutover
execution_preference.rationale: Reduces migration risk by validating each service before decommissioning monolith equivalent

compliance_context.value: HIPAA and HITECH; CMS mandatory NHSN HAI reporting under Conditions of Participation; CDC NHSN CDA document submission protocol v11; NHSN data submission security requirements
compliance_context.rationale: PHI in surveillance records plus mandatory federal reporting obligations

project.goal: Decompose legacy HAI surveillance monolith into microservices, restore NHSN submission
project.target_users: Infection preventionists, epidemiologists, clinical microbiologists
project.timeline: 6-month NHSN submission restoration

frontend.state: React Query for case data, Zustand for IP review workflow state

backend.pattern: Microservices with database-per-service, internal gRPC for service-to-service, REST for frontend API

security.auth: AWS Cognito with MFA
security.encryption: PostgreSQL at rest, TLS 1.3 in transit
security.compliance: HIPAA, CDC NHSN data handling requirements

data.cache: Redis 7 for HL7 deduplication and session tokens

devops.migration: Strangler Fig with legacy monolith parallel operation

constraints.parallel_operation: Legacy monolith must run alongside new services during migration

open_questions.nhsn_schema: CDC NHSN CDA schema version for CAUTI event type pending CDC confirmation

reasoner_reviews.product: The strangler fig migration approach is correct for this project: NHSN submission is the highest-priority capability to restore first because CMS penalties accrue monthly. Extracting the Lab Ingest service and Surveillance service first, and validating NHSN output matches the legacy system before cutover, is the safest path. CAUTI and CLABSI are correct MVP scope as they represent 60% of CMS-reportable HAI volume.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Database-per-service pattern is appropriate for this microservices decomposition. Three core services: lab_ingest (HL7 parsing and NHSN case definition evaluation), surveillance (HAI event classification and review workflow), and nhsn_submission (CDA document generation and CDC API submission). gRPC for internal lab_ingest-to-surveillance event publishing avoids a full message broker for MVP. Redis message deduplication key (HL7 message control ID) is critical to prevent duplicate HAI candidates from HL7 feed retransmissions.
reasoner_reviews.security: NHSN submission credentials (CDC API key) must be stored in AWS Secrets Manager, never in environment variables or code. Cognito MFA must be enforced for all infection control staff. Audit trail must capture every HAI classification decision and NHSN submission event with IP user identity for NHSN audit purposes.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: 6-month deadline is aggressive for a strangler fig migration. Risk: legacy parallel operation creates duplicate HL7 processing unless the HL7 router explicitly splits feed to either legacy or new system per message type. Recommend implementing HL7 message routing toggle (feature flag) per message type to avoid dual processing.

backend.service_design: Three Go microservices: lab_ingest_service (HL7 v2 ORM/ORU message parsing, NHSN CAUTI/CLABSI case definition evaluation, candidate event creation), surveillance_service (HAI event queue management, IP case classification and approval workflow, antibiogram data), nhsn_submission_service (CDA document generation per NHSN event type, CDC NHSN REST API submission, submission status tracking).
backend.api_patterns: Each service exposes a REST API on Gin. Internal service communication via gRPC: lab_ingest_service publishes HAI candidate events to surveillance_service via gRPC streaming. Frontend communicates with surveillance_service REST API via ALB. nhsn_submission_service has no direct frontend routes; triggered by surveillance_service via gRPC on IP classification approval.
backend.background_jobs: Monthly NHSN submission batch: aggregates all approved HAI events for the reporting period, generates CDA documents per device type (CAUTI, CLABSI), submits to CDC NHSN API. SIR computation job: monthly calculation of Standardized Infection Ratio per device type per clinical unit against NHSN baseline rates. HL7 feed gap detector: hourly check for missing expected HL7 messages from lab system; alerts on gaps over 2 hours.
backend.failure_handling: NHSN submission failures: retry with exponential backoff (3 attempts), then PagerDuty alert to on-call epidemiologist. HL7 parse errors: dead-letter queue in Redis for manual review; do not block processing of subsequent messages. gRPC retries with deadline propagation between services.

frontend.app_structure: React 18 SPA with route-based code splitting. Authenticated via Cognito JWT with MFA enforcement. Main sections: HAI Case Queue (IP review dashboard), Case Detail (culture results, device duration, NHSN criteria evaluation), NHSN Submission Status, Antibiogram Data Entry, SIR Analytics Dashboard.
frontend.pages_and_flows: HAI Case Queue: paginated table of candidate events with organism, clinical unit, device type, and NHSN criteria match status. IP reviews and classifies each candidate (confirm HAI / exclude with reason). Case Detail: displays culture results pulled from lab_ingest_service, device duration from EHR integration, automatic CAUTI/CLABSI criteria evaluation with criteria checkboxes, IP classification form. NHSN Submission Status: monthly submission history with CDA document download, CDC response codes, and SIR metrics per event type.
frontend.state_management: React Query for HAI case queue, case detail, and submission history. Zustand for IP classification form draft state (prevent loss on accidental navigation). Cognito MSAL for JWT token management.

security.auth_design: Cognito User Pool with MFA (TOTP) required for all users. Cognito JWT validated by Gin middleware on every API request. Session tokens stored in Redis with 8-hour TTL.
security.authorization_model: Go middleware checks Cognito group claim per route: INFECTION_PREVENTIONIST may classify and approve HAI events; EPIDEMIOLOGIST may trigger NHSN submission; PHYSICIAN may only read case details; ADMIN manages NHSN case definitions and clinical unit reference data.
security.audit_and_logging_controls: All HAI classification decisions logged to audit_events table with user_id, event_id, action (classify/exclude/approve), timestamp, and classification rationale. All NHSN submission attempts logged with submission_id, CDC response code, and user_id. Datadog log pipeline strips PHI field values from structured logs.

data.entities: infection_events (id UUID PK, patient_id UUID, clinical_unit_id UUID FK, device_type ENUM foley_catheter/central_line, event_type ENUM cauti/clabsi, onset_date DATE, nhsn_criteria_met JSONB, status ENUM candidate/confirmed/excluded, classified_by UUID, classified_at TIMESTAMP). organism_isolates (id UUID PK, event_id UUID FK, organism_name VARCHAR, organism_code SNOMED VARCHAR, antibiotic_resistance_profile JSONB, culture_date DATE, lab_accession_id VARCHAR). nhsn_submissions (id UUID PK, reporting_period DATE, event_type VARCHAR, cda_document_s3_key VARCHAR, submission_status ENUM pending/submitted/accepted/rejected, cdc_confirmation_number VARCHAR, submitted_at TIMESTAMP, submitted_by UUID FK). surveillance_criteria (id UUID PK, event_type VARCHAR, nhsn_version VARCHAR, criteria_definition JSONB, effective_from DATE).
data.storage_design: lab_ingest_service DB: hl7_messages (raw), organism_isolates. surveillance_service DB: infection_events, surveillance_criteria, clinical_units, antibiogram_data. nhsn_submission_service DB: nhsn_submissions, cda_templates. Redis: HL7 message control ID deduplication keys (24-hour TTL), Cognito session tokens.

devops.deployment_topology: Three ECS Fargate services behind individual ALBs (internal-facing). Lab ingest service ALB accepts HL7 TCP connections from lab system via NLB. Frontend served from S3/CloudFront. Cognito User Pool in us-east-1. RDS PostgreSQL per service in private subnet. Redis ElastiCache in private subnet.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions per service: go test with 80% coverage gate, golangci-lint, Docker build and ECR push, ECS rolling deployment. Strangler fig toggle: feature flag in AWS Parameter Store per HL7 message type controls whether legacy or new lab_ingest_service receives the feed.
devops.observability_stack: Datadog APM on all Fargate services. Custom metrics: HAI candidate event creation rate, IP classification throughput, NHSN submission success/failure rate, HL7 message parse error rate, SIR values per unit per month.
devops.rollback_strategy: ECS service rollback to previous task definition revision. RDS point-in-time restore with 7-day backup retention. Legacy monolith remains live until all services pass 30-day production validation; rollback is Strangler Fig feature flag toggle back to legacy.

ISS-001.title: NHSN CDA document generation not specified in Round 1
ISS-001.detail: Round 1 plan mentioned nhsn_submission_service but did not specify the CDA document structure, CDC NHSN API endpoint details, or retry logic for failed submissions. NHSN CDA generation is the technically hardest part of the project and the core MVP requirement.

ISS-002.detail: Round 1 plan described Strangler Fig pattern but did not define how HL7 lab feed messages are routed exclusively to either the legacy system or the new lab_ingest_service during the migration period, creating risk of dual HAI candidate creation.

Strangler Fig migration pattern is the correct approach for legacy parallel operation

Implement HL7 routing feature flag in AWS Parameter Store: key per message type (ORM^O01, ORU^R01), value 'legacy' or 'new_service'; NLB listener rule reads flag before routing

previous_plan.title: HAI Surveillance Microservices Decomposition v1
