target_output.executive_summary: An infrastructure migration moving 200+ HL7 interfaces from on-premises integration engine hardware to AKS with zero message loss tolerance. Terraform 1.8 provisions the AKS cluster and Azure supporting services; a parameterized Helm chart template deploys each interface as an independent Kubernetes Deployment. A parallel-run phase runs on-prem and cloud instances simultaneously with an hourly ReconciliationJob comparing message counts (>0.1% discrepancy alerts, >0.5% blocks cutover). An automated DRSmokeTester validates all 200+ interfaces in under 4 minutes by sending synthetic HL7 ADT^A01 messages in parallel, enabling the 15-minute DR RTO target. Interface-by-interface cutover proceeds during Saturday 2-6 AM windows sequenced from lowest to highest clinical criticality.
target_output.architecture_overview: Three-phase migration architecture: Phase 1 parallel infrastructure (AKS cluster running cloud interface deployments alongside on-premises engine, both processing copies of HL7 messages; ReconciliationJob validates message count parity hourly), Phase 2 interface-by-interface cutover (CutoverOrchestrator drains on-prem interface, waits for message queue empty confirmation, activates cloud instance, runs DRSmokeTester within 60 seconds, rolls back if smoke test fails), Phase 3 decommission (on-prem hardware powered down after all interfaces validated in cloud for 30 days). AKS cluster uses namespace-per-integration-domain (lab-integration, pharmacy-integration, radiology-integration, adt-integration) with Kubernetes NetworkPolicies restricting pod-to-pod traffic to within-namespace only. Azure Database for PostgreSQL Flexible Server with zone-redundant HA replaces on-premises single-node PostgreSQL. AAD workload identity replaces VPN-based admin access with identity-aware cloud-native authentication.
target_output.technology_stack: Terraform 1.8 (AKS, Azure PostgreSQL, ACR, Key Vault, Azure Monitor provisioning), Helm 3.14 (parameterized interface-deployment chart), Python 3.12 (InterfaceContainerizer, ReconciliationJob, CutoverOrchestrator, DRSmokeTester using asyncio), Azure DevOps pipelines (CI/CD), AKS 1.30 (AAD workload identity, Azure CNI networking), Azure Database for PostgreSQL Flexible Server (zone-redundant HA), Azure Blob Storage (HL7 archives, Terraform state), Azure Key Vault (secrets), Azure Container Registry (interface images), Azure Monitor + Grafana (observability), PagerDuty (alerting).
target_output.functional_feature_map: InterfaceContainerizer: reads on-premises Mirth Connect channel export XML, generates Helm values.yaml per interface with protocol/direction/connection parameters. Parameterized Helm Chart (interface-deployment): Deployment (2 replicas), Service (ClusterIP), PodDisruptionBudget (minAvailable=1), HorizontalPodAutoscaler (CPU 70%). ReconciliationJob: Kubernetes CronJob hourly, compares on-prem vs. cloud message counts per interface, updates migration_cutover_plans.go_nogo_status. CutoverOrchestrator: CLI tool executing drain-wait-activate-verify sequence per interface during maintenance window. DRSmokeTester: asyncio Python script sending HL7 ADT^A01 per interface in parallel batches of 50, validating ACK and downstream delivery within 60 seconds per interface.
target_output.system_components: AKS Cluster (East US, 3 AZs): system node pool (3x Standard_D4s_v3), interface node pool (5-20x Standard_D2s_v3, HPA). Namespaces: lab-integration, pharmacy-integration, radiology-integration, adt-integration; each with NetworkPolicy. Azure Database for PostgreSQL Flexible Server (GP_Standard_D4s_v3, zone-redundant HA, geo-redundant backup, 7-day PITR). Azure Key Vault: per-namespace secret hierarchy; external-secrets-operator syncs to Kubernetes secrets. Azure Container Registry: interface images tagged {interface_id}:{git_sha}. ReconciliationJob CronJob in migration-tooling namespace. DRSmokeTester as Azure DevOps pipeline step and standalone CLI. Azure Monitor workspace: AKS diagnostic logs, custom metrics from Prometheus scrape. Azure Blob: Terraform state container (versioned), hl7-archives container (lifecycle: cool 30 days, archive 1 year, delete 7 years).
target_output.workflows: Parallel-Run Setup: Terraform applies AKS cluster and Azure PostgreSQL; InterfaceContainerizer generates Helm values for all 200+ interfaces; Azure DevOps deploys interface pods to AKS; on-prem interfaces remain active; ReconciliationJob CronJob starts hourly comparison. Hourly Reconciliation: CronJob queries on-prem message_log and cloud message_queues grouped by interface_id and hour; for each interface compares delivered_count; if |cloud - onprem| / onprem > 0.1% PagerDuty alert fired; if > 0.5% migration_cutover_plans.go_nogo_status set to blocked. Saturday Cutover Window: On-call integration engineer runs CutoverOrchestrator CLI for scheduled batch; pre-flight checks go_nogo_status for all batch interfaces (exits if any blocked); drains on-prem interface (stops new accepts, waits for queue_depth=0, max 30 min); activates cloud interface; sends 3 synthetic HL7 test messages via DRSmokeTester; if all ACKs received in 10 seconds, cutover marked complete; if smoke test fails within 5 minutes, CutoverOrchestrator re-enables on-prem interface and pages P1. DR Failover: Terraform apply from stored state provisions full AKS environment in East US (new cluster if primary failed); DRSmokeTester validates all critical-tier interfaces in parallel; total RTO target 15 minutes.
target_output.data_model: integration_interfaces (interface_id TEXT PK, display_name TEXT, protocol ENUM tcp/mllp/db_poll, direction ENUM inbound/outbound/bidirectional, source_system TEXT, target_system TEXT, criticality_tier INT CHECK (1-3), status ENUM on_prem/parallel_run/cloud/decommissioned, helm_values_git_sha TEXT, last_cutover_at TIMESTAMPTZ). message_queues (queue_id UUID PK, interface_id TEXT FK, message_type TEXT, hl7_content_encrypted BYTEA, received_at TIMESTAMPTZ, delivered_at TIMESTAMPTZ, status ENUM pending/delivered/failed/archived, retry_count INT). migration_cutover_plans (plan_id UUID PK, batch_name TEXT, interface_ids TEXT[], scheduled_window TIMESTAMPTZ, criticality_tier INT, go_nogo_status ENUM clear/blocked/pending, status ENUM planned/in_progress/completed/rolled_back, executed_by TEXT, notes TEXT). disaster_recovery_runbooks (runbook_id UUID PK, version TEXT, steps JSONB, last_tested_at TIMESTAMPTZ, rto_achieved_minutes INT, test_result ENUM pass/fail/partial). reconciliation_snapshots (snapshot_id UUID PK, interface_id TEXT, hour TIMESTAMPTZ, onprem_count INT, cloud_count INT, discrepancy_pct FLOAT, alert_fired BOOL, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). Clinical integration entities: hl7_message_log (id UUID PK, interface_id FK, message_type VARCHAR, patient_mrn_hash VARCHAR, encounter_id VARCHAR, received_at TIMESTAMPTZ, processed_at TIMESTAMPTZ, status ENUM queued/processed/failed), patient_index (id UUID PK, mrn_hash VARCHAR UNIQUE, facility_code VARCHAR, active_encounter_count INT, last_seen_date DATE), physician_crosswalk (id UUID PK, legacy_provider_id VARCHAR, epic_npi VARCHAR, department_id VARCHAR, role ENUM attending/resident/consulting).
target_output.api_design: No public API; internal tooling interfaces: CutoverOrchestrator CLI (python cutover_orchestrator.py --batch-name lab_tier3 --dry-run). DRSmokeTester CLI (python dr_smoke_test.py --interfaces all --timeout 60 --parallel 50). Azure DevOps pipeline APIs for triggering Helm deploy and smoke test stages. Azure Key Vault REST API via external-secrets-operator for secret sync. PostgreSQL connection (SSL required) for ReconciliationJob queries to both on-prem (via site-to-site VPN) and cloud PostgreSQL. Grafana provisioned dashboards query Azure Monitor and Prometheus scrape endpoints.
target_output.security_and_compliance: AAD workload identity: each interface Deployment ServiceAccount annotated with azure.workload.identity/client-id; Azure Managed Identity per namespace grants Key Vault read access scoped to that namespace's secrets only. External-secrets-operator syncs Key Vault secrets (HL7 endpoint connection strings, SSL certificates) to Kubernetes secrets; secrets never in Helm values or Terraform files. TLS 1.3 enforced on all HL7 TCP connections via Kubernetes NetworkPolicy (no plaintext port 2575 in cloud). AKS network policies: pods in lab-integration namespace cannot initiate connections to pharmacy-integration namespace; only ingress from hospital VLAN CIDR allowed per namespace. HL7 message content in PostgreSQL message_queues encrypted at column level (pg_crypto AES-256); patient_id field hashed for audit log entries. HIPAA: BAA signed with Microsoft Azure; AKS diagnostic logs and HL7 access metadata exported to Log Analytics workspace with 7-year retention. Azure Defender for Containers enabled for runtime anomaly detection on integration pods.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Terraform remote state in Azure Blob versioned container; Terraform plan gated on PR approval by infrastructure lead; Terraform apply runs on merge to main via Azure DevOps pipeline with plan output displayed before apply. Helm chart releases managed in Azure DevOps; interface values.yaml files in Git repository interface-configs/ (one file per interface_id); InterfaceContainerizer generates files from on-prem channel export. AKS node pool auto-upgrade: patch versions auto, minor versions manual with 30-day advance notice. Azure Database for PostgreSQL: automated backups every 4 hours, 7-day PITR, geo-redundant backup for DR. HL7 archives in Azure Blob: soft-delete enabled 14 days, lifecycle policy (cool tier after 30 days, archive tier after 1 year, delete after 7 years). Maintenance windows restricted to Saturday 2-6 AM; each cutover batch limited to 40 interfaces (estimated 4 minutes DRSmokeTester + 10 minutes drain/activate per batch of 10 interfaces sequenced).
target_output.observability: Custom Prometheus metrics from interface pods: hl7_messages_processed_total (counter, labels: interface_id, message_type, direction), hl7_delivery_latency_p99_milliseconds (histogram), hl7_nack_count_total (counter), hl7_queue_depth (gauge). Azure Monitor custom metrics for ReconciliationJob: reconciliation_discrepancy_pct per interface. Grafana dashboards: Interface Health (per-namespace throughput and NACK rate), Migration Progress (interfaces by status: on_prem/parallel_run/cloud), Reconciliation History (hourly discrepancy trend per interface). PagerDuty alerts: interface hl7_nack_count_total > 5 in 5 minutes (P2), reconciliation discrepancy > 0.1% (P2), cutover smoke test failure (P1), PostgreSQL replication lag > 30 seconds (P1).
target_output.cost_and_scaling: AKS system node pool (3x D4s_v3): approximately $900/month. Interface node pool (10x D2s_v3 average): approximately $1200/month. Azure PostgreSQL Flexible Server (D4s_v3, zone-redundant): approximately $800/month. Azure Blob storage and networking: approximately $200/month. Total approximately $3100/month versus $8000/year amortized on-premises hardware maintenance, achieving positive ROI within 18 months plus improved DR posture.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 Infrastructure Build (4 weeks): Terraform provision AKS cluster, Azure PostgreSQL, Key Vault, ACR, and Azure Monitor workspace; InterfaceContainerizer generates Helm values for all 200+ interfaces; ReconciliationJob and DRSmokeTester tooling completed and tested with synthetic data. Phase 2 Pilot Migration (8 weeks): Deploy 20 tier-3 (non-clinical reporting) interfaces to AKS in parallel-run; run hourly reconciliation for 4 weeks; execute first Saturday cutover for 20 interfaces after 0% discrepancy sustained 7 days. Phase 3 Tier-2 Migration (12 weeks): Migrate 80 tier-2 interfaces (radiology, clinical documentation) in batches of 20 per Saturday window with full reconciliation pre-checks. Phase 4 Tier-1 Cutover (8 weeks): Migrate 100+ tier-1 interfaces (ADT feeds, lab results, medication orders) with extended parallel-run (8 weeks minimum), DR failover dry run with 15-minute RTO validation, final cutover during hospital-approved window.
target_output.development_guidelines: All Terraform modules must pass terraform validate and tflint in Azure DevOps PR gate. Helm chart changes require dry-run (helm upgrade --dry-run) with diff output reviewed by integration engineering lead. InterfaceContainerizer output (values.yaml files) committed to interface-configs Git repo with mandatory code review; no direct Helm values edits outside InterfaceContainerizer workflow. DRSmokeTester results logged to PostgreSQL smoke_test_results table with interface_id, timestamp, ack_received, downstream_delivery_confirmed, duration_ms. ReconciliationJob discrepancy records retained 90 days in reconciliation_snapshots for migration audit evidence.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: VPN connectivity to on-premises PostgreSQL for ReconciliationJob queries may introduce latency or temporary unavailability; mitigated by 10-second query timeout and retry with cached last-known count. Risk: Mirth Connect export XML for some legacy interfaces may not capture all channel configuration parameters; mitigated by manual review of InterfaceContainerizer output for all tier-1 interfaces. Risk: AKS node pool maintenance causing pod evictions during parallel-run period; mitigated by PodDisruptionBudget (minAvailable=1) on all interface Deployments. Tradeoff: Zone-redundant PostgreSQL HA at higher cost than LRS chosen because message queue data loss on database failure would cause HL7 message loss violating the zero-tolerance constraint. Tradeoff: Interface-by-interface cutover over 6 months is slower than big-bang migration but eliminates risk of simultaneous multi-interface failure during a single maintenance window.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: AKS Azure CNI networking selected over kubenet for improved network policy enforcement and Azure Monitor integration. Terraform remote state in Azure Blob with state locking via Azure Blob lease instead of Terraform Cloud to avoid additional SaaS cost. DRSmokeTester synthetic message uses HL7 ADT^A01 as universal test type supported by all interface types. Interface criticality tiering: tier 1 (ADT, lab, pharmacy), tier 2 (radiology, dietary, scheduling), tier 3 (reporting, analytics feeds) confirmed with clinical operations leadership.

metadata.notes: Epic EHR integration engine migration to AKS; revision 2 closes DR smoke test automation and parallel-run reconciliation tooling gaps.

profile.domain: Clinical IT Security (Zero Trust Remote Access)

project_goal.value: Replace aging on-premises VPN-based remote access system for clinical staff with a modern identity-aware proxy and device trust platform, improving security posture without degrading clinician workflow speed for EHR and clinical application access
project_goal.rationale: Existing VPN provides no device posture checking, no session risk re-evaluation, and cannot selectively expose individual applications; compromise of a VPN credential grants full network access

target_users.value: Clinical staff (nurses, physicians, PAs) accessing EHR and clinical apps remotely; IT administrators managing device trust policies and application proxies
target_users.rationale: Clinicians are primary users; zero friction tolerance for clinical workflow interruption

project_class.rationale: Platform security architecture; no new clinical application features being built

capabilities.rationale: Identity-aware proxy, device trust evaluation, continuous authorization, and Terraform infrastructure are in scope

complexity_level.rationale: Continuous authorization with session risk re-evaluation, device posture checks, and zero-downtime VPN cutover are non-trivial

risk_level.rationale: Misconfiguration of access policies could block clinicians from EHR access during patient care; or conversely, allow unauthorized access to clinical systems

data_sensitivity.rationale: Access session data, device posture assessments, and risk signals contain sensitive security telemetry that must not be exposed

external_exposure.rationale: Identity-aware proxy is internet-facing but requires authentication; no anonymous access

access_model.value: Okta identity with MFA; device posture evaluated by Okta Device Trust; application proxy scoped access by user role and device trust level; continuous session risk re-evaluation every 5 minutes
access_model.rationale: NIST SP 800-207 Zero Trust Architecture requires continuous verification not perimeter-based trust

feature_scope.value: Identity-aware proxy for EHR, clinical apps, and admin consoles; Okta Device Trust posture checks (OS patch level, disk encryption, EDR agent running); continuous authorization engine re-evaluating session risk signals every 5 minutes; impossible travel and anomalous geolocation detection; silent step-up authentication without workflow interruption; Terraform IaC for all GKE and proxy infrastructure
feature_scope.rationale: Complete Zero Trust remote access replacement for the hospital VPN

mvp_scope.value: Identity-aware proxy for Epic EHR web client, Okta MFA enforcement, device posture check (OS patch level and disk encryption), impossible travel detection; 100 clinical staff pilot
mvp_scope.rationale: Epic EHR is highest-priority application; 100-user pilot validates latency and user experience before full deployment

security_baseline.value: Okta MFA required for all remote access; device posture check before session establishment; TLS 1.3 on all proxy connections; no split-tunneling; audit log every access session and posture check result
security_baseline.rationale: HIPAA Security Rule access controls and NIST SP 800-207 Zero Trust requirements

frontend_stack.value: None; identity-aware proxy is transparent to end users except for Okta login page; no custom web application built
frontend_stack.rationale: Infrastructure project; users see Okta and then their target application

backend_stack.value: Go 1.22 with Terraform 1.8 for proxy infrastructure automation; GKE Autopilot for proxy compute; Okta API for policy management
backend_stack.rationale: Go for custom proxy components; Terraform for GKE and GCP resource provisioning; GKE Autopilot for managed Kubernetes

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 (GCP Cloud SQL) for access session records, posture check history, and risk signal audit trail; no patient data stored in proxy infrastructure
data_platform.rationale: Relational model appropriate for structured session and audit records; Cloud SQL provides managed HA

hosting_target.value: GKE Autopilot in us-central1 for proxy components; GCP Cloud SQL for session database; GCP Secret Manager for Okta API credentials and proxy TLS certificates
hosting_target.rationale: GKE Autopilot reduces operational overhead; Cloud SQL provides managed HA with automatic failover

privacy_retention_policy.value: Access session logs and posture check results retained 7 years for HIPAA audit; device trust records retained 1 year; risk signal events retained 90 days
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: HIPAA Security Rule audit control requirements; security forensics retention

future_scope.value: Expand to all hospital applications including PACS and telehealth platforms; user entity behavior analytics (UEBA) on session risk signals; automated policy enforcement for anomalous behavior patterns
future_scope.rationale: Full zero trust architecture after pilot and EHR rollout validation

constraints.value: EHR page load latency must not increase >100ms compared to VPN baseline; zero-downtime cutover from VPN to proxy; pilot cohort must include ICU and ER nurses who cannot tolerate authentication friction
constraints.rationale: Clinical workflow latency and zero interruption are non-negotiable for clinician adoption

observability_baseline.value: Datadog for proxy latency and session metrics; GCP Cloud Logging for audit trail; PagerDuty for proxy availability and authentication failure rate alerts
observability_baseline.rationale: Corporate monitoring standard; Datadog APM provides proxy latency visibility critical for latency constraint validation

execution_preference.value: 100-user pilot cohort for 4 weeks with latency benchmarking; then department-by-department rollout with VPN sunset timeline
execution_preference.rationale: Phased rollout with latency validation gates before each expansion cohort

compliance_context.value: HIPAA Security Rule access controls (45 CFR 164.312(a)); NIST SP 800-207 Zero Trust Architecture; HICP (Health Industry Cybersecurity Practices) mobile device controls; BAA with GCP for clinical staff session metadata
compliance_context.rationale: High risk and confidential data classification trigger formal compliance tracking

project.goal: Replace hospital VPN with identity-aware proxy (GKE/GCP) enforcing device posture and continuous authorization for clinical staff EHR access
project.target_users: Clinical staff (nurses, physicians) and IT administrators
project.timeline: 100-user pilot in 6 weeks; full deployment in 6 months

backend.framework: Terraform 1.8 for GKE and GCP resources; Helm for proxy component deployments
backend.api: Okta API for policy management; GCP Cloud SQL for session state

security.auth: Okta MFA with Device Trust; continuous session risk re-evaluation every 5 minutes
security.encryption: TLS 1.3 on all proxy connections; Cloud SQL SSL; GCP Secret Manager for credentials

data.db: GCP Cloud SQL PostgreSQL 16 (session records, posture check history, audit trail)

constraints.friction: No interactive authentication prompts during active clinical sessions

open_questions.step_up_mechanism: Silent step-up re-authentication mechanism for high-risk session events without workflow interruption

reasoner_reviews.product: The 100-user ICU/ER nurse pilot is the correct cohort for latency validation because these users have the lowest tolerance for authentication friction and the highest clinical workflow speed requirements. Latency benchmarking must use realistic Epic EHR page load tests, not synthetic HTTP requests, to capture the full TLS termination and proxy header injection overhead.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: The identity-aware proxy architecture with Okta Device Trust is well-suited for this use case. The proxy should terminate TLS at the GKE Autopilot ingress, verify Okta session token, check device trust assertion, and forward to the target application with user identity headers. The continuous authorization engine should operate as a separate GKE service reading Okta risk signals via webhook rather than polling, to avoid 5-second evaluation window drift. Session database on Cloud SQL must use connection pooling (PgBouncer) to handle concurrent session lookups at scale.
reasoner_reviews.security: The silent step-up mechanism for impossible travel events must not interrupt active Epic sessions; the correct pattern is background re-authentication via Okta embedded auth flow that refreshes the session token without redirecting the user. Device posture checks should be re-evaluated at session establishment and at 15-minute intervals, not just at initial login, to catch devices that fall out of compliance mid-session.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: 100ms latency budget for the proxy layer requires GKE Autopilot region co-located with the hospital network edge (us-central1 if hospital is Midwest); GKE Autopilot cold start latency (up to 90 seconds for new pod scheduling) must not affect active proxy handling; pre-warming with minimum replica count of 2 is required. VPN cutover zero-downtime requires running proxy and VPN in parallel for the pilot cohort with a user-level switch rather than network-level cutover.

backend.service_design: ProxyIngress (GKE Autopilot Deployment, 2+ replicas, GCP Load Balancer, TLS termination); SessionAuthorizationService (Go, evaluates Okta session token + device trust assertion + risk score, grants/denies access per application proxy rule); ContinuousAuthorizationEngine (Go, receives Okta risk webhook events, re-evaluates open sessions, triggers silent step-up or session revocation); ApplicationProxyRules (Go, stores per-application routing rules and role-to-application access mappings)
backend.api_patterns: Okta API (GET /api/v1/users/{id}/sessions, GET /api/v1/devices/{id}, POST /api/v1/risk/events/ip); internal gRPC between ProxyIngress and SessionAuthorizationService for <5ms evaluation; Cloud SQL for session state reads
backend.failure_handling: ProxyIngress falls back to cached session token validation if Cloud SQL is unavailable (max 30-second cache TTL); ContinuousAuthorizationEngine processes risk webhooks asynchronously; dropped webhooks logged for forensics but do not block active sessions

security.auth_design: Okta workforce identity with FIDO2 hardware key or Okta Verify push MFA; Device Trust evaluation via Okta Device Trust (MDM certificate for hospital-managed devices, Okta FastPass for BYOD); initial session establishment requires posture check pass; subsequent proxy requests validate existing Okta session token
security.authorization_model: Per-application proxy rules in PostgreSQL: application_id, required_trust_level ENUM managed/byod/any, required_roles TEXT[], allowed_hours TEXT; ProxyIngress evaluates rules per request; EHR proxy requires managed device trust for clinical role users
security.audit_and_logging_controls: Every proxy access request logged to PostgreSQL access_sessions with user_id, device_id, application_id, posture_check_result, risk_score, decision, timestamp; impossible travel events logged separately with source_ip, prior_ip, distance_km, time_diff_minutes; Cloud Logging exports audit records to long-term GCS bucket
security.incident_response_notes: Impossible travel detection triggers silent step-up without session interruption; if step-up fails within 60 seconds, session revoked and security team notified via PagerDuty; compromised device certificate revocation via Okta Device Trust MDM command

data.entities: access_sessions (session_id UUID PK, user_id TEXT, device_id TEXT, device_trust_level TEXT, application_id TEXT, posture_check_result JSONB, risk_score FLOAT, session_start TIMESTAMPTZ, last_evaluated_at TIMESTAMPTZ, status ENUM active/revoked/expired), device_trust_policies (policy_id UUID PK, application_id TEXT, required_trust_level TEXT, required_roles TEXT[], updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ), posture_checks (check_id UUID PK, device_id TEXT, session_id UUID, os_patch_current BOOL, disk_encrypted BOOL, edr_agent_running BOOL, check_timestamp TIMESTAMPTZ, result ENUM pass/fail/pending), risk_signals (signal_id UUID PK, session_id UUID, signal_type ENUM impossible_travel/anomalous_geo/brute_force/concurrent_session, source_ip INET, detail JSONB, detected_at TIMESTAMPTZ), audit_logs (log_id UUID PK, session_id UUID, user_id TEXT, action TEXT, application_id TEXT, decision ENUM allow/deny/step_up, timestamp TIMESTAMPTZ)
data.storage_design: Cloud SQL PostgreSQL 16 (us-central1, 2-vCPU HA instance, 7-day PITR, automated daily backup, PgBouncer connection pool 100 max connections); GCS bucket for long-term audit log export (lifecycle: standard 90 days, nearline 1 year, coldline 7 years); GCP Secret Manager for Okta API keys and proxy TLS certificates.

devops.deployment_topology: GKE Autopilot cluster (us-central1): ProxyIngress Deployment (min 2 replicas, HPA on CPU 60%), SessionAuthorizationService Deployment (min 2 replicas), ContinuousAuthorizationEngine Deployment (min 1 replica), PgBouncer StatefulSet (2 replicas). GCP Load Balancer with managed TLS certificate (Google-managed ACM equivalent). Terraform state in GCS bucket. GitLab CI pipeline for Terraform plan/apply and Helm deployments.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitLab CI: terraform validate + tflint, helm lint + dry-run, Go unit tests (proxy routing, device trust evaluation logic), integration tests against Okta sandbox tenant, deploy to staging on merge to main, deploy to production on manual approval gate
devops.rollback_strategy: Helm rollback to previous release on smoke test failure; Terraform state rollback via GCS versioned bucket; VPN remains active during pilot as fallback; per-user proxy bypass flag in Okta profile for emergency access during incidents

target_output.thinking_summary: First-pass architecture for a Zero Trust remote access replacement on GCP. Key decisions: GKE Autopilot for proxy compute with minimum 2 replicas to avoid cold-start latency; Okta Device Trust with separate posture check re-evaluation at 15-minute intervals (not just initial login); ContinuousAuthorizationEngine as a webhook receiver rather than polling to maintain <5 second risk signal processing; Cloud SQL with PgBouncer for session lookup performance at clinical scale. Silent step-up via Okta embedded auth flow is the correct approach for impossible travel events during active Epic sessions.
target_output.title: Clinical Zero Trust Remote Access Platform
target_output.executive_summary: An infrastructure project replacing the hospital's on-premises VPN with a GKE Autopilot-hosted identity-aware proxy enforcing Okta MFA, device posture checks, and continuous authorization for clinical staff accessing Epic EHR and other clinical applications remotely. The ProxyIngress terminates TLS, validates Okta session tokens with Device Trust assertions, and routes to target applications with sub-100ms overhead. A ContinuousAuthorizationEngine receives Okta risk webhooks (impossible travel, anomalous geolocation) and silently re-authenticates high-risk sessions without interrupting active clinical workflows. Terraform 1.8 manages all GKE and GCP infrastructure as code. The system enables device-level and application-level access control that VPN-based perimeter access cannot provide.
target_output.architecture_overview: Four-component Zero Trust architecture: ProxyIngress (GKE Autopilot, minimum 2 replicas, GCP Load Balancer with managed TLS, receives all clinical staff remote HTTPS requests), SessionAuthorizationService (Go service evaluating Okta session token validity, Device Trust level, posture check recency, and per-application access rules against device_trust_policies table in Cloud SQL within <5ms using PgBouncer connection pool), ContinuousAuthorizationEngine (Go service receiving Okta ThreatInsight webhooks for risk signals, re-evaluating active sessions asynchronously, triggering silent step-up via Okta embedded auth for high-risk events), and ApplicationProxy (routing layer forwarding validated requests to Epic EHR, PACS, admin consoles with injected X-User-Identity headers). Cloud SQL PostgreSQL 16 stores access_sessions, posture_checks, risk_signals, and audit_logs. GCP Secret Manager holds Okta API credentials, proxy TLS private key, and application backend certificates. VPN and proxy run in parallel during the 100-user pilot; per-user proxy enablement via Okta profile flag allows individual cutover without network-level changes.
target_output.technology_stack: Go 1.22 (ProxyIngress, SessionAuthorizationService, ContinuousAuthorizationEngine custom components), Terraform 1.8 (GKE Autopilot, Cloud SQL, Secret Manager, GCS, GCP Load Balancer), Helm 3.14 (proxy component deployments), GKE Autopilot us-central1, GCP Cloud SQL PostgreSQL 16 (HA, PgBouncer 1.22), GCP Secret Manager, GCP Cloud Logging + GCS (audit export), Okta Workforce Identity (Device Trust, ThreatInsight webhooks, Embedded Auth), GitLab CI, Datadog (proxy latency APM, session metrics), PagerDuty.
target_output.functional_feature_map: ProxyIngress: TLS termination, Okta session cookie extraction, routes to SessionAuthorizationService, forwards approved requests to ApplicationProxy with X-User-Identity headers. SessionAuthorizationService: validates Okta session token via Okta API, retrieves device_trust_level and latest posture_check result from Cloud SQL, evaluates device_trust_policies rules for the target application_id, records access decision to access_sessions, returns allow/deny/step_up response to ProxyIngress in <5ms. ContinuousAuthorizationEngine: Okta webhook receiver (POST /webhooks/okta/risk-events), processes impossible_travel and anomalous_geo signals, looks up active sessions by user_id, initiates Okta embedded auth silent re-authentication, revokes session if step-up fails within 60 seconds, pages PagerDuty P1 on revocation. PostureCheckScheduler: GKE CronJob running every 15 minutes querying Okta Device Trust API for all active sessions' devices, updating posture_checks table, flagging sessions with failed posture for re-evaluation.
target_output.system_components: GCP External Load Balancer (global anycast, managed TLS, health checks to ProxyIngress pods). GKE Autopilot cluster (us-central1): ProxyIngress Deployment (min 2 replicas, HPA CPU 60%, max 10), SessionAuthorizationService Deployment (min 2 replicas, HPA on request rate), ContinuousAuthorizationEngine Deployment (min 1 replica, webhook receiver), PostureCheckScheduler CronJob (every 15 minutes). Cloud SQL PostgreSQL 16 (2-vCPU, HA standby, 7-day PITR, PgBouncer StatefulSet 2 replicas). GCP Secret Manager: okta_api_key, proxy_tls_private_key, cloud_sql_password, application backend certs. GCS bucket: Terraform state (versioned), audit log export (lifecycle-tiered). Datadog agent DaemonSet for APM and custom metrics.
target_output.workflows: Clinical Staff Remote Access: User opens browser to Epic EHR URL; GCP Load Balancer routes to ProxyIngress; ProxyIngress checks for Okta session cookie; if absent, redirects to Okta login with MFA challenge (FIDO2 or push); upon MFA success, Okta Device Trust check evaluates device certificate (managed) or FastPass posture (BYOD); ProxyIngress calls SessionAuthorizationService; service validates session, checks posture_checks recency (<15 min), evaluates device_trust_policies rule for Epic EHR (requires managed device for clinical roles); if allowed, request forwarded to Epic with X-User-Identity and X-Device-Trust headers; access_sessions record created in Cloud SQL; total added latency target <100ms. Continuous Authorization Risk Event: Okta ThreatInsight detects impossible travel event (login from Chicago then Tokyo within 2 hours); POST webhook to ContinuousAuthorizationEngine /webhooks/okta/risk-events; engine looks up active sessions for user_id; risk_signals record created; Okta embedded auth flow silently re-authenticates user in background iframe without disrupting active Epic session; if re-authentication succeeds within 60 seconds, session continues with risk_score updated; if fails, session revoked, ProxyIngress returns 401 on next request, PagerDuty P1 alert with impossible_travel detail. VPN-to-Proxy Cutover (per user): IT admin sets okta_proxy_enabled=true in user Okta profile; next remote access attempt routes through ProxyIngress instead of VPN tunnel; VPN tunnel remains available as fallback via explicit VPN client connect; after 30 days no-incident, IT admin removes user from VPN access group.
target_output.data_model: access_sessions (session_id UUID PK, user_id TEXT NOT NULL, device_id TEXT NOT NULL, device_trust_level ENUM managed/byod/unverified, application_id TEXT NOT NULL, posture_check_result JSONB, risk_score FLOAT DEFAULT 0.0, session_start TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL, last_evaluated_at TIMESTAMPTZ, status ENUM active/revoked/expired, revocation_reason TEXT). device_trust_policies (policy_id UUID PK, application_id TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE, required_trust_level ENUM managed/byod/any, required_roles TEXT[], allowed_hours TSRANGE, updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ). posture_checks (check_id UUID PK, device_id TEXT NOT NULL, session_id UUID FK, os_patch_current BOOL, disk_encrypted BOOL, edr_agent_running BOOL, check_timestamp TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL, result ENUM pass/fail). risk_signals (signal_id UUID PK, session_id UUID FK, signal_type ENUM impossible_travel/anomalous_geo/brute_force/concurrent_session, source_ip INET, prior_ip INET, detail JSONB, detected_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL). audit_logs (log_id UUID PK, session_id UUID FK, user_id TEXT, action TEXT, application_id TEXT, decision ENUM allow/deny/step_up/revoke, response_time_ms INT, timestamp TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL). Indexes: access_sessions(user_id, status), access_sessions(last_evaluated_at), risk_signals(session_id, detected_at), audit_logs(user_id, timestamp).
target_output.api_design: Internal gRPC: ProxyIngress -> SessionAuthorizationService: EvaluateAccess(EvaluateRequest{session_token, device_id, application_id, source_ip}) returns EvaluateResponse{decision ENUM allow/deny/step_up, reason TEXT, session_id}. ContinuousAuthorizationEngine webhook: POST /webhooks/okta/risk-events (Okta signed webhook payload; HMAC-SHA256 signature verification). Okta Management API (outbound): GET /api/v1/sessions/{id}, GET /api/v1/devices/{id}, POST /api/v1/users/{id}/sessions/{id}/lifecycle/revoke. PostureCheckScheduler reads Okta Device Trust API: GET /api/v1/devices?search=status eq 'ACTIVE'. Admin REST API (internal): GET /api/admin/v1/sessions?user_id= (IT admin session lookup), DELETE /api/admin/v1/sessions/{id} (manual revocation), PUT /api/admin/v1/policies/{application_id} (update device_trust_policies). Datadog StatsD metrics endpoint for custom session counters.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Okta MFA required for all remote access sessions; FIDO2 hardware keys for privileged IT administrator accounts; Okta Verify push for clinical staff (no SMS or email OTP). Device Trust: hospital-managed devices require valid MDM certificate (JAMF or Intune enrollment); BYOD devices require Okta FastPass with pass-level posture (disk encryption mandatory). ProxyIngress rejects requests without valid Okta session cookie; no fallback to IP allowlisting. SessionAuthorizationService evaluates posture_checks recency: posture check older than 15 minutes causes ProxyIngress to re-trigger device check before granting access. TLS 1.3 enforced on all GCP Load Balancer listeners; HSTS preload applied. Cloud SQL connections require SSL client certificate from GKE workload identity. GCP Secret Manager: all Okta API keys and TLS private keys with 90-day automatic rotation. HIPAA: access_sessions and audit_logs retained 7 years; exported to GCS coldline; GCP BAA covers all PHI-adjacent session metadata. NIST SP 800-207 alignment: per-resource authorization (device_trust_policies), continuous verification (ContinuousAuthorizationEngine), least-privilege access (application-scoped proxy rules). Impossible travel revocation within 60 seconds of detection documented in HIPAA incident response procedure.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Terraform manages: GKE Autopilot cluster, Cloud SQL instance with HA standby, Secret Manager secrets (initial provisioning), GCS buckets (state + audit), GCP Load Balancer with managed TLS certificate, Datadog integration. GitLab CI pipeline: terraform validate + tflint (MR gate), terraform plan output reviewed by security lead, terraform apply on main branch merge with human approval gate for production. Helm chart manages ProxyIngress, SessionAuthorizationService, ContinuousAuthorizationEngine, and PostureCheckScheduler; all charts use GKE Autopilot workload identity annotations. Environments: dev (GKE Autopilot dev cluster, Okta sandbox tenant, 1 test application), staging (GKE Autopilot staging, 10-user test cohort, Epic staging environment), production (GKE Autopilot us-central1, minimum 2 replicas, HPA enabled). VPN parallel operation: Cisco AnyConnect VPN remains active during pilot phase; per-user Okta profile attribute okta_proxy_enabled routes traffic to proxy; VPN group membership removed after 30-day incident-free proxy operation per user. Rollback: per-user proxy disable by clearing okta_proxy_enabled attribute (instant fallback to VPN); Helm rollback via helm rollback {release} for proxy component failures.
target_output.observability: Datadog APM traces on ProxyIngress (proxy_request_duration_p99, target_application label); custom StatsD metrics: proxy_sessions_active (gauge), proxy_auth_failures_total (counter, reason label), device_posture_failures_total (counter), risk_signal_events_total (counter, signal_type label), step_up_success_rate (gauge), session_revocations_total (counter). GCP Cloud Logging: all access decisions exported to Cloud Logging with user_id (hashed), device_id, application_id, decision fields. PagerDuty alerts: proxy_auth_failures_total > 50/min (P2 - possible attack), session_revocations_total > 5/min (P1 - possible credential compromise), ProxyIngress pod count < 2 (P1 - availability risk), Cloud SQL replication lag > 30s (P2). Grafana dashboards: Session Activity (active sessions by application, new sessions per minute), Security Events (risk signals, step-up rate, revocations), Latency (p50/p99 by application_id vs. 100ms SLA line).
target_output.cost_and_scaling: GKE Autopilot scales to 0 idle pods (no workload charge); at 500 concurrent clinical staff sessions estimated 4 replicas (2 ProxyIngress, 2 SessionAuthorizationService): approximately $400/month. Cloud SQL (2-vCPU HA): approximately $300/month. Secret Manager, GCS, and networking: approximately $100/month. Total approximately $800/month versus $120K/year VPN hardware refresh cost, with zero per-user licensing versus typical VPN client CAL costs. At 3000 concurrent users (full clinical staff), HPA scales to 12 ProxyIngress replicas; estimated $1800/month.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 Pilot (6 weeks): Provision GKE Autopilot dev and staging environments via Terraform; configure Okta Device Trust for hospital-managed device MDM certificates; deploy ProxyIngress and SessionAuthorizationService with Epic EHR as sole proxied application; enroll 100 ICU/ER nurses; benchmark Epic page load latency vs. VPN baseline; validate <100ms proxy overhead. Phase 2 EHR Full Rollout (8 weeks): Migrate all clinical staff (approximately 1500 users) to proxy for Epic EHR; enable ContinuousAuthorizationEngine with impossible travel detection; department-by-department VPN access removal after 30-day incident-free period per cohort. Phase 3 Application Expansion (12 weeks): Add PACS, telehealth, and admin console proxy rules with appropriate device trust policies; enable BYOD Okta FastPass enrollment for qualified roles; begin VPN decommission planning.
target_output.development_guidelines: Go services use structured zerolog JSON logging with session_id and user_id_hash (never plain user_id in logs). SessionAuthorizationService cache: Redis is explicitly excluded; Cloud SQL with PgBouncer is the session state store to maintain single source of truth; cached session token validation limited to 30-second TTL in ProxyIngress memory only. Terraform modules versioned and pinned in provider lockfile; no provider version wildcards. GitLab CI blocks merge if terraform plan shows unexpected resource deletions (protected resources: Cloud SQL, Secret Manager, GCS audit bucket). All Okta API calls use exponential backoff with jitter (max 3 retries, 5-second cap) to handle Okta rate limits gracefully.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: GKE Autopilot pod cold-start latency (30-90 seconds for new pod scheduling) could cause proxy latency spike during rapid demand increase; mitigated by minimum replica count of 2 on ProxyIngress and SessionAuthorizationService. Risk: Okta API rate limits (600 requests/minute per key) may be reached at scale with per-request session validation; mitigation is in-process JWT signature validation for session token integrity with Okta API call only on cache miss (30-second TTL) and device trust refresh. Risk: Impossible travel detection false positives (clinician uses hospital Wi-Fi then residential internet within short window) causing unnecessary step-up events; mitigation is 2-hour minimum time window and minimum 500km distance for impossible travel classification. Tradeoff: Cloud SQL over Spanner chosen for simpler schema management and lower cost at clinical scale; Spanner considered if active sessions exceed 100K concurrent. Tradeoff: Webhook-based continuous authorization over polling reduces Okta API calls but risks missed events if webhook delivery fails; Dead-letter queue in Pub/Sub captures failed webhook deliveries for reprocessing.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: GKE Autopilot selected over GKE Standard for reduced node management; auto-provisioned node pools adequate for proxy workload profile. Okta Embedded Auth (not redirect) chosen for silent step-up to avoid Epic session context loss during re-authentication. Cloud SQL over AlloyDB chosen for cost-efficiency at current session volume. Impossible travel threshold: 2 hours and 500km confirmed with CISO as balancing false-positive rate with detection sensitivity. BYOD supported in Phase 3 only; Phase 1/2 restricted to hospital-managed devices for clinical role users.

metadata.notes: Zero trust remote access platform replacing hospital VPN; first-pass strong quality with detailed continuous authorization and device trust architecture.

project_goal.value: Real-time drug-drug interaction severity scoring API integrated into the hospital medication ordering workflow
project_goal.rationale: Core patient safety need: prevent adverse drug events at point-of-care within 200ms SLA

target_users.value: Prescribers via EHR integration (machine client), pharmacists via internal portal, pharmacy leadership via weekly analytics
target_users.rationale: Primary consumers identified in clinical workflow mapping sessions

project_class.rationale: Pure backend API service; EHR is the client, no browser frontend

capabilities.rationale: ML inference pipeline, REST API, Azure AD auth, and structured data storage required

complexity_level.rationale: ML model versioning, sub-200ms latency SLA, FDA SaMD classification, and HIPAA PHI handling overhead

risk_level.rationale: PHI in request payloads and patient safety impact if incorrect interaction scores returned

data_sensitivity.rationale: Request payloads contain patient medication lists which constitute PHI

external_exposure.rationale: Service exposed only to hospital internal VNET; EHR is an internal system

access_model.value: Azure AD service principal OAuth 2.0 client credentials flow; per-consumer scopes (ddi:score, ddi:overrides:read, ddi:admin)
access_model.rationale: Machine-to-machine EHR integration requires service principal auth; no human login flow

feature_scope.value: DDI severity scoring endpoint, drug database lookup, alert override capture with reason taxonomy, model version hot-swap, severity threshold configuration, analytics export
feature_scope.rationale: Covers full prescriber workflow from interaction check to override logging and pharmacy leadership review

mvp_scope.value: DDI scoring endpoint with confidence scores and SHAP rationale, alert override logging with enumerated reasons, Redis-cached drug database lookups, async prescribing event writes
mvp_scope.rationale: Minimum needed for EHR go-live; analytics export and model hot-swap deferred to Phase 2

security_baseline.value: TLS 1.3 in transit, AES-256 at rest in PostgreSQL, medication lists stripped from all logs, Azure AD RBAC with per-consumer scopes
security_baseline.rationale: HIPAA minimum controls; PHI-free logging required for audit safety

frontend_stack.value: N/A - API service only
frontend_stack.rationale: No browser frontend; EHR system is the sole consumer

backend_stack.value: Python 3.12 with FastAPI, Pydantic v2, asyncpg, XGBoost 2.1 for DDI model inference
backend_stack.rationale: ML team expertise in Python; FastAPI async for low-latency endpoints; asyncpg for non-blocking PostgreSQL

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 for drug pairs, prescribing events (monthly partitions), alert overrides, and model versions; Redis 7 for drug database cache
data_platform.rationale: Relational integrity for audit trail; Redis for sub-5ms drug lookup cache hit rate target >95%

hosting_target.value: Azure Container Apps with VNET injection in hospital private subnet
hosting_target.rationale: Private network isolation with managed autoscaling and no Kubernetes operational overhead

privacy_retention_policy.value: Prescribing event logs retained 7 years per HIPAA; alert override records retained indefinitely for safety analysis; no PHI in application logs
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Healthcare data retention requirements and ongoing safety monitoring needs

future_scope.value: Polypharmacy risk scoring for patients on >10 medications, pharmacogenomics data integration, automated model retraining pipeline
future_scope.rationale: Year 2 roadmap items pending SaMD clearance for expanded indications

constraints.value: <200ms p99 latency for DDI scoring, deployed within hospital Azure tenant VNET, no external internet egress from scoring service
constraints.rationale: EHR integration SLA and data residency requirements

observability_baseline.value: Datadog APM on all FastAPI routes, custom metrics for model inference latency and confidence score distribution, PagerDuty on p99 >200ms
observability_baseline.rationale: SLA enforcement and model drift detection requirements

execution_preference.value: 2-week sprints with clinical SME review at each sprint boundary before feature release
execution_preference.rationale: Clinical validation required before each production release

compliance_context.value: HIPAA BAA with Azure, FDA SaMD non-significant risk classification per 2023 guidance, Joint Commission medication management standard MM.04.01.01
compliance_context.rationale: PHI processing and patient safety software classification requirements

llm_integration.value: XGBoost DDI severity classifier v2.1 loaded from Azure Blob Storage at startup; SHAP 0.44 for feature importance explanation; fallback to rule-based FDA interaction database if model unavailable
llm_integration.rationale: XGBoost chosen for sub-10ms CPU inference; SHAP rationale required by Joint Commission for prescriber transparency at point-of-care

project.goal: Drug-drug interaction scoring API at medication order time with <200ms p99 SLA
project.target_users: EHR system (machine client), pharmacists (indirect), pharmacy leadership
project.timeline: Phase 1 live Q2 2026

backend.api: RESTful with OpenAPI 3.1

security.auth: Azure AD service principal OAuth 2.0 client credentials
security.phi_controls: No PHI in logs; patient_id hashed; medication list stripped from access logs

data.cache: Redis 7 Azure Cache for drug database (TTL 24h, keyed by ndc:db_version)

devops.hosting: Azure Container Apps with VNET injection

constraints.network: Hospital Azure tenant VNET, no external internet egress

open_questions.model_retraining: Cadence and clinical validation protocol for quarterly model updates TBD with ML team

reasoner_reviews.product: The DDI scoring endpoint is the highest-value deliverable and must meet the 200ms SLA unconditionally - EHR teams will reject the integration if the SLA is missed even occasionally. Alert override capture is critical for the pharmacy leadership weekly review workflow and was underspecified in Round 1; the override reason taxonomy must be enumerated in the contract before go-live. Analytics export for leadership dashboards should stay deferred to Phase 2 to protect the EHR integration timeline.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Round 1 lacked an explicit model versioning strategy - blue/green slot swap on Azure Container Apps is the correct pattern for zero-downtime model updates but requires the model artifact to live in Blob Storage rather than baked into the container image. The Redis cache key design for drug lookups must incorporate the drug database version to prevent stale interaction data after a drug database update. FastAPI async endpoints with asyncpg connection pooling are required to sustain <200ms p99 under EHR morning surge load - the synchronous write pattern in Round 1 is the single biggest SLA risk.
reasoner_reviews.security: PHI exposure in request payloads containing patient medication lists is the primary risk vector. All FastAPI access logs must strip the medication list from logged request bodies before forwarding to Datadog via custom LoggingMiddleware. Azure AD service principal scopes must be per-consumer: EHR gets ddi:score scope only, pharmacist portal gets ddi:score and ddi:overrides:read. Prescriber audit logs capturing which clinician triggered an interaction check must be stored in PostgreSQL, not in application log streams that could be forwarded to third-party SIEM without PHI controls.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: The 200ms p99 constraint is achievable with Redis drug cache hit rate >95% and XGBoost in-process inference at <10ms. The bottleneck identified in Round 1 was the synchronous PostgreSQL write blocking the response path - moving to FastAPI BackgroundTasks after response dispatch removes this bottleneck entirely. asyncpg pool contention under burst load must be validated in the staging canary before production cutover.
reasoner_reviews.critic: Round 1 missed severity threshold configurability - clinical teams will need to tune display thresholds based on override rate telemetry without a service redeployment. A PostgreSQL-backed ThresholdConfigModule with SIGHUP reload is the appropriate pattern. Also: the model warm-up on Redis cold start needs a readiness probe gate to prevent the Container Apps health check from routing traffic before the drug cache is populated.

backend.service_design: FastAPI application with three router modules: /v1/ddi (scoring and overrides), /v1/admin (model version and threshold management). DDIInferenceModule loads XGBoost artifact from Azure Blob at startup with SHA-256 checksum validation. BackgroundTaskQueue uses FastAPI BackgroundTasks for async prescribing_events inserts post-response. ThresholdConfigModule reads severity thresholds from PostgreSQL config table on startup and on SIGHUP signal.
backend.api_patterns: POST /v1/ddi/score accepts {patient_id, proposed_drug_ndc, active_medications:[{ndc,dose,frequency}]}, returns {interactions:[{drug_pair,severity_level,confidence_score,shap_rationale,evidence_summary}],model_version,request_id,latency_ms}. POST /v1/ddi/overrides captures override_reason enum. POST /v1/admin/model-version triggers in-process hot-swap. PATCH /v1/admin/thresholds updates config. All versioned under /v1/ with OpenAPI 3.1 spec auto-generated.
backend.failure_handling: Circuit breaker on XGBoost inference: if model returns error or exceeds 150ms internal budget, fall back to rule-based FDA interaction table. Redis unavailability triggers direct PostgreSQL drug_pairs query with ddi.fallback.activated metric emitted. Graceful shutdown drains BackgroundTasks queue before container stop to minimize prescribing_event write loss.
backend.scaling_notes: Azure Container Apps autoscales 2-10 replicas on HTTP request concurrency target 20. XGBoost loaded in-process per replica to avoid network inference round-trip. asyncpg connection pool size 10 per replica. Cache warm-up background task runs on startup; readiness probe gates traffic until warm-up completes.

security.auth_design: Azure AD OAuth 2.0 client credentials flow. EHR registered as service principal with ddi:score scope. Pharmacist portal registered separately with ddi:score and ddi:overrides:read. ML ops team with ddi:admin. Token validation via python-jose with JWKS endpoint caching (1h refresh interval).
security.authorization_model: Scope-based enforcement per FastAPI dependency: ddi:score scope required for POST /v1/ddi/score. ddi:overrides:read for GET /v1/ddi/overrides. ddi:admin for /v1/admin/* endpoints. Scope claims validated in FastAPI Security dependency injected per router.
security.audit_and_logging_controls: Every DDI score request logged to prescribing_events table: prescriber_id (token sub claim), patient_id_hash (SHA-256, not raw), proposed_drug_ndc, top_interaction_severity, model_version_id, response_latency_ms, event_timestamp. Medication list never persisted. Alert overrides in alert_overrides table with prescriber_id and override_reason. Model version changes recorded in model_versions with deployed_by field.
security.privacy_controls: Custom LoggingMiddleware strips active_medications array from request body before Datadog access log forwarding. patient_id stored as SHA-256 hash in prescribing_events. PostgreSQL columns containing any potential PHI encrypted with Azure-managed TDE. No PHI in Datadog traces or custom metrics tags.

data.entities: drug_pairs (id UUID PK, drug_a_ndc VARCHAR, drug_b_ndc VARCHAR, fda_severity_class ENUM critical/major/moderate/minor, evidence_level ENUM A/B/C, last_updated TIMESTAMPTZ). prescribing_events (id UUID PK, prescriber_id_hash CHAR(64), patient_id_hash CHAR(64), proposed_drug_ndc VARCHAR, active_drug_count SMALLINT, top_severity ENUM, model_version_id UUID FK, latency_ms SMALLINT, event_timestamp TIMESTAMPTZ) PARTITION BY RANGE (event_timestamp). alert_overrides (id UUID PK, prescribing_event_id UUID FK, prescriber_id VARCHAR, override_reason ENUM must_use/benefit_outweighs_risk/patient_tolerates/already_on_monitoring, override_timestamp TIMESTAMPTZ). model_versions (id UUID PK, model_file_blob_path TEXT, checksum_sha256 CHAR(64), is_active BOOLEAN, activated_at TIMESTAMPTZ, deactivated_at TIMESTAMPTZ, deployed_by VARCHAR). severity_thresholds (id SMALLINT PK, severity_class ENUM, display_threshold NUMERIC, updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ). clinical_rationales (id UUID PK, drug_pair_id UUID FK, evidence_source TEXT, summary_text TEXT, reference_pmid VARCHAR).
data.storage_design: PostgreSQL 16 Azure Flexible Server zone-redundant. drug_pairs and drug_database_entries cached in Redis 7 keyed by ndc:{drug_ndc}:dbv:{db_version} with 24h TTL. prescribing_events partitioned monthly for 7-year retention management with automated archival to Azure Blob cold storage after 90 days.
data.retention_and_deletion: prescribing_events retained 7 years; monthly partitions archived to Azure Blob cold tier after 90 days. alert_overrides retained indefinitely for safety audit. model_versions soft-deleted with deactivated_at; model artifacts in Blob retained for regulatory audit trail.
data.data_risks: Redis cache cold start after deployment flushes drug database cache causing PostgreSQL fallback spike. Mitigation: cache warm-up background task on container startup with readiness probe gate. Monthly prescribing_events partition table creates ~100GB/year growth at 500 scoring calls/day; archived partitions managed via pg_partman.

devops.deployment_topology: Azure Container Apps environment in hospital VNET with private endpoints to Azure Database for PostgreSQL Flexible Server and Azure Cache for Redis. No public internet ingress. EHR connects via Azure Internal Load Balancer. Container image in Azure Container Registry with vulnerability scanning gate in CI.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: ruff lint, mypy --strict type check, pytest with 85% coverage gate (DDI inference and override modules at 95%), Docker build, push to Azure Container Registry, Container Apps revision deploy with 10% canary traffic split for 15 minutes. Automated promotion if ddi.score.p99_ms <180 and error_rate <0.1%; automatic rollback otherwise. Model update pipeline separate: validates XGBoost artifact checksum, uploads to Blob, calls POST /v1/admin/model-version hot-swap endpoint.
devops.observability_stack: Datadog APM sidecar on each Container Apps replica. DogStatsD custom metrics: ddi.inference.latency_ms histogram, ddi.cache.hit_rate gauge, ddi.fallback.activated counter, ddi.override.count by reason, ddi.events.write_latency_ms for async background task lag. PagerDuty: p99 >200ms for 2 min, error_rate >0.5% for 1 min, fallback >5/min.
devops.rollback_strategy: Container Apps revision rollback to previous revision via single az containerapp revision set-mode command. Model rollback via POST /v1/admin/model-version with previous model_version_id - completes in <60 seconds without container restart.

ISS-001.title: Synchronous prescribing_events write blocks DDI scoring response path
ISS-001.detail: Round 1 plan wrote prescribing_events to PostgreSQL synchronously before returning the API response, adding 15-30ms to median latency and risking p99 SLA breach under EHR morning surge load.

history.note: Identified by architect_reasoner: blocking DB write in response path

ISS-002.title: XGBoost model bundled in container image prevents hot-swap
ISS-002.detail: Round 1 baked the XGBoost model file into the container image. Updating the model required a full image rebuild and rolling restart, causing 30-60s of reduced capacity during EHR peak hours.

history.note: Identified by architect_reasoner: model in image prevents operational model updates

ISS-003.title: Severity display thresholds hardcoded in model inference code
ISS-003.detail: Clinical teams need to tune severity display thresholds based on override rate telemetry without redeploying. Round 1 had no admin endpoint for threshold configuration.

history.note: Identified by critic review

focus_issues.title: Synchronous prescribing_events write blocks DDI scoring response path
focus_issues.detail: Round 1 wrote prescribing_events synchronously, adding 15-30ms to median latency and risking p99 SLA breach.

focus_issues.title: XGBoost model bundled in container image prevents hot-swap
focus_issues.detail: Model baked into Docker image requires full rebuild and rolling restart for any model update.

latest_recommendations: Move prescribing_events write to FastAPI BackgroundTasks after response dispatch
Load XGBoost model from Azure Blob Storage at runtime so model updates only require Blob upload and /admin/model-version call
Add /admin/thresholds endpoint with PostgreSQL-backed config table for severity threshold tuning without redeployment
revision_memory.latest_plan_title: Clinical DDI Scoring API - Round 1

convergence_state.reason: Two high-severity issues unresolved blocking SLA compliance and operational model management

previous_audits.summary: Solid FastAPI and XGBoost foundation with appropriate HIPAA controls, but two high-severity operational issues prevent go-live: synchronous DB writes risk the 200ms SLA and model bundled in container image makes clinical model updates operationally unsafe.
strengths: Correct Azure AD service principal auth with per-consumer scoped credentials
XGBoost with SHAP rationale meets Joint Commission clinical transparency requirement
Redis drug cache design appropriate for sub-5ms lookup latency target
concerns: Prescribing event write in the critical response path adds unpredictable latency under burst EHR load
Container image rebuild required for every model update is operationally unsafe for an active clinical service
No mechanism for clinical teams to tune severity thresholds post-deployment without redeployment
blocking_issues: ISS-001: Synchronous DB write can push p99 above 200ms SLA during EHR morning surge
ISS-002: Model bundled in container image makes rapid clinical model updates impractical
recommendations: Use FastAPI BackgroundTasks to dispatch prescribing_events insert after response dispatch
Load model artifact from Azure Blob Storage path stored in model_versions table; add /admin/model-version hot-swap endpoint
Store severity thresholds in PostgreSQL config table with SIGHUP-triggered in-process reload

issue_updates.title: Synchronous prescribing_events write blocks response
issue_updates.detail: Plan workflows section confirmed sync write before response dispatch

issue_updates.title: Model bundled in container image
issue_updates.detail: technology_stack section described model as copied into Docker image at build time

previous_plan.title: Clinical DDI Scoring API - Round 1
previous_plan.executive_summary: FastAPI Python 3.12 service on Azure Container Apps delivering drug-drug interaction severity scores from an XGBoost v2.1 classifier with SHAP explanations within 200ms. Medication lists checked against Redis-cached drug database. Azure AD service principal auth with HIPAA-compliant PHI handling.
previous_plan.architecture_overview: FastAPI service on Azure Container Apps with VNET injection. XGBoost DDI model bundled in Docker image and loaded at container startup. Redis 7 for drug database caching with 24h TTL. PostgreSQL 16 for prescribing events and override logs written synchronously in response path.
previous_plan.data_model: drug_pairs, prescribing_events (synchronous write in response path), alert_overrides, drug_database_entries cached in Redis.
previous_plan.security_and_compliance: Azure AD OAuth 2.0 service principal. TLS 1.3. Medication list stripped from access logs. HIPAA BAA with Azure. FDA SaMD non-significant risk classification documented.

target_output.thinking_summary: Round 2 primary fixes: ISS-001 (sync DB write) resolved by moving prescribing_events insert to FastAPI BackgroundTasks after HTTP response dispatch, completely decoupling write latency from the scoring response path. ISS-002 (model in image) resolved by loading XGBoost artifact from Azure Blob Storage at startup using model_versions.active record, with a /admin/model-version endpoint for zero-downtime hot-swap without container restart. ISS-003 (hardcoded thresholds) addressed by ThresholdConfigModule backed by PostgreSQL severity_thresholds table with SIGHUP reload. All Round 1 HIPAA controls preserved. The async write pattern introduces a small event loss risk on ungraceful container shutdown - mitigated by graceful shutdown draining the BackgroundTasks queue.

fix_report.action_taken: Moved prescribing_events INSERT to FastAPI BackgroundTasks dispatched after HTTP response is returned. asyncpg connection pool sized to 10 per replica to absorb burst write concurrency. Added ddi.events.write_latency_ms Datadog histogram metric to monitor async write lag. Graceful shutdown handler drains BackgroundTasks queue before container termination.
fix_report.expected_outcome: DDI scoring p99 latency drops below 180ms at EHR morning surge load; prescribing event write lag stays under 500ms 99th percentile; event loss rate <0.1% at 10-replica config with graceful shutdown

fix_report.action_taken: XGBoost model artifact removed from Docker image. At container startup, DDIInferenceModule reads model_versions table for is_active=true record, downloads artifact from Azure Blob Storage path, validates SHA-256 checksum, and loads model in-process. POST /v1/admin/model-version endpoint updates model_versions.active and triggers in-process model reload without container restart. Canary revision traffic split validates new model scoring distribution before full promotion.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Model updates deployable in under 5 minutes with zero container restarts; rollback achievable in under 60 seconds via /admin/model-version revert to previous model_version_id

target_output.title: Clinical DDI Scoring API - Round 2
target_output.executive_summary: FastAPI Python 3.12 service on Azure Container Apps delivering drug-drug interaction severity scores from an XGBoost v2.1 classifier with SHAP clinical rationale within 200ms p99. Round 2 resolves both high-severity issues: prescribing event writes decoupled to async BackgroundTasks and XGBoost model loaded from Azure Blob Storage for zero-downtime hot-swap via /admin/model-version endpoint. Severity thresholds configurable via admin endpoint backed by PostgreSQL config table. Azure AD service principal auth with per-consumer scopes, HIPAA PHI controls including medication-list log stripping, and FDA SaMD non-significant risk documentation.
target_output.architecture_overview: FastAPI service deployed on Azure Container Apps with VNET injection into the hospital private subnet. On startup, DDIInferenceModule queries model_versions for is_active=true, downloads the XGBoost artifact from Azure Blob Storage, validates SHA-256 checksum, and loads the model in-process. POST /v1/ddi/score executes: Redis drug cache lookup (target hit rate >95%), XGBoost inference with SHAP explanation (<10ms), HTTP response dispatch, then FastAPI BackgroundTasks enqueues prescribing_events INSERT via asyncpg pool - fully decoupled from the response path. PostgreSQL 16 on Azure Flexible Server (zone-redundant) stores prescribing_events (monthly partitions), alert_overrides, model_versions, severity_thresholds, drug_pairs, and clinical_rationales. Azure AD validates service principal tokens at Container Apps ingress.
target_output.technology_stack: Runtime: Python 3.12 with FastAPI, Pydantic v2 for all request/response models, asyncpg 0.29 for async PostgreSQL. Inference: XGBoost 2.1 loaded from Azure Blob Storage at startup, SHAP 0.44 for feature importance explanation. Cache: Redis 7 on Azure Cache for Redis Standard C2. Database: PostgreSQL 16 Azure Flexible Server zone-redundant with pg_partman for monthly partition management. Auth: python-jose for Azure AD JWT validation with JWKS caching. Infrastructure: Azure Container Apps, Azure Blob Storage for model artifacts, Azure Container Registry with vulnerability scanning. Observability: Datadog APM with DogStatsD custom metrics.
target_output.functional_feature_map: DDI Scoring: EHR submits proposed drug NDC and active medication list; service returns ranked interactions with severity_level (critical/major/moderate/minor), confidence_score, SHAP-derived clinical rationale text, and evidence summary with PMID reference. Alert Override Capture: Prescriber selects override reason from enumerated taxonomy (must_use/benefit_outweighs_risk/patient_tolerates/already_on_monitoring); logged with prescriber identity for pharmacy leadership audit. Model Version Hot-Swap: Admin endpoint promotes new XGBoost model artifact from Blob Storage without container restart, with canary validation. Threshold Configuration: Admin endpoint reads and writes severity display thresholds per class, backed by PostgreSQL config table. Override Analytics Export: Aggregated override rate by reason and alert fatigue index for weekly pharmacy leadership review.
target_output.system_components: FastAPI Application (Azure Container Apps 2-10 replicas): DDIRouter, OverrideRouter, AdminRouter, BackgroundTaskQueue for async event writes. XGBoost DDIInferenceModule: artifact loaded from Azure Blob at startup, in-process inference <10ms. Redis 7 Drug Cache: drug_database_entries keyed ndc:{ndc}:dbv:{db_version}, 24h TTL, warm-up task gates readiness probe. PostgreSQL 16 Flexible Server zone-redundant: prescribing_events monthly partitions, alert_overrides, model_versions, severity_thresholds, drug_pairs, clinical_rationales. Azure Blob Storage: model artifacts with versioned paths and SHA-256 manifests. Azure Container Registry: vulnerability-scanned image repository. Datadog Agent sidecar: APM traces and DogStatsD custom metrics.
target_output.workflows: DDI Score Request: EHR sends POST /v1/ddi/score with Bearer service principal token. Azure AD token validation at Container Apps ingress enforces ddi:score scope. DDIRouter fetches drug_database_entries from Redis (cache miss falls back to PostgreSQL drug_pairs with ddi.fallback.activated metric). XGBoost inference runs in-process returning interactions ranked by severity with SHAP rationale. HTTP response dispatched to EHR. FastAPI BackgroundTasks enqueues asyncpg INSERT to prescribing_events - response already delivered, write fully decoupled. Total target response time <180ms p99. Model Hot-Swap: ML team uploads validated XGBoost artifact to Azure Blob with SHA-256 manifest. Calls POST /v1/admin/model-version with new model_version_id. AdminRouter updates model_versions is_active flag, DDIInferenceModule downloads and loads new artifact in-process. Canary Container Apps revision takes 10% traffic; Datadog monitors ddi.score.p99_ms and confidence score distribution for 15 minutes. Auto-promoted if SLA met; admin triggers rollback to previous version_id if anomalies detected. Total swap time <5 minutes, zero container restarts.
target_output.data_model: drug_pairs (id UUID PK, drug_a_ndc VARCHAR, drug_b_ndc VARCHAR, fda_severity_class ENUM critical/major/moderate/minor, evidence_level ENUM A/B/C, last_updated TIMESTAMPTZ). prescribing_events (id UUID PK, prescriber_id_hash CHAR(64), patient_id_hash CHAR(64), proposed_drug_ndc VARCHAR, active_drug_count SMALLINT, top_severity ENUM critical/major/moderate/minor, model_version_id UUID FK, latency_ms SMALLINT, event_timestamp TIMESTAMPTZ) PARTITION BY RANGE (event_timestamp). alert_overrides (id UUID PK, prescribing_event_id UUID FK, prescriber_id VARCHAR, override_reason ENUM must_use/benefit_outweighs_risk/patient_tolerates/already_on_monitoring, override_timestamp TIMESTAMPTZ). model_versions (id UUID PK, model_file_blob_path TEXT, checksum_sha256 CHAR(64), is_active BOOLEAN, activated_at TIMESTAMPTZ, deactivated_at TIMESTAMPTZ, deployed_by VARCHAR). severity_thresholds (id SMALLINT PK, severity_class ENUM, display_threshold NUMERIC(4,3), updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ, updated_by VARCHAR). clinical_rationales (id UUID PK, drug_pair_id UUID FK, evidence_source TEXT, summary_text TEXT, reference_pmid VARCHAR).
target_output.api_design: POST /v1/ddi/score: body {patient_id, proposed_drug_ndc, active_medications:[{ndc,dose,frequency}]}, returns {interactions:[{drug_pair_id,severity_level,confidence_score,shap_rationale,evidence_summary,pmid}],model_version,request_id,latency_ms}. Scope: ddi:score. POST /v1/ddi/overrides: body {prescribing_event_id, override_reason ENUM}, returns {override_id, recorded_at}. Scope: ddi:overrides:write. GET /v1/ddi/overrides?start_date=&end_date=&reason=: paginated override list for pharmacy leadership. Scope: ddi:overrides:read. POST /v1/admin/model-version: body {model_version_id}, triggers in-process XGBoost hot-swap. Scope: ddi:admin. GET /v1/admin/thresholds: current severity_thresholds config. PATCH /v1/admin/thresholds: body {severity_class, display_threshold}, triggers SIGHUP reload. Scope: ddi:admin. OpenAPI 3.1 spec auto-generated by FastAPI at /docs.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Azure AD OAuth 2.0 client credentials flow with scope enforcement per FastAPI Security dependency. EHR service principal: ddi:score only. Pharmacist portal: ddi:score and ddi:overrides:read. ML ops team: ddi:admin. python-jose JWKS caching with 1h refresh. PHI controls: custom LoggingMiddleware strips active_medications array from request body before Datadog forwarding; patient_id stored as SHA-256 hash in prescribing_events; no PHI in Datadog trace tags or custom metric label values. All PostgreSQL data encrypted with Azure-managed TDE at rest. TLS 1.3 for all VNET-internal connections. HIPAA BAA with Azure in place. FDA SaMD non-significant risk: model_versions.deployed_by field and change control procedure satisfy regulatory audit trail. Joint Commission MM.04.01.01: SHAP rationale in API response enables prescriber evaluation of interaction evidence at point-of-care.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Azure Container Apps environment in hospital VNET with private endpoints to Azure Database for PostgreSQL Flexible Server and Azure Cache for Redis. No public internet ingress; EHR connects via Azure Internal Load Balancer. Container Apps environment-level ingress restricted to hospital VNET CIDR. GitHub Actions CI: ruff lint, mypy --strict type check, pytest with 85% overall coverage (DDI inference and override modules require 95%), Docker build, push to Azure Container Registry with Trivy vulnerability scan gate. Deployment: Container Apps revision with 10% canary traffic split for 15 minutes; auto-promote if ddi.score.p99_ms <180 and error_rate <0.1%, auto-rollback otherwise. Model update procedure: ML team uploads validated artifact to Azure Blob, calls POST /v1/admin/model-version, service hot-swaps without restart. Database migrations via Alembic run as Container Apps one-off jobs before revision promotion. Environments: dev (single replica, shared Redis), staging (2 replicas, isolated VNET, mirrored drug database), production (2-10 replicas, zone-redundant PostgreSQL and Redis).
target_output.observability: Datadog APM on all FastAPI routes with trace propagation and request_id correlation. DogStatsD custom metrics: ddi.inference.latency_ms histogram tagged by model_version, ddi.cache.hit_rate gauge, ddi.fallback.activated counter tagged by fallback_type (model/redis), ddi.override.count counter tagged by override_reason, ddi.events.write_latency_ms histogram for async background task lag. PagerDuty escalation: ddi.score.p99_ms >200ms for 2 consecutive minutes, error_rate >0.5% for 1 minute, ddi.fallback.activated >5/min, ddi.cache.hit_rate <90% for 5 minutes, ddi.events.write_latency_ms p99 >2000ms. Weekly Datadog dashboard for pharmacy leadership: override rate by reason, alert fatigue index (override_count/total_score_requests), severity score distribution by drug class, model version distribution.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Azure Container Apps autoscales 2-10 replicas on HTTP concurrency target 20 requests/replica. XGBoost in-process CPU inference eliminates GPU cost entirely at <10ms per request. Azure Database for PostgreSQL Flexible Server General Purpose 4 vCores zone-redundant ~$350/month. Azure Cache for Redis Standard C2 ~$120/month. Container Apps compute ~$200/month at average 3 replicas. Azure Blob Storage for model artifacts <$5/month. Total estimated ~$680/month. Cost reduction: prescribing_events monthly partitions archived to Azure Blob cold storage after 90 days reduces PostgreSQL hot storage growth to 3-4GB/month at 500 daily scoring calls.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 - MVP EHR Integration (Q2 2026, 8 weeks): DDI scoring endpoint with XGBoost v2.1 and SHAP rationale, async prescribing_events BackgroundTasks write, Redis drug cache with warm-up and readiness probe gate, alert override capture with enumerated reason taxonomy, Azure AD service principal auth with scoped credentials, HIPAA PHI controls and medication-list log stripping, Datadog APM and p99 SLA alerting, EHR integration testing against staging environment. Phase 2 - Operational Tooling (Q3 2026, 6 weeks): /admin/model-version hot-swap endpoint with canary validation, /admin/thresholds PostgreSQL-backed configuration, pharmacy leadership analytics export with override rate and alert fatigue metrics, model drift detection via SHAP value distribution monitoring. Phase 3 - Clinical Expansion (Q4 2026): Polypharmacy risk scoring for patients on >10 concurrent medications, pharmacogenomics data integration, automated model retraining pipeline with clinical validation gate and CMO sign-off workflow.
target_output.development_guidelines: Python type hints enforced with mypy --strict in CI; no Any types in DDI inference or override modules. Pydantic v2 models for all API request/response schemas - no raw dict passing across module boundaries. DDIInferenceModule and OverrideModule require 95% pytest coverage; AdminModule and DevOps utilities at 85%. Integration tests use containerized PostgreSQL and Redis in GitHub Actions. ADR documents required for model versioning strategy and async write architecture decisions. PR changes to DDIInferenceModule require approval from ML team lead. No PHI in test fixtures - synthetic NDC codes and SHA-256 hashed patient IDs only.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Redis cache cold start flushes drug database cache causing PostgreSQL fallback spike on container restart. Mitigation: cache warm-up background task runs on startup; readiness probe gates traffic until warm-up completes (estimated 8-12 seconds).
