Risk: XGBoost hot-swap on multiple concurrent replicas creates a brief window of mixed model versions serving requests. Mitigation: model_version_id included in every API response and recorded in prescribing_events for post-hoc audit; canary revision approach validates new model before full traffic shift. Tradeoff: async prescribing_events write via BackgroundTasks means a container crash after response dispatch but before task completion loses that event. Accepted: loss rate <0.1% at 10-replica config with graceful shutdown draining task queue. Risk: SHAP explanation generation adds 5-8ms per request for novel drug combinations not in interaction_severity_scores cache. Mitigation: SHAP results cached in interaction_severity_scores table; only computed fresh for previously unseen pairs.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Override reason taxonomy finalized as four values: must_use (no therapeutic alternative exists), benefit_outweighs_risk (prescriber clinical judgment), patient_tolerates (documented prior tolerance on record), already_on_monitoring (patient enrolled in active monitoring protocol). Model update cadence confirmed as quarterly with clinical validation protocol; emergency updates permitted via hot-swap endpoint with CMO sign-off documented in model_versions.deployed_by. Redis cache key format confirmed as ndc:{drug_ndc}:dbv:{drug_db_version} to prevent stale lookups after drug database schema updates.

metadata.notes: Round 2 DDI scoring API: async BackgroundTasks write and Blob-loaded XGBoost hot-swap resolve both high-severity SLA and operability issues.

project_goal.value: Securely ingest and de-identify pathology lab results from multiple external reference labs into the hospital LIS with end-to-end encryption and immutable audit trail at every processing step
project_goal.rationale: Eliminates manual lab result entry, reduces transcription errors, and enforces HIPAA Safe Harbor de-identification before research data routing

target_users.value: Pathology department LIS administrators, research data stewards, compliance officers reviewing audit trails
target_users.rationale: Primary operators of the pipeline and compliance reviewers identified in requirements workshops

project_class.rationale: Scheduled batch ingest and transform pipeline with no interactive UI; Airflow orchestrates processing steps

capabilities.rationale: Batch processing, external lab SFTP integration, and data transformation to LIS and research store

complexity_level.rationale: Well-defined transformation rules but HIPAA Safe Harbor de-identification and hash-chain audit integrity add significant security complexity

risk_level.rationale: PHI in lab result files, external partner-facing SFTP intake, and CLIA data integrity requirements for clinical lab results

data_sensitivity.rationale: Pathology lab results with patient identifiers constitute PHI under HIPAA

external_exposure.rationale: Encrypted SFTP ingestion point is accessible to external reference lab partners

access_model.value: Per-lab SFTP service accounts with public key authentication; Airflow tasks run as service roles with least-privilege IAM; no human interactive access to raw PHI files
access_model.rationale: Eliminate password-based auth for partner SFTP; IAM role separation prevents cross-lab data access

feature_scope.value: Encrypted SFTP ingest from multiple reference labs, in-memory decryption, schema validation, HIPAA Safe Harbor de-identification, hash-chain audit logging, dual routing to clinical LIS and research data store, failed-file quarantine and alerting
feature_scope.rationale: Covers full lab result lifecycle from partner delivery to clinical and research consumption

mvp_scope.value: Single reference lab SFTP ingest, in-memory AES-256 decryption, HL7 v2.5 schema validation, Safe Harbor de-identification for research routing, hash-chain audit log, LIS interface via HL7 MLLP
mvp_scope.rationale: Minimum for production go-live with Quest Diagnostics; additional lab partners added in subsequent sprints

security_baseline.value: SFTP with per-lab RSA-4096 public key auth, AES-256-GCM in-memory decryption (never written to disk unencrypted), TLS 1.3 for all internal connections, KMS-managed keys with automatic 90-day rotation
security_baseline.rationale: HIPAA minimum controls plus defense-in-depth for external partner ingestion point

frontend_stack.value: N/A - data pipeline only; Airflow web UI for operational monitoring
frontend_stack.rationale: No browser application; Airflow provides operational visibility

backend_stack.value: Python 3.12 with Apache Airflow 2.8, Paramiko for SFTP, HL7apy for HL7 v2.5 parsing
backend_stack.rationale: Airflow for orchestration with retry and alerting; HL7apy for standards-compliant message parsing

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 for pipeline_audit_logs, de_identification_maps, and reference_lab_orders; S3 for encrypted raw file quarantine and processed file archive
data_platform.rationale: PostgreSQL for queryable audit trail; S3 for durable encrypted file storage outside pipeline

hosting_target.value: ECS Fargate in hospital VPC; Airflow on MWAA (Amazon Managed Workflows for Apache Airflow)
hosting_target.rationale: Managed Airflow reduces operational overhead; ECS Fargate for stateless task workers in hospital VPC

privacy_retention_policy.value: Identified lab_results retained 7 years in LIS per CLIA and state lab regulations; de-identified research records retained indefinitely; raw encrypted SFTP files purged from pipeline after successful processing; hash-chain audit logs retained 10 years
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: CLIA lab record retention, HIPAA audit trail requirements, and research data permanence

future_scope.value: Additional reference lab partners (LabCorp, BioReference), real-time FHIR R4 DiagnosticReport push to clinical systems, automated re-identification for authorized clinical use cases
future_scope.rationale: Partner expansion and real-time delivery deferred pending LIS FHIR readiness

constraints.value: Raw PHI files must never be written to any disk in unencrypted form; no cross-lab data mixing in processing workers; each reference lab's data processed in isolated ECS task context
constraints.rationale: Defense-in-depth data isolation and breach blast radius minimization

observability_baseline.value: CloudWatch for Airflow DAG metrics and ECS task logs, SNS alerts on pipeline failure or quarantine events, daily audit report to compliance officers
observability_baseline.rationale: Operational monitoring and compliance reporting requirements

execution_preference.value: 2-week sprints with compliance officer sign-off on de-identification logic changes
execution_preference.rationale: Regulatory risk of incorrect de-identification requires formal review for logic changes

compliance_context.value: HIPAA Safe Harbor de-identification per 45 CFR 164.514(b), HITECH audit trail requirements, CLIA lab data integrity standards for clinical result routing
compliance_context.rationale: PHI processing, research data use, and clinical lab data integrity regulations all apply

project.goal: Ingest, de-identify, and route pathology lab results from external reference labs to clinical LIS and research store
project.target_users: LIS admins, research data stewards, compliance officers
project.timeline: Production go-live Q2 2026 with Quest Diagnostics; LabCorp added Q3

backend.parsing: HL7apy v2.5 for HL7 message parsing

security.auth: Per-lab RSA-4096 SFTP public key auth
security.encryption: AES-256-GCM in-memory decryption via AWS KMS data keys
security.phi_constraint: Never write decrypted PHI to disk

data.db: PostgreSQL 16 on RDS for audit logs and de-identification maps
data.storage: S3 with SSE-KMS for encrypted raw file archive and quarantine

devops.hosting: MWAA for Airflow, ECS Fargate for task workers

constraints.phi_disk: No unencrypted PHI on disk at any processing stage
constraints.isolation: Per-lab ECS task context to prevent cross-lab data mixing

open_questions.reidentification_workflow: Authorization workflow for research-to-clinical re-identification not yet specified

reasoner_reviews.product: Round 3 must close the remaining hash-chain integrity gap. The pipeline's de-identification audit value is only as strong as the tamper-evidence of the hash chain - if the chain can be rewritten, it provides no forensic value for HITECH audits. The compliance officer sign-off workflow for de-identification logic changes needs to be codified in the Airflow DAG versioning approach, not left as an informal process.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: The hash-chain implementation in Round 2 used SHA-256 of each audit record chained to the previous record hash, but the chain was stored in the same PostgreSQL table as mutable data. Round 3 must store the chain head in a separate append-only audit_chain_heads table with a PostgreSQL trigger preventing UPDATE or DELETE. The ECS task isolation per reference lab is correct architecture but the IAM role assignment per lab must be validated - Round 2 used a single shared task role which violates the cross-lab isolation constraint.
reasoner_reviews.security: The append-only constraint for pipeline_audit_logs must be enforced at the database level with a PostgreSQL row-level security policy and a separate audit_writer role granted only INSERT on the table - no application-layer-only enforcement. The quarantine bucket for failed files must use a separate KMS key from the processing key so a compromised processing key does not expose quarantined files. SFTP private key rotation for reference lab accounts needs a documented runbook - currently undefined.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: Per-lab ECS task isolation was stated in the contract but Round 2 plan used a single shared Airflow worker for all labs. The fix requires per-lab Airflow task groups with dedicated ECS task definitions each carrying the lab-specific IAM role. This adds ~20% DAG complexity but is non-negotiable given the constraint.

backend.service_design: Airflow DAG per reference lab (dag_lab_quest, dag_lab_labcorp) with TaskGroups: ingest, decrypt_validate, deidentify, route, audit_finalize. Each DAG runs on a dedicated ECS task definition with lab-specific IAM role. Python operators use Paramiko for SFTP pull, AWS KMS GenerateDataKey for in-memory AES-256-GCM decryption, HL7apy for message parsing.
backend.business_modules: SFTPIngestOperator: pulls files from lab SFTP, stores encrypted in S3 staging prefix, records reference_lab_orders entry. DecryptValidateOperator: generates KMS data key in memory, decrypts file in memory stream, validates HL7 v2.5 schema, quarantines invalid files to S3 quarantine prefix with SNS alert. DeidentifyOperator: applies Safe Harbor 18-identifier suppression, writes de_identification_maps entry with hash of original identifiers, produces de-identified HL7 message. AuditChainOperator: appends pipeline_audit_logs entry, computes SHA-256(entry_json + prev_chain_hash), writes chain head to audit_chain_heads append-only table.
backend.failure_handling: Failed SFTP pulls retry 3 times with exponential backoff before SNS quarantine alert. Schema validation failures quarantine the file to S3 with SSE-KMS using the quarantine-specific KMS key. DAG-level SLA miss triggers PagerDuty alert to on-call LIS admin. Failed de-identification (unexpected HL7 field) writes to quarantine and halts downstream routing for that specimen_record.

security.auth_design: Per-lab SFTP accounts with RSA-4096 public key authentication managed in AWS Transfer Family. Each lab SFTP account mapped to a separate IAM role with S3 PutObject permission scoped to that lab's ingest prefix only. No password authentication permitted on Transfer Family server.
security.authorization_model: Separate IAM task roles per reference lab: arn:aws:iam::ACCOUNT:role/lab-quest-pipeline-role with KMS decrypt permission for quest-specific data key alias only. Airflow MWAA service role has no direct S3 or KMS access - permissions delegated to ECS task roles at execution time.
security.audit_and_logging_controls: pipeline_audit_logs append-only enforced by PostgreSQL RLS: audit_writer role granted INSERT only; no UPDATE or DELETE on table. audit_chain_heads table protected by INSERT-only trigger that rejects any modification to existing rows. Hash chain: each entry SHA-256(json_canonical(record) + prev_hash). Chain head stored separately for O(1) chain continuation. All Airflow task logs stripped of any HL7 message content before CloudWatch forwarding.
security.secrets_management: Lab SFTP private keys stored in AWS Secrets Manager with 90-day rotation schedule. KMS CMK aliases per lab (alias/lab-quest-pipeline-key, alias/lab-quarantine-key). Airflow connections stored in MWAA-managed Secrets Manager backend, not in Airflow metadata DB.

data.entities: lab_results (id UUID PK, specimen_record_id FK, accession_number_hash CHAR(64), test_code_loinc VARCHAR, result_value TEXT, unit VARCHAR, reference_range VARCHAR, abnormal_flag BOOLEAN, collected_at TIMESTAMPTZ, reported_at TIMESTAMPTZ, lis_routed_at TIMESTAMPTZ). reference_lab_orders (id UUID PK, source_lab ENUM quest/labcorp/bioreference, s3_raw_key TEXT, s3_encrypted_key_arn TEXT, received_at TIMESTAMPTZ, processing_status ENUM pending/processing/routed/quarantined). de_identification_maps (id UUID PK, lab_result_id FK, original_identifiers_hash CHAR(64), suppressed_fields JSONB, deidentified_at TIMESTAMPTZ, suppression_method VARCHAR). pipeline_audit_logs (id UUID PK, reference_lab_order_id FK, processing_step ENUM ingest/decrypt/validate/deidentify/route/finalize, outcome ENUM success/failure/quarantine, prev_chain_hash CHAR(64), chain_hash CHAR(64), logged_at TIMESTAMPTZ). audit_chain_heads (id SMALLINT PK, current_head_hash CHAR(64), updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ). specimen_records (id UUID PK, accession_number_encrypted TEXT, patient_id_hash CHAR(64), collection_site VARCHAR, specimen_type VARCHAR).
data.storage_design: PostgreSQL 16 on RDS Multi-AZ for pipeline_audit_logs, de_identification_maps, reference_lab_orders, specimen_records. S3 buckets: s3://hospital-lab-ingest-staging (SSE-KMS, lab-specific prefixes), s3://hospital-lab-quarantine (separate KMS key), s3://hospital-lab-archive (Intelligent-Tiering for processed file archive). De-identified records routed to research PostgreSQL schema via separate RDS instance.
data.retention_and_deletion: lab_results in clinical LIS retained 7 years per CLIA. de_identified research records retained indefinitely. pipeline_audit_logs retained 10 years; archived to S3 Glacier after 2 years. reference_lab_orders S3 raw files purged after successful processing confirmed in audit_logs. S3 Object Lock COMPLIANCE mode on pipeline_audit_logs archive bucket prevents any deletion.
data.migration_notes: Round 1 audit_log table migrated to append-only pipeline_audit_logs with RLS in Round 3 via Alembic migration adding RLS policy and audit_chain_heads table with INSERT-only trigger.

devops.deployment_topology: MWAA environment in hospital VPC private subnets. ECS Fargate cluster for custom task workers in same VPC. AWS Transfer Family SFTP server in VPC with VPC endpoint - not public internet. RDS PostgreSQL Multi-AZ in database subnet group. S3 buckets accessed via VPC endpoints only.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: ruff lint, mypy, pytest with 90% coverage for de-identification module (HIPAA compliance criticality), DAG validation (airflow dags test), Docker build for ECS task images, push to ECR, MWAA DAG file sync to S3 DAG bucket, ECS task definition update via CloudFormation. De-identification logic changes require compliance_officer GitHub branch protection rule approval before merge to main.
devops.observability_stack: CloudWatch Metrics for MWAA DAG success/failure rates, ECS task CPU/memory, RDS connections. Custom CloudWatch metrics: lab_results.ingested_count by source_lab, lab_results.quarantined_count by failure_reason, audit_chain.integrity_checks_passed. SNS topic for quarantine events and DAG SLA misses. Weekly automated audit report: Python Lambda generates CSV of pipeline_audit_logs summary and emails to compliance officers.
devops.backup_and_recovery: RDS automated backups 35-day retention with PITR. S3 audit archive bucket with Object Lock COMPLIANCE mode. MWAA DAG files versioned in S3 with S3 Versioning. Disaster recovery: restore RDS from snapshot, re-process any files in s3://hospital-lab-ingest-staging not yet confirmed in audit_logs.

ISS-001.title: Hash-chain audit log stored in mutable PostgreSQL table
ISS-001.detail: Rounds 1 and 2 stored the de-identification hash chain in a standard PostgreSQL table with no DELETE or UPDATE prevention. Application-layer enforcement only is insufficient for HITECH forensic audit admissibility.

history.note: Identified by security reviewer
Application-layer enforcement added but DB-level constraint missing

ISS-002.title: Single shared IAM role for all reference lab ECS tasks violates cross-lab isolation constraint
ISS-002.detail: Round 2 plan used a single shared Airflow worker IAM role for all reference lab processing tasks. This violates the contract constraint requiring per-lab ECS task isolation and allows one lab's compromised task to access another lab's KMS-encrypted files.

history.note: Constraint stated in contract but not enforced in architecture
ECS task definitions added but all used same IAM role

ISS-003.title: Quarantine bucket uses same KMS key as processing bucket
ISS-003.detail: Rounds 1 and 2 used a single KMS CMK for both the lab ingest staging bucket and the quarantine bucket. A compromised processing key would expose quarantined files containing rejected PHI.

history.note: Identified by security specialist
KMS key separation not implemented

focus_issues.title: Single shared IAM role for all reference lab ECS tasks violates cross-lab isolation constraint
focus_issues.detail: Round 2 used a single shared Airflow worker IAM role for all lab processing tasks, violating the contract per-lab isolation constraint.

focus_issues.title: Quarantine bucket uses same KMS key as processing bucket
focus_issues.detail: Single KMS CMK for both staging and quarantine buckets; compromised processing key exposes quarantined PHI files.

latest_recommendations: Implement per-lab IAM task roles (alias/lab-quest-pipeline-role, alias/lab-labcorp-pipeline-role) each with KMS decrypt permission scoped to that lab's CMK alias only
Create a separate KMS CMK alias/lab-quarantine-key for the quarantine bucket, distinct from per-lab processing keys
Validate per-lab IAM role assignment in Airflow DAG task group configuration before production cutover

previous_plan.title: Pathology Lab Results Ingestion Pipeline - Round 2
previous_plan.executive_summary: Airflow 2.8 pipeline on MWAA ingesting HL7 v2.5 lab results from external reference labs via encrypted SFTP, de-identifying via HIPAA Safe Harbor rules, routing clinical results to LIS via HL7 MLLP and de-identified results to research PostgreSQL. Hash-chain audit log with PostgreSQL RLS append-only enforcement added in Round 2.
previous_plan.architecture_overview: MWAA Airflow DAG with per-lab TaskGroups. SFTP pull via Paramiko from AWS Transfer Family. In-memory AES-256-GCM decryption. HL7apy parsing and Safe Harbor de-identification. Dual routing: MLLP to LIS and INSERT to research PostgreSQL. Pipeline audit log with RLS append-only and hash-chain in audit_chain_heads. Single ECS task definition with shared IAM role (unresolved ISS-002).
previous_plan.security_and_compliance: PostgreSQL RLS INSERT-only on pipeline_audit_logs. audit_chain_heads INSERT-only trigger. Per-lab SFTP RSA-4096 public key auth. KMS in-memory decryption. Single KMS CMK for both staging and quarantine buckets (unresolved ISS-003).
previous_plan.deployment_and_operations: MWAA environment in hospital VPC. ECS Fargate task workers with per-lab task definitions (all using shared IAM role). AWS Transfer Family SFTP with VPC endpoint.

target_output.thinking_summary: Round 3 closes ISS-002 (shared IAM role) and ISS-003 (single KMS key for staging and quarantine). ISS-002 fix: each reference lab gets a dedicated IAM task role with KMS decrypt permission scoped exclusively to that lab's CMK alias. The Airflow DAG for each lab references the lab-specific ECS task definition which carries the lab-specific IAM role via task_role_arn. ISS-003 fix: a separate KMS CMK alias/lab-quarantine-key is created for the quarantine bucket, ensuring a compromised per-lab processing key cannot expose quarantined PHI files. Both fixes are non-breaking infrastructure changes deployable via CloudFormation before the next DAG run. ISS-001 remains resolved from Round 2.

fix_report.action_taken: Created per-lab IAM task roles: arn:aws:iam::ACCOUNT:role/lab-quest-pipeline-role with kms:Decrypt permission scoped to alias/lab-quest-pipeline-key only, and arn:aws:iam::ACCOUNT:role/lab-labcorp-pipeline-role scoped to alias/lab-labcorp-pipeline-key. Each lab's Airflow DAG references its dedicated ECS task definition with task_role_arn pointing to the lab-specific role. The shared airflow-worker-role retains only MWAA metadata DB access with no S3 or KMS permissions.
fix_report.expected_outcome: A compromised Quest Diagnostics processing task cannot decrypt LabCorp-encrypted S3 objects; contract per-lab isolation constraint fully satisfied. IAM boundary enforcement validated via IAM Access Analyzer policy simulation before production cutover.

fix_report.action_taken: Created KMS CMK alias/lab-quarantine-key in CloudFormation with key policy granting decrypt permission only to the quarantine-processor IAM role (used exclusively by the QuarantineReviewOperator). S3 quarantine bucket SSE-KMS configuration updated to alias/lab-quarantine-key. All per-lab processing CMKs (alias/lab-quest-pipeline-key, alias/lab-labcorp-pipeline-key) removed from quarantine bucket key policy.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Compromise of a per-lab processing KMS key provides no access to quarantined files encrypted under the separate quarantine CMK; blast radius of key compromise limited to in-flight files in that lab's staging prefix.

target_output.title: Pathology Lab Results Ingestion Pipeline - Round 3
target_output.executive_summary: Airflow 2.8 pipeline on MWAA ingesting HL7 v2.5 pathology lab results from external reference labs via AWS Transfer Family encrypted SFTP, performing in-memory AES-256-GCM decryption, HIPAA Safe Harbor de-identification per 45 CFR 164.514(b), and dual-routing clinical results to the LIS via HL7 MLLP and de-identified results to the research data store. Round 3 closes all security gaps: per-lab IAM task roles with KMS permissions scoped to individual lab CMK aliases (ISS-002), and a dedicated quarantine KMS CMK separate from processing keys (ISS-003). Immutable hash-chain audit log with PostgreSQL RLS append-only enforcement from Round 2 remains intact.
target_output.architecture_overview: MWAA Airflow environment in hospital VPC private subnets orchestrates per-lab DAGs (dag_lab_quest, dag_lab_labcorp). Each DAG's ECS task operators run on dedicated Fargate task definitions carrying lab-specific IAM roles with KMS decrypt permissions scoped to that lab's CMK alias only. Processing flow: Transfer Family SFTP pull to S3 staging (SSE-KMS, lab-specific prefix) -> in-memory AES-256-GCM decryption (KMS GenerateDataKey, key material never persisted) -> HL7apy v2.5 schema validation -> HIPAA Safe Harbor de-identification (18-identifier suppression, de_identification_maps written) -> dual routing: identified HL7 to LIS via MLLP, de-identified to research PostgreSQL RDS -> hash-chain audit entry appended to pipeline_audit_logs with chain head updated in audit_chain_heads. Failed files quarantined to S3 quarantine bucket encrypted with dedicated alias/lab-quarantine-key. PostgreSQL RLS enforces INSERT-only on pipeline_audit_logs; audit_chain_heads protected by INSERT-only trigger.
target_output.technology_stack: Orchestration: Apache Airflow 2.8 on Amazon MWAA. Python 3.12 operators with Paramiko 3.4 for SFTP, HL7apy 2.0 for HL7 v2.5 message parsing, cryptography 42.0 for AES-256-GCM in-memory decryption. Database: PostgreSQL 16 on RDS Multi-AZ for audit_logs, de_identification_maps, reference_lab_orders, specimen_records. Infrastructure: AWS Transfer Family for SFTP with VPC endpoint, ECS Fargate for task workers, S3 with SSE-KMS (per-lab and quarantine CMKs), AWS KMS with per-lab and quarantine CMK aliases, CloudFormation for all infrastructure. Observability: CloudWatch Metrics, SNS for operational alerts, Lambda-generated weekly audit report.
target_output.functional_feature_map: SFTP Ingest: Airflow SFTPOperator (Paramiko) pulls HL7 batch files from each reference lab's SFTP directory on scheduled trigger; files stored to lab-specific S3 prefix with SSE-KMS. In-Memory Decryption and Validation: KMS GenerateDataKey returns plaintext key in memory only; AES-256-GCM decryption of HL7 file in memory stream; HL7apy validates message structure and required MSH/OBR/OBX segments; invalid messages quarantined. HIPAA Safe Harbor De-identification: 18-identifier suppression (patient name, DOB, ZIP, dates, phone, SSN, MRN, accession number, etc.) with de_identification_maps entry recording original identifier hashes. Dual Routing: identified results forwarded to LIS via HL7 MLLP socket; de-identified results inserted into research PostgreSQL schema. Hash-Chain Audit: every processing step appended to pipeline_audit_logs with SHA-256 chaining; chain head in audit_chain_heads. Failed File Quarantine: schema or decryption failures write to S3 quarantine bucket (alias/lab-quarantine-key) with SNS notification to LIS admin.
target_output.system_components: AWS Transfer Family SFTP Server (VPC endpoint, no public internet, per-lab service accounts with RSA-4096 pub key auth). S3 Ingest Staging Buckets (per-lab prefix with SSE-KMS using lab-specific CMK). MWAA Airflow 2.8 (VPC private subnets, Secrets Manager backend for connections). ECS Fargate Task Workers (per-lab task definitions, per-lab IAM task roles with scoped KMS permissions). S3 Quarantine Bucket (SSE-KMS with alias/lab-quarantine-key, separate from all processing CMKs). RDS PostgreSQL 16 Multi-AZ (pipeline_audit_logs with RLS, de_identification_maps, reference_lab_orders, specimen_records). Research RDS PostgreSQL 16 (de-identified lab_results schema, separate instance). LIS MLLP Interface (TCP socket in hospital private subnet). AWS KMS (per-lab CMKs + quarantine CMK + 90-day automatic rotation). CloudWatch + SNS for operational monitoring and quarantine alerts.
target_output.workflows: Lab Result Ingest and Route: Airflow dag_lab_quest triggers on cron schedule (every 4 hours). SFTPIngestOperator pulls HL7 batch file from Quest SFTP directory using Paramiko with RSA key from Secrets Manager. File stored to s3://hospital-lab-ingest-staging/quest/ with SSE-KMS using alias/lab-quest-pipeline-key. DecryptValidateOperator generates KMS data key in memory (plaintext key never written to disk), decrypts HL7 file in-memory stream using AES-256-GCM, parses with HL7apy, validates MSH/OBR/OBX segment presence and LOINC test codes. DeidentifyOperator applies 18-identifier Safe Harbor suppression, writes de_identification_maps record with SHA-256 hash of original identifiers. RouteOperator sends identified HL7 to LIS MLLP socket, inserts de-identified record into research PostgreSQL. AuditChainOperator appends pipeline_audit_logs entry, computes SHA-256(canonical_json(entry) + prev_chain_hash), updates audit_chain_heads. Failed File Quarantine: On validation or decryption failure, QuarantineOperator moves encrypted file to s3://hospital-lab-quarantine/ with alias/lab-quarantine-key re-encryption, writes reference_lab_orders status to quarantined, publishes SNS notification with file key and failure_reason (never including PHI content).
target_output.data_model: lab_results (id UUID PK, specimen_record_id UUID FK, accession_number_hash CHAR(64), test_code_loinc VARCHAR(10), result_value TEXT, unit VARCHAR, reference_range VARCHAR, abnormal_flag BOOLEAN, collected_at TIMESTAMPTZ, reported_at TIMESTAMPTZ, lis_routed_at TIMESTAMPTZ, research_routed_at TIMESTAMPTZ). reference_lab_orders (id UUID PK, source_lab ENUM quest/labcorp/bioreference, s3_raw_key TEXT, s3_encrypted_key_arn TEXT, received_at TIMESTAMPTZ, processing_status ENUM pending/processing/routed/quarantined, failure_reason VARCHAR). de_identification_maps (id UUID PK, lab_result_id UUID FK, original_identifiers_hash CHAR(64), suppressed_fields JSONB, deidentified_at TIMESTAMPTZ, suppression_method VARCHAR default safe_harbor_18). pipeline_audit_logs (id UUID PK, reference_lab_order_id UUID FK, processing_step ENUM ingest/decrypt/validate/deidentify/route_lis/route_research/finalize/quarantine, outcome ENUM success/failure/quarantine, prev_chain_hash CHAR(64), chain_hash CHAR(64), step_metadata JSONB, logged_at TIMESTAMPTZ) - append-only via PostgreSQL RLS. audit_chain_heads (id SMALLINT PK CHECK (id=1), current_head_hash CHAR(64), updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ) - INSERT-only trigger. specimen_records (id UUID PK, accession_number_encrypted TEXT, patient_id_hash CHAR(64), source_lab ENUM, collection_site VARCHAR, specimen_type VARCHAR, received_at TIMESTAMPTZ). encryption_keys (id UUID PK, kms_key_alias VARCHAR, lab_name VARCHAR, key_purpose ENUM processing/quarantine, rotated_at TIMESTAMPTZ, next_rotation TIMESTAMPTZ).
target_output.api_design: No external HTTP API. Internal interfaces: Airflow REST API (MWAA-managed, internal VPC only) for DAG trigger and monitoring. LIS MLLP interface: HL7 v2.5 ORU^R01 messages sent to LIS TCP endpoint on port 2575 within hospital private subnet. Research PostgreSQL: direct INSERT by Airflow RouteOperator using Secrets Manager credentials. S3 event notifications to SNS for quarantine bucket PUT events. CloudWatch custom metrics published by Airflow operators: pipeline.lab_results.ingested, pipeline.lab_results.quarantined, pipeline.audit_chain.entries_written. SNS topics: arn:aws:sns:REGION:ACCOUNT:lab-pipeline-quarantine-alerts, arn:aws:sns:REGION:ACCOUNT:lab-pipeline-sla-misses.
target_output.security_and_compliance: SFTP Authentication: AWS Transfer Family per-lab service accounts with RSA-4096 public key authentication; password auth disabled globally on Transfer Family server; private keys in Secrets Manager with 90-day rotation runbook. PHI-Never-On-Disk Control: AES-256-GCM decryption uses KMS GenerateDataKey returning plaintext key in Python bytes object in memory only; no tempfile or disk write at any point; memory cleared after processing via explicit del and gc.collect(). Per-Lab KMS Isolation: alias/lab-quest-pipeline-key accessible only by lab-quest-pipeline-role; alias/lab-labcorp-pipeline-key accessible only by lab-labcorp-pipeline-role; alias/lab-quarantine-key accessible only by quarantine-processor-role. Quarantine Key Separation: alias/lab-quarantine-key is a distinct CMK with separate key policy; no per-lab processing role has kms:Decrypt on this alias. Audit Immutability: pipeline_audit_logs INSERT-only via PostgreSQL RLS policy (audit_writer role: INSERT only); audit_chain_heads INSERT-only trigger rejects any UPDATE or DELETE. HIPAA Safe Harbor: DeidentifyOperator suppresses all 18 identifier categories per 45 CFR 164.514(b); de_identification_maps records hash of original identifiers for authorized re-identification workflow. CLIA integrity: accession number hash in lab_results provides chain of custody from reference lab to LIS without exposing PHI in audit queries.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Infrastructure provisioned via CloudFormation stacks: KMSStack (per-lab CMKs + quarantine CMK), IAMStack (per-lab task roles with scoped KMS policies), S3Stack (ingest staging + quarantine + archive buckets with SSE-KMS), TransferFamilyStack (SFTP server VPC endpoint + per-lab service accounts), MWAAStack (Airflow environment in VPC private subnets with Secrets Manager backend), ECSStack (per-lab Fargate task definitions with lab-specific task_role_arn). GitHub Actions CI: ruff lint, mypy strict, pytest with 90% coverage for de-identification module, DAG integration test (airflow dags test dag_lab_quest in CI container), Docker build for ECS task images, ECR push, CloudFormation changeset deploy with manual approval gate for production IAM changes. De-identification logic changes require compliance_officer GitHub branch protection approval before merge. Environments: dev (localstack for S3/KMS/SFT simulation, PostgreSQL in Docker), staging (full AWS stack with synthetic lab data from HL7 test harness), production (MWAA, multi-AZ RDS, Transfer Family VPC endpoint).
target_output.observability: CloudWatch Metrics: pipeline.lab_results.ingested count by source_lab, pipeline.lab_results.quarantined count by source_lab and failure_reason, pipeline.audit_chain.entries_written, pipeline.deidentification.duration_ms. CloudWatch Alarms: any quarantine event triggers SNS to lab-pipeline-quarantine-alerts topic (LIS admin on-call). MWAA DAG SLA miss (>6h since last successful run per lab) triggers PagerDuty. Weekly compliance report: Lambda function queries pipeline_audit_logs with chain integrity verification (recomputes chain hashes for last 7 days), exports CSV summary of records_ingested/quarantined/routed per lab, emails to compliance officers via SES. Chain integrity failure (recomputed hash mismatch) triggers critical PagerDuty alert.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: MWAA small environment ~$300/month. ECS Fargate task workers billed per-execution (spot capacity not used for PHI workloads due to interruption risk) ~$50/month at current lab volume. RDS PostgreSQL Multi-AZ db.t3.medium ~$150/month for audit and operational DB. Research RDS db.t3.small ~$80/month. S3 storage and KMS API calls ~$30/month. Total ~$610/month. Scaling: MWAA worker count autoscales; ECS task concurrency limited to 1 per lab per run to prevent parallel PHI processing conflicts.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 - Quest Diagnostics MVP (Q2 2026, 6 weeks): Single-lab SFTP ingest with Transfer Family VPC endpoint, in-memory AES-256-GCM decryption, HL7 v2.5 Safe Harbor de-identification, dual routing to LIS MLLP and research PostgreSQL, hash-chain audit with RLS append-only, per-lab IAM role (Quest only), dedicated quarantine CMK, CloudWatch monitoring and SNS quarantine alerts. Phase 2 - LabCorp Onboarding (Q3 2026, 4 weeks): Second reference lab SFTP account, lab-labcorp-pipeline-role with scoped KMS CMK, dag_lab_labcorp DAG with parallel TaskGroup, expanded schema validation for LabCorp HL7 variant fields. Phase 3 - Real-Time and FHIR (Q4 2026): AWS Transfer Family AS2 or EventBridge for near-real-time delivery triggers, FHIR R4 DiagnosticReport conversion operator for LIS FHIR endpoint, BioReference onboarding.
target_output.development_guidelines: All HL7 parsing and de-identification logic covered at 90% pytest coverage minimum - HIPAA criticality justifies higher bar than other modules. In-memory PHI handling: Python bytes objects containing decrypted PHI must be explicitly del'd and gc.collect() called after use - enforced by custom Airflow operator base class. No HL7 message content in operator logs - log accession_number_hash only. Compliance officer approval required for any change to DeidentifyOperator safe_harbor_fields list. CloudFormation drift detection run weekly; any detected drift triggers immediate incident response. ADR for KMS key separation architecture and audit chain hash algorithm.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Python gc.collect() does not guarantee immediate memory zeroing of decrypted PHI - OS memory could retain key material briefly. Mitigation: mlock() via ctypes on the data key bytes to prevent OS from swapping to disk; acceptable residual risk given ECS Fargate memory isolation. Risk: MWAA DAG file S3 sync introduces a deployment lag where old DAG code may run for up to 60 seconds after new version upload. Mitigation: DAG file versioning via S3 object key includes Git commit hash; operator validates expected DAG version at task start. Tradeoff: Single-concurrent-task-per-lab constraint prevents parallelizing large batch files from the same lab - acceptable given typical lab result batch sizes (<500 records per delivery). Risk: KMS API rate limits (10,000 requests/10 seconds) could throttle GenerateDataKey calls at high ingestion volumes. Mitigation: batch file decryption uses a single data key per HL7 file, not per record.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Re-identification workflow for authorized clinical use of research records: will use a separate re-identification service with CMO-approved request process referencing de_identification_maps.id; out of scope for this pipeline. SFTP private key rotation runbook: quarterly rotation with 2-week overlap period where both old and new keys accepted by Transfer Family service account before old key removal. LabCorp HL7 variant fields: LabCorp uses custom Z-segments for reference ranges; HL7apy parser extended with LabCorp profile in Phase 2.

metadata.notes: Round 3 pathology pipeline: per-lab IAM role isolation and quarantine KMS separation close the final security gaps; moderate quality reflects the data pipeline domain with some thinner cost/scaling section.

project_goal.value: Windows desktop application for hospital pharmacy staff to manage automated dispensing cabinet (ADC) inventory, track controlled substance chain-of-custody, and reconcile discrepancies across multiple ADC vendors with a modular plugin architecture
project_goal.rationale: Replaces manual paper-based ADC reconciliation and eliminates the need for separate vendor-specific management tools

target_users.value: Pharmacy staff (pharmacists, pharmacy technicians), pharmacist-in-charge for discrepancy review, nursing staff for ADC access events
target_users.rationale: Primary users identified in pharmacy workflow analysis; nursing access events are read-only from nurse perspective

project_class.rationale: Windows thick-client required for ADC vendor SDK integration, offline capability, and low-latency ADC hardware communication

capabilities.rationale: WPF UI, ASP.NET Core local API, PostgreSQL, Azure AD auth, and multi-vendor ADC SDK plugins

complexity_level.rationale: Multi-vendor plugin architecture, real-time ADC sync, DEA controlled substance audit trail, and witness-verification workflow

risk_level.rationale: Controlled substance chain-of-custody; DEA 21 CFR Part 1304 regulatory compliance; discrepancy mishandling carries legal liability

data_sensitivity.rationale: Controlled substance dispensing records are regulated PHI; patient medication administration records in scope

external_exposure.rationale: Application runs on hospital pharmacy workstations connected to internal network only; no internet exposure

access_model.value: Azure Active Directory SSO with per-role RBAC: pharmacist (full access), pharmacy_technician (inventory and transactions), pharmacist_in_charge (discrepancy approval + reports), nursing (read-only ADC status)
access_model.rationale: Hospital-wide AAD for identity; role separation required for DEA audit trail integrity

feature_scope.value: ADC inventory dashboard by cabinet and unit, controlled substance transaction log with chain-of-custody, automated discrepancy detection and witness-verified reconciliation, multi-vendor plugin support (Pyxis, Omnicell, BD Rowa), eMAR order matching, pharmacist-in-charge approval workflow, DEA-compliant audit reports
feature_scope.rationale: Covers full controlled substance lifecycle from cabinet dispensing through reconciliation and regulatory reporting

mvp_scope.value: Pyxis ES plugin with real-time transaction sync, controlled substance chain-of-custody log, discrepancy detection with pharmacist notification, witness-verified count reconciliation, Azure AD SSO, DEA 222 form report generation
mvp_scope.rationale: Minimum for DEA compliance with existing Pyxis fleet; Omnicell and BD Rowa plugins in Phase 2

security_baseline.value: Azure AD SSO with MFA enforcement, AES-256 encryption for controlled substance records at rest, TLS 1.3 for all network communication, digital signatures on chain-of-custody records, screen lock after 5-minute inactivity
security_baseline.rationale: DEA record integrity and HIPAA PHI protection requirements

frontend_stack.value: WPF with C# .NET 8, MVVM pattern with CommunityToolkit.Mvvm, MaterialDesignThemes for hospital-grade UI
frontend_stack.rationale: WPF required for Windows-native ADC hardware integration; MVVM for testability; .NET 8 LTS for support lifecycle

backend_stack.value: ASP.NET Core 8 local API service, Entity Framework Core 8 with PostgreSQL provider, SignalR for real-time ADC event push
backend_stack.rationale: Local ASP.NET Core service bridges WPF to PostgreSQL and ADC vendor SDKs; SignalR for real-time cabinet event updates

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 local instance (Azure Arc-enabled) with Azure Backup; Redis 7 local instance for ADC event queue buffer
data_platform.rationale: PostgreSQL for relational chain-of-custody records; Redis buffer for high-frequency ADC transaction events before batch write

hosting_target.value: Azure Virtual Machines in hospital network (Windows Server 2022); application deployed via Azure DevOps Release Pipeline to pharmacy workstations
hosting_target.rationale: Pharmacy workstations on hospital domain managed via Azure Arc; ADC hardware integration requires local Windows environment

privacy_retention_policy.value: Controlled substance dispensing records retained 2 years on local PostgreSQL per DEA; archived to Azure Blob after 2 years for 5-year total retention; patient medication records follow HIPAA 6-year minimum
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: DEA 21 CFR 1304.04 retention requirements and HIPAA minimum retention

future_scope.value: Omnicell XT plugin, BD Rowa plugin, integration with pharmacy information system (PIS) for real-time order verification, mobile pharmacy technician app for wireless count verification
future_scope.rationale: Multi-vendor expansion and mobile support on Year 2 roadmap

constraints.value: Must operate offline for up to 4 hours if network connectivity to central server is interrupted; DEA audit reports must be cryptographically signed; .NET 8 only (no .NET Framework compatibility required)
constraints.rationale: Pharmacy continuity during network outages; DEA digital signature requirement for electronic records

observability_baseline.value: Azure Monitor for VM health metrics, Windows Event Log for application errors, daily automated DEA compliance report to pharmacy director
observability_baseline.rationale: Hospital IT monitoring standards and DEA reporting requirements

execution_preference.value: Azure DevOps sprints with 2-week iterations; pharmacy staff UAT at end of each sprint
execution_preference.rationale: Clinical workflow validation required at each iteration boundary

compliance_context.value: DEA 21 CFR Part 1304 controlled substance record-keeping, HIPAA for patient medication records, state pharmacy board ADC auditing regulations, Joint Commission Medication Management standard MM.01.01.03
compliance_context.rationale: Federal controlled substance law, HIPAA, and state pharmacy board requirements all apply to this system

project.goal: Windows desktop app for pharmacy ADC inventory management, controlled substance chain-of-custody, and discrepancy reconciliation
project.target_users: Pharmacists, pharmacy technicians, pharmacist-in-charge, nursing staff (read-only)
project.timeline: MVP with Pyxis ES plugin Q3 2026

frontend.real_time: SignalR client for ADC event updates

backend.real_time: SignalR hub for ADC event push

security.auth: Azure AD SSO with MSAL.NET, MFA enforced
security.encryption: AES-256 at rest, TLS 1.3 in transit
security.signatures: RSA-2048 digital signatures on chain-of-custody records

data.cache: Redis 7 local for ADC event buffer

devops.environments: dev (developer VM), staging (pharmacy test environment), production (pharmacy workstations)

open_questions.witness_verification: Does witness signature require secondary Azure AD authentication or biometric?

reasoner_reviews.product: The modular plugin architecture is the correct long-term approach for multi-vendor ADC support, but the MVP scope is correctly constrained to Pyxis ES. The discrepancy detection and witness-verified reconciliation workflow is the highest-risk feature from a regulatory standpoint - the witness signature mechanism needs to be decided before development starts (secondary AAD authentication vs biometric). The eMAR order matching feature, while valuable, should be deferred from MVP to reduce integration complexity with the eMAR system.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: WPF MVVM with a local ASP.NET Core service is the right architecture for this offline-capable desktop scenario. The plugin interface for ADC vendors must be defined as a .NET interface (IAdcVendorPlugin) with a well-defined contract before any vendor SDK integration begins - late plugin interface changes will break all existing integrations. The 4-hour offline constraint means the local PostgreSQL instance must be the source of truth for chain-of-custody during network outages, with a sync-on-reconnect mechanism to the central server. SignalR should use WebSockets with automatic fallback to long-polling for hospital network environments that sometimes block WebSocket upgrades.
reasoner_reviews.security: Digital signatures on chain-of-custody records are required by DEA for electronic records. Each chain_of_custody_records entry must be signed with the pharmacist's RSA-2048 key stored in Azure Key Vault or Windows Certificate Store - the key must be non-exportable. Discrepancy reports require two-pharmacist witness verification; the second witness must authenticate independently via Azure AD to provide their signature. Screen lock after 5 minutes is correct but the lock state must also suspend any in-progress ADC sync to prevent unattended data processing.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: The 4-hour offline constraint requires a local PostgreSQL instance as the authoritative store during network partitions, not a cache. The sync-on-reconnect mechanism must use optimistic locking with conflict detection - the pharmacy workstation is not the only source of writes to the central ADC state. The DEA digital signature requirement means RSA key management must be addressed in the MVP - Azure Key Vault with hardware-backed keys is the recommended approach.

frontend.app_structure: WPF application with Shell MVVM pattern. Main shell hosts NavigationView (MaterialDesign DrawerHost) with regions for: InventoryDashboard, TransactionLog, DiscrepancyWorkbench, CabinetManagement, Reports. Each region is a separate WPF UserControl with its own ViewModel injected via Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection. SignalR HubConnection managed as singleton service for real-time ADC event updates.
frontend.pages_and_flows: InventoryDashboard: live grid of dispensing_cabinets with current counts per slot, color-coded low-stock and discrepancy indicators. TransactionLog: searchable paginated log of inventory_transactions with chain-of-custody signature verification status indicator. DiscrepancyWorkbench: discrepancy_reports queue, expected vs actual count comparison, witness count entry form with secondary pharmacist authentication prompt. Reports: DEA 222 form generation with digital signature workflow, daily controlled substance audit report.
frontend.state_management: CommunityToolkit.Mvvm ObservableObject for VM properties. RelayCommand for actions. SignalR event handlers update ObservableCollections on UI thread via DispatcherHelper. Offline state tracked by ConnectivityService - UI shows offline banner and disables sync-dependent actions when network unreachable.
frontend.frontend_risks: WPF data binding performance with large inventory_transactions collections (>10,000 records) may cause UI lag. Mitigation: virtualized ItemsControl with ObservableRangeCollection and server-side pagination. ADC vendor SDK callbacks arrive on background threads; all SignalR and SDK callbacks must marshal to UI thread via Application.Current.Dispatcher.

backend.service_design: ASP.NET Core 8 minimal API running as Windows Service on the pharmacy workstation. Two primary service domains: AdcSyncService (orchestrates ADC vendor plugin polling and event ingestion) and ChainOfCustodyService (manages inventory_transactions, chain_of_custody_records, and digital signatures). IAdcVendorPlugin interface loaded via .NET Assembly.LoadFrom from plugins/ directory for runtime vendor extensibility.
backend.api_patterns: SignalR hub /hubs/adc-events broadcasts real-time inventory updates to WPF client. REST endpoints: GET /api/v1/cabinets (cabinet inventory summary), GET /api/v1/transactions?cabinet_id=&drug=&start= (paginated transaction log), POST /api/v1/discrepancies/{id}/reconcile (submit witness-verified count), POST /api/v1/reports/dea-222 (generate signed DEA report). All endpoints require AAD JWT via MSAL.NET.
backend.business_modules: IAdcVendorPlugin contract: ConnectAsync(), GetInventoryAsync(cabinetId), StreamTransactionsAsync(), DisconnectAsync(). PyxisEsPlugin implements interface using Pyxis ES REST SDK. ChainOfCustodyService: each inventory_transaction generates a chain_of_custody_records entry signed with pharmacist RSA key via RSACryptoServiceProvider. DiscrepancyService: compares actual ADC count to expected_count derived from running transaction balance; creates discrepancy_reports when delta exceeds threshold.
backend.failure_handling: OfflineSyncQueue: when PostgreSQL central sync fails, transactions written to local offline_queue table; ReconnectWorker periodically retries sync with conflict detection via row-level optimistic locking (row_version column). ADC plugin connection failures logged to Windows Event Log; UI notified via SignalR. DEA report signing failure aborts report generation with explicit error - never generates unsigned report silently.

security.auth_design: Azure AD SSO via MSAL.NET PublicClientApplication with device code flow (no browser popup on kiosk workstations). Token cache persisted to Windows DPAPI-encrypted file. MFA enforced via AAD Conditional Access policy on pharmacy_admin app registration. Screen lock triggered by 5-minute idle timer; re-authentication required on resume.
security.authorization_model: RBAC via AAD group claims: PharmacyStaff group -> pharmacist and pharmacy_technician roles, PharmacistInCharge group -> discrepancy approval and report generation, NursingStaff group -> read-only cabinet status. ASP.NET Core authorization policies enforce per-endpoint role requirements.
security.audit_and_logging_controls: Every controlled substance dispensing event creates a chain_of_custody_records entry with SHA-256 hash of the prior record in the chain (hash chain). Each entry signed with dispensing pharmacist's RSA-2048 key stored in Azure Key Vault (hardware-backed, non-exportable). Discrepancy reconciliation entries require two independent RSA signatures (pharmacist + witness). Application events written to Windows Event Log and forwarded to Azure Monitor.
security.security_risks: DEA key compromise: if pharmacist RSA signing key is compromised, historical chain_of_custody_records signatures cannot be retroactively invalidated. Mitigation: keys stored as non-exportable in Azure Key Vault hardware HSM; key usage logged and anomalous signing events trigger alert.

data.entities: dispensing_cabinets (id UUID PK, vendor ENUM pyxis/omnicell/bd_rowa, cabinet_name VARCHAR, location_unit VARCHAR, is_active BOOLEAN, last_sync_at TIMESTAMPTZ). controlled_substances (id UUID PK, ndc VARCHAR, drug_name VARCHAR, schedule DEA_schedule ENUM II/III/IV/V, unit_of_measure VARCHAR). inventory_transactions (id UUID PK, cabinet_id FK, controlled_substance_id FK, transaction_type ENUM dispense/return/waste/restock, quantity NUMERIC, performed_by_user_id VARCHAR, patient_id_hash CHAR(64), order_id VARCHAR, transaction_at TIMESTAMPTZ, row_version INTEGER). chain_of_custody_records (id UUID PK, inventory_transaction_id FK UNIQUE, prev_record_hash CHAR(64), record_hash CHAR(64), pharmacist_signature BYTEA, witness_signature BYTEA, signed_at TIMESTAMPTZ). discrepancy_reports (id UUID PK, cabinet_id FK, controlled_substance_id FK, expected_count NUMERIC, actual_count NUMERIC, delta NUMERIC, detected_at TIMESTAMPTZ, status ENUM open/witness_count_pending/reconciled/escalated, resolved_at TIMESTAMPTZ). adc_vendor_interfaces (id UUID PK, cabinet_id FK, plugin_assembly_name VARCHAR, endpoint_url TEXT, last_successful_poll TIMESTAMPTZ).
data.storage_design: Local PostgreSQL 16 instance on pharmacy workstation VM as source of truth. Azure Arc-enabled for central management and Azure Backup. Redis 7 local instance buffers high-frequency ADC transaction events (ring buffer, max 10,000 events) before batch write to PostgreSQL. Offline operations persist to local PostgreSQL only; offline_queue table tracks records pending central sync.
data.schema_notes: chain_of_custody_records.prev_record_hash creates tamper-evident linked chain. inventory_transactions.row_version enables optimistic locking for offline sync conflict detection. patient_id_hash stores SHA-256 of patient MRN - never raw MRN in transaction records.
data.migration_notes: Alembic-style EF Core migrations managed via Azure DevOps pipeline; migration run as separate step before application update deployment to pharmacy workstations.

devops.deployment_topology: Azure VMs running Windows Server 2022 for staging and production pharmacy workstations. Application published as self-contained .NET 8 single-file executable. Azure DevOps Release Pipeline deploys via WinRM to Azure Arc-enrolled workstations. PostgreSQL 16 and Redis 7 installed as Windows Services via Chocolatey in initial provisioning runbook.
devops.ci_cd_design: Azure DevOps Pipeline: dotnet build, dotnet test with 85% coverage gate, dotnet publish --self-contained, Chocolatey package creation, Release Pipeline with manual approval gate for production pharmacy workstations. WPF UI automation tests via WinAppDriver (Microsoft Windows Application Driver) for critical discrepancy reconciliation workflow.
devops.environments: dev (developer Windows VM with Pyxis ES simulator), staging (pharmacy test environment with Pyxis ES test cabinet), production (pharmacy workstations, change windows restricted to 02:00-04:00 local time to avoid dispensing hours)
devops.devops_risks: Offline workstation update deployment: if a pharmacy workstation is offline during release pipeline execution, the workstation will run stale application version. Mitigation: pending update indicator in system tray; forced update on next network reconnect if version delta > 2 releases.

target_output.thinking_summary: This is a Windows desktop application for a highly regulated pharmacy use case requiring DEA-compliant controlled substance chain-of-custody. The key architectural decisions are: WPF MVVM with a local ASP.NET Core service for offline capability, IAdcVendorPlugin interface as the extensibility contract for multi-vendor ADC support, and RSA-2048 digital signatures via Azure Key Vault for DEA-compliant electronic records. The 4-hour offline constraint makes the local PostgreSQL instance the authoritative store, not a sync cache. The witness verification for discrepancy reconciliation requires two independent Azure AD authentications - the open question about biometric vs secondary AAD auth must be resolved before the discrepancy reconciliation sprint.
target_output.title: Pharmacy ADC Inventory and Chain-of-Custody Desktop Application
target_output.executive_summary: Windows WPF desktop application built on .NET 8 for hospital pharmacy staff to manage automated dispensing cabinet inventory, track controlled substance chain-of-custody with DEA-compliant digital signatures, and reconcile discrepancies through a witness-verified approval workflow. Designed with an IAdcVendorPlugin interface for multi-vendor ADC support (Pyxis ES for MVP, Omnicell and BD Rowa in Phase 2). Local ASP.NET Core 8 service with PostgreSQL 16 provides 4-hour offline operational continuity with sync-on-reconnect to the central pharmacy server. Azure AD SSO with MFA and RSA-2048 signing keys in Azure Key Vault satisfy DEA 21 CFR Part 1304 electronic record requirements.
target_output.architecture_overview: Three-layer Windows desktop architecture: WPF MVVM shell communicates with a locally-hosted ASP.NET Core 8 minimal API service via localhost HTTP and SignalR WebSocket. The local ASP.NET Core service hosts ADC vendor plugin adapters (loaded at runtime via Assembly.LoadFrom from the plugins/ directory), the ChainOfCustodyService for RSA-signed transaction records, and the DiscrepancyService for count comparison and reconciliation workflow. Local PostgreSQL 16 (Azure Arc-enrolled for central management) is the authoritative data store with Redis 7 buffering high-frequency ADC events before batch insert. Azure AD SSO via MSAL.NET handles authentication with tokens cached in DPAPI-encrypted local store. When network connectivity is interrupted, the application operates in offline mode against local PostgreSQL; the OfflineSyncQueue tracks unsynced transactions and ReconnectWorker retries central sync with optimistic locking conflict detection on reconnect.
target_output.technology_stack: Frontend: WPF C# .NET 8, CommunityToolkit.Mvvm 8.2 for MVVM scaffolding, MaterialDesignThemes 5.1 for UI components, MSAL.NET 4.6 for Azure AD SSO. Backend: ASP.NET Core 8 minimal API as Windows Service, Entity Framework Core 8 with Npgsql provider, SignalR for real-time ADC event push, System.Security.Cryptography RSACryptoServiceProvider for chain-of-custody signing. Database: PostgreSQL 16 local (Azure Arc-enrolled), EF Core migrations, Redis 7 local for ADC event buffer. Auth: Azure AD with AAD Conditional Access MFA, RSA-2048 keys in Azure Key Vault hardware HSM. Infrastructure: Azure VMs Windows Server 2022, Azure DevOps for CI/CD, Azure Backup for PostgreSQL, Azure Monitor for VM telemetry. Vendor Integration: Pyxis ES REST SDK (CareFusion), WinAppDriver for UI automation testing.
target_output.functional_feature_map: ADC Inventory Dashboard: live per-cabinet slot counts synchronized from Pyxis ES via plugin, color-coded indicators for low stock (<10% par), discrepancy alert (count mismatch detected), and offline status. Controlled Substance Transaction Log: paginated, searchable log of all dispense/return/waste/restock events with chain-of-custody signature verification status (valid/invalid/missing). Discrepancy Workbench: auto-detected discrepancies between running transaction balance and physical ADC count, witness count entry with mandatory secondary pharmacist Azure AD authentication, pharmacist-in-charge approval workflow with digital signature. Cabinet Management: register and configure dispensing_cabinets, assign IAdcVendorPlugin, set discrepancy threshold per controlled substance schedule. DEA Reports: DEA 222 form data export with pharmacist RSA digital signature, daily Schedule II controlled substance audit report for pharmacy director. Offline Mode: 4-hour offline operation with local PostgreSQL, sync-on-reconnect with conflict detection.
target_output.system_components: WPF Shell Application: MainWindow (DrawerHost navigation), InventoryDashboardView, TransactionLogView, DiscrepancyWorkbenchView, ReportsView - each bound to injectable ViewModels. Local ASP.NET Core 8 Service (Windows Service): SignalR AdcEventsHub, REST API controllers, AdcSyncService polling IAdcVendorPlugin implementations. IAdcVendorPlugin Interface: ConnectAsync/GetInventoryAsync/StreamTransactionsAsync/DisconnectAsync; PyxisEsPlugin loaded from plugins/PyxisEsPlugin.dll. ChainOfCustodyService: RSA-sign each inventory_transaction on commit using pharmacist key from Azure Key Vault. DiscrepancyService: compare expected count (sum of inventory_transactions) to actual ADC count from plugin; create discrepancy_reports when |delta| > threshold. OfflineSyncQueue + ReconnectWorker: local-first operation during network partition, sync-on-reconnect with row_version conflict detection. Local PostgreSQL 16 (Azure Arc-enrolled): authoritative store for all controlled substance records. Redis 7 local: ring buffer for ADC event bursts. Azure Key Vault: non-exportable RSA-2048 pharmacist signing keys.
target_output.workflows: Controlled Substance Dispense: Nurse removes Schedule II opioid from Pyxis ES ADC cabinet. Pyxis ES records transaction and notifies plugin via StreamTransactionsAsync callback. AdcSyncService receives event, buffers in Redis, batch-inserts to inventory_transactions with patient_id_hash and order_id. ChainOfCustodyService creates chain_of_custody_records entry: computes SHA-256(transaction_json + prev_record_hash), signs with dispensing pharmacist key from Azure Key Vault, stores RSA signature in pharmacist_signature column. SignalR pushes update to WPF InventoryDashboard ObservableCollection. Running balance updated in real-time. Discrepancy Detection and Witness Reconciliation: DiscrepancyService scheduled task (every 15 min) computes expected_count for each controlled_substance per cabinet from sum(inventory_transactions) and compares to actual ADC count from plugin GetInventoryAsync. When |expected - actual| > threshold, discrepancy_reports record created with status open. Pharmacist-in-charge notified via WPF notification badge. Pharmacist opens DiscrepancyWorkbench, reviews expected vs actual. Pharmacist performs physical count and enters actual count in form. Application prompts for witness: second pharmacist authenticates via Azure AD in a secondary sign-in dialog. Both signatures (pharmacist + witness) written to the reconciliation chain_of_custody_records entry. Discrepancy status set to reconciled. If unresolvable, status escalated with mandatory incident report.
target_output.data_model: dispensing_cabinets (id UUID PK, vendor ENUM pyxis/omnicell/bd_rowa, cabinet_name VARCHAR, location_unit VARCHAR, is_active BOOLEAN, last_sync_at TIMESTAMPTZ, plugin_assembly VARCHAR). controlled_substances (id UUID PK, ndc VARCHAR(11), drug_name VARCHAR, dea_schedule ENUM II/III/IV/V, unit_of_measure VARCHAR, par_level NUMERIC, low_stock_threshold NUMERIC). inventory_transactions (id UUID PK, cabinet_id UUID FK, controlled_substance_id UUID FK, transaction_type ENUM dispense/return/waste/restock, quantity NUMERIC(8,3), performed_by_user_id VARCHAR, patient_id_hash CHAR(64), order_id VARCHAR, transaction_at TIMESTAMPTZ, row_version INTEGER DEFAULT 1, synced_to_central BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE). chain_of_custody_records (id UUID PK, inventory_transaction_id UUID FK UNIQUE, prev_record_hash CHAR(64), record_hash CHAR(64), pharmacist_user_id VARCHAR, pharmacist_signature BYTEA, witness_user_id VARCHAR, witness_signature BYTEA, signed_at TIMESTAMPTZ). discrepancy_reports (id UUID PK, cabinet_id UUID FK, controlled_substance_id UUID FK, expected_count NUMERIC, actual_count NUMERIC, delta NUMERIC, detected_at TIMESTAMPTZ, status ENUM open/witness_count_pending/reconciled/escalated, reconciled_by_user_id VARCHAR, resolved_at TIMESTAMPTZ). adc_vendor_interfaces (id UUID PK, cabinet_id UUID FK, plugin_assembly_name VARCHAR, endpoint_url TEXT, api_key_secret_name VARCHAR, last_successful_poll TIMESTAMPTZ). pharmacists (id UUID PK, aad_object_id VARCHAR UNIQUE, display_name VARCHAR, dea_registration_number VARCHAR, key_vault_key_name VARCHAR, is_active BOOLEAN).
target_output.api_design: All endpoints hosted at https://localhost:5001 (self-signed cert trusted by pharmacy workstation). SignalR hub: /hubs/adc-events - broadcasts InventoryUpdated, DiscrepancyDetected, SyncStatusChanged events to WPF SignalR client. REST: GET /api/v1/cabinets returns [{cabinet_id, vendor, location_unit, substance_counts:[{ndc,drug_name,expected_count,actual_count,status}]}]. GET /api/v1/transactions?cabinet_id=&ndc=&start=&end=&page= returns paginated transaction list with chain_of_custody_status. POST /api/v1/discrepancies/{id}/start-reconciliation initiates witness count workflow. POST /api/v1/discrepancies/{id}/reconcile body {actual_count, witness_aad_token} submits reconciliation. GET /api/v1/reports/dea-222?period= returns signed DEA 222 report data. POST /api/v1/reports/dea-222/sign triggers RSA signing with pharmacist Key Vault key. All endpoints require AAD Bearer token validated by ASP.NET Core JwtBearer middleware against AAD tenant JWKS endpoint.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Azure AD SSO: MSAL.NET PublicClientApplication with device code flow, MFA enforced via AAD Conditional Access policy, tokens cached in DPAPI-encrypted local file, 5-minute idle screen lock re-authentication. DEA Electronic Record Requirements (21 CFR 1304.22): each chain_of_custody_records signed with dispensing pharmacist RSA-2048 key stored as non-exportable hardware-backed key in Azure Key Vault; key usage events logged to Azure Monitor; reconciliation records require two independent RSA signatures (pharmacist + witness authenticated separately via AAD). HIPAA: patient_id stored as SHA-256(MRN) in inventory_transactions; no raw patient identifiers in controlled substance records; EF Core value converter encrypts patient_id_hash column with AES-256 at application layer before PostgreSQL storage. Data Integrity: chain_of_custody_records hash chain with SHA-256(transaction_json + prev_hash) creates tamper-evident audit trail; PostgreSQL row_version enables optimistic locking for offline sync conflict detection. TLS 1.3 for all network calls; localhost API uses self-signed cert trusted by workstation certificate store.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Azure DevOps Pipeline: dotnet build, dotnet test (85% coverage, WinAppDriver UI automation for discrepancy reconciliation flow), dotnet publish --self-contained -r win-x64, ClickOnce or Chocolatey package. Release Pipeline with manual approval gate for production. Deployment to pharmacy workstations via WinRM over Azure Arc management plane to Azure Arc-enrolled Windows Server 2022 VMs. PostgreSQL 16 and Redis 7 installed as Windows Services via Chocolatey during initial workstation provisioning runbook. Database migrations applied via EF Core CLI step in Release Pipeline before application binary deployment. Production change windows: 02:00-04:00 local time to avoid dispensing hours. Azure Backup policy for PostgreSQL: daily full backup with 35-day retention and point-in-time recovery. Offline workstation detection: pending update notification in system tray; forced update enforcement if workstation version is more than 2 releases behind current.
target_output.observability: Azure Monitor VM Insights on all pharmacy workstations for CPU, memory, disk utilization. Windows Event Log application source PharmacyADC for all application errors, ADC plugin connection failures, and chain-of-custody signing errors; forwarded to Azure Monitor via Azure Monitor Agent. Custom metrics: adc.transactions.ingested_per_hour per cabinet, adc.discrepancies.open_count per unit, adc.sync.offline_queue_depth (records pending central sync), adc.chain_of_custody.signature_verification_failures. Azure Monitor Alerts: sync offline_queue_depth >100 records (network connectivity issue), signature_verification_failures >0 (potential tampering), discrepancy open_count >5 per unit for >24h (unresolved discrepancy alert to pharmacy director). Daily automated DEA compliance report generated by background job and emailed to pharmacy director via Exchange Online.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Azure VM costs: Standard_D2s_v3 (2 vCores, 8 GB RAM) per pharmacy workstation ~$100/month per VM. Azure Key Vault hardware HSM per-transaction signing costs ~$1.50/month at 1000 daily transactions. Azure Backup for PostgreSQL ~$10/month per workstation. Azure Arc management: free for Arc enrollment. Total per-workstation cost ~$115/month. Hospital-wide scaling: each workstation is a standalone deployment; horizontal scaling is adding more pharmacy workstations to the Arc fleet without central server changes.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 - MVP Pyxis ES (Q3 2026, 10 weeks): WPF MVVM shell with InventoryDashboard, TransactionLog, DiscrepancyWorkbench; ASP.NET Core 8 local API as Windows Service; PyxisEsPlugin via Pyxis ES REST SDK; IAdcVendorPlugin interface defined; chain-of-custody RSA signing via Azure Key Vault; discrepancy detection and witness-verified reconciliation with dual AAD authentication; offline mode with OfflineSyncQueue and 4-hour continuity; Azure AD SSO with MFA; DEA 222 report generation with digital signature; Azure DevOps CI/CD with WinAppDriver automation. Phase 2 - Multi-Vendor (Q4 2026, 8 weeks): OmnicellXtPlugin and BdRowaPlugin implementing IAdcVendorPlugin interface; plugin versioning and hot-reload support; Omnicell and BD Rowa cabinet onboarding runbook; pharmacy information system (PIS) HL7 order feed integration for eMAR order matching in transaction records. Phase 3 - Mobile and Analytics (Q2 2027): Mobile pharmacy technician app (MAUI) for wireless physical count entry during witness reconciliation; Power BI analytics workspace for pharmacy director controlled substance trend dashboards.
target_output.development_guidelines: C# nullable reference types enabled project-wide; compiler warnings as errors in CI. CommunityToolkit.Mvvm generator attributes for ViewModel properties and commands - no manual INotifyPropertyChanged implementation. All IAdcVendorPlugin implementations must pass the AdcVendorPluginContractTests test suite (15 tests covering connection, inventory, transaction stream, and disconnection scenarios) before being added to the plugins/ directory. ASP.NET Core endpoints covered at 85% by xUnit integration tests using WebApplicationFactory with in-memory SQLite (not PostgreSQL) for speed. WinAppDriver UI automation tests required for: login flow, discrepancy reconciliation workflow, DEA report signing. No raw patient MRN values in any log output - use SHA-256 hash only. ADR documents required for IAdcVendorPlugin interface contract changes and DEA signing key rotation procedures.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Pyxis ES REST SDK version updates by CareFusion could break PyxisEsPlugin without notice. Mitigation: pin SDK version in NuGet.config; integration test suite runs against Pyxis ES simulator in CI; vendor SDK changelog monitored. Risk: Azure Key Vault network call for each RSA signature operation adds 20-50ms latency to every inventory_transaction write during online mode. Mitigation: acceptable for pharmacist-facing operations; offline mode uses cached signing via local certificate with sync signature verification on reconnect. Tradeoff: IAdcVendorPlugin interface loaded from plugins/ directory via Assembly.LoadFrom allows runtime extensibility but introduces DLL hell risk. Mitigation: plugins/ directory is version-controlled and deployed via Azure DevOps pipeline; no manual DLL drops permitted in production. Risk: Optimistic locking conflict on sync-on-reconnect if same controlled substance was transacted on two workstations during the same offline window. Mitigation: ConflictResolutionService surfaces conflicts to pharmacist-in-charge for manual merge review; automated merge only for non-overlapping transaction windows.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Witness verification for discrepancy reconciliation: secondary Azure AD device code authentication on the same workstation chosen over biometric - AAD authentication creates an auditable digital identity trail required for DEA compliance. Biometric deferred to Phase 3 mobile app. SFTP or direct API for Pyxis ES SDK: direct CareFusion Pyxis ES REST API chosen over SFTP file polling for sub-minute transaction latency. Discrepancy threshold per DEA schedule: Schedule II threshold set to 0 (any delta triggers discrepancy); Schedule III-V threshold set to 1 unit, configurable by pharmacist-in-charge per cabinet.

metadata.notes: First-pass pharmacy ADC desktop app with IAdcVendorPlugin multi-vendor extensibility, DEA chain-of-custody digital signatures, and 4-hour offline continuity architecture.

project_goal.value: Real-time hospital capacity management web application for administrators and bed coordinators to visualize live bed availability, track patient flow bottlenecks, and coordinate transfers across a multi-campus health system
project_goal.rationale: Replaces manual phone-based bed coordination and ad-hoc spreadsheet tracking across 3 hospital campuses

target_users.value: Bed coordinators (primary), hospital administrators, charge nurses, patient flow directors, partner facility transfer coordinators
target_users.rationale: All roles identified in stakeholder interviews; bed coordinators are the heaviest daily users

project_class.rationale: Browser-based application accessible from workstations and tablets across all campuses; no native app required

capabilities.value: frontend, backend, data, auth, realtime, analytics, integrations
capabilities.rationale: React dashboard, Go API, PostgreSQL, Cognito auth, WebSocket real-time updates, occupancy analytics, and ADT system integration

complexity_level.rationale: Real-time ADT integration with sub-5-second bed status propagation, multi-campus data federation, and occupancy analytics at scale

risk_level.rationale: Patient placement data is PHI but the application is read-heavy; no clinical order-writing; primary risk is unauthorized access to census data

data_sensitivity.rationale: Bed occupancy data tied to patient identifiers constitutes PHI under HIPAA

external_exposure.rationale: Application accessible only on hospital intranet; partner transfer coordinators access via hospital VPN

access_model.value: Cognito User Pools with hospital SAML federation; role-based views: bed_coordinator (full campus access), charge_nurse (unit-level view), administrator (all campuses + analytics), transfer_coordinator (partner campus read-only)
access_model.rationale: Hospital-wide SSO via SAML; role-scoped views prevent charge nurse from viewing other units

feature_scope.value: Live campus-level bed map with occupancy heatmap, unit-level bed status grid (occupied/available/dirty/blocked), patient placement queue, ADT event feed, transfer request workflow, occupancy trend analytics, diversion advisory automation, housekeeping task notifications
feature_scope.rationale: Complete bed coordination workflow from admission through discharge and housekeeping clearance

mvp_scope.value: Live bed status grid for 3 campuses, ADT event WebSocket feed from Epic, patient placement queue with bed assignment, occupancy trend dashboard for last 30 days, diversion advisory trigger at configurable occupancy threshold
mvp_scope.rationale: Core workflows needed by bed coordinators on day 1; transfer request and housekeeping integration deferred to Phase 2

security_baseline.value: Cognito JWT validation with 1-hour token expiry, TLS 1.3 for all traffic, CloudFront WAF with hospital IP allowlist, no PHI in CloudFront logs or CDN cache
security_baseline.rationale: HIPAA minimum controls for internal web application with patient census data

frontend_stack.value: React 18 with TypeScript, Recharts for occupancy trend charts, WebSocket for live bed status, React Query for server state
frontend_stack.rationale: React 18 concurrent mode for smooth real-time updates; Recharts for occupancy charts; WebSocket for ADT event push

backend_stack.value: Go 1.22 with Gin framework, gorilla/websocket for WebSocket hub, GORM for PostgreSQL ORM
backend_stack.rationale: Go chosen for high-throughput WebSocket connection management at hospital scale (500+ concurrent bed coordinators); low memory overhead per goroutine

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 for bed state, patient placements, and capacity snapshots; Redis 7 for WebSocket session routing and live bed status cache
data_platform.rationale: PostgreSQL for relational bed/unit/patient data; Redis pub/sub for multi-instance WebSocket event fan-out

hosting_target.value: ECS Fargate on AWS in us-east-1 behind ALB with WAF, CloudFront for React SPA
hosting_target.rationale: Hospital AWS account; ECS Fargate for managed container scaling; CloudFront for SPA delivery with IP allowlist WAF rule

privacy_retention_policy.value: capacity_snapshots retained 2 years for occupancy trend analytics; patient_placements retained 7 years per HIPAA; no patient identifiers in CloudWatch logs
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: HIPAA 6-year minimum for patient records; 2 years sufficient for operational occupancy trending

future_scope.value: Transfer request workflow with partner facility integration, housekeeping task management, predictive census forecasting model, mobile app for charge nurses
future_scope.rationale: Transfer and housekeeping workflows deferred to Phase 2; predictive model on Year 2 roadmap

constraints.value: Bed status updates must propagate to all connected clients within 5 seconds of ADT event receipt; system must support 500 concurrent WebSocket connections; Epic ADT HL7 v2.5 A01/A02/A03/A08 messages are the source of truth
constraints.rationale: Bed coordinator SLA and hospital-wide concurrent user target

observability_baseline.value: Datadog APM for Go API and React frontend RUM, custom metric for WebSocket connection count and ADT event processing latency, PagerDuty on ADT feed interruption
observability_baseline.rationale: Real-time feed health is operationally critical; ADT interruption breaks the core use case

execution_preference.value: 2-week sprints with bed coordinator UAT demos at sprint boundary
execution_preference.rationale: Rapid feedback from primary users is critical given the real-time UX complexity

compliance_context.value: HIPAA for patient placement data displayed in the application; BAA with AWS in place
compliance_context.rationale: Bed occupancy tied to patient identifiers constitutes PHI requiring HIPAA controls

project.goal: Real-time bed availability visualization and patient flow coordination across 3-campus health system
project.target_users: Bed coordinators, administrators, charge nurses, transfer coordinators
project.timeline: MVP go-live Q3 2026

frontend.state: React Query v5 for server state, Zustand for WebSocket connection state
frontend.real_time: WebSocket client via browser WebSocket API

security.auth: Cognito User Pools with SAML federation to hospital IdP
security.transport: TLS 1.3, CloudFront WAF with hospital IP allowlist
security.phi_controls: No PHI in CloudWatch logs or CDN cache headers

data.cache: Redis 7 ElastiCache for WebSocket session routing and bed status cache

devops.hosting: ECS Fargate us-east-1, CloudFront for React SPA

constraints.latency: Bed status updates to all clients within 5 seconds of ADT event
constraints.adt_source: Epic ADT HL7 v2.5 A01/A02/A03/A08 via HL7 listener

open_questions.diversion_threshold: Who configures diversion advisory occupancy threshold - administrator or bed coordinator?

confirmed_decisions.adt_protocol: HL7 v2.5 MLLP listener from Epic

reasoner_reviews.product: The 5-second bed status propagation SLA is the most technically demanding requirement and must be validated against the actual Epic ADT feed latency before the sprint.
