target_output.title: Community Health Fair Landing Page Architecture
target_output.executive_summary: A fully static Astro 4 site deployed on Firebase Hosting with global CDN edge delivery for the hospital system annual community health fair event series. Contentful headless CMS provides event content authoring with webhook-triggered rebuilds delivering updates live within 3 minutes. The architecture handles 50K+ concurrent visitors during registration windows with no origin server load, achieving Lighthouse performance scores >=95 enforced in CI.
target_output.architecture_overview: Zero-backend static architecture: Astro 4 generates fully prebuilt HTML pages at build time by fetching event schedules, speaker bios, health screening listings, and sponsor data from the Contentful Delivery API. Built artifacts deploy to Firebase Hosting which distributes them to GCP CDN edge nodes globally. Astro island hydration selectively loads JavaScript only for the countdown timer and Google Maps embed components, keeping total client JS under 10KB. Contentful publish events trigger GitHub Actions via webhook to rebuild and redeploy within 3 minutes. No origin server handles visitor traffic during peak registration windows.
target_output.technology_stack: Build: Astro 4.x with TypeScript, Tailwind CSS with hospital brand design tokens, Contentful JS SDK for build-time data fetching. CDN and Hosting: Firebase Hosting on GCP with automatic SSL and global edge distribution. Images: Cloudinary with responsive srcsets generating WebP and AVIF variants. Analytics: Google Analytics 4 with anonymize_ip enabled. CI/CD: GitHub Actions with @lhci/cli for Lighthouse CI gating. CMS: Contentful Spaces with Content Delivery API and webhook integration.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Event Schedule section listing all health fair dates, times, and venue locations sourced from Contentful event entries. Health Screening Listings with available screenings per event including cholesterol, blood pressure, BMI, and diabetes risk checks. Speaker Bios grid for featured health educators and physicians. Sponsor Recognition section with tiered logo grid. Registration CTA buttons linking to per-event Eventbrite pages. Parking and Transit info with embedded Google Map. Responsive mobile-first layout.
target_output.system_components: Contentful Spaces with event, screening, speaker, and sponsor content types. GitHub Actions CI/CD pipeline handling build, Lighthouse gate, and deploy. Firebase Hosting with global CDN for static asset serving and SSL termination. Cloudinary for image transformation and delivery. Google Analytics 4 for pageview and CTA interaction tracking. Eventbrite as external registration destination via outbound links.
target_output.workflows: Content Update Workflow: Marketing editor publishes event schedule changes in Contentful; Contentful fires a webhook POST to the GitHub Actions repository_dispatch endpoint with event_type: content_updated; a 60-second debounce prevents duplicate builds on rapid consecutive edits; the Astro build fetches fresh Contentful data, generates static pages, runs Lighthouse CI with >=95 performance threshold, and deploys to Firebase Hosting; changes are live globally within 3 minutes. Traffic Spike Workflow: During registration window open, up to 50K concurrent visitors request event pages; Firebase Hosting CDN edge nodes serve prebuilt HTML from cache with no origin requests, maintaining p99 load times under 800ms globally.
target_output.data_model: No database. Content sourced from Contentful at build time. Content types: events (title, date, venue_name, venue_address, eventbrite_url, featured_screenings[], speaker_ids[]). health_screenings (name, description, eligibility_notes, available_at_events[]). speakers (name, title, affiliation, bio_text, headshot_asset). sponsors (name, tier ENUM platinum/gold/silver/community, logo_asset, website_url). All content versioned in Contentful with draft/published workflow.
target_output.api_design: No server-side API. Build-time only: Contentful Delivery API GET /spaces/{space_id}/entries?content_type=event called during astro build. GA4 Measurement Protocol not used server-side; client-side gtag.js fires health_fair_cta_click and screening_detail_expand events to GA4. Registration links are plain external hrefs to Eventbrite with UTM parameters appended for campaign attribution.
target_output.security_and_compliance: HTTPS enforced by Firebase Hosting for all traffic with HTTP-to-HTTPS redirect. Content Security Policy: default-src 'self'; script-src 'self' https://www.googletagmanager.com; img-src 'self' https://res.cloudinary.com data:; connect-src 'self' https://www.google-analytics.com. Subresource integrity hashes on GA4 gtag.js script tag. No cookies beyond GA4 analytics; GDPR cookie consent banner for EU visitors. IP anonymization enabled in GA4 config. WCAG 2.1 AA accessibility enforced via axe-core in CI.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions pipeline triggers on push to main and on Contentful webhook (repository_dispatch event_type: content_updated). Steps: (1) bun install, (2) astro build fetching Contentful Delivery API, (3) lhci autorun with lighthouserc.json asserting performance >=95, accessibility >=90, best-practices >=90, SEO >=95 as required check, (4) firebase deploy --only hosting to production channel. PR branches auto-deploy to Firebase preview channels generating unique preview URLs for stakeholder review. Rollback: Firebase Hosting version history enables instant one-click rollback to any previous deploy via CLI or console.
target_output.observability: Google Analytics 4 custom events: health_fair_cta_click (properties: event_name, destination_eventbrite_id), map_interaction, screening_detail_expand. Firebase Hosting dashboard monitors bandwidth consumption target under 5GB per event window and cache hit ratio target above 99%. GitHub Actions build duration tracked; alert if Astro build exceeds 5 minutes indicating Contentful API slowness. Lighthouse CI scores logged per deploy for performance trend monitoring.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Firebase Hosting Spark free tier covers 10GB/month bandwidth and 1GB storage. Estimated peak event window bandwidth 3-5GB for 50K visitors at 80KB average page weight after CDN caching. Contentful free tier supports 25K API calls/month; build-time API calls estimated under 500/month. Total infra cost $0-$15/month. Cloudinary free tier handles image delivery. CDN scaling is automatic with no configuration required.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (2 weeks): Core event schedule page, registration CTA links to Eventbrite, location and parking info, mobile-responsive Tailwind layout, GA4 integration, Firebase Hosting deployment with Lighthouse CI gate and Contentful webhook integration. Phase 2 (1 week): Health screening listings, speaker bios grid, sponsor logo grid. Phase 3 post-launch: Multi-year event archive section, WCAG 2.2 AA accessibility audit, CMS content type expansion for volunteer sign-up linking to a future form service.
target_output.development_guidelines: Astro TypeScript strict mode. Tailwind class composition via cva for component variants. All Contentful fetch logic in src/lib/contentful.ts with typed interfaces matching content models. Lighthouse CI required check in GitHub Actions; deploys blocked if any score drops below threshold. axe-core accessibility audit in CI. Image optimization mandatory via Cloudinary transformation URLs. PR previews reviewed by marketing team before merge to main.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Contentful API outage blocks site rebuild during critical pre-event content updates. Mitigation: last successful build artifacts remain live on Firebase Hosting; manual trigger retries available via GitHub Actions UI. Risk: GA4 script blocked by ad blockers reducing analytics coverage for up to 30% of visitors. Accepted tradeoff: no server-side analytics without adding backend complexity that violates the static-only constraint. Tradeoff: registration links are external Eventbrite redirects rather than an integrated booking flow; simplicity favored over feature completeness for MVP.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Confirmed no server-side form handling; volunteer sign-up deferred to v2 using a third-party form service. Confirmed GA4 with IP anonymization satisfies analytics requirements without a consent management platform for MVP. Confirmed Contentful Delivery API (not Preview API) used at build time for published-content-only rendering.

metadata.notes: Static Astro 4 landing page revision closing CMS webhook and Lighthouse CI gaps; no server-side runtime throughout.

data_platform.rationale: Relational for care plan and readings data, Redis for coaching message queue

hosting_target.value: GCP Cloud Run for backend; Firebase Hosting not applicable for mobile
hosting_target.rationale: Cloud Run serverless scales to zero between patient sessions

privacy_retention_policy.value: Patient readings retained 7 years per state health records law; coaching message history retained 2 years; right to deletion supported within 30 days
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Healthcare data retention requirements

future_scope.value: Telehealth video visit integration, medication adherence tracking, population health analytics dashboard for care teams
future_scope.rationale: Year 2 roadmap items

constraints.value: Must pass App Store and Google Play healthcare data policy review; Bluetooth sync must support Accu-Chek and Omron devices; budget under $800/month infra
constraints.rationale: Platform and hardware constraints identified in discovery

observability_baseline.value: Grafana + Prometheus for Cloud Run metrics; Firebase Crashlytics for mobile crash reporting; custom analytics events for reading log completion rates
observability_baseline.rationale: Engineering observability standard

execution_preference.value: Buildkite CI/CD pipeline with automated TestFlight and internal track deployments
execution_preference.rationale: Buildkite selected for Bluetooth integration test parallelization

compliance_context.value: HIPAA Privacy and Security Rules; Apple App Store healthcare data policy; Google Play Developer Policy for health apps; FDA SaMD guidance for patient-reported outcomes
compliance_context.rationale: PHI handling and app store compliance requirements

project.goal: Chronic disease self-management mobile app for diabetes and hypertension patients
project.target_users: Enrolled adult patients
project.timeline: Q2 MVP, Q3 Bluetooth sync

frontend.bluetooth: flutter_blue_plus plugin for Accu-Chek and Omron device pairing

backend.api: RESTful with OpenAPI 3.1

security.auth: Firebase Auth email/password and Google SSO
security.encryption: Cloud SQL CMEK, TLS 1.3, certificate pinning

data.cache: Redis 7 for coaching message queue and session tokens

constraints.stores: App Store and Google Play approval

reasoner_reviews.product: The reading log + coaching message core loop is the right MVP focus. Bluetooth sync is correctly deferred to v1.1 as it adds significant testing complexity. Care plan goal display motivates daily engagement. The 90-day summary report is a high-value feature for clinic visits.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Flutter on Cloud Run with Riverpod is a well-matched stack. Cloud Run scales to zero overnight when patient usage drops, keeping costs under $800/month. Redis as a coaching message queue is appropriate but needs a dead-letter queue for failed delivery tracking. Buildkite pipeline parallelization for Bluetooth hardware-in-loop tests is the correct DevOps choice.
reasoner_reviews.security: Certificate pinning prevents man-in-the-middle attacks on patient glucose data in transit. Firebase Auth with email verification is appropriate for patient self-service. Cloud SQL CMEK with Cloud KMS ensures PHI encrypted at rest under customer-controlled keys. The prior plan was missing a HIPAA audit log for reading access events.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: Cloud Run minimum instances set to 1 prevents cold-start latency for morning reading log peak traffic. Accu-Chek and Omron Bluetooth GATT profiles are well-documented; flutter_blue_plus supports both. App Store healthcare data privacy label must be configured before submission.
reasoner_reviews.critic: Round 2 plan still has a thin observability section missing reading log completion rate tracking and coaching message delivery metrics which are the primary product KPIs.

frontend.app_structure: Flutter app with feature-based folder structure: lib/features/readings (log, history, trend chart), lib/features/coaching (message list, acknowledge), lib/features/care_plan (goals, progress), lib/features/bluetooth (device pairing, sync status), lib/features/auth (login, onboarding)
frontend.pages_and_flows: Onboarding: Firebase Auth login/register -> condition selection (diabetes/hypertension/both) -> care plan display. Home: daily reading status card, pending coaching message badge, upcoming goal. Log Reading: manual entry form with Riverpod validation, Bluetooth sync button. History: sparkline trend charts for glucose and BP readings over 7/30/90 days.
frontend.state_management: Riverpod 2.x providers: ReadingNotifier (AsyncNotifier for CRUD), CoachingMessageNotifier (message queue polling), CarePlanProvider (cached goals), BluetoothSyncNotifier (device pairing state machine)
frontend.frontend_risks: Bluetooth GATT characteristic differences between Accu-Chek Guide Me and Omron Evolv require device-specific parsing logic; abstract behind a DeviceReadingParser interface to contain complexity

backend.service_design: FastAPI application with domain modules: readings (log, history, trend aggregation), coaching_engine (rule evaluation, message scheduling), care_plan (goals, progress calculation), bluetooth_proxy (not needed server-side), auth_middleware (Firebase JWT verification)
backend.api_patterns: RESTful with OpenAPI 3.1. All endpoints require Firebase JWT in Authorization header. Pagination via cursor-based approach for reading history. POST /readings validates glucose range 20-600 mg/dL and BP range systolic 60-220.
backend.background_jobs: Coaching message scheduler: runs every 15 minutes evaluating patient reading trends against personalized coaching rule sets; enqueues triggered messages to Redis. 90-day summary generator: async Cloud Run job triggered pre-appointment by clinic calendar integration.
backend.failure_handling: Idempotency key on POST /readings prevents duplicate logs from Bluetooth sync retries. Redis dead-letter queue captures failed coaching message deliveries for retry with exponential backoff.

security.auth_design: Firebase Auth issues JWTs verified by FastAPI firebase-admin middleware on every request; token refresh handled by Flutter firebase_auth SDK automatically
security.authorization_model: Single patient role; patients can only read/write their own readings, care plan, and coaching messages enforced by patient_id filter on all queries
security.audit_and_logging_controls: Cloud Logging structured audit events for PHI access: reading_accessed, care_plan_viewed, coaching_message_delivered with patient_id, timestamp, and Cloud Run instance ID
security.privacy_controls: Cloud SQL CMEK with Cloud KMS patient-specific encryption context; soft-delete pattern for readings supporting right-to-deletion; no PHI in Cloud Logging message bodies

data.entities: patients (id, firebase_uid, condition ENUM diabetes/hypertension/both, enrolled_at). readings (id, patient_id, reading_type ENUM glucose/systolic/diastolic, value_numeric, unit, source ENUM manual/bluetooth, device_model, logged_at). care_plan_goals (id, patient_id, goal_type, target_value, target_unit, active_from, active_until). coaching_messages (id, patient_id, trigger_rule_id, message_text, channel ENUM push/in_app, status ENUM pending/delivered/acknowledged, delivered_at). clinic_appointments (id, patient_id, scheduled_at, visit_type).
data.storage_design: Cloud SQL PostgreSQL 16 with CMEK for all PHI tables; Redis 7 for coaching message pending queue and patient session tokens
data.retention_and_deletion: Readings retained 7 years via Cloud SQL automated backup with archival to Cloud Storage Coldline after year 1; soft delete with 30-day hard purge for right-to-deletion requests

devops.deployment_topology: Cloud Run backend service (min 1 instance, max 10) in us-central1; Firebase backend not used; Cloud SQL in us-central1 with private IP; Redis on Memorystore in same region
devops.ci_cd_design: Buildkite pipeline: (1) flutter analyze + dart test unit suite, (2) pytest backend with coverage >=80%, (3) Bluetooth hardware-in-loop tests on physical Accu-Chek and Omron devices in Buildkite agent pool, (4) Cloud Run deploy staging, (5) integration smoke tests, (6) TestFlight and Firebase App Distribution upload for staging, (7) manual gate to production Cloud Run deploy
devops.environments: dev (Cloud Run --no-traffic revision), staging (Cloud Run 100% traffic, test Firebase project), production (Cloud Run with min-instances 1)
devops.observability_stack: Prometheus metrics from Cloud Run: request_latency_p99, coaching_message_queue_depth, bluetooth_sync_success_rate. Grafana dashboards for reading log daily active users, coaching message delivery rate, and p99 API latency. Firebase Crashlytics for mobile crash rate per Flutter version.
devops.rollback_strategy: Cloud Run traffic splitting enables instant rollback by routing 100% traffic back to previous revision; Buildkite pipeline auto-triggers rollback if post-deploy smoke tests fail

ISS-001.title: Missing HIPAA audit log for PHI access events
ISS-001.detail: Round 1 plan had no audit logging specification for patient reading access, required for HIPAA Security Rule compliance.

ISS-002.title: Observability missing product KPI metrics
ISS-002.detail: Observability section covers infrastructure metrics but omits reading log completion rate and coaching message delivery rate which are the primary product health indicators.

ISS-003.title: No idempotency handling for Bluetooth sync duplicate reads
ISS-003.detail: Bluetooth sync retries on connection drop could submit duplicate readings; no idempotency key mechanism specified in round 1.

focus_issues.title: Observability missing product KPI metrics
focus_issues.detail: Observability section covers infrastructure metrics but omits reading log completion rate and coaching message delivery rate which are the primary product health indicators.

latest_recommendations: Add reading log completion rate (daily active loggers / enrolled patients) and coaching message delivery rate (delivered / scheduled) as named Grafana dashboard panels with alert thresholds

previous_plan.title: Chronic Disease Care Mobile App - Round 2 Architecture
previous_plan.executive_summary: Flutter and FastAPI chronic disease self-management app on GCP Cloud Run with Firebase Auth, supporting glucose and BP logging, coaching messages, and Bluetooth device sync for Accu-Chek and Omron devices.
previous_plan.architecture_overview: Flutter mobile client communicates over TLS to FastAPI on Cloud Run backed by Cloud SQL PostgreSQL for readings and care plan data and Redis for coaching message queue.
previous_plan.security_and_compliance: Firebase Auth JWTs verified on every request. Cloud SQL CMEK encryption. Cloud Logging audit events for PHI access. Certificate pinning in Flutter client.
previous_plan.observability: Prometheus metrics from Cloud Run: request_latency_p99, coaching_message_queue_depth. Grafana dashboards. Firebase Crashlytics for mobile crashes.

target_output.thinking_summary: Round 3 revision targeting ISS-002: the observability section needs reading log completion rate and coaching message delivery rate added as first-class Grafana panels with alert thresholds. These are the product KPIs that drive clinical program value. ISS-001 (audit log) and ISS-003 (idempotency) are resolved. Architecture is otherwise sound for a Flutter/FastAPI/Cloud Run chronic disease management app.

fix_report.action_taken: Expanded observability section to include reading_log_completion_rate (daily_active_loggers / enrolled_patients, target >60%), coaching_message_delivery_rate (delivered / scheduled, target >95%), and coaching_message_acknowledgement_rate (acknowledged / delivered) as named Grafana panels with PagerDuty alerts when completion rate drops below 40% for 3 consecutive days.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Care program managers can monitor patient engagement KPIs in real time and operations team receives alerts on meaningful engagement drops, not just infrastructure faults.

target_output.title: Chronic Disease Care Management Mobile App Architecture
target_output.executive_summary: A Flutter mobile application on GCP backed by FastAPI on Cloud Run enabling patients with diabetes or hypertension to log glucose and blood pressure readings, receive personalized coaching messages, track care plan goals, and sync readings from Accu-Chek and Omron Bluetooth devices. Firebase Auth secures patient access; Cloud SQL PostgreSQL stores PHI with CMEK encryption; Buildkite CI/CD handles TestFlight and internal track releases with hardware-in-loop Bluetooth tests.
target_output.architecture_overview: Flutter single codebase deployed to iOS and Android communicates over TLS 1.3 to a FastAPI service on Cloud Run (min 1 instance, max 10) in us-central1. Cloud Run authenticates requests via Firebase Admin JWT verification. Reading and care plan data persist in Cloud SQL PostgreSQL 16 with CMEK encryption on a private VPC. Redis 7 on Memorystore holds the coaching message pending queue and session tokens. Background Cloud Run jobs evaluate reading trends every 15 minutes to generate contextual coaching messages. Bluetooth sync for Accu-Chek Guide Me and Omron Evolv devices uses flutter_blue_plus GATT profiles with device-specific parsing abstracted behind a DeviceReadingParser interface.
target_output.technology_stack: Mobile: Flutter 3.x with Dart, Riverpod 2.x, flutter_blue_plus, firebase_auth, firebase_crashlytics. Backend: Python 3.12, FastAPI, SQLAlchemy 2.0 async, firebase-admin for JWT verification, Pydantic v2. Database: Cloud SQL PostgreSQL 16 with CMEK, Redis 7 on Memorystore. Infrastructure: GCP Cloud Run, Cloud SQL, Memorystore, Cloud KMS, Cloud Logging. CI/CD: Buildkite with hardware-in-loop Bluetooth agent pool, TestFlight and Firebase App Distribution for staging.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Reading Log module for manual glucose and BP entry with Riverpod validation enforcing clinical range bounds (glucose 20-600 mg/dL, systolic 60-220 mmHg). Bluetooth Sync module pairing Accu-Chek Guide Me and Omron Evolv devices and importing readings with idempotency key deduplication. Coaching Message module delivering rule-triggered personalized messages via push notification and in-app inbox. Care Plan Goals module displaying targets set by the care team with progress indicators. Trend History module with sparkline charts for 7/30/90-day glucose and BP trends. 90-Day Summary Report generator for clinic visit preparation.
target_output.system_components: Flutter mobile client (iOS and Android). FastAPI on Cloud Run with min 1 instance to avoid cold starts during morning logging peak. Cloud SQL PostgreSQL 16 with CMEK on private VPC. Redis 7 on Memorystore for coaching queue and sessions. Cloud Run coaching scheduler job (every 15 minutes). Firebase Auth for patient identity. Cloud KMS for CMEK key management. Cloud Logging for PHI audit events. Firebase Crashlytics for mobile crash reporting. Buildkite CI/CD with Bluetooth device agent pool.
target_output.workflows: Reading Log Workflow: Patient opens app in morning, selects reading type (glucose or blood pressure), enters value or taps Bluetooth sync, Flutter validates clinical range, POST /readings with idempotency key (patient_id + device_id + reading_timestamp), FastAPI persists to Cloud SQL and publishes event to coaching scheduler, Riverpod refreshes trend chart. Coaching Message Workflow: Cloud Run scheduler evaluates patient reading trends against personalized rule set every 15 minutes, generates coaching_message record with status pending, enqueues to Redis coaching queue, worker delivers via FCM push notification to patient device and sets status to delivered, patient opens in-app inbox and acknowledges setting status acknowledged.
target_output.data_model: patients (id UUID PK, firebase_uid, condition ENUM diabetes/hypertension/both, enrolled_at, deleted_at). readings (id UUID PK, patient_id FK, reading_type ENUM glucose/systolic/diastolic, value_numeric DECIMAL, unit TEXT, source ENUM manual/bluetooth, device_model TEXT, idempotency_key TEXT UNIQUE, logged_at TIMESTAMPTZ, deleted_at). care_plan_goals (id UUID PK, patient_id FK, goal_type TEXT, target_value DECIMAL, target_unit TEXT, active_from DATE, active_until DATE). coaching_messages (id UUID PK, patient_id FK, trigger_rule_id TEXT, message_text TEXT, channel ENUM push/in_app, status ENUM pending/delivered/acknowledged, delivered_at TIMESTAMPTZ, acknowledged_at TIMESTAMPTZ). clinic_appointments (id UUID PK, patient_id FK, scheduled_at TIMESTAMPTZ, visit_type TEXT).
target_output.api_design: RESTful API at /api/v1, Firebase JWT required on all routes. POST /readings (body: reading_type, value_numeric, unit, source, device_model, idempotency_key; validates clinical range). GET /readings?patient_id={id}&days=90 (cursor-paginated reading history). GET /care-plan/goals (active goals for authenticated patient). GET /coaching/messages?status=pending (unread coaching messages). PATCH /coaching/messages/{id}/acknowledge (mark message acknowledged). GET /reports/90-day-summary (pre-visit summary PDF generation triggered async).
target_output.security_and_compliance: Firebase Auth email/password with email verification required before PHI access; optional Google SSO. FastAPI firebase-admin middleware verifies JWT on every request. All patient data queries filtered by patient_id from JWT claims to prevent cross-patient access. Certificate pinning in Flutter via http_certificate_pinning package. Cloud SQL CMEK with Cloud KMS patient-scoped encryption context. TLS 1.3 enforced on Cloud Run. Cloud Logging structured audit events: reading_accessed, care_plan_viewed, coaching_message_delivered with patient_id, firebase_uid, endpoint, and Cloud Run revision. HIPAA BAA with GCP. Soft delete with 30-day hard purge for right-to-deletion requests per HIPAA Privacy Rule.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Buildkite pipeline stages: (1) flutter analyze + dart test unit suite, (2) pytest backend with >=80% coverage gate, (3) Bluetooth hardware-in-loop tests on physical Accu-Chek and Omron agents in Buildkite pool, (4) Cloud Run deploy to staging revision with --no-traffic, (5) integration smoke tests against staging, (6) TestFlight upload for iOS and Firebase App Distribution for Android internal testing, (7) manual approval gate, (8) Cloud Run migrate-traffic 100% to new revision in production. Rollback: Cloud Run traffic split back to previous revision within 30 seconds. Cloud SQL managed automated backups daily with 7-day retention.
target_output.observability: Prometheus metrics from Cloud Run exported to Grafana: api_request_latency_p99 (alert >500ms), coaching_message_queue_depth (alert >500), Cloud Run instance count. Product KPI Grafana panels: reading_log_completion_rate (daily_active_loggers / enrolled_patients, alert when <40% for 3 consecutive days), coaching_message_delivery_rate (delivered / scheduled per day, alert when <90%), coaching_message_acknowledgement_rate (acknowledged / delivered per week). Firebase Crashlytics tracks mobile crash-free session rate by Flutter version (alert <99%). PagerDuty escalation for all alert thresholds.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Cloud Run 1 min instance + up to 10 on demand: ~$120/month at 500 daily active patients. Cloud SQL db-n1-standard-2 with 100GB SSD: ~$200/month. Memorystore Redis basic 1GB: ~$50/month. Total estimated $400-$600/month, well under $800 budget. Cloud Run max-instances cap prevents cost spikes. Cloud SQL read replica added if 90-day summary report queries impact write latency.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (6 weeks): Firebase Auth onboarding, manual glucose and BP reading log, care plan goals display, coaching message delivery via push and in-app, Cloud Run backend, Buildkite CI/CD pipeline. Phase 2 (4 weeks): Bluetooth sync for Accu-Chek Guide Me and Omron Evolv, idempotency-safe reading import, device pairing onboarding flow. Phase 3 (3 weeks): 90-day summary PDF report, trend sparkline charts for 7/30/90 days, clinic appointment integration for pre-visit summary trigger.
target_output.development_guidelines: Dart strict mode with flutter analyze as required CI step. All FastAPI routes must have Pydantic v2 request/response models with clinical range validators. Reading type and status fields must use Python Enum classes, not raw strings. PHI must never appear in Cloud Run structured log message bodies. Buildkite pipeline blocks on hardware Bluetooth tests; no deploy to staging without passing. PR requires 1 senior review.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: flutter_blue_plus Bluetooth connection instability on Android 14 with power management changes. Mitigation: idempotency key on POST /readings handles retry duplicates; user-facing retry UI on sync failure. Risk: Cloud Run cold starts during off-peak hours despite min 1 instance setting if instance recycles. Mitigation: Cloud Run startup probe with /health endpoint. Tradeoff: Riverpod over BLoC for simpler async state but less explicit event audit trail; acceptable for patient-facing app without complex event sourcing requirements.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Confirmed Cloud Run over GKE for simplified operations at this scale. Confirmed flutter_blue_plus supports Accu-Chek Guide Me BLE GATT profile. Confirmed Buildkite over GitHub Actions for hardware-in-loop Bluetooth test support. Soft delete strategy confirmed for HIPAA right-to-deletion compliance.

metadata.notes: Round 3 revision for Flutter chronic disease app closing product KPI observability gap; ISS-001 and ISS-003 resolved in prior rounds.

project_goal.rationale: Replacing a paper-based and spreadsheet-driven credentialing process

target_users.value: Credentialing coordinators, medical staff office directors, committee chairs, and HR administrators across 5 hospital sites
target_users.rationale: Identified in stakeholder discovery workshops

project_class.rationale: Browser-based multi-site application for internal staff

capabilities.value: frontend, backend, data, auth, admin_panel, integrations
capabilities.rationale: Full internal web app with admin controls and NPDB integration

complexity_level.rationale: Multi-step approval workflows with external verification integrations

risk_level.rationale: Confidential provider data but not patient PHI; internal access only

data_sensitivity.rationale: Provider malpractice history, DEA license, NPDB query results are sensitive business data

mvp_scope.rationale: Core credentialing workflow for Q3 pilot at 2 hospitals

observability_baseline.rationale: Corporate Azure monitoring standard

compliance_context.rationale: Credentialing app must maintain compliance audit trail for accreditation surveys

project.goal: Multi-site physician credentialing and privileging management system
project.target_users: Credentialing coordinators, MSO directors, committee chairs
project.timeline: Q3 2-hospital pilot, Q4 full rollout

frontend.styling: PrimeVue component library with internal design tokens
frontend.state: Pinia for credential application form state and workflow step tracking

backend.api: RESTful API, Django Admin for MSO director admin panel

security.auth: Azure AD SSO with MSAL
security.encryption: TLS 1.3, Azure Disk Encryption at rest
security.mfa: Enforced via Azure AD Conditional Access

data.db: Azure Database for PostgreSQL Flexible Server 16
data.task_queue: Celery with Redis broker for verification status updates

reasoner_reviews.product: The credentialing application intake + primary source verification checklist + committee approval chain is the correct MVP core. Re-credentialing cycle automation with 120-day advance reminders is high-value for credentialing coordinators who currently track cycles in spreadsheets. NPDB integration is non-negotiable for compliance.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Django with DRF and Django Admin is well-suited here: Admin provides the MSO director panel without custom build, and DRF handles the API for the Vue 3 SPA. Celery with Redis broker is appropriate for primary source verification status polling jobs. The multi-hospital RBAC model needs careful design with hospital-scoped permissions to prevent one site coordinator seeing another site's provider records.
reasoner_reviews.security: Azure AD Conditional Access enforcing MFA is the right enterprise control. Hospital-scoped RBAC must be enforced at the Django queryset level, not just UI-level. NPDB query responses contain sensitive adverse action history and must not be logged or cached beyond the session.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: Azure App Service P2v3 with autoscale handles the modest credentialing coordinator concurrency (estimated 20-50 concurrent users). PostgreSQL Flexible Server general-purpose 2 vCores is sufficient. Total infra cost estimated $700-$900/month within budget.
reasoner_reviews.critic: First-round plan had a weak re-credentialing cycle automation section that described the reminder workflow in generic terms without specifying the cycle expiry logic, the 120-day advance notification chain, or the automated NPDB query trigger at re-credentialing time.

frontend.app_structure: Vue 3 SPA with feature-based structure: src/features/credentialing (new application wizard, verification checklist), src/features/privileges (request, approval, grant), src/features/recredentialing (cycle dashboard, document upload), src/features/admin (committee management, site config), src/features/providers (profile, history)
frontend.pages_and_flows: Provider Search and Profile view. New Credentialing Application wizard (6 steps: personal info, education, training, malpractice history, references, submit). Verification Checklist board with status per primary source. Committee Review queue with approve/defer/deny actions. Privilege Request form with procedure-level privilege selection. Re-credentialing Dashboard showing cycle expiry countdown.
frontend.state_management: Pinia stores: CredentialingApplicationStore (multi-step form state with autosave), VerificationChecklistStore (polling status updates from backend), PrivilegeStore (cached privilege catalog), ReCredentialingStore (cycle status per provider)
frontend.frontend_risks: Multi-step application wizard state must survive browser refresh; implement Pinia persistence via localStorage with encryption of draft form data

backend.service_design: Django app modules: credentialing (Application, PrimarySourceVerification models), privileges (PrivilegeRequest, PrivilegeGrant, PrivilegeCatalog), recredentialing (ReCredentialingCycle, CycleDocument), committees (Committee, Meeting, VotingRecord), providers (ProviderProfile, LicenseRecord, MalpracticeRecord)
backend.api_patterns: DRF ViewSets with hospital-scoped queryset filtering on every endpoint. Nested routers for /providers/{id}/credentials and /providers/{id}/privileges. JWT from Azure AD MSAL verified by django-azure-ad middleware.
backend.background_jobs: Celery tasks: daily re_credentialing_expiry_scan (flags cycles expiring in 120 days, sends coordinator notification), npdb_query_submitter (submits NPDB queries at application intake and re-credentialing trigger), verification_status_poller (checks external verification source APIs every 4 hours)
backend.failure_handling: NPDB API failures queued for retry with 3 exponential backoff attempts; failed after 3 attempts triggers manual coordinator alert. Celery task status tracked in Django PeriodicTaskResult for audit.

security.auth_design: Azure AD SSO via MSAL; Django social-auth-app-django with Azure AD backend; MFA enforced via Azure AD Conditional Access policy
security.authorization_model: Django custom permission classes: HospitalScopedPermission filters querysets to user.hospital_site memberships; roles: credentialing_coordinator (read/write own site applications), mso_director (read all sites, approve), committee_chair (voting on assigned committees), system_admin (full access)
security.audit_and_logging_controls: Django signal post_save on Application, PrivilegeGrant, and NPDBQueryResult writes to CredentialingAuditLog table with user, action, record_id, hospital_site, and timestamp. NPDB responses must not appear in Django log output.
security.security_risks: NPDB adverse action data must be access-controlled at the field level; committee members should see summary only, not raw NPDB query XML response

data.entities: providers (id, npi, first_name, last_name, specialty, dea_number_encrypted, created_at). credentialing_applications (id, provider_id, hospital_site_id, status ENUM draft/submitted/in_review/approved/denied, submitted_at). primary_source_verifications (id, application_id, source_type ENUM medical_school/residency/npdb/state_license/dea/malpractice_insurer, status ENUM pending/verified/flagged/unable_to_verify, completed_at). privilege_grants (id, provider_id, hospital_site_id, privilege_code, granted_by_committee_id, effective_date, expiry_date, status ENUM active/suspended/revoked). recredentialing_cycles (id, provider_id, hospital_site_id, cycle_start, cycle_expiry, status ENUM active/reminder_sent/in_progress/completed, reminder_sent_at).
data.storage_design: Azure Database for PostgreSQL Flexible Server with zone-redundant HA. Sensitive fields (DEA number, malpractice settlement amounts) encrypted at application layer using django-encrypted-model-fields. Documents stored in Azure Blob Storage with provider-scoped SAS tokens.
data.retention_and_deletion: Credentialing records retained 10 years via Azure Backup. NPDB query XML responses stored encrypted in Blob Storage with 7-year retention per data use agreement. Audit logs retained 3 years in PostgreSQL with archival to Azure Blob after year 1.

devops.deployment_topology: Azure App Service P2v3 plan with autoscale (2-4 instances) for Django backend; Azure Static Web Apps for Vue 3 build artifacts; Azure Database for PostgreSQL Flexible Server; Azure Cache for Redis; Azure Blob Storage for documents
devops.ci_cd_design: Azure DevOps Pipeline: pylint + pytest with >=80% coverage, vue-tsc + vitest unit tests, Docker build, push to Azure Container Registry, az webapp deploy to staging slot, integration smoke tests, manual approval gate, swap staging slot to production
devops.environments: dev (single instance, reduced DB SKU), staging (deployment slot with production DB clone), production (P2v3 autoscale, zone-redundant PostgreSQL)
devops.observability_stack: Azure Monitor Application Insights instrumented in Django via opencensus-ext-azure; custom events for credentialing_application_submitted, npdb_query_completed, privilege_granted; Azure Monitor alerts on 5xx error rate >1% and App Service CPU >80%

previous_plan.title: Physician Credentialing System - Initial Architecture
previous_plan.executive_summary: Django 5 backend with Vue 3 SPA for multi-site physician credentialing and privileging management on Azure App Service with PostgreSQL and Celery task queue for NPDB polling.
previous_plan.security_and_compliance: Azure AD SSO with MFA enforced. RBAC roles for coordinator, director, committee_chair, admin. TLS 1.3. Audit log table for credentialing actions.
previous_plan.workflows: New provider applies online, coordinator initiates primary source verifications, committee reviews application, privileges granted or denied.

target_output.thinking_summary: Round 2 revision for a physician credentialing web app. Fixing ISS-002 (high severity) first: Django HospitalScopedPermission as base ViewSet mixin filtering all querysets to user.hospital_site memberships prevents cross-site data leakage. Fixing ISS-001: specifying the re-credentialing cycle formula (expiry = appointment_date + 2 years, Celery daily scan at T-120, T-60, T-30 days) and NPDB query trigger at cycle initiation. Plan quality is moderate as the spec dictates.

fix_report.action_taken: Added HospitalScopedPermission Django REST Framework mixin class that overrides get_queryset() to filter all ViewSets by HospitalSiteMembership.objects.filter(user=request.user).values_list('hospital_site_id', flat=True), ensuring coordinators at site A cannot retrieve provider records for site B regardless of UI controls.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Multi-site credential isolation enforced at database query level, preventing accidental or deliberate cross-site data access.

fix_report.action_taken: Specified re-credentialing cycle formula: cycle_expiry = initial_appointment_date + 730 days (2-year standard); Celery daily re_credentialing_expiry_scan checks cycles with expiry in 120, 60, and 30 days and sends tiered notifications (coordinator at T-120, coordinator + provider at T-60, coordinator + mso_director at T-30); re-credentialing initiation automatically triggers npdb_query_submitter Celery task.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Credentialing coordinators receive advance notice with sufficient lead time to complete re-credentialing before cycle expiry, and NPDB queries are never omitted at re-credentialing.

target_output.title: Multi-Site Physician Credentialing and Privileging System Architecture
target_output.executive_summary: A Django 5 and Vue 3 web application for hospital credentialing departments managing physician and APP credentialing, privileging, and re-credentialing across a 5-hospital system on Azure App Service. Azure AD SSO with hospital-scoped RBAC enforced at Django queryset level prevents cross-site data access. Celery workers handle primary source verification polling, NPDB query submission, and automated re-credentialing cycle reminders. TJC credentialing record retention and NPDB data use agreement compliance built in.
target_output.architecture_overview: Vue 3 TypeScript SPA with Pinia state management communicates over TLS 1.3 to a Django 5 REST API on Azure App Service authenticated via Azure AD MSAL. Django Admin serves as the MSO director admin panel without custom build. All DRF ViewSets inherit HospitalScopedPermission to filter querysets by the requesting user's hospital site memberships, preventing cross-site credential access. PostgreSQL 16 on Azure Flexible Server stores credentialing records with application-layer field encryption for DEA numbers and malpractice data. Celery workers with Redis broker handle NPDB query submission, primary source verification status polling, and daily re-credentialing expiry scanning.
target_output.technology_stack: Frontend: Vue 3 with TypeScript, Composition API, Pinia, PrimeVue component library, MSAL.js for Azure AD token acquisition. Backend: Python 3.12, Django 5, Django REST Framework, django-azure-ad, Celery 5, django-encrypted-model-fields. Database: Azure Database for PostgreSQL Flexible Server 16 with zone-redundant HA. Cache/Queue: Azure Cache for Redis 7. Storage: Azure Blob Storage with provider-scoped SAS tokens. Infrastructure: Azure App Service P2v3, Azure DevOps Pipelines, Azure Monitor Application Insights.
target_output.functional_feature_map: New Credentialing Application wizard capturing provider personal info, medical education, training history, malpractice insurance, and peer references with multi-step Pinia form state. Primary Source Verification Checklist tracking verification status per source (medical school, residency program, NPDB, state license, DEA, malpractice insurer) with Celery-driven status polling. Committee Review Queue for committee chairs to vote approve/defer/deny with recorded voting rationale. Privilege Grant workflow with procedure-level privilege catalog selection and effective/expiry dates. Re-credentialing Cycle Dashboard showing days-to-expiry per provider with automated reminder cadence. Django Admin for MSO directors to configure hospital sites, committee compositions, and privilege catalogs.
target_output.system_components: Vue 3 SPA served from Azure Static Web Apps. Django 5 REST API on Azure App Service P2v3 with autoscale 2-4 instances. HospitalScopedPermission mixin on all ViewSets. Celery worker pool (2 workers) on Azure App Service separate plan. Azure Database for PostgreSQL Flexible Server with zone-redundant HA. Azure Cache for Redis 7 (Celery broker and session cache). Azure Blob Storage for credentialing documents and NPDB response archives. Azure AD MSAL for SSO and MFA enforcement. Azure Monitor Application Insights for APM. CredentialingAuditLog Django model for accreditation evidence.
target_output.workflows: New Credentialing Workflow: Credentialing coordinator initiates application for new provider, completes 6-step wizard including education, training, malpractice history, references; submission triggers Celery npdb_query_submitter for immediate NPDB query and verification_status_poller jobs for all primary sources; coordinator monitors checklist board; on all verifications complete, application routes to committee queue; committee chair reviews with NPDB summary and votes; MSO director final approval triggers privilege_grant records for approved procedures. Re-Credentialing Workflow: Celery daily scan identifies cycles expiring in 120 days and creates re-credentialing application pre-populated from existing provider record; coordinator notified at T-120 (review packet), T-60 (provider + coordinator reminder), T-30 (coordinator + MSO director escalation); coordinator initiates application which triggers new NPDB query automatically; committee review and approval updates cycle_expiry to appointment_date + 730 days.
target_output.data_model: providers (id UUID PK, npi VARCHAR UNIQUE, first_name, last_name, specialty, dea_number_encrypted, created_at). hospital_site_memberships (id, user_id FK, hospital_site_id FK, role ENUM credentialing_coordinator/mso_director/committee_chair/system_admin). credentialing_applications (id UUID PK, provider_id FK, hospital_site_id FK, status ENUM draft/submitted/in_review/approved/denied, submitted_at, decided_at). primary_source_verifications (id UUID PK, application_id FK, source_type ENUM medical_school/residency/npdb/state_license/dea/malpractice_insurer, status ENUM pending/verified/flagged/unable_to_verify, verified_by, completed_at). privilege_grants (id UUID PK, provider_id FK, hospital_site_id FK, privilege_code, privilege_name, granted_by_committee_id FK, effective_date DATE, expiry_date DATE, status ENUM active/suspended/revoked). recredentialing_cycles (id UUID PK, provider_id FK, hospital_site_id FK, cycle_start DATE, cycle_expiry DATE, status ENUM active/reminder_sent/in_progress/completed, reminder_120_sent_at, reminder_60_sent_at, reminder_30_sent_at). credentialing_audit_log (id UUID PK, user_id, action, model_name, record_id, hospital_site_id, timestamp TIMESTAMPTZ).
target_output.api_design: DRF API at /api/v1/. GET/POST /providers (filtered by hospital site). GET /providers/{id}/credentials (application history). POST /credentialing-applications (initiate new application). GET /credentialing-applications?status=in_review (coordinator queue). PATCH /credentialing-applications/{id}/submit. GET /primary-source-verifications?application_id={id} (checklist). POST /committee-reviews/{application_id}/vote (committee chair action). GET/POST /privilege-grants (active privileges for provider). GET /recredentialing-cycles?expiry_before=2026-09-01 (upcoming expirations). All responses are hospital-site scoped via HospitalScopedPermission.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Azure AD SSO with MSAL; MFA enforced via Azure AD Conditional Access for all users. HospitalScopedPermission DRF mixin overrides get_queryset() with .filter(hospital_site_id__in=user_hospital_sites) on all ViewSets. DEA number and malpractice settlement amounts encrypted at application layer with django-encrypted-model-fields using Azure Key Vault-backed keys. NPDB query XML responses stored in Azure Blob Storage with provider-scoped SAS tokens; never logged. CredentialingAuditLog records every create/update on credentialing_applications, privilege_grants, and npdb_query_results with user, action, record_id, hospital_site, and timestamp for TJC accreditation evidence. TLS 1.3 enforced on App Service.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Azure DevOps Pipeline: (1) pylint + mypy type checks, (2) pytest with >=80% coverage gate, (3) vitest + vue-tsc frontend checks, (4) Docker build and push to Azure Container Registry, (5) az webapp deploy to staging slot, (6) Celery worker deploy to separate App Service, (7) Django migrate, (8) integration smoke tests against staging, (9) manual approval gate, (10) slot swap staging to production. Environments: dev (single instance, Basic DB tier), staging (deployment slot with anonymized production DB clone), production (P2v3 autoscale 2-4 instances, zone-redundant PostgreSQL Flexible Server). Celery beat scheduler on separate Azure App Service plan to avoid worker/beat CPU contention.
target_output.observability: Azure Monitor Application Insights with opencensus-ext-azure tracking request latency per endpoint and exception rate. Custom Application Insights events: credentialing_application_submitted, npdb_query_completed (duration_ms), privilege_granted, recredentialing_reminder_sent. Azure Monitor alert on App Service 5xx rate >1% and Celery queue backlog >50 tasks. Dashboard panels: pending NPDB queries over 24 hours, re-credentialing cycles expiring in <30 days count, application approval cycle time average.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Azure App Service P2v3 ($300/month per instance, autoscale 2-4). PostgreSQL Flexible Server General Purpose 2 vCores ($200/month with zone-redundant HA). Azure Cache for Redis C1 ($55/month). Azure Blob Storage minimal ($10/month for credentialing documents). Total estimated $750-$950/month within $1200 budget. Autoscale policy: scale out at CPU >70% for 5 minutes, scale in at CPU <30% for 10 minutes.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (8 weeks): New provider credentialing application wizard, primary source verification checklist with manual status updates, committee review and voting, basic privilege grant/deny, Azure AD SSO with hospital-scoped RBAC. Phase 2 (4 weeks): Automated NPDB query submission via Celery, primary source verification API polling (DEA, state license), re-credentialing cycle dashboard with manual reminders. Phase 3 (4 weeks): Automated re-credentialing reminder cadence (T-120/60/30), privilege catalog management in Django Admin, TJC-ready credentialing audit report export.
target_output.development_guidelines: Python type annotations required; mypy strict mode in CI. DRF ViewSets must inherit HospitalScopedPermission; CI lint check enforces this. django-encrypted-model-fields required for DEA and malpractice fields; PR reviewer checklist item. NPDB XML response must not appear in any Django log call; enforced by log scrubbing middleware. Frontend Vue components must use TypeScript strict mode and defineProps with full type declarations. vitest unit tests required for Pinia store actions.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: NPDB API rate limits during re-credentialing bulk processing. Mitigation: Celery tasks rate-limited to 5 concurrent NPDB queries with jittered backoff. Risk: Azure AD Conditional Access policy changes breaking MFA enforcement silently. Mitigation: weekly Azure AD policy audit in runbook. Tradeoff: Django Admin for MSO director panel trades flexibility for build speed; acceptable for internal admin users. Tradeoff: Celery beat on separate App Service adds cost but avoids worker starvation during heavy NPDB polling windows.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Confirmed Django Admin sufficient for MSO director admin panel without custom Vue build. Confirmed Azure Blob Storage with SAS tokens for NPDB response archival rather than PostgreSQL BYTEA. Confirmed 2-year re-credentialing cycle as default with committee-level override capability. Confirmed hospital-scoped RBAC enforced at queryset level.

metadata.notes: Round 2 revision closing hospital-scoped RBAC queryset enforcement gap and re-credentialing cycle logic for multi-site physician credentialing system.

profile.domain: Telemedicine Urgent Care Virtual Visit Platform

project_goal.value: Full-stack telemedicine platform for urgent care virtual visits enabling patients to queue for on-demand WebRTC video consultations with on-call physicians, with real-time wait time estimates and provider dashboard for concurrent encounter management
project_goal.rationale: Core urgent care virtual visit use case

target_users.value: Patients seeking urgent care and on-call physicians managing concurrent virtual encounters
target_users.rationale: Primary user populations identified in discovery

project_class.rationale: Full-stack platform with patient-facing and physician-facing surfaces

capabilities.value: frontend, backend, data, auth, realtime, integrations, analytics, payments
capabilities.rationale: Full telemedicine platform with payment processing

complexity_level.rationale: Real-time WebRTC video, queue management, and payment processing in a HIPAA context

risk_level.rationale: Patient PHI, real-time clinical interactions, and payment card data

data_sensitivity.rationale: Encounter notes, diagnoses, and prescriptions are PHI

external_exposure.rationale: Patient-facing platform accessible on public internet

access_model.value: AWS Cognito user pools: patient and physician roles; MFA required for physicians, optional for patients
access_model.rationale: Cognito handles patient self-registration and physician enterprise SSO

feature_scope.value: Patient virtual queue with real-time wait estimates, WebRTC video session management, physician encounter dashboard, structured encounter note completion, after-visit summary delivery, prescription routing, copay and self-pay payment collection, payer claim submission
feature_scope.rationale: Full urgent care telehealth feature set

mvp_scope.value: Patient queue entry with chief complaint, WebRTC video session launch, basic encounter note, and Stripe copay collection
mvp_scope.rationale: Core video visit flow for Q3 pilot

security_baseline.value: Cognito MFA for physicians, TLS 1.3, PHI encrypted at rest via RDS KMS, HIPAA BAA with AWS, Stripe for PCI-DSS scope isolation
security_baseline.rationale: HIPAA and PCI-DSS minimum controls

frontend_stack.value: Next.js 15 with TypeScript
frontend_stack.rationale: Next.js App Router SSR for SEO and fast initial page load for patient queue

backend_stack.value: Node.js 22 with TypeScript and Express
backend_stack.rationale: TypeScript end-to-end; Express lightweight for high-concurrency WebSocket connections

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 on Aurora with RDS KMS encryption; Redis 7 on ElastiCache for queue state and session
data_platform.rationale: Aurora multi-AZ for high availability; Redis for real-time queue state

hosting_target.value: AWS ECS Fargate for backend; Vercel for Next.js frontend
hosting_target.rationale: Vercel for Next.js 15 App Router edge functions; Fargate for Node backend

privacy_retention_policy.value: Encounter notes and PHI retained 7 years; payment transaction records retained 7 years per financial regulations; session recordings not permitted
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Healthcare and financial record retention requirements

future_scope.value: Scheduled telehealth appointments, specialty virtual care routing, EHR integration for encounter note export, insurance eligibility verification
future_scope.rationale: Year 2 roadmap

constraints.value: WebRTC via Twilio Video; Stripe for payment processing; AWS us-east-1 only; budget under $3000/month
constraints.rationale: Vendor and infrastructure constraints confirmed in architecture review

observability_baseline.value: Datadog APM with ECS Fargate sidecar; custom metrics for queue depth, video session success rate, payment processing latency
observability_baseline.rationale: Datadog corporate standard

execution_preference.value: GitHub Actions CI/CD with trunk-based development and feature flags
execution_preference.rationale: Engineering standard workflow

compliance_context.value: HIPAA Privacy and Security Rules; HITECH; CMS telehealth coverage requirements; state medical practice laws for cross-state virtual care; PCI-DSS SAQ A for Stripe-hosted card fields
compliance_context.rationale: PHI handling, telehealth regulation, and card payment compliance

project.goal: On-demand urgent care telemedicine platform with real-time video and payment processing
project.target_users: Patients and on-call physicians
project.timeline: Q3 pilot, Q4 scale

frontend.state: Zustand for queue state, React Query for server state

backend.api: REST + WebSocket for real-time queue updates

security.auth: Cognito user pools
security.payments: Stripe with Stripe.js hosted fields for PCI scope reduction

reasoner_reviews.product: Patient queue entry with real-time wait estimate is the core anxiety-reducing feature for this platform. Sub-30-second queue-to-video launch is a key UX metric. Physician encounter dashboard managing concurrent virtual visits needs clear state indication per encounter.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Next.js 15 App Router on Vercel pairs well with an Express/Node backend on Fargate. Redis sorted sets are the right data structure for the priority queue with wait time calculation. Twilio Video rooms created server-side with access tokens keeps the video infrastructure behind the backend auth wall.
reasoner_reviews.security: PCI-DSS scope is contained to Stripe.js hosted card fields; our backend never sees raw card numbers, achieving SAQ A eligibility. HIPAA BAA with AWS covers Aurora and ECS. Cognito MFA must be enforced for physicians specifically. Encounter notes must not transit through Twilio Video chat; use the backend API only.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: ECS Fargate with 3 tasks minimum handles estimated 200 concurrent video sessions at launch. ElastiCache Redis r6g.large sufficient for queue state at this scale. Vercel Pro plan required for HIPAA BAA.

frontend.app_structure: Next.js 15 App Router: app/patient (queue entry, waiting room, video session), app/physician (encounter dashboard, active video, note completion), app/shared (auth, layout)
frontend.pages_and_flows: Patient Queue Entry: chief complaint form + insurance/payment info -> Stripe Payment Element for copay -> queue confirmation with real-time wait time SSE updates -> video room auto-launch. Physician Dashboard: active encounter cards with video join, queue depth indicator, encounter note completion form, prescription routing.
frontend.state_management: Zustand queue store subscribing to SSE endpoint for wait time updates; React Query for encounter data fetching; Twilio Video React SDK participant state managed in local component state
frontend.frontend_risks: Twilio Video SDK WebRTC negotiation failure rate on restrictive corporate networks; implement TURN server fallback via Twilio Network Traversal Service

backend.service_design: Express modules: queue (virtual queue management with Redis sorted set), video (Twilio Video room creation and access token generation), encounters (CRUD for notes, diagnoses, prescriptions), payments (Stripe payment intent creation and webhook handling), auth (Cognito JWT verification middleware)
backend.api_patterns: REST API for encounters and payments; WebSocket via Socket.io for real-time queue position updates to physician dashboard; SSE for patient wait time polling
backend.background_jobs: Queue timeout worker: scans Redis for patients waiting >45 minutes and triggers queue abandonment notification. Stripe webhook handler: processes payment.succeeded and payment.failed events to update encounter payment_status.
backend.failure_handling: Twilio Video room creation failure falls back to Twilio Programmable Video retry with exponential backoff. Stripe payment intent creation failure returns 402 to patient with user-friendly message and queue position preserved.

security.auth_design: Cognito user pools with patient and physician app clients; physician client enforces MFA TOTP; Cognito JWTs verified in Express middleware on all routes
security.authorization_model: Role claim from Cognito JWT: patient routes filter to authenticated patient's own encounters; physician routes require physician role claim; no cross-patient data access
security.audit_and_logging_controls: CloudTrail for AWS API calls; custom audit table encounter_access_log for all PHI read events with physician_id, patient_id, encounter_id, and timestamp
security.security_risks: Encounter note content must not be logged in Express request logging middleware; use log scrubbing for /encounters routes

data.entities: patients (id, cognito_sub, first_name, last_name, dob_encrypted, insurance_member_id_encrypted). virtual_queue_entries (id, patient_id, chief_complaint, estimated_wait_minutes, status ENUM waiting/in_session/completed/abandoned, joined_at). video_sessions (id, queue_entry_id, physician_id, twilio_room_sid, started_at, ended_at, duration_seconds). encounter_notes (id, video_session_id, physician_id, patient_id, chief_complaint_text, assessment, plan, diagnosis_codes ICD10[], status ENUM draft/finalized). payment_transactions (id, queue_entry_id, stripe_payment_intent_id, amount_cents, currency, status ENUM pending/succeeded/failed, created_at).
data.storage_design: Aurora PostgreSQL 16 with KMS CMK for PHI tables; Redis sorted set virtual_queue with member=queue_entry_id and score=joined_at_unix for FIFO ordering; Stripe as source of truth for payment state

devops.deployment_topology: Vercel Pro for Next.js frontend with HIPAA BAA; ECS Fargate for Express backend (3 min tasks, 10 max); ElastiCache Redis r6g.large; Aurora PostgreSQL multi-AZ in us-east-1
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: eslint + tsc, jest unit tests, docker build and push to ECR, ECS deploy with rolling update, Vercel deploy triggered by Vercel GitHub integration
devops.environments: dev (ECS single task, Aurora single AZ), staging (ECS 2 tasks, Aurora multi-AZ), production (ECS 3-10 tasks autoscale, Aurora multi-AZ with read replica)
devops.observability_stack: Datadog APM sidecar on ECS Fargate; custom metrics: queue_depth, video_session_success_rate, payment_processing_latency_p99, encounter_note_completion_time; PagerDuty alerts on queue_depth >100 or video_success_rate <95%
