previous_plan.title: Telemedicine Urgent Care Platform - Round 1 Architecture
previous_plan.executive_summary: Real-time telemedicine platform with Next.js 14 frontend and Node.js Express backend for urgent care virtual visits.
previous_plan.technology_stack: Frontend: Next.js 14 with TypeScript, Pages Router, Twilio Video SDK. Backend: Node.js 22 Express with TypeScript. Auth: custom JWT service with RSA-256 signing.
previous_plan.security_and_compliance: Custom JWT service issues signed tokens for patients and physicians. TLS 1.3. PHI encrypted at rest. Stripe for payments.
previous_plan.api_design: POST /auth/token for JWT issuance. POST /payments/charge sends card token to Stripe from backend.

target_output.thinking_summary: Round 2 revision with three contract violations to fix: ISS-001 (Next.js 14 vs 15), ISS-002 (custom JWT vs Cognito), and ISS-003 (PCI scope). The plan quality is intentionally flawed - while I am addressing the focus issues in the fix_report, the revised plan will still contain a subtle internal inconsistency: the deployment_and_operations section will reference the Vercel frontend as requiring no HIPAA BAA while the contract security_baseline explicitly requires a Vercel Pro HIPAA BAA, creating a compliance gap that an auditor should flag.

fix_report.action_taken: Replaced all Next.js 14 Pages Router references with Next.js 15 App Router patterns throughout technology_stack, functional_feature_map, and workflows sections.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Plan technology stack matches contract frontend_stack specification enabling correct implementation.

fix_report.action_taken: Removed custom JWT issuance service; replaced with AWS Cognito user pools using patient and physician app clients with Cognito JWT verification in Express middleware via aws-jwt-verify library.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Auth implementation matches contract access_model Cognito specification and eliminates custom key management overhead.

fix_report.action_taken: Specified Stripe.js Payment Element hosted card fields so raw card numbers never reach the backend; backend only creates PaymentIntent and receives PaymentIntent ID from frontend, achieving PCI SAQ A scope.
fix_report.expected_outcome: PCI-DSS scope reduced to SAQ A; no card data handled in backend code or stored in Aurora.

target_output.title: Telemedicine Urgent Care Virtual Visit Platform Architecture
target_output.executive_summary: A Next.js 15 App Router and Node.js Express full-stack telemedicine platform on AWS ECS Fargate enabling patients to join on-demand urgent care video queues, pay copays via Stripe, and complete WebRTC video encounters with on-call physicians. Cognito user pools authenticate both patient and physician roles. Aurora PostgreSQL stores encounter notes and PHI with KMS encryption. Redis sorted sets manage the real-time virtual queue. Twilio Video handles WebRTC room creation and TURN server fallback.
target_output.architecture_overview: Next.js 15 App Router deployed on Vercel communicates over HTTPS to an Express API on ECS Fargate. Patients enter the virtual queue via the Next.js patient flow; the backend maintains queue position in a Redis sorted set and pushes wait time updates via SSE. When a physician accepts the next patient, the backend creates a Twilio Video room and returns access tokens to both participants. Cognito user pools issue JWTs verified by aws-jwt-verify in Express middleware. Stripe.js Payment Element collects copay card data client-side; the backend creates a PaymentIntent and receives only the ID. Aurora PostgreSQL with KMS CMK stores encounter notes, diagnoses, and patient PHI. Note: Vercel deployment does not have a HIPAA BAA configured, which creates a compliance gap for any PHI in Next.js server actions or edge functions.
target_output.technology_stack: Frontend: Next.js 15 with TypeScript, App Router, Tailwind CSS, Zustand for queue state, React Query, @twilio/video React hooks, Stripe.js Payment Element. Backend: Node.js 22, Express with TypeScript, aws-jwt-verify for Cognito JWT validation, Twilio Video Node SDK, Stripe Node SDK. Database: Aurora PostgreSQL 16 with KMS CMK, ElastiCache Redis 7. Infrastructure: ECS Fargate, ALB, ECR, Cognito user pools, CloudWatch Logs, Datadog APM sidecar.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Patient Queue Entry: Next.js 15 App Router page with chief complaint multi-select, insurance lookup, and Stripe Payment Element for copay pre-authorization. Virtual Waiting Room: SSE-driven real-time wait time counter with queue position. WebRTC Video Session: Twilio Video room with camera/mic controls, participant tiles, and in-session chat for non-PHI messages. Physician Encounter Dashboard: active encounter cards showing patient chief complaint, queue depth badge, one-click video join. Encounter Note Completion: structured SOAP note form with ICD-10 autocomplete, plan text, and prescription routing dropdown. After-Visit Summary: automated generation on note finalization with patient email delivery.
target_output.system_components: Next.js 15 App Router on Vercel Pro (HIPAA BAA status unclear in this revision). Express API on ECS Fargate (3 min, 10 max instances) behind ALB with WAF. Cognito user pools (patient and physician app clients, physician MFA TOTP enforced). ElastiCache Redis r6g.large for virtual queue sorted set and session tokens. Aurora PostgreSQL 16 multi-AZ with KMS CMK. Twilio Video rooms for WebRTC. Stripe for copay payment processing with hosted card fields. Datadog APM sidecars on ECS tasks.
target_output.workflows: Patient Queue Workflow: Patient completes chief complaint form, Stripe Payment Element pre-authorizes copay, POST /queue-entries creates queue entry in Redis sorted set with score=joined_at_unix; SSE stream /queue/wait-time pushes updated estimates every 30 seconds; physician accepts next patient triggering Twilio Video room creation via POST /video-sessions; Cognito access tokens exchanged for Twilio Video access tokens; video session auto-launches in browser. Encounter Completion Workflow: Physician completes SOAP note with ICD-10 codes and plan, POST /encounter-notes with finalized=true triggers after-visit summary generation, Stripe captures pre-authorized payment, note status updated to finalized.
target_output.data_model: patients (id UUID PK, cognito_sub TEXT, first_name, last_name, dob_encrypted BYTEA, insurance_member_id_encrypted BYTEA, created_at). virtual_queue_entries (id UUID PK, patient_id FK, chief_complaint TEXT[], status ENUM waiting/in_session/completed/abandoned, payment_intent_id TEXT, joined_at TIMESTAMPTZ). video_sessions (id UUID PK, queue_entry_id FK, physician_id FK, twilio_room_sid TEXT, started_at TIMESTAMPTZ, ended_at TIMESTAMPTZ). encounter_notes (id UUID PK, video_session_id FK, physician_id FK, patient_id FK, subjective TEXT, objective TEXT, assessment TEXT, plan TEXT, diagnosis_codes TEXT[], status ENUM draft/finalized, finalized_at TIMESTAMPTZ). payment_transactions (id UUID PK, queue_entry_id FK, stripe_payment_intent_id TEXT, amount_cents INT, status ENUM pending/captured/failed).
target_output.api_design: REST API at /api/v1. POST /queue-entries (patient joins queue; body: chief_complaint[], payment_intent_id). GET /queue/wait-time (SSE stream; physician_count, estimated_wait_minutes). POST /video-sessions (backend creates Twilio room; returns twilio_access_token for patient and physician). POST /encounter-notes (physician creates note). PATCH /encounter-notes/{id}/finalize (triggers after-visit summary and Stripe capture). GET /physician/queue (active queue for physician dashboard). All routes require Cognito JWT in Authorization header.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Cognito user pools with physician app client enforcing MFA TOTP; patient app client with optional MFA. aws-jwt-verify library validates Cognito JWTs in Express middleware. Stripe.js Payment Element hosted card fields: backend creates PaymentIntent via Stripe API, returns client_secret to frontend, Stripe.js confirms payment client-side - raw card numbers never reach Express API, achieving PCI SAQ A. Aurora KMS CMK encrypts PHI at rest. TLS 1.3 on ALB. WAF rules blocking SQLi and XSS on ALB. HIPAA BAA with AWS covers Aurora and ECS. Note: Vercel HIPAA BAA not confirmed for this deployment - Next.js server actions or middleware touching PHI would fall outside HIPAA BAA coverage.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions: eslint + tsc + jest, docker build and push to ECR, ECS rolling deploy to staging, integration smoke tests, manual gate, ECS rolling deploy to production. Vercel deploys triggered by GitHub push via Vercel GitHub integration with no HIPAA configuration applied. ECS Fargate autoscale on ALB RequestCountPerTarget >500 per task. Aurora multi-AZ with automated backups. ElastiCache Redis cluster mode disabled (single node r6g.large) for simplicity. Secrets in AWS Secrets Manager with 30-day Stripe webhook secret rotation.
target_output.observability: Datadog APM sidecar on each ECS task with distributed traces. Custom metrics: virtual_queue_depth (alert >100), video_session_success_rate (alert <95%), payment_capture_success_rate (alert <99%), encounter_note_completion_time_p90. CloudWatch Logs for ECS stdout. PagerDuty escalation for all production alerts.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: ECS Fargate 3 min tasks at $0.04048/vCPU/hr: ~$200/month baseline. Aurora db.r6g.large multi-AZ: ~$400/month. ElastiCache r6g.large: ~$150/month. Twilio Video: ~$400/month at 500 daily sessions (0.004/min * 10 min avg * 500). Stripe fees pass-through to encounter billing. Vercel Pro: ~$20/month. Total ~$1300/month well under $3000 budget.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (8 weeks): Patient queue entry with chief complaint and Stripe copay pre-auth, physician dashboard with queue view, Twilio Video session launch, basic SOAP note, Cognito auth. Phase 2 (4 weeks): ICD-10 autocomplete, after-visit summary email, Stripe capture on note finalization, encounter history. Phase 3 (4 weeks): Prescription routing integration, payer claim submission, physician performance analytics dashboard.
target_output.development_guidelines: TypeScript strict mode on frontend and backend. ESLint with @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any enforced in CI. Encounter note content must not appear in Express request logs; use log scrubbing middleware on /encounter-notes routes. Stripe webhook signature verification required in payment handler. Cognito JWT claims must be the sole source of role authorization; no role stored in DB. PHI fields in Aurora must use encrypted column types.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Vercel deployment without HIPAA BAA exposes PHI if Next.js server actions or edge middleware process encounter data. Mitigation incomplete in this revision - PHI access in Next.js server components must be audited. Risk: Twilio Video TURN server dependency for patients on restrictive networks; mitigated by Twilio Network Traversal Service automatic fallback. Tradeoff: ECS Fargate over Lambda for WebSocket connection persistence; Lambda cold starts incompatible with sub-second queue update requirements.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Confirmed Twilio Video over Amazon Chime per existing Twilio vendor relationship. Confirmed Stripe SAQ A eligibility via Stripe.js hosted fields - backend never handles raw card data. Auth provider corrected to Cognito per contract. Next.js version corrected to 15 App Router per contract.

metadata.notes: Intentionally flawed: fix_report addresses three ISS violations but revised plan introduces Vercel HIPAA BAA gap flagged in architecture_overview and security sections, teaching auditor to catch new compliance gaps introduced during revision.

project_goal.value: Modernize a 15-year-old Delphi-based clinical nutrition management desktop application used by hospital dietitians to calculate enteral and parenteral nutrition formulas, track feeding tolerances, and document nutritional assessments
project_goal.rationale: Replace aging Delphi codebase with maintainable modern stack

target_users.value: Registered dietitians and clinical nutrition support teams in hospital inpatient settings
target_users.rationale: Primary end users identified in discovery sessions

project_class.rationale: Electron desktop app deployed to dietitian workstations within hospital network

capabilities.rationale: Desktop app with backend API, EHR integration, and auth

complexity_level.rationale: Legacy replacement with domain-specific formula calculation logic

risk_level.rationale: Handles patient PHI and clinical nutrition orders that influence patient care

data_sensitivity.rationale: Nutritional assessments, feeding orders, and anthropometric measurements are PHI

external_exposure.rationale: Deployed on hospital intranet workstations only

access_model.value: Azure Active Directory SSO with dietitian and supervisor roles
access_model.rationale: Hospital Azure AD used for all intranet applications

feature_scope.value: Nutritional assessment documentation, caloric and protein requirements calculation using Harris-Benedict and Mifflin-St Jeor equations, enteral and parenteral formula recommendation, feeding tolerance tracking, EHR lab data import, feeding order routing to prescribing physician
feature_scope.rationale: Full clinical nutrition workflow from legacy system

mvp_scope.value: Nutritional assessment form, Harris-Benedict caloric calculation, basic enteral formula recommendation, and Azure AD login
mvp_scope.rationale: Core assessment and calculation workflow for pilot

security_baseline.value: Azure AD SSO with MFA, TLS 1.3 for API calls, PHI encrypted at rest in Azure Database for PostgreSQL with Azure Disk Encryption, no PHI in Electron process logs
security_baseline.rationale: HIPAA minimum controls for hospital intranet desktop app

frontend_stack.value: Electron with React 18 and TypeScript
frontend_stack.rationale: Electron provides desktop packaging for workstation deployment; React for UI consistency

backend_stack.value: Python 3.12 with FastAPI
backend_stack.rationale: FastAPI for nutrition calculation API; Python has strong scientific computing libraries for formula validation

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 on Azure Database for PostgreSQL
data_platform.rationale: Relational data for nutrition assessments and formula records

hosting_target.value: Azure Virtual Machines for FastAPI backend; Electron app distributed via SCCM to hospital workstations
hosting_target.rationale: Hospital IT standard for intranet application deployment

privacy_retention_policy.value: Nutritional assessment records retained 7 years per state health records law; feeding orders retained as part of medical record
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Healthcare data retention requirements

future_scope.value: Parenteral nutrition compounding integration with pharmacy, automated refeeding syndrome risk scoring, dietitian caseload dashboard
future_scope.rationale: Year 2 roadmap items

constraints.value: Must run on Windows 10/11 workstations; offline mode required for brief network interruptions; budget under $1500/month
constraints.rationale: Hospital workstation and network constraints

observability_baseline.value: Azure Monitor for VM health and API availability; basic structured logging from FastAPI
observability_baseline.rationale: Minimal observability acceptable for internal intranet tool

execution_preference.value: GitHub Actions with SCCM-based Electron installer distribution
execution_preference.rationale: Hospital IT requires SCCM for workstation software management

compliance_context.value: HIPAA Privacy and Security Rules; Joint Commission nutrition care standards; ASPEN clinical nutrition practice guidelines for formula accuracy
compliance_context.rationale: PHI handling and clinical nutrition care standards compliance

project.goal: Modernize Delphi clinical nutrition desktop app for hospital dietitians
project.target_users: Registered dietitians and nutrition support teams
project.timeline: Q3 MVP pilot, Q4 full rollout

frontend.state: Zustand for patient context and form state
frontend.packaging: electron-builder for Windows installer via SCCM

backend.api: REST API internal to hospital network

security.auth: Azure AD SSO via MSAL in Electron

data.offline: Electron local SQLite cache for brief offline operation

reasoner_reviews.product: The core nutrition assessment + formula calculation loop is the right MVP focus. Offline mode for brief network interruptions is critical for ICU dietitian workflows. EHR lab import saves significant manual data entry time for each assessment.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Electron with React 18 is appropriate for a Windows workstation deployment. SQLite local cache for offline mode is a reasonable approach given brief interruption requirements. FastAPI on Azure VM handles the nutrition calculation API; the formula calculation logic should be in Python backend service classes validated against ASPEN reference values.
reasoner_reviews.security: Azure AD SSO with MSAL in Electron main process is the correct approach. PHI must not appear in Electron main process console logs. Local SQLite cache must be encrypted using Electron safeStorage API.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: Azure VM Standard_B2s at $35/month handles the modest dietitian concurrent usage. PostgreSQL Flexible Server basic tier sufficient. SCCM distribution of Electron installer aligns with hospital IT requirements.

frontend.app_structure: Electron app with main process (Azure AD MSAL auth, local SQLite cache sync, IPC handlers) and renderer process (React 18 SPA with feature routes for assessments, formulas, feeding tolerance, EHR import)
frontend.pages_and_flows: Patient Search (MRN lookup or EHR sync). Nutritional Assessment form with auto-populated labs from EHR. Formula Calculator with Harris-Benedict and Mifflin-St Jeor tabs. Feeding Tolerance log with trend chart. Feeding Order routing screen.
frontend.state_management: Zustand store: PatientContextStore (active patient MRN, imported labs), AssessmentFormStore (multi-section form draft state), OfflineSyncStore (pending uploads when offline)
frontend.frontend_risks: Electron safeStorage API for SQLite encryption uses OS credential store; must be tested on Windows 10 and 11 separately due to OS keychain differences

backend.service_design: FastAPI modules: assessments (CRUD for nutritional_assessments), formula_calculator (Harris-Benedict, Mifflin-St Jeor, protein calculation endpoints), feeding_tolerance (feeding_tolerance_records CRUD), ehr_proxy (HL7 lab result fetch from hospital integration engine)
backend.api_patterns: REST API at /api/v1; Azure AD JWT verification via azure-ad-fastapi middleware; all endpoints require dietitian or supervisor role claim
backend.failure_handling: EHR lab fetch failures return cached lab values if available with stale_data flag; feeding order submission failures queued in Electron local SQLite for retry on reconnect

security.auth_design: MSAL.js in Electron main process acquires Azure AD token silently on app launch; token passed to FastAPI as Bearer in all requests
security.authorization_model: Dietitian role: read/write own assessments and any patient in assigned caseload. Supervisor role: read all assessments for audit.
security.audit_and_logging_controls: FastAPI structured logs to Azure Monitor for PHI access events: assessment_accessed, feeding_order_submitted with dietitian_id and patient_mrn_hash

data.entities: patients (id, mrn_hash, display_name_encrypted, dob_encrypted, admission_unit). nutritional_assessments (id, patient_id, dietitian_id, height_cm, weight_kg, bmi, assessment_date, diagnosis_notes, caloric_requirement_kcal, protein_requirement_g). feeding_orders (id, assessment_id, formula_name, rate_ml_hr, route ENUM ng/nj/peg/tpn, ordered_by_physician_id, status ENUM draft/submitted/active). feeding_tolerance_records (id, patient_id, recorded_at, gastric_residual_ml, stool_output, nausea_grade, notes). anthropometric_measurements (id, patient_id, measurement_date, weight_kg, muac_cm, calf_circumference_cm).
data.storage_design: Azure Database for PostgreSQL 16 with Azure Disk Encryption for PHI tables. Electron local SQLite database encrypted via safeStorage for offline caching of active patient assessments.

devops.deployment_topology: FastAPI on Azure VM Standard_B2s behind Azure Application Gateway in hospital VNet; PostgreSQL on Azure Database for PostgreSQL Flexible Server in same VNet; Electron installer built by GitHub Actions and delivered via SCCM package to dietitian workstations
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: pylint + pytest for FastAPI, npm test for Electron renderer, electron-builder for Windows NSIS installer, upload artifact to SCCM distribution point via Azure File Share copy
devops.environments: dev (local docker-compose), staging (Azure VM with test PostgreSQL), production (Azure VM with HA PostgreSQL)
devops.observability_stack: Azure Monitor VM metrics for CPU and memory. FastAPI structured JSON logs to Azure Log Analytics. Basic alert on API 5xx error rate >2%.

ISS-001.title: Offline mode SQLite encryption not specified
ISS-001.detail: Local SQLite cache containing patient PHI must be encrypted; round 1 plan did not specify encryption mechanism.

ISS-002.title: Nutrition formula calculation validation not specified
ISS-002.detail: ASPEN compliance requires formula calculation results to be validated against reference values; no validation or unit test specification for Harris-Benedict and Mifflin-St Jeor implementations.

ISS-003.title: EHR integration HL7 message type not specified
ISS-003.detail: EHR lab import section did not specify which HL7 message types are used to fetch lab results from the hospital integration engine.

focus_issues.title: Nutrition formula calculation validation not specified
focus_issues.detail: No validation or unit test specification for Harris-Benedict and Mifflin-St Jeor calculation implementations.

previous_plan.title: Clinical Nutrition Desktop Application - Round 2 Architecture
previous_plan.executive_summary: Electron and React 18 desktop replacement for Delphi clinical nutrition app on Azure VM, with FastAPI backend for formula calculations and Azure AD SSO.
previous_plan.technology_stack: Electron with React 18, TypeScript, Zustand. FastAPI Python 3.12. PostgreSQL 16 on Azure. Electron safeStorage for SQLite cache encryption.

target_output.thinking_summary: Round 3 revision targeting ISS-002: adding FormulaCalculatorService pytest suite with ASPEN reference case validation. This is a weak quality row per spec - most sections meet minimum word counts but are thin on specifics. The fix_report correctly addresses the focus issue but the overall plan lacks depth in cost_and_scaling, development_guidelines, and observability.

fix_report.action_taken: Added FormulaCalculatorService Python class with pytest unit test suite validating Harris-Benedict REE calculation against ASPEN reference case (male, 70kg, 175cm, age 45: expected 1724 kcal/day) and Mifflin-St Jeor equation against same reference case (expected 1695 kcal/day); tests run as required CI check in GitHub Actions.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Nutrition formula calculations validated against clinical reference values in CI, ensuring ASPEN compliance for every code change.

target_output.title: Clinical Nutrition Management Desktop Application Architecture
target_output.executive_summary: An Electron and React 18 desktop application deployed via SCCM to hospital dietitian workstations, replacing a 15-year-old Delphi nutrition management system. FastAPI backend on Azure VM handles nutritional assessment storage, Harris-Benedict and Mifflin-St Jeor caloric calculations, and EHR lab data import via HL7 ORU R01. Azure AD SSO authenticates dietitians. Local SQLite cache encrypted via Electron safeStorage enables brief offline operation.
target_output.architecture_overview: Electron desktop app runs on Windows 10/11 dietitian workstations within the hospital intranet. The Electron main process handles Azure AD MSAL token acquisition and manages a local SQLite database encrypted via safeStorage for offline caching of active patient assessments. The React 18 renderer process communicates with the FastAPI backend on an Azure VM via TLS 1.3 REST API calls using the Azure AD Bearer token. FastAPI connects to Azure Database for PostgreSQL for persistent storage of nutritional assessments, formula records, and feeding tolerance logs. EHR lab data is imported via HL7 ORU R01 messages fetched from the hospital integration engine endpoint.
target_output.technology_stack: Desktop: Electron 30 with React 18, TypeScript, Zustand, electron-builder for Windows NSIS installer. Backend: Python 3.12, FastAPI, SQLAlchemy 2.0, Pydantic v2, azure-ad-fastapi for JWT verification. Database: Azure Database for PostgreSQL 16 with Azure Disk Encryption. Local cache: better-sqlite3 with Electron safeStorage encryption. CI/CD: GitHub Actions with pytest and electron-builder. Distribution: SCCM package pointing to Azure File Share artifact.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Nutritional Assessment module with multi-section form capturing weight, height, BMI, relevant diagnoses, and lab values imported from EHR. Formula Calculator computing Harris-Benedict and Mifflin-St Jeor resting energy expenditure with activity and injury factor multipliers. Enteral Formula Recommendation based on caloric target, fiber tolerance, and diagnosis flags. Feeding Tolerance log capturing gastric residual volumes, stool output, and nausea scores per nursing shift. Feeding Order routing screen generating order recommendation for prescribing physician review. Anthropometric measurements tracker for weight trend and MUAC monitoring.
target_output.system_components: Electron desktop client on hospital workstations. Electron safeStorage encrypted local SQLite for offline patient assessment cache. FastAPI on Azure VM Standard_B2s behind Azure Application Gateway in hospital VNet. Azure Database for PostgreSQL 16 Flexible Server. Azure AD for SSO and role-based access. Hospital HL7 integration engine endpoint for lab data import. GitHub Actions for CI and electron-builder artifact generation. SCCM for workstation installer distribution.
target_output.workflows: Nutritional Assessment Workflow: Dietitian opens patient by MRN, app fetches latest labs via HL7 ORU R01 from integration engine and populates assessment form, dietitian enters anthropometric measurements and clinical observations, formula calculator computes caloric and protein requirements, dietitian selects enteral formula and rate, generates feeding order recommendation routed to prescribing physician as draft order. Feeding Tolerance Round Workflow: Dietitian opens daily tolerance view, app pulls gastric residual volumes and stool output from nursing flowsheet import, dietitian documents tolerance status and adjusts formula rate or composition if indicated, updates saved to PostgreSQL and queued to offline SQLite if network unavailable.
target_output.data_model: patients (id UUID PK, mrn_hash TEXT, display_name_encrypted TEXT, dob_encrypted TEXT, admission_unit TEXT). nutritional_assessments (id UUID PK, patient_id FK, dietitian_id TEXT, height_cm NUMERIC, weight_kg NUMERIC, bmi NUMERIC, caloric_requirement_kcal NUMERIC, protein_requirement_g NUMERIC, assessment_date DATE, notes TEXT). nutrition_diagnoses (id UUID PK, assessment_id FK, diagnosis_code TEXT, diagnosis_label TEXT). feeding_orders (id UUID PK, assessment_id FK, formula_name TEXT, rate_ml_hr NUMERIC, route ENUM ng/nj/peg/tpn, status ENUM draft/submitted/active). feeding_tolerance_records (id UUID PK, patient_id FK, recorded_at TIMESTAMPTZ, gastric_residual_ml NUMERIC, nausea_grade SMALLINT, stool_output TEXT, notes TEXT). anthropometric_measurements (id UUID PK, patient_id FK, measurement_date DATE, weight_kg NUMERIC, muac_cm NUMERIC).
target_output.api_design: REST at /api/v1. GET /patients/{mrn}/labs (fetch HL7 lab import). POST /assessments (create nutritional assessment). GET /assessments/{id} (retrieve with formula results). POST /formula/calculate (body: weight_kg, height_cm, age, sex, activity_factor, injury_factor; returns kcal_harris_benedict, kcal_mifflin_stjour, protein_g). POST /feeding-orders (create draft order). POST /feeding-tolerance (log tolerance record). Azure AD Bearer JWT required on all routes.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Azure AD SSO with MSAL in Electron main process; MFA enforced via Azure AD Conditional Access policy. Azure AD JWT verified by azure-ad-fastapi middleware on FastAPI. All PHI queries filtered by authenticated dietitian caseload assignment. Local SQLite cache encrypted via Electron safeStorage using OS credential store. TLS 1.3 enforced on Application Gateway. Azure Disk Encryption on PostgreSQL VM and database. PHI fields must not appear in FastAPI structured log messages. Azure Monitor audit events for assessment_accessed and feeding_order_submitted with dietitian_id and patient_mrn_hash. HIPAA BAA with Azure covers PostgreSQL and VM.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions pipeline: pylint + mypy, pytest with FormulaCalculatorService ASPEN validation suite as required check, electron-builder Windows NSIS installer build, upload to Azure File Share SCCM distribution point. Azure VM Standard_B2s with Ubuntu 22.04 running FastAPI via systemd service. Azure Application Gateway in hospital VNet routes HTTPS to FastAPI. PostgreSQL Flexible Server in same VNet with private endpoint. SCCM distributes Electron installer to dietitian workstation OU on release tag. Rollback via SCCM previous installer version redeployment.
target_output.observability: Azure Monitor VM metrics for CPU and memory on FastAPI host. FastAPI structured JSON logs to Azure Log Analytics with custom query for feeding_order_submitted rate and assessment_accessed count per day. Azure Monitor alert on API 5xx rate >2% and VM CPU >85%. Electron renderer process errors reported to Azure Application Insights via applicationinsights-node SDK. No specific SLA dashboards in this revision.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Azure VM Standard_B2s $35/month. PostgreSQL Flexible Server Basic $60/month. Application Gateway WAF_v2 $180/month. Total ~$280/month, under $1500 budget. Scaling limited to vertical VM resize if dietitian concurrency grows beyond 30 simultaneous users.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (8 weeks): Azure AD login, patient search by MRN, nutritional assessment form, Harris-Benedict caloric calculation, basic enteral formula recommendation, PostgreSQL persistence. Phase 2 (4 weeks): HL7 ORU R01 lab import from EHR integration engine, Mifflin-St Jeor formula, offline SQLite cache with safeStorage encryption. Phase 3 (4 weeks): Feeding tolerance log, feeding order routing to physician, anthropometric measurement tracker.
target_output.development_guidelines: Python strict type annotations with mypy in CI. FormulaCalculatorService pytest suite must validate Harris-Benedict output against ASPEN reference case (male 70kg 175cm age 45 expected 1724 kcal/day) and Mifflin-St Jeor against same case (expected 1695 kcal/day) as required CI check. Electron security hardening: contextIsolation true, nodeIntegration false, sandbox true in BrowserWindow. PHI must not appear in FastAPI log message bodies. Electron safeStorage must be used for all local SQLite writes containing patient data.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: SCCM deployment delays from hospital IT approval cycles could extend release timelines by weeks. Mitigation: stage releases in SCCM test collection before production OU deployment. Risk: Electron safeStorage OS keychain behavior differs between Windows 10 and Windows 11 DPAPI versions. Mitigation: test installer on both OS versions in CI using Windows Server 2019 and 2022 GitHub Actions runners. Tradeoff: Azure VM over Azure Container Apps for FastAPI to maintain hospital VNet private endpoint connectivity without public endpoint exposure.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Confirmed HL7 ORU R01 as the message type for EHR lab import via hospital Mirth Connect integration engine. Confirmed Electron safeStorage over node-keytar for SQLite encryption as safeStorage is built-in to Electron 30. Confirmed SCCM over Microsoft Intune for distribution due to hospital air-gap network policy.

metadata.notes: Round 3 revision for Delphi legacy modernization closing formula validation gap; weak quality with thin observability and cost sections as intended.

project_goal.rationale: Core clinical decision support objective to reduce triage variability and improve ED throughput

project.target_users: ED triage nurses and clinical informatics team

frontend.state: React Query for inference results, Zustand for triage session state

backend.api: RESTful with OpenAPI 3.0, versioned under /api/v1

open_questions.model_retraining_cadence: Determine weekly vs monthly retraining schedule based on pilot drift data

reasoner_reviews.product: The dual-model ESI recommendation approach addresses the core clinical need. Nurse override capture is critical for feedback loop integrity and SaMD post-market surveillance. The performance dashboard must surface confidence intervals not just point estimates to support clinical governance.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: ONNX Runtime on GPU Fargate tasks is the right call for sub-300ms inference. The ensemble fusion layer needs a well-defined fallback: if either model returns an error or exceeds timeout, the system must degrade gracefully to recommendation_unavailable status without blocking triage encounter creation. Redis for session caching reduces duplicate inference calls when nurses navigate back to the same encounter.
reasoner_reviews.security: PHI isolation between inference and training pipelines is the highest security priority. The inference endpoint must never log raw chief complaint text in application logs - only de-identified token embeddings for debugging. Cognito MFA for all roles including read-only clinical informatics users is non-negotiable given SaMD classification.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: The $3000/month budget is tight for GPU Fargate tasks. Architecture should use GPU instances only for the inference service and standard Fargate for API routing and UI serving. Auto-scaling the GPU task to zero during low-census overnight periods can reduce costs significantly.
reasoner_reviews.critic: Round 1 missed specifying the de-identification pipeline for training data preparation. The audit trail for nurse overrides must be append-only and include the original AI recommendation alongside the nurse-selected level to satisfy FDA SaMD post-market requirements.

backend.service_design: Three FastAPI services: InferenceService (GPU Fargate) handling ONNX model loading and ensemble fusion; TriageAPIService (standard Fargate) handling encounter lifecycle and nurse override recording; ModelManagementService handling version promotion and S3 artifact registry
backend.api_patterns: POST /api/v1/triage/predict accepts structured vitals and chief complaint text, returns ESI recommendation 1-5 with confidence distribution and explanation tokens; PATCH /api/v1/triage/:encounter_id/override records nurse decision with mandatory override reason code and original AI recommendation
backend.failure_handling: Circuit breaker on InferenceService: if GPU task unresponsive for 2 consecutive health checks, TriageAPIService returns recommendation_unavailable status without blocking encounter creation; dead-letter queue for failed prediction audit log writes
backend.scaling_notes: InferenceService auto-scales on SQS depth for async path and direct latency metric for sync path; scale to zero after 30 min idle during 0200-0600 census trough to control GPU task costs
backend.background_jobs: Nightly drift evaluation job comparing AI ESI vs nurse-confirmed ESI by model version; nightly de-identification job processing encounter archive through AWS Comprehend Medical before writing to restricted S3 training corpus bucket

frontend.app_structure: React SPA with route-based code splitting: /triage (intake and recommendation display), /encounters (encounter queue with react-window virtualization), /dashboard (model performance metrics), /admin (model version management)
frontend.pages_and_flows: Triage Intake: structured vitals form with chief complaint free-text field, submit triggers POST /predict, recommendation card displays ESI 1-5 with color coding, confidence probability bar, and top-3 ClinicalBERT attention tokens per ONC CDS transparency requirement. Override Flow: nurse selects different ESI with mandatory dropdown reason code; both AI and nurse ESI recorded in submission payload.
frontend.performance_notes: Recommendation card renders within 50ms of API response using React.memo; chief complaint textarea debounced 300ms before enabling submit; encounter queue virtualized with react-window for high-census EDs

security.auth_design: Cognito user pool with HIPAA-eligible configuration, MFA enforced for all four roles; Cognito hosted UI redirect for SSO compatibility with hospital IdP
security.authorization_model: triage_nurse: read/write triage encounters and overrides for own shift; charge_nurse: read all encounters and approve model version promotions; clinical_informatics: read model metrics and prediction audit log; admin: full CRUD on model versions and user management
security.audit_and_logging_controls: Append-only prediction_audit_log table records encounter_id, model_version, predicted_esi, confidence_json, nurse_esi, override_reason, nurse_id, timestamp; CloudTrail captures all S3 model artifact reads; application logs strip PHI fields before Datadog forwarding
security.incident_response_notes: If prediction accuracy drops below 80% on overnight drift report, PagerDuty page to clinical_informatics on-call; automatic model version rollback to last confirmed-good version if nurse override rate exceeds 40% in any 4-hour window

data.entities: triage_encounters (id UUID, patient_mrn_encrypted, arrival_timestamp, chief_complaint_text, vitals_json, encounter_status). model_predictions (id UUID, encounter_id FK, model_version_id FK, predicted_esi INT, confidence_json, explanation_tokens_json, inference_latency_ms). nurse_overrides (id UUID, encounter_id FK, original_esi INT NOT NULL, confidence_json NOT NULL, nurse_esi INT, override_reason ENUM, nurse_id FK, override_at). model_versions (id UUID, model_name, version_tag, artifact_s3_key, status ENUM draft/staging/production/deprecated, accuracy_at_promotion)
data.storage_design: PostgreSQL 16 RDS Multi-AZ for operational tables; S3 model-artifacts bucket for ONNX files; S3 training-corpus bucket with bucket policy restricting writes to de-id job IAM role only; S3 prediction-audit bucket with object lock (WORM) for SaMD trail
data.retention_and_deletion: triage_encounters retained 7 years with archival to S3 Glacier at 2 years; prediction audit logs retained 3 years in S3 Standard-IA; de-identified training corpora retained indefinitely in S3 Glacier Deep Archive

devops.deployment_topology: us-east-1: ALB with WAF -> TriageAPIService (ECS Fargate standard, 2-6 tasks) -> InferenceService (ECS Fargate GPU, 0-3 tasks with scale-to-zero); ModelManagementService (1 Fargate task, clinical_informatics access only)
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: mypy + ruff lint -> pytest 85% coverage gate including inference integration tests with mock ONNX models -> build three Docker images -> push ECR -> deploy TriageAPIService rolling update -> integration tests against staging -> manual SaMD validation gate -> deploy InferenceService GPU task definition
devops.observability_stack: Datadog APM sidecar on all ECS tasks; custom metrics: inference_latency_p95, esi_distribution_by_level, override_rate_by_shift, model_accuracy_7d_rolling; Datadog dashboard for clinical informatics
devops.rollback_strategy: Model rollback via ModelManagementService API promoting previous version; service rollback via ECS task definition previous revision; database rollback via RDS point-in-time recovery to pre-migration snapshot

previous_plan.title: ED Triage AI Acuity Scoring System - Round 1
previous_plan.executive_summary: AI-assisted triage acuity scoring using ClinicalBERT and XGBoost ensemble on ONNX Runtime, serving ESI recommendations to triage nurses via React 18 on ECS Fargate GPU tasks with PostgreSQL 16.
previous_plan.architecture_overview: Triage nurses submit structured vitals and chief complaint text to FastAPI inference endpoint; ONNX Runtime runs ClinicalBERT and XGBoost in parallel, fuses predictions, returns ESI 1-5 with confidence. React 18 SPA displays recommendation with override capture.
previous_plan.data_model: triage_encounters (id, patient_mrn_encrypted, chief_complaint_text, vitals_json). model_predictions (id, encounter_id, model_version, predicted_esi, confidence, latency_ms). nurse_overrides (id, encounter_id, nurse_esi, override_reason, nurse_id) - missing original_esi and confidence columns.

target_output.thinking_summary: Round 2 revision addresses two high-severity issues. ISS-001: adding AWS Comprehend Medical PHI de-identification pipeline before encounter data writes to S3 training corpus bucket, enforced by IAM policy restricting bucket writes to de-id job role. ISS-002: extending nurse_overrides table with original_esi INT and confidence_json JSONB populated from the linked model_predictions row at override time, giving FDA post-market surveillance the full AI recommendation context. Also addressing ONC CDS transparency by returning explanation_tokens from ClinicalBERT attention in the predict response and rendering them in the triage UI recommendation card.

fix_report.action_taken: Added nightly AWS Comprehend Medical de-identification pipeline step that processes encounter archive, replaces PHI spans with surrogate tokens ([PATIENT], [DATE], [PROVIDER]), and writes de-identified records to a restricted S3 training-corpus bucket. IAM policy on the bucket denies writes from all principals except the de-id job execution role. Documented in workflows, data_model, deployment_and_operations, and security_and_compliance.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Retraining corpus preparation is now HIPAA-compliant and satisfies the explicit no-PHI-in-training-payloads constraint. Training jobs consume only de-identified encounter text.

fix_report.action_taken: Extended nurse_overrides table with original_esi INT NOT NULL and confidence_json JSONB NOT NULL columns populated from the linked model_predictions row at override submission time. Updated PATCH /triage/:id/override endpoint to read original prediction before writing override record. Updated data_model, api_design, and workflows sections.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Override audit trail now captures both AI recommendation and nurse decision for every override event, enabling FDA SaMD post-market outcome analysis and override pattern investigation by the clinical informatics team.

target_output.title: ED Triage AI Acuity Scoring System
target_output.executive_summary: A HIPAA-compliant AI clinical decision support system for emergency department triage, using a ClinicalBERT and XGBoost ensemble served via ONNX Runtime on ECS Fargate GPU tasks, delivering ESI acuity recommendations to triage nurses within 300 ms. The system captures nurse overrides with full original AI recommendation context for FDA SaMD post-market surveillance, runs a PHI de-identification pipeline before retraining corpus preparation via AWS Comprehend Medical, and surfaces ClinicalBERT attention tokens in the triage UI per ONC CDS transparency requirements.
target_output.architecture_overview: Three-service ECS Fargate architecture: TriageAPIService (standard Fargate, 2-6 tasks) handles encounter lifecycle, nurse override capture, and session management behind ALB with WAF; InferenceService (GPU Fargate, 0-3 tasks with scale-to-zero during 0200-0600 census trough) loads ClinicalBERT ONNX and XGBoost artifacts from S3 at startup and serves parallel inference requests; ModelManagementService (standard Fargate, 1 task) handles version promotion and artifact registry. PostgreSQL 16 RDS Multi-AZ stores triage_encounters, model_predictions, and nurse_overrides. A nightly background job submits encounter archive batches to AWS Comprehend Medical PHI detection, replaces identified spans with surrogate tokens, and writes de-identified text to a restricted S3 training-corpus bucket enforced by IAM bucket policy. React 18 SPA served via CloudFront connects to TriageAPIService over TLS 1.3.
target_output.technology_stack: Frontend: React 18, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, React Query for inference result fetching, Zustand for triage session state, react-window for encounter queue virtualization. Backend: Python 3.12, FastAPI async, ONNX Runtime 1.17 with ClinicalBERT and XGBoost models, Pydantic v2 validation. Inference Models: ClinicalBERT (BioBERT fine-tuned on MIMIC triage notes, ONNX export), XGBoost 2.0 tabular model for structured vitals (spo2, hr, rr, sbp, gcs). Data: PostgreSQL 16 RDS Multi-AZ, Redis 7 ElastiCache for inference session deduplication, S3 (model artifacts, training corpus, prediction audit WORM). Infra: ECS Fargate standard and GPU task definitions, ALB, WAF, CloudFront, Cognito HIPAA-eligible pool, KMS CMK, AWS Comprehend Medical, CloudTrail.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Triage Intake Module: structured vitals form (SpO2, HR, RR, SBP, temperature, GCS) with chief complaint free-text; submit triggers POST /triage/predict. ESI Recommendation Display: color-coded ESI 1-5 badge, confidence probability bar across all five levels, top-3 ClinicalBERT attention tokens shown as clinical explanation per ONC CDS transparency mandate. Nurse Override Workflow: dropdown ESI selector with mandatory override reason code (acuity_underestimated, acuity_overestimated, insufficient_data, clinical_gestalt, other); original AI recommendation and confidence stored alongside nurse decision. Model Performance Dashboard: 7-day rolling accuracy by model version, override rate trend by shift, ESI distribution comparison chart AI vs nurse-confirmed. Model Version Management: clinical informatics uploads new ONNX artifacts, charge_nurse approves promotion from staging to production.
target_output.system_components: CloudFront (static SPA assets), ALB with WAF (SQLi/XSS rules, rate limit 200 req/min per Cognito user), TriageAPIService (ECS Fargate 2-6 tasks, Python FastAPI), InferenceService (ECS Fargate GPU 0-3 tasks, ONNX Runtime with ClinicalBERT + XGBoost), ModelManagementService (ECS Fargate 1 task), PostgreSQL 16 RDS Multi-AZ (triage_encounters, model_predictions, nurse_overrides, model_versions), Redis 7 ElastiCache (inference session dedup cache), S3 model-artifacts bucket (ONNX model files), S3 training-corpus bucket (de-identified encounter text, write-restricted IAM policy), S3 prediction-audit bucket (object lock WORM for SaMD trail), Cognito HIPAA-eligible user pool, KMS CMK (patient_mrn encryption), AWS Comprehend Medical (de-id pipeline), CloudTrail (all API and S3 artifact access events).
target_output.workflows: ESI Recommendation Flow: Nurse submits triage form with vitals JSON and chief complaint text to POST /triage/encounters/:id/predict. TriageAPIService checks Redis for duplicate encounter hash, forwards to InferenceService. InferenceService runs ClinicalBERT ONNX tokenization and XGBoost scoring in parallel asyncio tasks, fuses via weighted softmax ensemble (BERT weight 0.6, XGBoost 0.4), returns predicted_esi 1-5 with confidence_json and top attention explanation tokens. TriageAPIService writes model_predictions row, returns recommendation within 300ms p95 SLA. Nurse Override Flow: Nurse selects different ESI level and mandatory reason code, submits PATCH /triage/:id/override. API reads original_esi and confidence_json from linked model_predictions row, writes nurse_overrides record with both AI and nurse decisions; override rate metric incremented in Datadog. PHI De-identification Pipeline: Nightly EventBridge-triggered ECS task queries encounter archive for records older than 30 days, batches chief_complaint_text to AWS Comprehend Medical PHI API, replaces [PATIENT] [DATE] [PROVIDER] spans in result, writes de-identified records to restricted S3 training-corpus bucket under de-id job IAM role only.
target_output.data_model: triage_encounters (id UUID PK, patient_mrn_encrypted TEXT KMS, arrival_timestamp TIMESTAMPTZ, chief_complaint_text TEXT, vitals_json JSONB {spo2 INT, hr INT, rr INT, sbp INT, temp DECIMAL, gcs INT}, encounter_status ENUM pending/recommended/overridden/finalized, assigned_nurse_id UUID FK, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). model_predictions (id UUID PK, encounter_id UUID FK UNIQUE, model_version_id UUID FK, predicted_esi INT CHECK(1-5), confidence_json JSONB {esi_1..esi_5 FLOAT}, explanation_tokens_json JSONB, inference_latency_ms INT, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). nurse_overrides (id UUID PK, encounter_id UUID FK UNIQUE, original_esi INT NOT NULL, confidence_json JSONB NOT NULL, nurse_esi INT CHECK(1-5), override_reason ENUM acuity_underestimated/acuity_overestimated/insufficient_data/clinical_gestalt/other, nurse_id UUID FK, override_at TIMESTAMPTZ). model_versions (id UUID PK, model_name TEXT, version_tag TEXT, artifact_s3_key TEXT, promoted_at TIMESTAMPTZ, promoted_by UUID FK, status ENUM draft/staging/production/deprecated, accuracy_at_promotion DECIMAL).
target_output.api_design: POST /api/v1/triage/encounters (create encounter, returns encounter_id and status). POST /api/v1/triage/encounters/:id/predict (trigger inference; returns {predicted_esi, confidence_json, explanation_tokens, inference_latency_ms}). PATCH /api/v1/triage/encounters/:id/override (body {nurse_esi, override_reason}; reads original_esi+confidence from model_predictions, writes nurse_overrides). GET /api/v1/triage/encounters?status=pending&shift=current (nurse queue, cursor-paginated). GET /api/v1/models/versions (list model versions with status and accuracy_at_promotion). POST /api/v1/models/versions/:id/promote (charge_nurse role only; transitions version to production status). GET /api/v1/analytics/performance?model_version_id=&window_days=7 (returns accuracy, override_rate, esi_distribution for clinical informatics dashboard). All endpoints require Cognito JWT Bearer; roles enforced per operation by FastAPI dependency.
target_output.security_and_compliance: HIPAA controls: KMS CMK encryption for patient_mrn_encrypted at rest, TLS 1.3 for all service-to-service and client-to-ALB communication, Cognito MFA enforced for all four roles with HIPAA-eligible pool configuration, CloudTrail logging all S3 model artifact reads and Cognito authentication events, append-only prediction audit records in S3 object-lock bucket. PHI de-identification enforcement: AWS Comprehend Medical pipeline with surrogate replacement runs before any data reaches S3 training-corpus bucket; IAM bucket policy denies writes from all principals except the de-id ECS task execution role. SaMD compliance: ONC CDS transparency satisfied by returning explanation_tokens in predict response and rendering top-3 attention tokens in triage UI recommendation card; nurse override audit trail stores both original AI ESI and nurse ESI for FDA SaMD post-market outcome analysis. WAF on ALB blocks SQLi and XSS; rate limit 200 req/min per Cognito identity.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions CI pipeline: mypy strict + ruff lint -> pytest with 85% coverage gate including inference integration tests with serialized mock ONNX fixtures -> build three Docker images (TriageAPIService, InferenceService, ModelManagementService) -> push to ECR -> deploy TriageAPIService rolling update to staging -> run integration test suite with anonymized encounter fixtures -> manual SaMD validation gate requiring charge_nurse and clinical_informatics sign-off -> deploy InferenceService GPU task definition with blue-green swap. Environments: dev (single AZ, CPU-only mock inference via ONNX model stubs), staging (multi-AZ, real GPU InferenceService, anonymized encounter fixtures), production (multi-AZ us-east-1a/1b, ALB with WAF, GPU InferenceService scales 0-3 tasks on p95 latency metric, TriageAPIService scales 2-6 tasks on CPU 60% threshold). InferenceService scale-to-zero: EventBridge schedules ECS desired count to 0 at 0200; warm-up trigger on first ALB request restores task within 45 seconds. RDS credentials in AWS Secrets Manager with 30-day rotation. Alembic migrations run as one-off ECS task at deploy time.
target_output.observability: Datadog APM sidecar on all ECS tasks with distributed trace propagation between TriageAPIService and InferenceService calls. Custom Datadog metrics: inference_latency_p95 (monitor: alert if >250ms sustained 5 min, SLA at 300ms), esi_recommendation_distribution (track shift in ESI level frequencies as early drift signal), nurse_override_rate (alert if >40% in any 4-hour rolling window), model_accuracy_7d_rolling (alert if <80%). Clinical informatics Datadog dashboard: real-time override rate by shift, ESI distribution comparison AI vs nurse-confirmed, accuracy trend line by model_version_id. PagerDuty: clinical_informatics on-call for accuracy alerts; DevOps on-call for latency SLA breach; automatic model rollback trigger if override rate alert persists >30 minutes.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: GPU Fargate inference task (~g4dn.xlarge equivalent at $1.20/hr) scaled 0-3 tasks: approximately $600/month at typical ED census with scale-to-zero overnight saving ~$280/month. Standard Fargate for TriageAPIService and ModelManagementService approximately $200/month. RDS Multi-AZ PostgreSQL 16 approximately $400/month. ElastiCache Redis, S3 (three buckets), CloudFront, AWS Comprehend Medical API calls approximately $250/month. Total estimated $1,450-$1,850/month within the $3,000 budget. Multi-ED scaling path: add Fargate tasks horizontally per ED; add RDS read replica for analytics query isolation from transactional load.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 Clinical Pilot MVP (10 weeks): triage intake form with vitals and chief complaint, dual-model ONNX inference endpoint returning ESI and explanation tokens, nurse override capture with original AI recommendation stored, AWS Comprehend Medical de-identification pipeline for training corpus, Cognito MFA RBAC, HIPAA KMS encryption, SaMD validation documentation, GitHub Actions CI/CD pipeline. Phase 2 Monitoring and Scale (4 weeks): model performance dashboard for clinical informatics, Datadog custom metrics and PagerDuty escalation rules, InferenceService auto-scale with overnight scale-to-zero, Redis inference session deduplication. Phase 3 Health System Rollout (6 weeks): multi-ED Fargate deployment, HL7 FHIR ADT feed integration for automatic encounter pre-population from EDIS, model version management UI with charge_nurse approval gate, automated monthly retraining pipeline from de-identified corpus.
target_output.development_guidelines: Python 3.12 with strict mypy type checking enforced in CI. Ruff for linting with no-ignore policy on security rules. pytest with 85% coverage gate; inference tests use serialized ONNX model fixtures to avoid GPU dependency in CI. ONNX model updates require staging accuracy validation run achieving >80% accuracy before production promotion gate opens. All PHI column writes use KMS-encrypted fields; Datadog sensitive data scanner rule configured to reject log lines containing MRN or DOB patterns. ADR documents required for any change to model architecture, ensemble weights, or override schema. PRs touching inference logic require clinical informatics review sign-off.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: InferenceService GPU task cold start during overnight scale-to-zero takes ~45 seconds, potentially violating 300ms latency SLA on first post-census encounter. Mitigation: suppress PagerDuty latency alert during 0200-0600 window; maintain single warm task on ED high-census nights based on admission forecast. Risk: AWS Comprehend Medical PHI detection false negatives leaving residual PHI in de-identified training corpus. Mitigation: secondary regex pass for MRN format (8-digit numeric) and DOB patterns before corpus write; training-corpus bucket write restricted to de-id job role by IAM policy. Tradeoff: ONNX Runtime over TorchServe reduces memory footprint and cold-start time at the cost of a more complex model export and quantization pipeline. Tradeoff: PostgreSQL over a specialized vector database for explanation token storage is sufficient at single-ED scale but will require re-evaluation at multi-ED deployment volumes.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Selected AWS Comprehend Medical over Presidio for de-identification due to better clinical entity coverage (MRN, provider names in ED note conventions). Confirmed ONNX Runtime over TorchServe based on memory footprint and cold-start benchmarks on g4dn.xlarge. Model version promotion confirmed to require dual sign-off from charge_nurse and clinical_informatics per SaMD governance policy. Monthly retraining cadence selected based on expected concept drift rate from pilot data analysis.

metadata.notes: Round 2 revision resolving HIPAA de-id pipeline gap and SaMD override audit trail for ED triage AI acuity scoring system.

profile.domain: Hospital IT Security - Active Directory Governance

project_goal.value: Continuously audit and remediate misconfigured privileged service accounts in Active Directory, targeting clinical application service accounts with excessive permissions such as Domain Admin membership or missing password expiry
project_goal.rationale: Reduce attack surface from over-privileged clinical application service accounts exploitable in lateral movement attacks

target_users.value: Hospital IT security team, Active Directory administrators, CISO and compliance team
target_users.rationale: Primary operators running remediations and secondary consumers reviewing audit findings and compliance reports

project_class.rationale: Batch-driven AD enumeration and remediation pipeline with admin control interface; not a hosted web platform

capabilities.rationale: AD integration via PyAD and PowerShell, batch audit jobs, remediation pipeline, and admin review interface

complexity_level.rationale: AD enumeration and policy evaluation are well-understood patterns; dual-approval workflow adds governance complexity

risk_level.rationale: Tool operates with elevated AD read permissions and executes privileged remediation actions; misoperation could disrupt clinical applications

data_sensitivity.rationale: Stores privilege assignments, AD group membership, and service account credentials metadata for clinical systems

external_exposure.rationale: Tool runs within hospital Azure tenant; no external network access required

access_model.value: Azure AD RBAC: security_analyst (read audit findings, approve low-risk remediations), ad_admin (execute remediations, manage baselines), ciso (read all reports, override escalation holds), service_account (tool execution identity with scoped AD read permissions)
access_model.rationale: Least-privilege access model required by CIS Controls v8 and internal security policy

feature_scope.value: Nightly AD service account enumeration, privilege baseline policy evaluation, risk scoring, dual-approval remediation workflow, signed PowerShell execution, immutable SIEM audit trail, admin findings dashboard, compliance report generation
feature_scope.rationale: End-to-end coverage from discovery through remediation with audit trail for SOC 2 and HIPAA compliance evidence

mvp_scope.value: Nightly enumeration and baseline policy evaluation, risk-scored findings report, manual approval workflow for password rotation and group membership removal, SIEM audit trail integration
mvp_scope.rationale: Minimum for security team to begin addressing highest-risk service accounts immediately

security_baseline.value: Tool execution identity uses scoped AD read-only service account; PowerShell remediation scripts signed with hospital code-signing certificate; all remediation actions require dual human approval before execution; audit trail written to Azure Sentinel immutable workspace
security_baseline.rationale: Privileged operation safety controls to prevent unauthorized or accidental remediation of clinical application accounts

frontend_stack.value: None; admin panel delivered as a lightweight Flask web interface for internal use
frontend_stack.rationale: No frontend framework needed; simple server-rendered admin panel sufficient for IT security team internal tool

backend_stack.value: Python 3.12 with PyAD for LDAP queries and PowerShell subprocess integration via pywinrm
backend_stack.rationale: PyAD provides clean Python AD interface; PowerShell integration required for signed script execution on domain controllers

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 on Azure Database for PostgreSQL for operational data; Azure Sentinel workspace for immutable audit trail
data_platform.rationale: PostgreSQL for audit findings and workflow state; Sentinel for SIEM integration and immutable change log

hosting_target.value: Azure Virtual Machines within hospital Azure tenant (us-east region); no external network egress required
hosting_target.rationale: Must operate within hospital Azure tenant boundary; VM hosting ensures AD network connectivity

privacy_retention_policy.value: Audit findings retained 7 years for SOC 2 and HIPAA audit evidence; remediation action logs retained indefinitely in Azure Sentinel; service account metadata purged 90 days after account deletion
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: SOC 2 Type II evidence retention and HIPAA Security Rule access control audit requirements

future_scope.value: Extension to Azure AD Privileged Identity Management integration for JIT access governance; automated change ticket creation in ServiceNow; cross-forest enumeration for M&A integration scenarios
future_scope.rationale: Hospital security maturity roadmap beyond initial AD service account remediation

constraints.value: All remediation scripts must be signed by hospital code-signing certificate; dual human approval required before any AD change; tool must not store AD service account passwords in plaintext; execution window limited to 0100-0400 maintenance window for batch remediation
constraints.rationale: Safety constraints for privileged AD operations in a clinical environment where account disruption could impact patient care systems

observability_baseline.value: Azure Monitor for VM health and job execution metrics; Azure Sentinel for SIEM alerting on unauthorized remediation attempts or policy evaluation failures
observability_baseline.rationale: Sentinel already deployed as hospital SIEM; Azure Monitor native for Azure VM workloads

execution_preference.value: Iterative delivery with security team validation at each phase; no CI deployment to production without security lead sign-off
execution_preference.rationale: High-risk privileged tool requires security review at every delivery milestone

compliance_context.value: HIPAA Security Rule Section 164.312(a)(1) access controls; NIST SP 800-53 AC-6 least privilege; CIS Controls v8 Control 5 (account management); SOC 2 Type II CC6.1 and CC6.2 access management; hospital change management policy requiring CAB approval for AD changes
compliance_context.rationale: Multiple overlapping compliance frameworks apply to privileged account management in a healthcare environment

project.goal: Automated nightly audit and dual-approval remediation of over-privileged AD service accounts for clinical applications
project.target_users: Hospital IT security team and AD administrators
project.timeline: Q2 MVP for highest-risk account remediation, Q3 full automation

backend.framework: PyAD LDAP queries, pywinrm for signed PowerShell execution, Flask admin panel
backend.api: Internal REST endpoints for admin panel only

security.auth: Azure AD authentication for admin panel; tool execution identity is scoped read-only AD service account
security.encryption: TLS 1.3 for all internal communication; no plaintext credential storage; signed PowerShell scripts
security.compliance: HIPAA, NIST SP 800-53 AC-6, CIS Controls v8, SOC 2 Type II

data.siem: Azure Sentinel immutable workspace for audit trail

devops.hosting: Azure Virtual Machines within hospital tenant
devops.environments: dev, staging, production (all within hospital Azure tenant)

constraints.signing: All PowerShell scripts signed by hospital code-signing cert
constraints.approval: Dual human approval required before any AD change execution

open_questions.baseline_scope: Confirm whether baseline policy covers Azure AD cloud-only accounts or on-premises AD only

reasoner_reviews.product: The nightly enumeration and risk-scoring approach is well-suited to the operational cadence of a hospital security team. The dual-approval workflow is essential but needs a clear escalation path when approvers are unavailable during the maintenance window - a timed holdover mechanism should prevent remediations from expiring and re-queuing unnecessarily.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: PyAD over raw LDAP is the right abstraction for the enumeration layer. The privilege baseline policy engine should be externally configurable (YAML or database-driven) rather than hardcoded, allowing the security team to update rules without code deployments. The signed PowerShell execution via pywinrm needs explicit error handling for WinRM connectivity failures mid-remediation to prevent partial changes.
reasoner_reviews.security: The most critical gap in round 1 was insufficient detail on the dual-approval workflow integrity. The approval state must be stored in the database with approver identity and timestamp, not just a boolean flag, to provide the audit evidence required by SOC 2 CC6.2. PowerShell script signing verification should occur at execution time on the target DC, not just at script generation time.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: The 0100-0400 execution window constraint is manageable for batch operations but the admin panel must clearly indicate which accounts have pending remediations outside the window with estimated next execution time. The Azure DevOps pipeline must gate production deployments on security lead sign-off using environment protection rules.

backend.service_design: Three Python modules: ADEnumerator (PyAD LDAP queries for service account discovery and attribute collection), PolicyEvaluator (configurable baseline policy engine scoring each account against privilege rules), RemediationExecutor (pywinrm signed PowerShell dispatch with dual-approval gate and rollback capability)
backend.api_patterns: Internal Flask REST API: GET /api/findings (paginated audit findings with risk scores), POST /api/approvals/:finding_id (first and second approver sign-off), POST /api/remediations/:finding_id/execute (trigger RemediationExecutor after dual approval confirmed), GET /api/reports/compliance (SOC 2 evidence report generation)
backend.failure_handling: WinRM connectivity failure during remediation: log partial execution state to change_tickets table, halt remaining steps, page AD admin via Azure Monitor alert. Dual-approval timeout: finding marked approval_expired after 72 hours, re-queued in next nightly findings batch. PostgreSQL write failure: remediation blocked until database connectivity restored.
backend.background_jobs: Nightly enumeration job (0100 start): PyAD LDAP query for all service accounts with SPN or service account OU membership, attribute collection (password_last_set, memberOf, delegations, spns), risk scoring, findings upsert to PostgreSQL. Weekly compliance report job: aggregate resolved and unresolved findings by risk level and application, generate SOC 2 evidence PDF.

security.auth_design: Azure AD authentication for Flask admin panel via MSAL with OAuth2 authorization code flow; tool execution identity is a dedicated read-only AD service account with LDAP query permissions only; RemediationExecutor impersonates a separate privileged AD service account stored in Azure Key Vault, retrieved per execution and immediately discarded
security.authorization_model: security_analyst role: view findings and add first approval; ad_admin role: view findings, add either approval, and trigger execution; ciso role: read-only access to all findings and compliance reports with escalation override capability
security.secrets_management: Privileged remediation service account credentials stored in Azure Key Vault with access policy allowing only RemediationExecutor managed identity; PowerShell code-signing certificate stored in Key Vault with no export permission
security.audit_and_logging_controls: Every remediation action written to change_tickets table (approver_1_id, approver_1_at, approver_2_id, approver_2_at, executed_by, executed_at, ps_script_hash, outcome) and forwarded to Azure Sentinel Log Analytics workspace as immutable CEF events; policy evaluation results logged to audit_findings table with policy_version and evaluated_at

data.entities: service_accounts (id UUID, sam_account_name, distinguished_name, associated_application, password_last_set, password_expires, spns_json, group_memberships_json, last_enumerated_at, risk_score DECIMAL). audit_findings (id UUID, service_account_id FK, policy_rule_id FK, severity ENUM critical/high/medium/low, finding_type ENUM excessive_privilege/password_expiry/stale_account/misconfigured_spn, detail TEXT, status ENUM open/pending_approval/remediated/accepted, found_at, resolved_at). remediation_actions (id UUID, finding_id FK, action_type ENUM password_rotation/group_removal/delegation_removal, approver_1_id UUID, approver_1_at, approver_2_id UUID, approver_2_at, executed_at, ps_script_hash TEXT, outcome ENUM success/failed/partial, error_detail TEXT). risk_scores (id UUID, service_account_id FK, score DECIMAL, score_components_json, computed_at).
data.storage_design: PostgreSQL 16 Azure Database for PostgreSQL for operational tables with connection pooling via PgBouncer; Azure Sentinel Log Analytics workspace for immutable change event stream; Azure Key Vault for remediation credentials and code-signing certificate
data.retention_and_deletion: audit_findings and remediation_actions retained 7 years in PostgreSQL with archival to Azure Blob cold tier at 2 years; service_account metadata purged 90 days after last_enumerated_at when account no longer exists in AD; Sentinel workspace retention set to indefinite

devops.deployment_topology: Two Azure VMs: EnumerationVM (runs nightly enumeration and policy evaluation jobs, read-only AD access) and RemediationVM (isolated VM with privileged AD service account access, accessible only from EnumerationVM and admin panel via private Azure VNet); Flask admin panel runs on EnumerationVM, accessible only from IT security VLAN
devops.ci_cd_design: Azure DevOps pipeline: bandit security lint + pytest 80% coverage -> build Python wheel -> deploy to staging VM -> security lead manual approval gate in Azure DevOps environment protection -> deploy to production EnumerationVM; RemediationVM deployments require separate CAB ticket approval per hospital change management policy
devops.observability_stack: Azure Monitor VM health metrics (CPU, disk, network) with alerts for job execution failures; Azure Sentinel SIEM alerts for unauthorized admin panel access attempts, remediation execution outside maintenance window, and policy evaluation error rate spikes
devops.rollback_strategy: Remediation rollback script generated alongside every change action; rollback scripts stored in Azure Blob with immutable retention policy and referenced in change_tickets; manual rollback via ad_admin role triggers rollback PowerShell execution with same dual-approval gate

ISS-001.title: Dual-approval audit trail insufficient for SOC 2 CC6.2 evidence
ISS-001.detail: Round 1 approval workflow stored a boolean approved flag without capturing approver identities or timestamps. SOC 2 CC6.2 requires evidence of who approved each privileged action and when, not just that approval occurred.

history.note: Identified in security review; approval table schema missing approver_1_id, approver_1_at, approver_2_id, approver_2_at columns

ISS-002.title: Policy baseline engine hardcoded; cannot update rules without redeployment
ISS-002.detail: Round 1 described policy evaluation as hardcoded Python logic, requiring a code deployment to add or update privilege baseline rules. Security team needs to adjust thresholds (e.g., password age limits, allowed group memberships) without engineering involvement.

history.note: Noted in architect_reasoner review; policy rules should be database-driven or YAML configurable

focus_issues.title: Dual-approval audit trail insufficient for SOC 2 CC6.2 evidence
focus_issues.detail: Approval records must capture approver identity and timestamp, not just boolean flag.

focus_issues.title: Policy baseline engine hardcoded; cannot update without redeployment
focus_issues.detail: Baseline rules must be database-driven or YAML-configurable for security team self-service.

latest_recommendations: Extend remediation_actions table with approver_1_id, approver_1_at, approver_2_id, approver_2_at columns for SOC 2 CC6.2 evidence
Implement database-driven policy rule table (policy_rules) allowing security team to add, update, and version privilege baseline rules through admin panel without code deployments
Add ps_script_hash to remediation_actions for PowerShell signing verification evidence
revision_memory.latest_plan_title: Hospital AD Privileged Account Remediation Tool - Round 1

convergence_state.reason: One high and one medium severity issue unresolved; score 7.1 near but below passing threshold

previous_audits.summary: Solid architecture for AD enumeration and remediation with correct dual-VM isolation design, but approval audit trail lacks required SOC 2 identity evidence and policy engine is not configurable without code changes.
strengths: Dual-VM isolation (EnumerationVM vs RemediationVM) correctly limits privileged AD access surface
Azure Key Vault for remediation credentials with managed identity retrieval is the right secrets design
Nightly enumeration job with risk scoring matches operational cadence of hospital security team
concerns: Approval workflow records only a boolean flag without approver identities or timestamps, failing SOC 2 CC6.2 audit evidence requirement
Policy evaluation rules hardcoded in Python; security team cannot adjust thresholds without engineering deployment
blocking_issues: remediation_actions table missing approver_1_id, approver_1_at, approver_2_id, approver_2_at - SOC 2 CC6.2 evidence gap
recommendations: Extend remediation_actions schema with full approver identity and timestamp columns for both approvals
Introduce policy_rules table in PostgreSQL with admin panel CRUD, allowing security team to manage baseline rules without code deployment
