previous_plan.title: Hospital AD Privileged Account Remediation Tool - Round 1
previous_plan.executive_summary: Python automation tool on Azure VMs for nightly AD service account enumeration, privilege baseline evaluation, and dual-approval remediation via signed PowerShell scripts, with PostgreSQL for findings storage and Azure Sentinel for SIEM integration.
previous_plan.architecture_overview: EnumerationVM runs nightly PyAD LDAP scan of all service accounts, evaluates against hardcoded privilege baseline rules, generates risk-scored findings in PostgreSQL. Dual-approval workflow gates RemediationVM execution of signed PowerShell scripts.
previous_plan.data_model: service_accounts (id, sam_account_name, risk_score, last_enumerated_at). audit_findings (id, service_account_id, severity, finding_type, status). remediation_actions (id, finding_id, action_type, approved BOOLEAN, executed_at, outcome) - missing approver identity columns.
previous_plan.security_and_compliance: Azure AD auth for admin panel, Key Vault for credentials, signed PowerShell scripts, dual-approval workflow. SOC 2 and HIPAA noted but approval audit trail does not capture approver identity or timestamp.

target_output.thinking_summary: Round 2 addresses ISS-001 by extending the remediation_actions schema with explicit approver identity and timestamp columns for both approvers, providing the SOC 2 CC6.2 audit evidence chain. ISS-002 is resolved by introducing a database-driven policy_rules table with version tracking, allowing the security team to add and update privilege baseline rules through the Flask admin panel without code deployments. PowerShell script hash stored in remediation_actions provides signing verification evidence for SOC 2.

fix_report.action_taken: Extended remediation_actions table with approver_1_id UUID, approver_1_at TIMESTAMPTZ, approver_2_id UUID, approver_2_at TIMESTAMPTZ, and ps_script_hash TEXT columns. Updated PATCH /api/approvals/:finding_id endpoint to capture requesting user identity from Azure AD token and store with timestamp on each approval step. Updated data_model, api_design, and security_and_compliance sections.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Every remediation action now records which two approvers signed off and when, providing complete SOC 2 CC6.2 audit evidence chain including approver identity, role, and approval timestamp.

fix_report.action_taken: Introduced policy_rules table in PostgreSQL with columns for rule_id, rule_name, finding_type, evaluation_logic_json, threshold_value, severity, active, version, and created_by. Flask admin panel now includes a Policy Rules CRUD interface restricted to ad_admin role, allowing security team to activate, deactivate, and version baseline rules without code deployments. PolicyEvaluator module updated to load active rules from database at job start rather than hardcoded constants.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Security team can add new privilege rules (e.g., flag accounts with AdminSDHolder attribute set unexpectedly) and adjust thresholds (e.g., password age from 90 to 60 days) through the admin panel, effective at next nightly enumeration run.

target_output.title: Hospital AD Privileged Service Account Remediation Tool
target_output.executive_summary: A security-hardened Python automation tool for hospital IT security teams running nightly Active Directory enumeration of clinical application service accounts, evaluating each against a database-driven privilege baseline policy engine, and executing approved remediations via hospital-code-signed PowerShell scripts with a full dual-approval audit trail satisfying SOC 2 CC6.2 and HIPAA Security Rule access control requirements. All remediation actions record approver identities with timestamps and PowerShell script hashes, forwarded as immutable CEF events to Azure Sentinel.
target_output.architecture_overview: Two-VM Azure architecture within the hospital tenant private VNet: EnumerationVM hosts the nightly PyAD LDAP enumeration job, database-driven PolicyEvaluator, and Flask admin panel accessible only from the IT security VLAN. RemediationVM is isolated with privileged AD service account access, reachable only from EnumerationVM over the private VNet for signed PowerShell execution via pywinrm. PostgreSQL 16 on Azure Database for PostgreSQL stores service_accounts, audit_findings, remediation_actions (with full approver identity chain), and policy_rules. Azure Key Vault holds remediation service account credentials and the code-signing certificate, retrieved per execution via managed identity and never persisted in memory beyond the execution session. All remediation events stream to Azure Sentinel as immutable CEF records.
target_output.technology_stack: Backend: Python 3.12, PyAD 0.6 for LDAP queries, pywinrm 0.4 for signed PowerShell dispatch, Flask 3.0 for admin panel, SQLAlchemy 2.0 ORM. Admin Panel: Flask with Jinja2 server-rendered templates, Bootstrap 5, MSAL Python for Azure AD OAuth2. Data: PostgreSQL 16 Azure Database for PostgreSQL, PgBouncer for connection pooling. Secrets: Azure Key Vault with managed identity access. SIEM: Azure Sentinel Log Analytics workspace (CEF connector). CI/CD: Azure DevOps Pipelines. Monitoring: Azure Monitor + Azure Sentinel alerting.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Nightly AD Enumeration: PyAD LDAP query discovers all accounts with SPN or service account OU membership; attribute collector gathers password_last_set, memberOf groups, Kerberos delegations, and SPNs. Database-driven Policy Evaluation: PolicyEvaluator loads active policy_rules from PostgreSQL at job start, evaluates each account against rules (Domain Admin membership, password expiry enforcement, constrained vs unconstrained delegation, AdminSDHolder flag), assigns risk scores and severity-tagged findings. Dual-Approval Workflow: security_analyst adds first approval with identity captured from Azure AD token; ad_admin adds second approval; RemediationExecutor queues action for next maintenance window execution. Signed PowerShell Remediation: Password rotation, group membership removal, and delegation reconfiguration scripts retrieved from Key Vault, signed by hospital cert, dispatched via pywinrm, outcome and script hash recorded. Policy Rule Management: Admin panel CRUD for policy_rules with version history, activation toggle, and threshold editing restricted to ad_admin role. Compliance Reporting: Weekly SOC 2 evidence PDF aggregating remediation audit trail with approver identities.
target_output.system_components: EnumerationVM (Azure VM Standard_D2s_v3): nightly enumeration job, PolicyEvaluator, Flask admin panel (IT security VLAN only), Azure Monitor agent. RemediationVM (Azure VM Standard_D2s_v3, isolated subnet): RemediationExecutor service, pywinrm WinRM client, Key Vault managed identity for credential retrieval. Azure Database for PostgreSQL (General Purpose, zone-redundant HA): service_accounts, audit_findings, remediation_actions, policy_rules tables with PgBouncer. Azure Key Vault (premium tier for HSM-backed cert): remediation service account password, PowerShell code-signing certificate, Flask secret key. Azure Sentinel Log Analytics workspace: immutable CEF audit event stream. Azure Active Directory: admin panel OAuth2 authentication, EnumerationVM managed identity, RemediationVM managed identity. Azure DevOps: CI/CD pipeline with environment protection gates.
target_output.workflows: Nightly Enumeration and Scoring (0100 start): EventBridge-equivalent Azure Automation schedule triggers enumeration job on EnumerationVM. PyAD LDAP queries AD for all accounts with servicePrincipalName attribute or membership in ServiceAccounts OU. For each account, attribute collector retrieves password_last_set, memberOf, msDS-AllowedToDelegateTo, and AdminSDHolder flag. PolicyEvaluator loads active policy_rules rows from PostgreSQL, evaluates each account, generates audit_finding records with severity and detail. Existing findings updated to current scan; new findings inserted with status=open. Risk score computed and stored in service_accounts.risk_score. Dual-Approval Remediation Workflow: security_analyst views findings dashboard, selects finding, clicks Approve (First); Flask records approver_1_id from Azure AD token and approver_1_at timestamp in remediation_actions row, status set to pending_second_approval. ad_admin provides second approval; approver_2_id and approver_2_at recorded, status set to approved, scheduled for next maintenance window. Remediation Execution (0200-0400 window): RemediationExecutor queries approved remediations, retrieves signed PowerShell script for action_type from Key Vault, verifies script hash matches stored ps_script_hash, dispatches via pywinrm to target DC. Outcome, error_detail, and executed_at written to remediation_actions. CEF event with full approver chain forwarded to Sentinel.
target_output.data_model: service_accounts (id UUID PK, sam_account_name TEXT UNIQUE, distinguished_name TEXT, associated_application TEXT, password_last_set TIMESTAMPTZ, password_never_expires BOOLEAN, spns_json JSONB, group_memberships_json JSONB, admin_sd_holder BOOLEAN, last_enumerated_at TIMESTAMPTZ, risk_score DECIMAL). audit_findings (id UUID PK, service_account_id UUID FK, policy_rule_id UUID FK, severity ENUM critical/high/medium/low, finding_type ENUM excessive_privilege/password_expiry/stale_account/misconfigured_spn/unconstrained_delegation, detail TEXT, status ENUM open/pending_approval/pending_execution/remediated/accepted_risk, found_at TIMESTAMPTZ, resolved_at TIMESTAMPTZ). remediation_actions (id UUID PK, finding_id UUID FK, action_type ENUM password_rotation/group_removal/delegation_removal/account_disable, approver_1_id UUID NOT NULL, approver_1_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL, approver_2_id UUID NOT NULL, approver_2_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL, executed_at TIMESTAMPTZ, ps_script_hash TEXT NOT NULL, outcome ENUM success/failed/partial, error_detail TEXT). policy_rules (id UUID PK, rule_name TEXT, finding_type ENUM, evaluation_logic_json JSONB, threshold_value TEXT, severity ENUM, active BOOLEAN, version INT, created_by UUID FK, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ).
target_output.api_design: GET /api/findings?severity=high&status=open&application= (paginated audit findings with risk scores and associated service account). POST /api/approvals/:finding_id/approve-first (security_analyst role; records approver_1_id from Azure AD token and approver_1_at). POST /api/approvals/:finding_id/approve-second (ad_admin role; records approver_2_id and approver_2_at, schedules for execution window). GET /api/remediations/:finding_id (remediation action detail including approver chain and outcome). GET /api/reports/compliance?from=&to= (SOC 2 evidence report with full approver audit trail). GET /api/policy-rules (list active and inactive policy rules with version history). POST /api/policy-rules (ad_admin; create new rule or new version of existing rule). PATCH /api/policy-rules/:id/toggle (ad_admin; activate or deactivate rule effective next enumeration run). All endpoints authenticated via Azure AD MSAL Bearer token.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Access controls: Azure AD OAuth2 MSAL authentication for all admin panel endpoints; four-role RBAC model enforced per Flask route; tool execution identity uses read-only LDAP service account; RemediationExecutor retrieves privileged credentials from Key Vault via managed identity per execution session only. Secrets: no AD credentials stored in application code or database; Key Vault HSM-backed storage with managed identity access policy. PowerShell signing: all remediation scripts signed with hospital HSM-backed code-signing certificate; script hash verified at execution time on RemediationVM by comparing against ps_script_hash in remediation_actions. Audit trail: every remediation_action row contains approver_1_id, approver_1_at, approver_2_id, approver_2_at (satisfying SOC 2 CC6.2 evidence chain); CEF events forwarded to Azure Sentinel immutable workspace; Sentinel alert rules fire on execution outside 0100-0400 window or consecutive WinRM failures. HIPAA Security Rule: AD service accounts associated with clinical applications are tagged; findings for those accounts flagged with hipaa_scope=true for compliance reporting priority.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Azure DevOps pipeline: bandit security lint + safety dependency check + pytest 80% coverage -> build Python wheel and package -> deploy to staging VM -> run integration tests with mock AD LDAP fixture and mock pywinrm -> security lead manual approval gate in Azure DevOps environment protection rule -> deploy to production EnumerationVM via SSH. RemediationVM deployments require separate CAB ticket per hospital change management policy; deployment runbook includes pre-deployment verification that Key Vault access policies are intact. Environments: dev (local VM with mock LDAP server via test containers), staging (Azure VM in non-production tenant with synthetic AD test data), production (hospital tenant VMs, real AD, Sentinel integration active). Maintenance window enforcement: Azure Automation schedule triggers RemediationExecutor only within 0100-0400 window; out-of-window execution requests logged as unauthorized attempts and trigger Sentinel alert.
target_output.observability: Azure Monitor VM metrics with alerts for enumeration job failures (CPU spike with no database writes within expected window) and RemediationVM connectivity loss. Azure Sentinel analytics rules: alert on admin panel authentication failures exceeding 5 in 10 minutes (brute force indicator), alert on remediation_actions records with executed_at outside 0100-0400 window (unauthorized execution), alert on PolicyEvaluator error rate > 10% of accounts in single run (indicating AD connectivity or LDAP query issue). Weekly findings summary email to CISO via Azure Logic Apps: open critical and high findings by clinical application, remediation completion rate, policy rule version changes in past week.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Two Standard_D2s_v3 Azure VMs at approximately $140/month each. Azure Database for PostgreSQL General Purpose (2 vCores) approximately $200/month. Azure Key Vault premium tier approximately $20/month. Azure Sentinel ingestion at estimated 1 GB/day approximately $100/month. Azure DevOps and monitoring approximately $50/month. Total estimated $650/month. Scaling path: enumeration job scales to larger AD forests by increasing PyAD query page size and parallelizing account attribute collection with asyncio; PostgreSQL scales vertically to 4 vCores if findings volume exceeds 50,000 accounts.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (8 weeks): nightly PyAD enumeration for all service accounts, five initial privilege baseline rules in policy_rules table (Domain Admin membership, password never expires, unconstrained Kerberos delegation, stale account >180 days, AdminSDHolder flag), dual-approval workflow with full approver identity capture, signed PowerShell for password rotation and group membership removal, Azure Sentinel CEF integration, Flask admin panel with findings dashboard and policy rule management. Phase 2 Extended Rules and Reporting (4 weeks): SPN misconfiguration rules (Kerberoastable accounts), weekly SOC 2 compliance evidence report PDF, CISO email summary via Logic Apps, staging environment with synthetic AD test data. Phase 3 Advanced Automation (4 weeks): ServiceNow change ticket integration for CAB approval workflow, automated rollback script generation and storage, cross-OU enumeration for Azure AD hybrid accounts, JIT access integration with Azure AD PIM for admin panel elevation.
target_output.development_guidelines: Python 3.12 with mypy strict mode. Bandit for security linting enforced in CI with no high-severity findings allowed. pytest with 80% coverage including integration tests against mock LDAP server (ldap3 mock mode) and pywinrm mock fixtures. All PowerShell script templates stored in version control; script generation at execution time via templating with sanitized account name inputs to prevent injection. No AD credentials in environment variables or config files; Key Vault retrieval mandatory for all privileged operations. ADR documents required for new policy rule types and any change to approval workflow state machine. Staging environment runs with synthetic AD test accounts only; production AD credentials never used in dev or staging.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: pywinrm WinRM connectivity failure mid-remediation leaving an AD account in partially modified state (e.g., group removed but password not rotated). Mitigation: RemediationExecutor checks WinRM connectivity before beginning multi-step remediations; partial execution logged with error_detail and finding reverted to open status for re-approval. Risk: policy_rules table misconfiguration (overly broad rule) triggering mass findings for valid service accounts of critical clinical applications. Mitigation: new policy rules deploy in monitor-only mode (active=false, severity=informational) for one enumeration cycle before promotion to enforce status. Tradeoff: Flask server-rendered admin panel over a React SPA reduces frontend complexity appropriate for an internal IT tool with a small user base; acceptable for this scope. Tradeoff: separate RemediationVM over executing remediations from EnumerationVM provides defense-in-depth for the privileged execution identity but adds VM cost and network hop latency.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Confirmed policy_rules will be database-driven with YAML import capability for bulk rule deployment at tool initialization. Confirmed ps_script_hash stored in remediation_actions references SHA-256 hash of the signed script bytes, not the template, providing post-execution verification evidence. Confirmed baseline scope covers on-premises AD service accounts only in Phase 1; Azure AD cloud-only accounts deferred to Phase 3 PIM integration.

metadata.notes: Round 2 revision adding SOC 2 CC6.2 compliant approver identity audit trail and database-driven policy rule engine for hospital AD privileged account remediation tool.

profile.domain: Hospital Data Engineering - OMOP CDM Migration

project_goal.value: CLI tool to validate, transform, and bulk-load historical patient encounter CSV exports from legacy systems into the hospital OMOP CDM research database, supporting both dry-run validation and full load modes
project_goal.rationale: Data engineering team needs repeatable, auditable ETL for OMOP CDM population from multiple legacy source systems

target_users.value: Hospital data engineering team, research informatics team
target_users.rationale: Primary operators running migrations and validating output quality before committing to research database

project_class.rationale: Local command-line tool invoked by data engineers; no hosted service or browser UI required

capabilities.rationale: CSV processing pipeline, OMOP vocabulary lookup, PostgreSQL bulk insert, validation reporting

complexity_level.rationale: Polars-based CSV processing at scale plus OMOP concept mapping adds complexity beyond simple ETL

risk_level.rationale: PHI in CSV files on local filesystem; no network exposure but data handling requires HIPAA controls

data_sensitivity.rationale: Patient encounter CSVs contain identified PHI including MRN, DOB, and diagnosis codes

external_exposure.rationale: CLI runs locally on hospital EC2 instances within private subnet; no external API endpoints

access_model.value: OS-level access control; CLI invoked under data_engineer IAM role with read access to source S3 bucket and write access to OMOP research database schema only
access_model.rationale: Minimal privilege: tool reads source CSVs and writes to target OMOP schema; no admin database access

feature_scope.value: Multi-command CLI with validate, transform, load, and dry-run commands; configurable validation rules per source system; OMOP concept mapping via vocabulary lookup; PostgreSQL COPY bulk insert; validation error report output; incremental load tracking
feature_scope.rationale: Full ETL cycle from CSV validation through OMOP population with checkpointing for resumable large loads

mvp_scope.value: omop-load validate and omop-load run commands for encounter and demographics domains; standard OMOP concept mapping; dry-run mode with error report CSV output
mvp_scope.rationale: Minimum for first migration wave covering encounter_occurrence and person OMOP domains

security_baseline.value: Source CSV files accessed from S3 via IAM role; database credentials from AWS Secrets Manager; no PHI written to stdout or log files; temp files with PHI written to encrypted EBS volume only
security_baseline.rationale: HIPAA transmission and storage controls for PHI on local filesystem and database connections

frontend_stack.value: None; CLI only with rich terminal output for progress and error display
frontend_stack.rationale: No UI required for a data engineering CLI tool

backend_stack.value: Python 3.12 with Click for command parsing and Polars for high-throughput DataFrame processing
backend_stack.rationale: Click provides clean multi-command CLI structure; Polars handles large CSV files with lazy evaluation and column-level operations

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 OMOP CDM research database on EC2; source CSVs from S3; OMOP vocabulary tables pre-loaded in research database
data_platform.rationale: OMOP CDM standard target; PostgreSQL COPY for bulk insert performance; vocabulary in-database for concept mapping joins

hosting_target.value: EC2 instances within hospital private subnet (us-east-1); no internet egress required
hosting_target.rationale: PHI data must remain within hospital AWS VPC; no external connectivity needed

privacy_retention_policy.value: Source CSVs retained 90 days post-migration on S3 then deleted; OMOP research database records retained per institutional data governance policy; migration load_batch logs retained 2 years for audit
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: HIPAA minimum necessary retention; institutional data governance for research database access

future_scope.value: Extension to additional OMOP domains (measurement, drug_exposure, condition_occurrence); support for HL7 FHIR R4 JSON source format in addition to CSV; incremental delta load mode for ongoing sync
future_scope.rationale: Research database will expand to full OMOP CDM coverage across additional clinical domains

constraints.value: Must process files up to 50 GB on EC2 instances with 32 GB RAM; dry-run mode must complete validation without any database writes; no third-party OMOP ETL frameworks (use direct COPY commands)
constraints.rationale: Memory constraint drives Polars lazy evaluation choice; dry-run integrity critical for data engineer trust; avoid framework dependencies for maintainability

observability_baseline.value: AWS CloudWatch for EC2 instance metrics during batch runs; structured JSON log file per load batch with row counts and error summaries; load_batches table tracks every run with status and statistics
observability_baseline.rationale: Auditable load history and CloudWatch for infrastructure visibility during large migrations

execution_preference.value: Iterative development with data engineering team validation after each domain implementation; pytest unit tests for all mapping logic before production use
execution_preference.rationale: Data quality correctness is paramount; validation by domain experts required at each step

compliance_context.value: HIPAA Privacy Rule minimum necessary standard for PHI in research datasets; institutional IRB data use agreement for patient data in research database; de-identification required before data made available to external researchers
compliance_context.rationale: PHI processing for research purposes triggers HIPAA and institutional IRB obligations

project.goal: Repeatable, auditable CSV-to-OMOP CDM ETL CLI with validation, concept mapping, and dry-run mode
project.target_users: Hospital data engineering and research informatics teams
project.timeline: Q2 person and visit_occurrence domains, Q3 full encounter and observation domains

backend.framework: Click multi-command CLI, Polars lazy DataFrames, psycopg3 for COPY protocol
backend.api: No API; CLI commands only

security.auth: IAM role for S3 source bucket access; AWS Secrets Manager for database credentials
security.encryption: Encrypted EBS for temp files; TLS for database connections
security.compliance: HIPAA minimum necessary; institutional IRB DUA

data.source: S3 bucket with source system CSV exports
data.vocabulary: OMOP vocabulary tables pre-loaded in research database

constraints.memory: Process 50 GB files on 32 GB RAM using Polars lazy evaluation
constraints.dry_run: No DB writes in dry-run mode

open_questions.chunking_strategy: Determine optimal Polars chunk size for 50 GB files on 32 GB RAM through benchmarking

reasoner_reviews.product: The dry-run mode is the most critical feature for data engineer trust. The validation error report must clearly distinguish between fatal errors blocking load (unmapped mandatory concept codes, null required fields) and warnings that are acceptable in partial loads, with row-level identification for manual review.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Polars lazy evaluation with scan_csv is the correct approach for 50 GB files on 32 GB RAM. The OMOP concept mapping join should be performed as a Polars lazy join against a pre-loaded vocabulary DataFrame rather than a row-by-row database lookup, which would be prohibitively slow at scale. The PostgreSQL COPY protocol via psycopg3 copy_expert is the right bulk insert mechanism.
reasoner_reviews.security: The round 1 plan had a significant flaw: it described writing intermediate transformed DataFrames to /tmp without specifying encrypted storage. On EC2, /tmp is on the root EBS volume which may not be encrypted. The constraint specifies temp files with PHI must be on encrypted EBS. This needs explicit handling in the architecture.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: The no-framework constraint means vocabulary mapping logic must be implemented directly. A pre-loaded vocabulary cache from the database (loaded once per run into memory as a Polars DataFrame) satisfies this and avoids per-row database round-trips.

backend.service_design: Multi-command Click CLI: omop-load validate (validation-only pass, outputs error report), omop-load run (full ETL to OMOP), omop-load dry-run (validate + transform + row count report, no DB writes), omop-load status (show load_batch history). Core pipeline modules: SourceReader (Polars scan_csv with schema inference), ValidationEngine (configurable rule evaluation per source system), ConceptMapper (vocabulary join via pre-loaded Polars DataFrame), OMOPTransformer (domain-specific field mapping to OMOP CDM columns), BulkLoader (psycopg3 COPY protocol to PostgreSQL)
backend.api_patterns: No API; Click command entry points with --source-file, --domain, --source-system, --batch-id, --dry-run, --config flags. Exit codes: 0 success, 1 validation errors present, 2 fatal error blocking load. JSON summary written to --output-report path.
backend.failure_handling: Validation failures in dry-run: collect all errors to validation_errors list, write error report CSV, exit code 1. COPY protocol failure mid-batch: rollback current COPY transaction, update load_batches record with status=failed and last_row_loaded for resumption. Vocabulary lookup miss: log unmapped_concept_warning, insert OMOP concept_id=0 (standard for unmapped), increment unmapped_count in load summary.
backend.background_jobs: No daemon background jobs; CLI is invoked directly. Checkpoint mechanism: load_batches table tracks batch_id, source_file, domain, rows_validated, rows_loaded, rows_failed, status, started_at, completed_at for audit and resumption.

security.auth_design: No user authentication; CLI runs under data_engineer IAM role on EC2; S3 source bucket access via instance profile; database credentials retrieved from AWS Secrets Manager at startup using boto3
security.secrets_management: Database connection string assembled from Secrets Manager response at runtime; never written to disk or logged; temp file paths use Python tempfile.mkdtemp on explicitly specified encrypted EBS mount point (/data/tmp), not system /tmp
security.privacy_controls: PHI fields (patient_mrn, dob, name_fields) never written to stdout, log files, or error reports; error reports reference row index and source_id only; validation error messages use field names and error types without PHI values

data.entities: encounter_records (source CSV rows: patient_id, encounter_date, encounter_type_local_code, diagnosis_codes_local[], provider_id, facility_code). patient_demographics (source CSV: patient_id, dob, sex_local_code, race_local_code, ethnicity_local_code). concept_mappings (vocabulary table join: source_code, source_vocabulary_id, target_concept_id, domain_id, valid_start_date, valid_end_date). omop_concepts (pre-loaded from OMOP concept table: concept_id, concept_name, domain_id, vocabulary_id, concept_code). load_batches (id UUID, batch_id TEXT, source_file TEXT, domain TEXT, source_system TEXT, rows_validated INT, rows_loaded INT, rows_failed INT, unmapped_count INT, status ENUM pending/running/completed/failed, started_at, completed_at).
data.storage_design: Source CSVs on S3 (hospital data lake bucket, versioned); OMOP CDM tables on PostgreSQL 16 in research database schema; vocabulary tables (concept, concept_relationship, concept_synonym) pre-loaded in same PostgreSQL instance; temp transformed DataFrames written to /data/tmp on encrypted EBS volume, deleted after successful COPY
data.migration_notes: Initial OMOP vocabulary load (OMOP v5.4 Athena download) performed separately by DBA before tool deployment. Tool assumes vocabulary tables are current; vocabulary version recorded in load_batch metadata for lineage.

devops.deployment_topology: Python package installed via pip on hospital EC2 instances in data engineering subnet; no containerization required; installed under virtual environment in /opt/omop-loader/
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: mypy + ruff lint -> pytest 85% coverage with synthetic OMOP fixture data -> build wheel -> upload to internal PyPI (CodeArtifact) -> data engineering team installs on EC2 via pip install omop-loader --index-url (internal CodeArtifact)
devops.observability_stack: Structured JSON log file per batch run written to /var/log/omop-loader/<batch_id>.json with schema: {batch_id, domain, rows_validated, rows_loaded, rows_failed, unmapped_count, error_summary, duration_seconds, completed_at}. CloudWatch Logs agent tails log directory and ships to omop-loader CloudWatch log group. Load_batches table queryable for historical run audit.
devops.rollback_strategy: OMOP domain rollback via --rollback-batch <batch_id> subcommand: deletes all rows from target OMOP domain table where load_batch_id matches; rollback logged to load_batches as a rollback event record

ISS-001.title: Temp file PHI written to unencrypted /tmp instead of encrypted EBS mount
ISS-001.detail: Round 1 plan described intermediate Polars DataFrames written to /tmp for intermediate storage between transform and COPY phases. The security constraint specifies PHI temp files must use encrypted EBS mount at /data/tmp. EC2 root volume /tmp may not be encrypted per hospital storage policy.

previous_plan.executive_summary: Python CLI using Click and Polars for bulk CSV-to-OMOP CDM ETL with validation, concept mapping, and dry-run mode for hospital data engineering teams migrating legacy encounter data.
