target_output.thinking_summary: Round 2 corrects two high-severity architectural violations from Round 1. ISS-001: replacing all /tmp intermediate storage with /data/tmp on the explicitly encrypted EBS data volume, enforced via a --tmp-dir flag defaulting to /data/tmp with a startup check that the path exists and is on an encrypted filesystem. ISS-002: removing the web dashboard entirely and replacing with omop-load status (tabular batch history to stdout) and omop-load report (JSON and CSV summary output) CLI subcommands, keeping the tool entirely local with no server components. All plan sections reviewed to remove any server-side or web component references.

fix_report.action_taken: Replaced all /tmp references with /data/tmp on encrypted EBS mount. Added --tmp-dir CLI flag (default /data/tmp) with startup validation checking path exists and is on an encrypted filesystem via lsblk. Temp files created with tempfile.mkdtemp(dir=tmp_dir) and cleaned up in a finally block regardless of success or failure. Updated security_and_compliance, workflows, and deployment_and_operations sections.
fix_report.expected_outcome: All PHI intermediate transform files are written exclusively to the encrypted EBS data volume, satisfying HIPAA storage control and the explicit contract constraint.

fix_report.action_taken: Removed web dashboard from Phase 3 and all plan sections. Replaced with two new CLI subcommands: omop-load status (queries load_batches table, renders tabular output to stdout via rich.table) and omop-load report --batch-id (outputs JSON and CSV summary of validation errors and load statistics for a completed batch). No server components remain in any section.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Tool is a pure CLI with no server, web, or network listening components in any phase, consistent with cli_tool project class and local_only external_exposure contract fields.

target_output.title: OMOP CDM Migration CLI Tool
target_output.executive_summary: A Python CLI tool built with Click and Polars for hospital data engineering teams to validate, transform, and bulk-load historical patient encounter CSV exports into an OMOP CDM v5.4 research database. The tool provides validate, run, dry-run, status, and report subcommands, processes files up to 50 GB via Polars lazy evaluation, performs OMOP concept mapping through a pre-loaded vocabulary DataFrame, and bulk-inserts via PostgreSQL COPY protocol. All intermediate PHI files are written exclusively to an encrypted EBS mount at /data/tmp with automatic cleanup. No server or web components; entirely local CLI.
target_output.architecture_overview: Single Python package installed on hospital EC2 instances (data engineering subnet) providing a multi-command Click CLI. On invocation, the pipeline stages execute sequentially: SourceReader (Polars scan_csv lazy frame from S3 or local path), ValidationEngine (configurable rule evaluation loading rules from YAML config or database), ConceptMapper (vocabulary join via pre-loaded in-memory Polars DataFrame from OMOP concept tables), OMOPTransformer (domain-specific field mapping to target OMOP CDM columns), BulkLoader (psycopg3 COPY protocol streaming rows to PostgreSQL). Intermediate DataFrames that exceed memory threshold are serialized to /data/tmp on the encrypted EBS data volume via Polars sink_parquet, not /tmp. Load progress tracked in load_batches PostgreSQL table for audit and resumption. No web, API, or daemon components.
target_output.technology_stack: Runtime: Python 3.12 in virtual environment at /opt/omop-loader/venv. CLI: Click 8.1 multi-command structure with --source-file, --domain, --source-system, --batch-id, --dry-run, --config, --tmp-dir flags. DataFrames: Polars 0.20 with lazy scan_csv and sink_parquet for memory-constrained processing. Database: psycopg3 3.1 with COPY protocol via connection.cursor().copy() for bulk insert. Progress display: rich library for tabular status and progress bars to stdout. Secrets: boto3 Secrets Manager client for database credentials retrieval at startup. Distribution: Python wheel published to AWS CodeArtifact internal PyPI.
target_output.functional_feature_map: omop-load validate --source-file --domain --source-system: scans CSV with Polars, evaluates all validation rules, outputs error report to --output-report CSV with columns (row_index, source_id, field_name, error_type, severity). omop-load run --source-file --domain --source-system [--batch-id]: full ETL pipeline from CSV through concept mapping and COPY insert; creates load_batch record; updates progress in real-time via rich progress bar. omop-load dry-run: identical to run pipeline but skips COPY insert and database writes; outputs row count summary and unmapped concept report. omop-load status [--domain] [--days=30]: queries load_batches table, renders tabular summary to stdout via rich.table showing batch_id, domain, rows_loaded, rows_failed, unmapped_count, status, completed_at. omop-load report --batch-id: outputs JSON and CSV summary of a completed batch including error breakdown by validation rule and unmapped source codes.
target_output.system_components: EC2 instance (hospital data engineering subnet, us-east-1): Python 3.12 venv with omop-loader package. Polars 0.20 lazy engine for CSV scanning and transformation. psycopg3 with COPY protocol for PostgreSQL bulk insert. boto3 Secrets Manager client for DB credential retrieval. /data/tmp directory on encrypted EBS data volume (confirmed encrypted via lsblk at startup, abort if not). S3 source bucket (hospital data lake): read access via EC2 instance profile IAM role. PostgreSQL 16 OMOP research database: write access to research_omop schema only; OMOP vocabulary tables (concept, concept_relationship) pre-loaded and available for vocabulary join queries. load_batches audit table in research_omop schema. CloudWatch Logs agent: tails /var/log/omop-loader/ directory, ships JSON batch logs to omop-loader CloudWatch log group.
target_output.workflows: Full Load Workflow: Data engineer invokes omop-load run --source-file s3://hospital-datalake/legacy-exports/encounters_2024.csv --domain visit_occurrence --source-system epic_2018. CLI retrieves database credentials from Secrets Manager, connects to PostgreSQL. SourceReader calls Polars scan_csv(source_file) returning a LazyFrame. ValidationEngine loads active rules from config YAML, evaluates mandatory fields (visit_occurrence_id, person_id, visit_start_date) and concept code presence, collects validation_errors list. ConceptMapper performs Polars lazy join of source visit_type_source_code against pre-loaded concept LazyFrame (loaded once at startup via read_database into in-memory Polars DataFrame) to resolve OMOP visit_concept_id. OMOPTransformer maps source columns to OMOP visit_occurrence schema (visit_occurrence_id, person_id, visit_concept_id, visit_start_date, visit_end_date, visit_type_concept_id, provider_id, care_site_id). BulkLoader streams transformed rows through psycopg3 COPY protocol in 100,000-row chunks; progress bar updated per chunk. On completion, load_batches record updated with rows_loaded, rows_failed, unmapped_count, status=completed. Dry-Run Workflow: Same pipeline stages execute but BulkLoader is replaced by a NullLoader that counts rows and accumulates unmapped concept statistics; no database writes occur; summary JSON output to stdout and --output-report path. Intermediate parquet files written to /data/tmp with cleanup in finally block.
target_output.data_model: encounter_records (source CSV domain: patient_id TEXT, encounter_date DATE, encounter_type_local_code TEXT, diagnosis_codes_local TEXT[], icd_version INT, provider_npi TEXT, facility_code TEXT). patient_demographics (source CSV domain: patient_id TEXT, dob DATE, sex_local_code TEXT, race_local_code TEXT, ethnicity_local_code TEXT). concept_mappings (OMOP concept table join key: source_code TEXT, source_vocabulary_id TEXT -> concept_id INT, domain_id TEXT, valid_start_date DATE, valid_end_date DATE). omop_visit_occurrence (target OMOP domain: visit_occurrence_id BIGINT, person_id BIGINT, visit_concept_id INT, visit_start_date DATE, visit_end_date DATE, visit_type_concept_id INT, provider_id BIGINT, care_site_id BIGINT, visit_source_value TEXT, visit_source_concept_id INT). load_batches (id UUID PK, batch_id TEXT UNIQUE, source_file TEXT, domain TEXT, source_system TEXT, rows_validated INT, rows_loaded INT, rows_failed INT, unmapped_count INT, vocabulary_version TEXT, status ENUM pending/running/completed/failed/rolled_back, started_at TIMESTAMPTZ, completed_at TIMESTAMPTZ).
target_output.api_design: No network API. CLI subcommand interface via Click: omop-load validate [OPTIONS], omop-load run [OPTIONS], omop-load dry-run [OPTIONS], omop-load status [OPTIONS], omop-load report --batch-id [OPTIONS]. Exit codes: 0 success, 1 validation errors present (non-fatal), 2 fatal error aborting load. Stdout: rich progress bars and tabular status output. Stderr: error messages. File output: --output-report writes validation_errors CSV or JSON load summary to specified path. No HTTP endpoints, no daemon ports.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Access controls: CLI runs under data_engineer EC2 IAM role with S3 read permission on hospital-datalake bucket and PostgreSQL CONNECT plus INSERT/SELECT on research_omop schema only; no DDL or admin permissions. Secrets: database credentials retrieved from AWS Secrets Manager using boto3 at startup; never stored in config files, environment variables, or logs. PHI temp file storage: all intermediate Polars parquet files created via tempfile.mkdtemp(dir=tmp_dir) where tmp_dir defaults to /data/tmp; startup check verifies /data/tmp is on an encrypted EBS volume via lsblk --fs --json parsing; CLI aborts with exit code 2 if unencrypted. PHI in logs: error reports reference row_index and source_id only; PHI field values (patient_id, dob) never written to stdout, stderr, log files, or error reports. HIPAA minimum necessary: source CSV files on S3 versioned and lifecycle policy deletes 90 days post-migration. IRB DUA: OMOP research database access restricted to approved research projects by PostgreSQL row-level security per project schema.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Package distribution: GitHub Actions pipeline (mypy strict + ruff lint -> pytest 85% coverage using synthetic OMOP fixture data in Docker PostgreSQL -> build wheel -> publish to AWS CodeArtifact internal PyPI). Installation on EC2: data engineer runs pip install omop-loader --index-url https://<org>.d.codeartifact.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/pypi/internal/simple in /opt/omop-loader/ virtual environment. Encrypted EBS setup: /data/tmp directory must exist on encrypted EBS volume mounted at /data; infrastructure team provision and document encrypted volume at EC2 launch. Environments: dev (local PostgreSQL via Docker compose with OMOP DDL fixture, synthetic encounter CSV, no real PHI), staging (hospital EC2 with de-identified encounter subset, same PostgreSQL instance with separate staging_omop schema), production (hospital EC2 data engineering subnet, real PHI source CSVs from S3, production OMOP research database). No CI deployment to production; data engineering lead manually installs production version from CodeArtifact after staging validation.
target_output.observability: Structured JSON log file written per batch run to /var/log/omop-loader/<batch_id>.json with fields: batch_id, domain, source_system, source_file, rows_validated, rows_loaded, rows_failed, unmapped_count, duration_seconds, error_summary (top 5 error types by count), completed_at. CloudWatch Logs agent tails /var/log/omop-loader/ and ships to omop-loader CloudWatch log group; CloudWatch Alarm fires if rows_failed > 5% of rows_validated in any batch (SNS email to data-engineering team). load_batches table queryable via omop-load status for historical audit. CloudWatch EC2 metrics (CPU, memory via CW agent) monitored during large batch runs.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: EC2 instance cost depends on existing data engineering fleet; no additional VM required as tool installs on existing instances. PostgreSQL OMOP research database on existing hospital RDS or EC2. S3 storage for source CSVs estimated at $0.023/GB/month for 90-day retention window before lifecycle deletion; 50 GB file generates approximately $1.15/month in storage. CloudWatch Logs ingestion at estimated 1 MB/batch approximately $0.005 per batch run. Total incremental cost near zero on existing infrastructure. Scaling to larger files: Polars lazy evaluation handles files exceeding RAM via chunked parquet sinks to /data/tmp; COPY throughput scales linearly with PostgreSQL iops.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (6 weeks): omop-load validate, run, and dry-run for person and visit_occurrence OMOP domains; OMOP concept mapping for visit_type and gender vocabularies; dry-run error report CSV output; load_batches audit table; /data/tmp encrypted temp storage; Secrets Manager credential retrieval; GitHub Actions CI pipeline. Phase 2 Extended Domains (4 weeks): condition_occurrence domain with ICD-10-CM concept mapping; observation and measurement domains; omop-load status subcommand with rich tabular output; omop-load report subcommand with JSON and CSV batch summary; rollback subcommand. Phase 3 Advanced Features (4 weeks): YAML-configurable validation rule sets per source system; incremental delta load mode tracking load_watermark by encounter_date; HL7 FHIR R4 JSON source format support alongside CSV; performance benchmarking report for 50 GB file processing.
target_output.development_guidelines: Python 3.12 with strict mypy type checking; all Click command functions fully typed. Ruff for linting with no-ignore policy. pytest with 85% coverage; all concept mapping functions and OMOP domain transformers must have unit tests with synthetic OMOP vocabulary fixtures. Integration tests use Docker PostgreSQL with OMOP CDM DDL (available from OHDSI GitHub) and synthetic encounter CSV files; no real PHI in any test fixtures. PHI handling rule: functions that touch PHI-containing fields are decorated with @phi_handling and reviewed at PR time; these functions must never pass field values to logging calls. OMOP CDM version pinned to v5.4 in config; vocabulary version recorded in load_batch metadata for lineage.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: OMOP concept mapping gaps for source system local codes not present in OMOP vocabulary (common for legacy system proprietary codes). Mitigation: unmapped concepts inserted as concept_id=0 per OMOP convention; unmapped_count tracked in load_batches; omop-load report surfaces top unmapped source codes for manual curation by informatics team. Risk: 50 GB file processing on 32 GB RAM instance exceeding memory budget if Polars lazy evaluation triggers full materialization on complex joins. Mitigation: ConceptMapper pre-filters vocabulary DataFrame to domains relevant to current run before join; sink_parquet chunked writes to /data/tmp as safety valve. Tradeoff: YAML-configurable validation rules over hardcoded Python for Phase 3 adds parser complexity but enables source-system-specific rule sets without code deployments. Tradeoff: Click over argparse provides cleaner multi-command structure with built-in help generation at the cost of one additional dependency.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Confirmed /data/tmp as encrypted EBS mount point; startup lsblk check enforces this contractually. Confirmed no web or server components in any phase; monitoring via CLI subcommands and CloudWatch only. Optimal Polars chunk size for 50 GB files to be determined via benchmarking in Phase 1 dev; initial target 500,000 rows per chunk based on column count estimates. OMOP vocabulary version v5.4 with Athena August 2025 release pinned for Phase 1.

metadata.notes: Round 2 corrects two high-severity architectural violations from Round 1: PHI temp files in unencrypted /tmp and a web dashboard component contradicting cli_tool project class. Marked flawed to represent the prior round's violation pattern.

profile.domain: Hospital Quality Reporting - CMS IQR/VBP Submission

project_goal.value: Automate extraction, aggregation, and CMS-formatted submission of quality measure data for IPPS Inpatient Quality Reporting and Value-Based Purchasing programs by pulling from multiple clinical and billing source systems and formatting to program specifications
project_goal.rationale: Manual CMS quality reporting is error-prone and time-intensive; automation reduces submission errors and staff burden during quarterly reporting windows

target_users.value: Hospital quality department analysts, quality director, CMS submission coordinator
target_users.rationale: Primary operators running measure calculations and secondary reviewers approving submissions before CMS upload

project_class.rationale: Batch-driven ETL and submission pipeline with no public-facing UI; Airflow orchestration of measure calculation DAGs

capabilities.rationale: Airflow DAGs for orchestration, Snowflake for data warehouse queries, multi-source integration (EHR, billing, lab), and CMS submission file generation

complexity_level.rationale: Multiple CMS measure specifications with complex denominator/numerator logic, cross-system data integration, exception/exclusion handling, and strict CMS file format validation

risk_level.rationale: Incorrect CMS submissions result in financial penalties; PHI processed from multiple source systems; integration with external CMS QRDA/flat-file submission portals

data_sensitivity.rationale: Patient encounter data including diagnoses, procedures, and outcomes is PHI used in measure denominator/numerator calculations

external_exposure.rationale: Pipeline operates within hospital AWS environment; CMS submission file upload is performed manually by coordinator from secure workstation, not via automated API

access_model.value: Okta SSO for Airflow webserver: quality_analyst (trigger DAGs, view run logs), quality_director (approve submission files, view all reports), cms_coordinator (download submission files, mark as submitted), data_engineer (DAG deployment, pipeline configuration)
access_model.rationale: Role-differentiated access required; submission approval workflow requires director sign-off before CMS coordinator can download files

feature_scope.value: Quarterly Airflow DAGs for SEP-1 sepsis bundle, PC-01 elective delivery, VTE-1 VTE prophylaxis, and PSI-90 composite measures; denominator population extraction from Snowflake; exception and exclusion evaluation; CMS XML/flat-file submission package generation; pre-submission validation against CMS acceptance criteria; error report with actionable corrections; submission audit trail
feature_scope.rationale: Initial four measures cover highest-value IQR and VBP program measures for the hospital

mvp_scope.value: SEP-1 sepsis bundle and VTE-1 VTE prophylaxis measure calculation DAGs; CMS flat-file submission package generation; pre-submission validation with error threshold blocking; submission_batches audit table
mvp_scope.rationale: SEP-1 and VTE-1 are highest-impact IQR measures for initial delivery; flat-file format simpler than QRDA XML for MVP

security_baseline.value: Okta SSO MFA for all Airflow webserver access; Snowflake data sharing with row-level security per measure program; AWS Secrets Manager for all source system credentials; PHI fields not written to Airflow logs; submission files encrypted at rest on S3 with server-side encryption
security_baseline.rationale: HIPAA Security Rule controls for PHI in quality measure pipeline and CMS submission files

frontend_stack.value: None; Airflow native webserver UI for DAG monitoring and triggering; no custom frontend
frontend_stack.rationale: MWAA provides managed Airflow webserver; no custom UI development needed for an internal automation tool

backend_stack.value: Python 3.12 with Apache Airflow 2.9 on AWS MWAA for orchestration; Snowflake Python connector for data warehouse queries
backend_stack.rationale: MWAA reduces Airflow operational overhead; Snowflake for data warehouse queries against consolidated clinical and billing data

data_platform.value: Snowflake (multi-cluster warehouse) as primary data source for denominator and numerator queries; S3 for submission file output and audit storage
data_platform.rationale: Snowflake hosts consolidated clinical data from Epic and billing feeds; S3 for durable submission file storage with encryption

hosting_target.value: AWS MWAA (Managed Workflows for Apache Airflow) in us-east-1; Snowflake AWS us-east-1 deployment
hosting_target.rationale: Managed Airflow eliminates scheduler and worker management; Snowflake co-located in us-east-1 minimizes data transfer costs

privacy_retention_policy.value: CMS submission files retained 10 years on S3 for Medicare audit evidence; source encounter data remains in Snowflake per hospital data governance policy; measure calculation intermediate results purged 90 days post-submission
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: CMS audit and Medicare Conditions of Participation require 10-year retention of quality reporting evidence

future_scope.value: QRDA III XML submission format for electronic CMS portal upload; additional measures (PC-01, PSI-90 composite); OQR outpatient quality reporting program; automated CMS portal upload via QualityNet Secure Portal API when available
future_scope.rationale: Full IQR/VBP program coverage and eventual CMS portal API automation are the Year 2 roadmap

constraints.value: Submission package must pass CMS pre-acceptance validation with zero critical errors before coordinator download is enabled; measure logic must be versioned to CMS specification year (FY2025, FY2026); Airflow DAG code reviewed by quality director before production deployment
constraints.rationale: CMS imposes financial penalties for late or invalid submissions; measure specification versioning required for multi-year reporting continuity

observability_baseline.value: AWS CloudWatch for MWAA environment health; Airflow task-level logging to CloudWatch; SNS email to quality_director on DAG failure or pre-submission validation error threshold breach
observability_baseline.rationale: Quality team needs immediate notification of pipeline failures given quarterly submission deadlines

execution_preference.value: Quarterly release cycle aligned to CMS reporting periods; Jenkins CI for DAG deployment; quality director sign-off required for production DAG changes
execution_preference.rationale: CMS reporting periods drive release cadence; governance gate prevents untested measure logic reaching production

compliance_context.value: CMS IPPS Inpatient Quality Reporting (IQR) program requirements; CMS Value-Based Purchasing (VBP) Total Performance Score methodology; HIPAA Privacy and Security Rules for PHI in quality measure datasets; hospital Medicare Conditions of Participation requiring accurate quality data submission; OIG compliance program for quality data integrity
compliance_context.rationale: Incorrect quality submissions trigger financial penalties under VBP; HIPAA applies to PHI in measure datasets; OIG compliance program requires data integrity controls

project.goal: Automated CMS IQR/VBP quality measure calculation, file generation, and pre-submission validation pipeline
project.target_users: Hospital quality department analysts, quality director, CMS submission coordinator
project.timeline: Q2 SEP-1 and VTE-1 MVP, Q4 full four-measure suite

backend.framework: Apache Airflow 2.9 on AWS MWAA; Snowflake Python connector; custom measure calculation modules
backend.api: No external API; Airflow webserver for internal DAG management

security.auth: Okta SSO MFA for Airflow webserver
security.encryption: S3 SSE-KMS for submission files; TLS for Snowflake connections

data.storage: S3 for submission files and audit trail
data.sources: Epic EHR feed, billing system feed, lab system feed via Snowflake tables

constraints.submission_gate: Zero critical pre-submission validation errors before coordinator download
constraints.measure_versioning: CMS specification year tracked per submission batch
constraints.governance: Quality director review required for production DAG deployment

open_questions.sep1_denominator_source: Confirm SEP-1 denominator population source table in Snowflake (clinical_encounters or billing_claims)

reasoner_reviews.product: The measure-specific DAG approach is correct for CMS IQR reporting. Each measure DAG must be independently triggerable and restartable from the denominator extraction step without re-running already-completed tasks. The pre-submission validation gate blocking coordinator download until zero critical errors is essential and must be enforced at the data layer, not just the UI, to prevent accidental submission of invalid files.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Snowflake is well-suited for the complex multi-table denominator and numerator queries required by CMS measure specifications. Separate Snowflake warehouses for measure calculation (XL, autoscale) and reporting queries (M, fixed) prevents reporting queries from competing with quarterly calculation runs. The measure calculation modules should be pure Python functions independent of Airflow to enable unit testing outside DAG context.
reasoner_reviews.security: PHI flows through Airflow task logs by default if measure calculation functions log patient-level detail. All measure calculation tasks must use structured logging that records encounter counts and error type summaries, never encounter IDs or diagnoses. CMS submission files contain PHI (facility CCN, aggregate measures) but not individual patient data in flat-file format; however QRDA XML in future scope will contain patient-level data and will require additional controls.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: The Jenkins CI pipeline must enforce quality director sign-off via a Jenkins input step before production DAG deployment, not just documentation policy. The CMS specification year versioning should be a first-class metadata field on submission_batches, not buried in filename conventions.

backend.service_design: Airflow DAG per CMS measure: sep1_measure_dag, vte1_measure_dag, pc01_measure_dag, psi90_measure_dag. Each DAG: DenominatorExtractOperator (Snowflake query for eligible encounter population), ExclusionEvaluatorOperator (apply measure-specific exclusion logic), NumeratorCalculatorOperator (evaluate compliance criteria per encounter), ExceptionHandlerOperator (flag documented exceptions), FileGeneratorOperator (produce CMS flat-file or QRDA XML), ValidationOperator (run CMS acceptance criteria checks). Shared MeasureConfig class loads specification year and rule definitions from measure_specifications Snowflake table.
backend.api_patterns: No external API. Airflow REST API (internal, Okta-authenticated) used by webserver UI. Custom submission_approvals Airflow plugin provides approve and download endpoints accessible to quality_director and cms_coordinator roles.
backend.failure_handling: Airflow task retry: 2 retries with 5-minute delay for Snowflake connection failures. Pre-submission validation failure: ValidationOperator sets submission_batch.status=validation_failed, triggers SNS alert to quality_director, blocks download endpoint from serving file. Partial measure calculation: failed tasks marked with Airflow upstream_failed sensor; allows successful measures to proceed to file generation independently.
backend.background_jobs: Quarterly scheduled DAGs (configurable cron per CMS reporting period: IQR Q1 data due March 1, etc.). Weekly denominator reconciliation DAG comparing Snowflake encounter counts against billing system to detect source data gaps 60 days before submission deadline.

security.auth_design: Okta SAML 2.0 SSO integration with MWAA Airflow webserver; all four roles mapped to Airflow role-based access (quality_analyst=Viewer, quality_director=User+custom approve plugin, cms_coordinator=custom download plugin, data_engineer=Admin). MFA enforced via Okta policy.
security.secrets_management: All source system credentials (Snowflake connection string, billing API keys) stored in AWS Secrets Manager; Airflow connections reference Secrets Manager backend, never hardcoded in DAG files. S3 submission output bucket uses SSE-KMS with CMK.
security.privacy_controls: Measure calculation Python functions use structured logging recording only encounter_count, numerator_count, exclusion_count, exception_count per log statement; no encounter_id, patient_mrn, or diagnosis codes in any Airflow task log. CMS flat-file submission format contains facility-level aggregate data only; QRDA XML future scope requires patient pseudonymization review before implementation.

data.entities: quality_measures (id UUID, measure_id TEXT e.g. SEP-1, measure_name, cms_program ENUM iqr/vbp, specification_year INT, active BOOLEAN). measure_populations (id UUID, measure_id FK, submission_batch_id FK, encounter_id_hash TEXT, population_flag ENUM denominator/numerator/exclusion/exception, evaluated_at TIMESTAMPTZ). submission_batches (id UUID, batch_name TEXT, measure_ids TEXT[], cms_program, specification_year INT, reporting_period_start DATE, reporting_period_end DATE, denominator_count INT, numerator_count INT, measure_rate DECIMAL, status ENUM calculating/validation_failed/ready_for_review/approved/submitted, approved_by UUID, approved_at TIMESTAMPTZ, submitted_at TIMESTAMPTZ, file_s3_key TEXT). program_specifications (id UUID, measure_id FK, specification_year INT, denominator_sql TEXT, numerator_sql TEXT, exclusion_sql TEXT, exception_criteria_json JSONB, cms_acceptance_rules_json JSONB, effective_date DATE).
data.storage_design: Snowflake as query engine for denominator/numerator population calculation (reading from clinical_encounters, billing_claims, lab_results, medication_administrations Snowflake tables). PostgreSQL via MWAA metadata database for Airflow DAG state. S3 submission-files bucket (SSE-KMS, versioned, 10-year lifecycle) for generated CMS flat files and QRDA XML. S3 measure-logs bucket for intermediate population calculation exports (90-day lifecycle).
data.analytics_events: Submission rate trend by measure and fiscal year; denominator size deviation alert (>10% change from prior quarter triggers quality director review); exception rate anomaly detection per measure.

devops.deployment_topology: AWS MWAA environment (mw1.medium, autoscaling 1-5 workers) in hospital VPC private subnet (us-east-1). Snowflake AWS us-east-1 deployment with private link to hospital VPC. S3 buckets in us-east-1 with VPC endpoint. Jenkins server on EC2 in hospital CI/CD subnet.
devops.ci_cd_design: Jenkins pipeline: bandit + pylint -> pytest (90% coverage for measure calculation functions) -> deploy DAG files to MWAA S3 DAG bucket -> Jenkins input step requiring quality director approval (manual) -> activate new DAG version in MWAA. Measure calculation function unit tests use synthetic CMS measure fixture data meeting published CMS specifications.
devops.observability_stack: CloudWatch for MWAA worker health and task duration metrics. Airflow task logs shipped to CloudWatch Logs. SNS topic subscribed by quality_director email: alerts for DAG failure, pre-submission validation error count exceeding threshold, and denominator reconciliation gap detected. CloudWatch dashboard: measure calculation duration by quarter, submission_batch status pipeline funnel.
devops.rollback_strategy: DAG rollback: revert to previous DAG file version in MWAA S3 bucket via Jenkins rollback job requiring data_engineer approval. Submission batch rollback: mark submission_batch.status=retracted, generate corrected submission in new batch; original batch archived in S3 with retracted tag.

target_output.thinking_summary: Designing a CMS IQR/VBP quality reporting automation pipeline on AWS MWAA with Snowflake as the data warehouse. Key architectural decisions: per-measure Airflow DAGs with independent restartability, pure Python measure calculation modules testable outside Airflow context, pre-submission validation gate blocking file download until zero critical CMS acceptance errors, and measure specification versioning as first-class database metadata. No frontend beyond MWAA native webserver; no external API exposure. PHI never written to Airflow logs; submission files contain only facility-level aggregates in flat-file format.
target_output.title: CMS IQR/VBP Quality Measure Reporting Automation Pipeline
target_output.executive_summary: An Airflow-orchestrated automation pipeline on AWS MWAA that extracts denominator-eligible patient encounter populations from Snowflake, evaluates CMS measure logic for SEP-1, VTE-1, PC-01, and PSI-90, generates CMS-formatted flat-file submission packages, and runs pre-submission acceptance validation blocking coordinator download until zero critical errors. Measure specifications are versioned by CMS fiscal year in a database table, enabling multi-year reporting without code changes. All PHI is isolated to Snowflake query results and never written to Airflow task logs or submission files.
target_output.architecture_overview: AWS MWAA Airflow 2.9 environment (private subnet, us-east-1) orchestrates four measure-specific DAGs connecting to Snowflake via private link for denominator and numerator population queries. Each DAG executes sequentially: denominator extraction via Snowflake operator, exclusion evaluation via Python operator calling pure-function measure calculation module, numerator calculation, exception flag evaluation, CMS flat-file generation, and pre-submission validation against CMS acceptance criteria rules loaded from program_specifications table. Generated submission files are written to S3 with SSE-KMS encryption. A custom Airflow plugin exposes approve and download endpoints for quality_director and cms_coordinator roles authenticated via Okta SAML. Submission audit trail maintained in submission_batches PostgreSQL table. Jenkins CI pipeline with quality director approval gate governs production DAG deployment.
target_output.technology_stack: Orchestration: Apache Airflow 2.9 on AWS MWAA (mw1.medium, 1-5 autoscaling workers). DAG language: Python 3.12 with airflow.providers.snowflake.operators.snowflake.SnowflakeOperator for data extraction. Measure calculation: pure Python 3.12 modules (no Airflow dependency) for denominator/numerator logic, testable with pytest. Data warehouse: Snowflake multi-cluster (XL warehouse for quarterly calculation runs, M warehouse for weekly reconciliation). File generation: Python xml.etree.ElementTree for future QRDA XML; csv.writer for MVP flat-file format. Validation: custom CMS acceptance rules engine reading cms_acceptance_rules_json from program_specifications. Auth: Okta SAML 2.0 with MWAA Airflow webserver. CI/CD: Jenkins with input approval step. Storage: S3 with SSE-KMS. Database (Airflow metadata): Aurora PostgreSQL 14 managed by MWAA.
target_output.functional_feature_map: SEP-1 Sepsis Bundle DAG: identifies encounters meeting sepsis-3 criteria in denominator, evaluates 3-hour and 6-hour bundle compliance elements from medication_administrations and lab_results Snowflake tables, applies clinical exclusions (comfort measures, hospice), generates CMS flat-file with numerator rate and exclusion counts. VTE-1 VTE Prophylaxis DAG: denominator of eligible surgical encounters from clinical_encounters, evaluates VTE prophylaxis administration within 24 hours of admission from medication_administrations, applies post-surgical exclusion criteria. Pre-submission Validation Engine: loads cms_acceptance_rules_json from program_specifications for current specification_year, evaluates submission file against each rule (denominator minimum count, rate range plausibility, required field completeness), produces error report with rule_id, severity, and correction guidance. Submission Approval Workflow: quality_director reviews Airflow DAG output and measure rates via submission review panel in custom plugin, approves submission_batch; cms_coordinator download endpoint unlocked only after approval. Denominator Reconciliation DAG: weekly comparison of Snowflake encounter counts against billing_claims source to detect data pipeline gaps.
target_output.system_components: AWS MWAA Environment (mw1.medium, 1-5 workers, private subnet us-east-1a/1b): Airflow scheduler, workers, webserver with Okta SAML. Snowflake Multi-cluster Warehouse (AWS us-east-1, private link to hospital VPC): clinical_encounters, billing_claims, lab_results, medication_administrations tables; measure_populations and program_specifications tables. Aurora PostgreSQL 14 (MWAA-managed Airflow metadata): DAG run state, task instance logs. S3 submission-files bucket (SSE-KMS CMK, versioned, 10-year lifecycle policy): generated CMS flat files and future QRDA XML. S3 measure-logs bucket (SSE-S3, 90-day lifecycle): intermediate population calculation exports. S3 dags bucket (MWAA-connected): Airflow DAG Python files deployed by Jenkins. AWS Secrets Manager: Snowflake connector credentials, billing API keys, Okta SAML certificate. CloudWatch Logs: Airflow task logs, MWAA worker metrics. SNS quality-alerts topic: DAG failure and validation error notifications to quality_director. Jenkins EC2: CI pipeline for DAG deployment with approval gate.
target_output.workflows: Quarterly Measure Calculation Run: quality_analyst manually triggers sep1_measure_dag in Airflow webserver for the applicable reporting period (or EventBridge scheduled trigger fires at start of CMS data submission window). DenominatorExtractOperator runs SnowflakeOperator with denominator SQL for FY-specified specification_year, writing encounter population to measure_populations Snowflake table with population_flag=denominator. ExclusionEvaluatorOperator calls pure Python evaluate_sep1_exclusions() against denominator population, updates population_flag=exclusion for qualifying encounters. NumeratorCalculatorOperator calls evaluate_sep1_bundle_compliance() checking medication_administrations and lab_results for bundle element completion, updates population_flag=numerator. FileGeneratorOperator reads measure_populations summary counts, formats CMS flat-file with CCN, measure ID, denominator, numerator, exclusion counts, and measure rate, writes to S3 submission-files/sep1/<batch_id>.txt with SSE-KMS. ValidationOperator loads cms_acceptance_rules_json from program_specifications, evaluates submission file (denominator >= 25 minimum, rate 0-100%, required fields present), writes validation_errors list to submission_batches record; if critical errors > 0, marks status=validation_failed and triggers SNS alert. Pre-submission Approval: quality_director receives SNS notification of completed DAG, reviews measure rates and error report in Airflow custom plugin panel, clicks Approve Submission; plugin updates submission_batch.status=approved, records approved_by and approved_at. cms_coordinator navigates to download panel, downloads submission file for manual upload to QualityNet. Denominator Reconciliation: Weekly DAG queries Snowflake billing_claims for encounter count in rolling 90-day window, compares against clinical_encounters count, triggers SNS alert if deviation > 10%.
target_output.data_model: quality_measures (id UUID PK, measure_id TEXT UNIQUE e.g. SEP-1, measure_name TEXT, cms_program ENUM iqr/vbp, specification_year INT, active BOOLEAN, updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ). program_specifications (id UUID PK, measure_id TEXT FK, specification_year INT, denominator_sql TEXT, numerator_sql TEXT, exclusion_sql TEXT, exception_criteria_json JSONB, cms_acceptance_rules_json JSONB, effective_date DATE, UNIQUE(measure_id, specification_year)). measure_populations (id UUID PK, measure_id TEXT FK, submission_batch_id UUID FK, encounter_id_hash TEXT NOT NULL, population_flag ENUM denominator/numerator/exclusion/exception, evaluated_at TIMESTAMPTZ). submission_batches (id UUID PK, batch_name TEXT, measure_ids TEXT[], cms_program ENUM iqr/vbp, specification_year INT NOT NULL, reporting_period_start DATE, reporting_period_end DATE, denominator_count INT, numerator_count INT, exclusion_count INT, exception_count INT, measure_rate DECIMAL, validation_errors_json JSONB, status ENUM calculating/validation_failed/ready_for_review/approved/submitted/retracted, approved_by UUID, approved_at TIMESTAMPTZ, submitted_at TIMESTAMPTZ, file_s3_key TEXT, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). abstracted_data_elements (id UUID PK, encounter_id_hash TEXT, measure_id TEXT FK, element_name TEXT, element_value TEXT, source_system TEXT, abstracted_at TIMESTAMPTZ).
target_output.api_design: No external HTTP API. Internal Airflow REST API (Okta-authenticated, internal VPC only): GET /api/v1/dags (list measure DAGs with last run status), POST /api/v1/dags/:dag_id/dagRuns (trigger measure calculation run). Custom Airflow plugin endpoints: POST /quality/submissions/:batch_id/approve (quality_director role only; sets status=approved, records approved_by and approved_at), GET /quality/submissions/:batch_id/download (cms_coordinator role; returns S3 presigned URL valid 1 hour; only if status=approved), GET /quality/submissions?cms_program=iqr&year=2025 (list submission batches with status and measure rates). All plugin endpoints authenticated via Okta SAML session cookie from Airflow webserver.
target_output.security_and_compliance: HIPAA controls: Okta MFA enforced for all Airflow webserver access; Snowflake private link eliminates PHI traversal over public internet; S3 submission-files bucket SSE-KMS with CMK; Airflow task logs ship to CloudWatch with CloudWatch log data protection policy masking encounter_id patterns; PHI never written to Airflow task logs (measure calculation modules log only aggregate counts). CMS IQR/VBP compliance: pre-submission validation engine enforces CMS acceptance criteria before coordinator download; specification_year field on submission_batches ensures correct measure logic version applied; submission audit trail (approved_by, approved_at, submitted_at) satisfies CMS audit evidence requirements. OIG compliance: denominator reconciliation DAG detects source data gaps that could result in artificially low denominators; quality director notified immediately of anomalies. Access controls: Okta role-to-Airflow-role mapping restricts DAG deployment to data_engineer, submission approval to quality_director, and file download to cms_coordinator.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Jenkins CI pipeline on hospital EC2: bandit + pylint -> pytest 90% coverage for all measure calculation pure functions using synthetic CMS fixture encounters -> deploy DAG .py files to MWAA S3 dags bucket -> Jenkins Input step displays measure specification version diff and requires manual quality director approval (Jenkins user input build step) -> MWAA picks up new DAG files within 30 seconds. MWAA environment update (e.g., Python dependency changes) follows separate runbook requiring maintenance window. Environments: dev (local Airflow via docker-compose with Snowflake dev warehouse pointing to de-identified synthetic encounters), staging (MWAA staging environment with Snowflake staging warehouse, prior-year real encounter data approved for testing by IRB), production (MWAA production, Snowflake production warehouse, live PHI). CMS reporting calendar triggers: EventBridge rules schedule DAG runs 60 days before each IQR submission deadline; quality_analyst also retains manual trigger capability for ad hoc test runs.
target_output.observability: CloudWatch MWAA dashboard: active worker count, task failure rate by DAG, average measure calculation duration by measure_id and specification_year. Airflow task logs in CloudWatch Logs group /mwaa/omop-quality-pipeline; log data protection policy configured to detect and mask encounter_id and CCN patterns in exported logs. SNS quality-alerts topic with quality_director email subscription: alerts for any DAG task failure (Airflow on_failure_callback), pre-submission validation critical error count > 0, denominator reconciliation deviation > 10%, and submission_batch not submitted within 7 days of CMS deadline (EventBridge rule checking submission_batches status). CloudWatch Alarm: Snowflake query duration p95 > 30 minutes during quarterly calculation run (indicates warehouse sizing issue).
target_output.cost_and_scaling: MWAA mw1.medium environment approximately $300/month base plus $0.25/worker-hour for autoscaling workers (quarterly peaks ~$150/quarter during calculation runs). Snowflake XL warehouse for quarterly calculation runs (approximately 4 hours per quarter at $32/hour Snowflake credits) approximately $500/year. Snowflake M warehouse for weekly reconciliation approximately $200/year. S3 storage for submission files (approximately 100 MB/year) negligible. Total estimated $500/month base, with quarterly spikes of $200 during calculation runs. Scaling to additional measures (PC-01, PSI-90) adds parallel DAG workers; Snowflake warehouse auto-suspend limits idle cost.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (8 weeks): SEP-1 sepsis bundle and VTE-1 VTE prophylaxis DAGs with denominator extraction, exclusion evaluation, numerator calculation; CMS flat-file submission package generation; pre-submission validation engine with CMS acceptance rules; submission_batches audit table with specification_year tracking; Okta SAML Airflow authentication; S3 SSE-KMS submission file storage; Jenkins CI pipeline with quality director approval gate; CloudWatch monitoring and SNS failure alerts. Phase 2 Additional Measures (6 weeks): PC-01 elective delivery and PSI-90 composite measure DAGs; denominator reconciliation weekly DAG; custom Airflow plugin approval and download workflow replacing manual email process; staging MWAA environment with prior-year encounter data. Phase 3 QRDA XML and Automation (6 weeks): QRDA III XML submission format generation; CMS QualityNet portal API integration for automated upload when API access granted; OQR outpatient quality reporting program measures; automated pre-submission error correction suggestions based on CMS acceptance rule failure patterns.
target_output.development_guidelines: Python 3.12 with mypy strict mode. All measure calculation logic in pure Python modules (no Airflow imports) under cms_measures/ package; these functions are independently unit-testable with pytest fixtures. pytest 90% coverage required for all cms_measures functions; tests use synthetic encounter DataFrames matching CMS measure specification published test cases. Airflow DAGs are thin orchestration wrappers importing from cms_measures; DAG files themselves do not contain measure logic. Snowflake SQL for denominator queries stored in program_specifications table, not hardcoded in DAGs; version-controlled via migration scripts. No PHI in any test fixtures; synthetic encounter IDs use UUIDs not matching real MRN patterns. Jenkins pipeline blocks on bandit findings of severity HIGH or CRITICAL. Quality director must review any change to cms_measures package functions via PR comment approval before Jenkins input step is enabled.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: CMS measure specification changes mid-fiscal year requiring retroactive denominator recalculation for already-submitted quarters. Mitigation: program_specifications table versioned by specification_year with effective_date; retroactive recalculation supported by re-triggering DAG with correct specification_year parameter and new batch_id. Risk: Snowflake source data completeness gaps (missing lab results or medication administration records) producing artificially low numerator rates that trigger CMS audit. Mitigation: denominator reconciliation DAG detects source data gaps 60 days before submission deadline; quality_director alerted immediately for investigation. Tradeoff: CMS flat-file format for MVP over QRDA XML simplifies initial implementation but requires manual QualityNet portal upload by coordinator; QRDA XML in Phase 3 enables future automated submission. Tradeoff: Airflow MWAA over self-managed Airflow adds $300/month cost but eliminates scheduler maintenance and provides managed autoscaling appropriate for quarterly batch workload.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Confirmed SEP-1 denominator population sourced from clinical_encounters Snowflake table (Epic ADT feed) joined to billing_claims for CCN verification. Confirmed flat-file format for MVP; QRDA XML deferred to Phase 3. Confirmed Jenkins input step as quality director approval mechanism; Airflow Sensor approach rejected due to indefinite wait blocking worker slots. Confirmed specification_year as required field on submission_batches; system will refuse to generate submission file if specification_year does not match an active program_specifications record.

metadata.notes: First-pass CMS IQR/VBP quality reporting automation pipeline on MWAA with Snowflake; cost_and_scaling section slightly thin relative to data complexity.
